title: SILVER LAKE — EMILY — 8-6-16
text:
# SILVER LAKE



## 1 · Program Snapshot

Chipinaw / Silver Lake Toured both Chip and SL at the same time. Glenn gave us the tour. He mentioned that typically he is more back office and Michael and David usually do the tours. We were first brought to their welcome center/lodge which was beautiful. Very nice looking camp overall. They have large garage doors in almost every building making it easy for cross ventilation. Both camps are on the same property. A dirt road in the middle separates them. Shared facilities: HORSES / CIRCUS / OUTDOOR ADVENTURE / NATURE / SKATE PARK / GYMNASTICS / HEALTH CENTER All other facilities are separate. Its almost like there are 4 camps: SL Girls, SL Boys, Chip Girls, Chip Boys. There are 4 camp moms, one for each “camp”. Once in a while they will have an evening activity with both camps by age group. At Free Play, siblings etc can meet in the middle of the two camps. A week before camp is new family day where the families come up to camp for the day to get acquainted. Chipinaw: 7 weeks only, 400 campers, co-ed, ages 8 – 15. 10 th grade CIT, 11 th grade, Waiter/Waitress. Color war is 5 days at the end of the summer. Silver Lake: 4 wks, 3 wks, or 7 wks, co-ed, ages 8 – 15, 1 st , 300, 2 nd , 200, about 70 kids are 7 wk. They do mix partial and full summer campers. Rookie week: Sun-Sat. $995, $400 goes towards enrollment if they sign up.



## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Glenn gave us the tour. He mentioned that typically he is more back office and Michael and David usually do the tours. Its almost like there are 4 camps: SL Girls, SL Boys, Chip Girls, Chip Boys. There are 4 camp moms, one for each “camp”. Chipinaw: 7 weeks only, 400 campers, co-ed, ages 8 – 15. 10 th grade CIT, 11 th grade, Waiter/Waitress. Silver Lake: 4 wks, 3 wks, or 7 wks, co-ed, ages 8 – 15, 1 st , 300, 2 nd , 200, about 70 kids are 7 wk. Religion: CHIP: About 98% of the kids are Jewish. SL: 1 st session 90% Jewish, 2 nd session (more international) 65% Jewish. No uniform for the campers but staff is always in staff shirts.



## 3 · Living Quarters

Bunks: 8-10 campers w/ 2-3 counselors per bunk. A lot of the bunks share a back room (toilets, sinks on both sides w/ showers in between) All the beds are wood and each bed has a closet next to it for clothing. There are also plastic drawers that they campers bring. Between every two beds is a divider which forms little areas. There are outlets by every bed. Every camper is given their own laundry bag. Turnaround time is 1 day. There are some bunks set up where there is a main lounge room and then 4 rooms off of it with about 6-8 kids in each. Signs were on a lot of the doors stressing NO NUTS in bunk. Once a week on laundry day, the cleaning people spray every mattress w/ Lysol.



## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Water activity: CHIP: 1 heated pool where all swim instruction takes place every until 7 th grade. S.L: just built new pool right next to the lake with a large water slide. TOWEL SERVICE FOR CHIP. SL will get it in 2017. The Lake is semi private and has 3 entrances. Silver Lake, CHIP and Fishing. There are 3 motorized boats out at any given time between the two camps. There are toys on both sides. Canoes, Kayaks, Paddle boards, Paddle boats, Sailboats, Tubing and Jet skiing.



## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

6 periods in the day: 2 group sport, 2 water, 2 electives which are chosen the day before for the following day. Once in a while they will have an evening activity with both camps by age group. At Free Play, siblings etc can meet in the middle of the two camps. Campers are able to have private \[gymnastics\] lessons during rest hour at no additional cost. Trips: Both camps go to Dorney Park. Each division also gets one night a week out of camp for evening activity. Older kids go to canteen every night after evening activity.



## 6 · Program Highlights

Shared facilities: HORSES / CIRCUS / OUTDOOR ADVENTURE / NATURE / SKATE PARK / GYMNASTICS / HEALTH CENTER. Gymnastics building is about 4 years old. It still seems brand new. They have all of the necessary equipment: balance beam, bars, vault, spring floor, tumble track. The room has a HUGE fan that keeps it very cool. Campers are able to have private lessons during rest hour at no additional cost. Shared between both camps. Arts & Crafts: CHIP: Huge building. Looks like an apartment building in Florida where all of the doors are outside and the hall way is outside. Each “room” is a different form of art including: Fine Arts, Needlecraft, Model building, Magic (shared w/ SL), Cooking room, Stained glass, T-shirt making, Leather, Beading, Ceramics w/ 4 potters wheels and a darkroom. It’s pretty extensive. This is where we saw the most action as far as kids and energy. There was music playing throughout and kids sitting on the ports outside doing art as well. S.L: A & C building is called SLAP – Silver Lake Art Pavilion: They are looking to redo this building. It’s much less impressive than CHIP. Right now It is basically a HUGE porch with all different sections for different kids of art and inside is wood working. With that said, the cooking program at S.L. takes place in the old canteen space. The kids were making apple pie. While the boys were waiting for it to bake, they were crocheting\! They are currently both sharing CHIP’s theater but in the process of restoring another building at S.L. Recently upgraded this facility and put in stadium seating. Media/Computers: Both camps have a similar building. CHIPs is twice the size (for twice as many campers). There are 10-20 computers that the kids use for digital editing. A couple of girls were making patterns for tote bags, stationary, and t-shirts. Both of the buildings have A/C. There is also a Radio Shack on both camps where campers play music through the speaker system. Sports/Competiton: CHIP is more competitive than SL. They are part of the Mountain League and play intercamp w/ the neighboring camps. Horseback riding is on the premise and no additional cost. Kids can take part in stable management for the horses as well. “the barn rats”. They take campers out of camp for Golf at no additional cost about 20 min away. Kids are able to get private tennis lessons for additional cost. Other than that there is soccer, basketball, softball, archery, a skate park, gaga pits, aerobic studio, ATV’s w a trail and mini ones for the little kids, Tennis, beach volleyball, flag football, lacrosse, spin and more. Looking to redo the Ropes Course for next year. Currently there is a zip line, high and low elements and a climbing wall. Trips: Both camps go to Dorney Park. Each division also gets one night a week out of camp for evening activity. They have longer trips for the older kids which differ by camp. (can see on website) Besides SL Art and a new ropes course, they are also looking to put a roof on the SL hockey rink.



## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

1 Doctor and 7 nurses on staff in the Health Center. There is A/C along w/ TVs in the rooms for the kids when they’re sick. Sullivan Hospital in the closest. They will take a Diabetic. Dining Hall: CHIP Boys side and girls side two separate dining halls connected by the kitchen the middle. They are still in the process of renovating it and building a huge porch outside where the older kids will sit. 1 seating. Rectangular tables. Separate chairs. Meals served family style by waiters/waitresses (former campers). This is a KOSHER camp. Separate dishes for meat and dairy. They can handle all allergies and a woman named Laura heads this up for both camps. Peanut butter is offered in small packets. The dining room is opened all day w/ fruit and drinks available. There is also a snack after 4 th period. Dining Hall: SL Similar to CHIP but smaller. A/C and Fans. Canteen: Two identical structures, one for each camp. Pavilion type building w/ no walls. Campers get canteen 5 x a week. It is included in the tuition and they are allowed 1 drink and 1 other item. They do have soda as an option. Older kids go to canteen every night after evening activity.



## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Very nice looking camp overall. We were first brought to their welcome center/lodge which was beautiful. They have large garage doors in almost every building making it easy for cross ventilation. Its almost like there are 4 camps: SL Girls, SL Boys, Chip Girls, Chip Boys. There are 4 camp moms, one for each “camp”. Religion: CHIP: There is a Friday night service where everyone wears white. Blessings at every meal. SL: 1 st session 90% Jewish, 2 nd session (more international) 65% Jewish. CHIP is more competitive than SL. There was music playing throughout \[A\&C\] and kids sitting on the ports outside doing art as well. While the boys were waiting for it to bake, they were crocheting\! No uniform for the campers but staff is always in staff shirts.



## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Buses are shared for the 2 camps: 5 LI, 1 NYC, 4 NJ (2 paramus, 2 marlboro), Van from MD and 75 kids from FL. Electronics: The campers are allowed one handheld. There is no wifi, but they can have movies, shows or games on them. They are only allowed to be used for a certain amount of time in the bunks and then they are put in a safe. 2 phone calls a summer or 1 per session at SL. Only Flat packages are allowed 9 x 12. A week before camp is new family day where the families come up to camp for the day to get acquainted. Rookie week: Sun-Sat. $995, $400 goes towards enrollment if they sign up. Bar/Bat Mitzvah tutoring is offered for $50. Kids are able to get private tennis lessons for additional cost. Horseback riding is on the premise and no additional cost. They take campers out of camp for Golf at no additional cost about 20 min away. Canteen is included in the tuition.



## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Silver Lake offers 4 wks, 3 wks, or 7 wks, co-ed, ages 8 – 15, with about 70 kids doing 7 wk, and they do mix partial and full summer campers. Both camps are on the same property with a dirt road in the middle, share major areas like HORSES / CIRCUS / OUTDOOR ADVENTURE / NATURE / SKATE PARK / GYMNASTICS / HEALTH CENTER, and SL just built a new pool next to the lake with a large water slide. The day runs 6 periods (2 group sport, 2 water, 2 electives chosen the day before), the program is KOSHER and can handle all allergies, and campers are allowed one handheld (no wifi) with limited use in bunks. SL is 1 st session \~90% Jewish and 2 nd session \~65% Jewish (more international), with buses from LI/NYC/NJ, a van from MD, and 75 kids from FL.



### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Welcome center/lodge was beautiful; very nice looking camp overall.
  - Large garage doors in almost every building make cross ventilation easy.
  - Shared high-end facilities (HORSES, CIRCUS, OUTDOOR ADVENTURE, SKATE PARK, GYMNASTICS, HEALTH CENTER).
  - Gymnastics building \~4 years old, fully equipped, HUGE fan; private lessons during rest hour at no additional cost.
  - Extensive A\&C at CHIP; music playing and lots of energy.
  - SL cooking program in the old canteen space; kids were making apple pie.
  - New SL pool by the lake with a large water slide; robust lake fleet (canoes, kayaks, SUPs, sailboats, tubing, jet skiing).
  - Health Center has A/C and TVs; 1 Doctor and 7 nurses; will take a Diabetic.
  - KOSHER operation with allergy management led by Laura; dining room open all day with fruit and drinks.
  - Trips to Dorney Park; weekly out-of-camp evening activity by division.



#### Lowlights

  - SL A\&C (SLAP) is much less impressive than CHIP and is slated for a redo; basically a huge porch with sections.
  - CHIP dining hall still in the process of renovating; building a huge porch for older kids.
  - Looking to redo the Ropes Course for next year.
  - SL will get towel service in 2017.
  - No wifi for campers; only one handheld with limited use.
  - Only flat packages (9 x 12) are allowed.



#### Similar Camps

  - Iroquois Springs
  - Timber Lake West
  - Lokanda
  - Indian Head
  - Canadensis



## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Chipinaw / Silver Lake Toured both Chip and SL at the same time. Glenn gave us the tour. He mentioned that typically he is more back office and Michael and David usually do the tours. We were first brought to their welcome center/lodge which was beautiful. Very nice looking camp overall. They have large garage doors in almost every building making it easy for cross ventilation. Both camps are on the same property. A dirt road in the middle separates them. Shared facilities: HORSES / CIRCUS / OUTDOOR ADVENTURE / NATURE / SKATE PARK / GYMNASTICS / HEALTH CENTER All other facilities are separate. Its almost like there are 4 camps: SL Girls, SL Boys, Chip Girls, Chip Boys. There are 4 camp moms, one for each “camp”. Once in a while they will have an evening activity with both camps by age group. At Free Play, siblings etc can meet in the middle of the two camps. A week before camp is new family day where the families come up to camp for the day to get acquainted. Chipinaw: ? 7 weeks only, 400 campers, co-ed, ages 8 – 15. 10 th grade CIT, 11 th grade, Waiter/Waitress. ? Also looking at Lokanda, Indian Head, Canadensis when they tour Chip. ? Color war is 5 days at the end of the summer. Silver Lake: ? 4 wks, 3 wks, or 7 wks, co-ed, ages 8 – 15, 1 st , 300, 2 nd , 200, about 70 kids are 7 wk. ? They do mix partial and full summer campers. ? Rookie week: Sun-Sat. $995, $400 goes towards enrollment if they sign up. ? Also looking at Iroquois Springs, Timber Lake West when they tour SL Buses are shared for the 2 camps: 5 LI, 1 NYC, 4 NJ (2 paramus, 2 marlboro), Van from MD and 75 kids from FL. There is no uniform for the campers but staff is always in staff shirts. 6 periods in the day: 2 group sport, 2 water, 2 electives which are chosen the day before for the following day. 1 Doctor and 7 nurses on staff in the Health Center. There is A/C along w/ TVs in the rooms for the kids when they’re sick. Sullivan Hospital in the closest. They will take a Diabetic. Electronics: The campers are allowed one handheld. There is no wifi, but they can have movies, shows or games on them. They are only allowed to be used for a certain amount of time in the bunks and then they are put in a safe. 2 phone calls a summer or 1 per session at SL. Only Flat packages are allowed 9 x 12. Religion: CHIP: There is a Friday night service where everyone wears white. Blessings at every meal. About 98% of the kids are Jewish. Bar/Bat Mitzvah tutoring is offered for $50. SL: 1 st session 90% Jewish, 2 nd session (more international) 65% Jewish Bunks: 8-10 campers w/ 2-3 counselors per bunk. A lot of the bunks share a back room (toilets, sinks on both sides w/ showers in between) All the beds are wood and each bed has a closet next to it for clothing. There are also plastic drawers that they campers bring. Between every two beds is a divider which forms little areas. There are outlets by every bed. Every camper is given their own laundry bag. Turnaround time is 1 day. There are some bunks set up where there is a main lounge room and then 4 rooms off of it with about 6-8 kids in each. Signs were on a lot of the doors stressing NO NUTS in bunk. Once a week on laundry day, the cleaning people spray every mattress w/ Lysol. Dining Hall: CHIP Boys side and girls side two separate dining halls connected by the kitchen the middle. They are still in the process of renovating it and building a huge porch outside where the older kids will sit. 1 seating. Rectangular tables. Separate chairs. Meals served family style by waiters/waitresses (former campers). This is a KOSHER camp. Separate dishes for meat and dairy. They can handle all allergies and a woman named Laura heads this up for both camps. Peanut butter is offered in small packets. The dining room is opened all day w/ fruit and drinks available. There is also a snack after 4 th period. Dining Hall: SL Similar to CHIP but smaller. A/C and Fans. Canteen: Two identical structures, one for each camp. Pavilion type building w/ no walls. Campers get canteen 5 x a week. It is included in the tuition and they are allowed 1 drink and 1 other item. They do have soda as an option. Older kids go to canteen every night after evening activity. Gymnastics building is about 4 years old. It still seems brand new. They have all of the necessary equipment: balance beam, bars, vault, spring floor, tumble track. The room has a HUGE fan that keeps it very cool. Campers are able to have private lessons during rest hour at no additional cost. Shared between both camps. Arts & Crafts: CHIP: Huge building. Looks like an apartment building in Florida where all of the doors are outside and the hall way is outside. Each “room” is a different form of art including: Fine Arts, Needlecraft, Model building, Magic (shared w/ SL), Cooking room, Stained glass, T-shirt making, Leather, Beading, Ceramics w/ 4 potters wheels and a darkroom. It’s pretty extensive. This is where we saw the most action as far as kids and energy. There was music playing throughout and kids sitting on the ports outside doing art as well. S.L: A & C building is called SLAP – Silver Lake Art Pavilion: They are looking to redo this building. It’s much less impressive than CHIP. Right now It is basically a HUGE porch with all different sections for different kids of art and inside is wood working. With that said, the cooking program at S.L. takes place in the old canteen space. The kids were making apple pie. While the boys were waiting for it to bake, they were crocheting\! They are currently both sharing CHIP’s theater but in the process of restoring another building at S.L. Recently upgraded this facility and put in stadium seating. Media/Computers: Both camps have a similar building. CHIPs is twice the size (for twice as many campers). There are 10-20 computers that the kids use for digital editing. A couple of girls were making patterns for tote bags, stationary, and t-shirts. Both of the buildings have A/C. There is also a Radio Shack on both camps where campers play music through the speaker system. Sports/Competiton: CHIP is more competitive than SL. They are part of the Mountain League and play intercamp w/ the neighboring camps. Horseback riding is on the premise and no additional cost. Kids can take part in stable management for the horses as well. “the barn rats”. They take campers out of camp for Golf at no additional cost about 20 min away. Kids are able to get private tennis lessons for additional cost. Other than that there is soccer, basketball, softball, archery, a skate park, gaga pits, aerobic studio, ATV’s w a trail and mini ones for the little kids, Tennis, beach volleyball, flag football, lacrosse, spin and more. Looking to redo the Ropes Course for next year. Currently there is a zip line, high and low elements and a climbing wall. Trips: Both camps go to Dorney Park. Each division also gets one night a week out of camp for evening activity. They have longer trips for the older kids which differ by camp. (can see on website) Water activity: CHIP: 1 heated pool where all swim instruction takes place every until 7 th grade. S.L: just built new pool right next to the lake with a large water slide. TOWEL SERVICE FOR CHIP. SL will get it in 2017. The Lake is semi private and has 3 entrances. Silver Lake, CHIP and Fishing. There are 3 motorized boats out at any given time between the two camps. There are toys on both sides. Canoes, Kayaks, Paddle boards, Paddle boats, Sailboats, Tubing and Jet skiing. Besides SL Art and a new ropes course, they are also looking to put a roof on the SL hockey rink.




### Author: EMILY

### Updated: 2025-08-27

title: SUMMIT TRAVEL — SHARI — 7-1-23
text:
# SUMMIT TRAVEL



## 1 · Program Snapshot

Summit Camp – Shari & Lisa B. toured by Shep Baum 1 July 2023. BASIC DATA – various session options; 2, 3, 4, 9 wk options. About 30 kids do full summer. About 250 campers at a time at sleepaway program. Ages: @ sleepaway camp 7–21, Travel Program 15–23 (Costa Rica, Greece & Italy, Royal Caribbean Cruise), Weekender during school year 13–21 (1 wknd/ month at camp, 5 days in FL @ Disney over New Years. Available for 1st time campers in addition to current campers), The Center program 18–23 (life skills, jobs, social interaction, independence). They also run similar programs in NYC and are expanding there. Additional Notes: They help a wide range and get referrals from Lee Mar and Akeela.



## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Has a 12 y/o daughter w/ learning disabilities and growth challenges (see story on website) so he and his wife bought Summit. He knows every child & staff member at camp IN DEPTH. See website for description of camper population. They also do have some neurotypical campers who may have social issues w/ no friends at other camps/were kicked out but through Summit’s programming and focus on social skills they find their happy place. They "just don’t fit in somewhere else" as Shep described similar to The Bad News Bears, wide-mixture of types of campers. They may be great with adults but it’s peers they struggle with. He CANNOT take: suicidal, psychiatric, schizophrenic, or physically harmful to self or others. Counselors are educators, studying education/special education. They have more staff than campers. Three seminars/wk for counselors. Staff members are closely evaluated whether they’re trying their hardest, have to be 100% there. He sent a staff member home not b/c he did anything wrong but Shep felt he wasn’t doing the best he could. He felt terrible having to do so but said he couldn’t take a chance and feels strongly about the camper care and attention given to the campers 24/7. Counselors return 3–4 years usually. Summit goals: having fun, gain independence and self esteem, work of social skills and executive functioning, making friends. He said they’re not going to change them, they help them gain life skills (which does ultimately help change them). He attended Echo Lake and took on the tradition of giving out Fuzzies at Summit during flagpole. They had 2 non-binary (bunked by gender at birth) and 2 transgender (bunked by how they identified) campers in 2023. He had the largest influx of 16 year olds in 2023 as 1st time campers and they did great. He says they’re the "surrounders of good people."



## 3 · Living Quarters

ACCOMODATIONS – traditional cabins, spacious, 6–8 campers w/ 3–4 staff members, electricity, fans, bunk beds have railings, facilities in bunk, laundry done weekly and comes back as a group sort and fold (life skill). The Center does their own laundry.



## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

AQUATICS – swimming lessons optional. Semi-private lake, banana boating but no other motorized activities. Aquapark, kayaks, canoes, row boats, no SUPS, heated pool.



## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

PROGRAMMING – 7 periods a day. Wake up to soft music. Have breakfast, chores, 2 periods, rest hour, lunch, 3 periods, other activities with a friend. Mostly single sex activities except at The Center and all are structured with some choice within the structures.



## 6 · Program Highlights

ACTIVITIES – The Cave, bounce houses, movie theatre, Chill Out Room for quiet time, salon for personal hygiene lessons, Life Skill Building w/ Home Ec, cooking, etc. Martial arts, yoga, videography, science, mini-golf and many more. CREATIVE – Engineering and edible engineering, 3d Printing, DJ mixing and editing, computers, green screen, woodworking, ceramics. ORIENTEERING – high ropes, low ropes, zip line, climbing wall, mountain biking, trampoline, archery, survival, OLS. NATURE – goats, chickens. TRIPS – some Delaware River row boating.



## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

MEDICAL – 6 nurses, virtual Dr, Wayne Memorial 12 miles away. Can take a diabetic probably but need to have in-depth chat with parents to assess if they can handle. RELIGIOUS LIFE – optional Fri PM candles and prayers by Shep, surprisingly a lot of kids attend. Saturday service offered but not every week. No traditional dinner, no transport to Church. DINING – industrial looking, very basic, air conditioned. Sit by cabin, two seatings, family style (older kids serve). They’re not kosher anymore but most of their meat is kosher b/c they’ve always shared delivery with kosher girls camp across lake so just kept it when he bought camp (have 3 kids who are kosher and parents send some food but they can provide also). Variety of options (vegan, pasta, salad bar, etc). Nut aware (serve WOW butter). Can handle allergies. 3 snacks/ day.



## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Having visited Summit under previous ownership, Lisa and I were extremely impressed and moved by the care, energy and genuine attention they are giving to their community. When one of the campers came up to Shep to say "thank you for making me feel good yesterday" we were teary and felt like he truly gave so much to his campers. We recommend seeing it for yourself to get the true essence of the program and the campers. He took on the tradition of giving out Fuzzies at Summit during flagpole. Shep has a dog that does roam freely (Oreo) and the kids love him.



## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Bus to camp, no buses home. Parents pick up at end of child’s session. Some chaperoned flights. COMMUNICATION – No phone calls home, one way email, limit time on electronics but no cellular or wifi capabilities. Packages allowed but no food (they open it and he joked that he eats it if they send). Post up to 400 photos daily, facial recognition. Mondays they send a movie from the night before. Shep asks that we NOT check the box on our leads for parent requesting no phone calls. He MUST speak with each parent to assess if the child will be a good fit. Perhaps put in comment session wait 1 wk to contact parent instead. Extra costs: Unknown.



## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Summit helps a wide range of campers who "just don’t fit in somewhere else," with a structured 7-period day and a focus on social skills, executive functioning, independence, and making friends. They have more staff than campers, counselors are educators/special educators, and Shep knows every child and staff member IN DEPTH. Ages span 7–21 at sleepaway with additional programs for 13–23 (Weekender, The Center) and 15–23 travel options, with 2, 3, 4, and 9 week sessions (about 250 campers at a time; \~30 do full summer). Communication is tightly managed (no phone calls home; one-way email), travel is bus to camp with parent pick-up at session end, and they post up to 400 photos daily with facial recognition.



### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Care, energy and genuine attention to the community; a camper thanked Shep for "making me feel good yesterday."
  - More staff than campers; counselors are educators/studying special education; three seminars/wk; strong accountability.
  - Summit goals: fun, independence, self esteem, social skills/executive functioning, making friends.
  - Broad program menu (life skills, engineering/3D printing, ropes/zip, aquatics with heated pool, semi-private lake).
  - Medical coverage: 6 nurses, virtual Dr; Wayne Memorial 12 miles away.
  - Dining is nut aware with lots of options; air-conditioned dining hall.
  - Programs beyond camp: Travel (15–23), Weekender, The Center (18–23).
  - Traditions and culture (Fuzzies at flagpole; Oreo the camp dog).



#### Lowlights

  - Bus to camp only; parents must pick up at end of session.
  - No phone calls home; limited electronics; no cellular or wifi capabilities.
  - He CANNOT take suicidal, psychiatric, schizophrenic, or physically harmful to self or others.
  - Dining hall is industrial looking, very basic.
  - Info gaps from tour (e.g., specifics like where buses are from).
  - Not kosher; most meat is kosher but formal kosher support is limited (parents may send some food).
  - No SUPS; no other motorized lake activities beyond banana boating.



#### Similar Camps

  - Lee Mar
  - Akeela



## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

title: TASIS ENGLAND — SHARI — 2-8-24
text:
# TASIS ENGLAND



## 1 · Program Snapshot

Marc-Pierre Jansen. Located in Italian speaking Southern part of Switzerland—1 hr from Milan. Airport mostly used is Milan Malpensa (50 mins away). Zurich train about 2 hrs away. Parents mostly fly w/ students and bring them to the grounds, but the program can provide transportation. 40–45 nationalities represented at the program each year. Two sessions offered. Fees include everything upon arrival EXCEPT spending money, travel to & from the program and some optional sports if chosen. Boarding and day offered at all age levels except Minnows (described below). Why TASIS Switzerland? - All students take academics. Language and Enrichment. - Activities resemble summer camp: water activities, hikes, evening activities, games. - Travel woven in on weekends. - Performing arts woven in for all (ie: singing, learn ukulele, musical production performed on last night for parents). 4 Programs: High School (14–17 y/o), Middle School (11–13 y/o), Elementary School Chateau Des Enfants (7–10 y/o), Minnows (4–6 y/o, day only).



## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

High School (14–17 y/o) enrolls about 180 students total (some years 165–170). Middle School (11–13 y/o) is about 130 students. Elementary School Chateau Des Enfants (7–10 y/o) is about 100 students (70% leave at the end of the day so around 30 boarders). Minnows (4–6 y/o) is about 30–40 students and is day only. 40–45 nationalities represented each year. Teachers are mostly American and Canadian; some from Italy and France as well. 12–14 students per class in the academic courses. Students take 1 major and 1 minor per session; they can change courses within the 1st week if they’re really unhappy if there’s still space in other classes. Classes are pretty academic but don’t get credit b/c they’re not there long enough (½ credit at some schools available but very rare). They must have proficiency in English language unless they’re coming specifically for ESL. They used to offer 6 levels of English, now they only offer 3. Les Tapies – Fernando Gonzales – 12–18 y/o (mostly 14–16 and can do 18 year old as long as they understand there is a lot of structure). Teachers there are professionals or practicing artists and teachers; 8 staff to 27 students.



## 3 · Living Quarters

Boarding and day offered at all age levels except Minnows. Minnows operates about 100 yards from main campus (day only). High School reports to mentors at 7:30 pm; dorm and lights out 11 pm. Middle School has family style dinner and lights out 10 pm. Elementary has bedtime circle and goodnight in own language, chocolate and milk, lights out 9 pm. Les Tapies housing: live in 6 separate houses w/ 4–5 students per house and 2 students per room.



## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Activities resemble summer camp with water activities. Elementary PM activities include choice of swim. Weekends for Elementary are relaxed on campus with activities available such as paddleboarding. Trips may include beach days.



## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

High School: Morning all assemble for orientation and announcements for the day, choose a course (ESL, writing, French, shark tank-like class, fine arts, musical theatre, digital photography, filmmaking, architecture, fashion cooking, intro to investing, STEAM technology) and do it for 3–3.5 hrs (12–14 students per class). Lunch. Minor chosen and all taught by teachers who are passionate about subject area but not as intense as the morning classes. Sports in afternoon 2x/wk. Dinner. Report to mentors at 7:30, evening activity, cultural night 1x/wk, dorm and lights out 11 pm. Weekends – day trips in Switzerland and Northern Italy in groups of \~20 students to each location (eg: tour of old city in Milan, see The Last Supper; or train to Zurich, attend museum, chocolate factory, or FIFA Museum and then back for dinner). Free time is limited and lots of check-ins with staff. Optional trips offered – extra cost – Italy (Venice & Rome, Florence) & Switzerland (Interlaken & Bernese). All offered both sessions. Middle School: Same structure as HS program but fewer offerings (runs completely separate from HS; may be some overlap w/ talented MS athletes to play w/ HSers but very few). Family style dinner, sit together & facilitate conversation & community. Lights out 10 pm. Trips offered but more curtailed than HS trips. Elementary (Chateau Des Enfants): Nurturing; all choose a language (English, French, Italian). Morning meeting, language classes, sports, lunch family style by language (sit w/ teacher), rest time, singing class, STEAM hour class in language they’re studying, art courses, PM activities (choice of swim, arts, STEAM, etc). Older kids in musical theatre class. Bedtime circle and goodnight in own language, chocolate and milk, lights out 9 pm. Weekends – no trips, but travel curtailed for age group on Saturdays (Luzerne transport museum, beach, etc) and Sundays relaxed on campus with activities available (paddleboarding, hb riding, hanging out, etc). Minnows (day only): Kids enjoy music, languages (English, French, Italian), outdoor activities, rest time, lunch, story telling; most leave at 3 pm, some stay and attend elementary program until 5 pm. Academics: Students take 1 major and 1 minor per session and can change in week 1 if space. Classes are academic but generally for enrichment; ½ credit at some schools is rare. Les Tapies (3 weeks): Unique, small program focused ONLY on painting & drawing (most popular, about 50% of students), architecture (Fernando is the teacher), and photography. 27 students max; fills in May usually. 8–10 hrs/day 3x/wk in major (they equate 3 wks of work to a whole year of school work: \~4 hrs in the morning and \~3 hrs in the afternoon and some evenings). Minors are 3 hrs 3x/wk and give students the opportunity to try other things outside their major. Some evenings they’ll watch films in their subject area or attend lectures. They arrive on Saturday and go right to the open market to immerse themselves in the community. Excursions devoted to augmenting what they learn in the classroom.



## 6 · Program Highlights

Why TASIS Switzerland? All students take academics with Language and Enrichment. Activities resemble summer camp (water activities, hikes, evening activities, games). Travel is woven in on weekends. Performing arts woven in for all (singing, learn ukulele), with a musical production performed on the last night for parents. Cultural night 1x/wk. Family style dinners for Middle School and by-language lunches for Elementary help facilitate conversation and community. Elementary includes STEAM hour and art courses, with older kids in musical theatre class. Optional trips (extra cost) to Italy (Venice, Rome, Florence) and Switzerland (Interlaken & Bernese) are offered both sessions. Les Tapies is a unique, small program focused only on painting & drawing, architecture, and photography; lots of time spent outdoors with natural landscapes; hand-on, serious students learning in-depth in their subject area; passionate students of the arts improve ability while there.



## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Free time is limited and there are lots of check-ins with staff; HS students report to mentors at 7:30 pm. Family style meals are part of the program (MS family style dinner; Elementary lunch family style by language). Elementary bedtime circle and goodnight in own language, chocolate and milk. Les Tapies: Food is outstanding, home cooked, local ingredients; allergies and specific food needs accommodated easily. Medical at Les Tapies: meds given out by staff member; doctor is 5 min away in town; hospital 45 min away.



## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Nurturing, structured, and internationally diverse (40–45 nationalities). Performing arts and cultural experiences are woven in (cultural night weekly; musical production on the last night). Family style meals facilitate conversation & community. Students feel accomplished and excited about their subject areas upon completion. Les Tapies emphasizes that they are not tourists, they’re students; the students pick Les Tapies because they want to be there; passionate students of the arts, with lots of time outdoors and immersion (open market on arrival Saturday).



## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Located in Italian speaking Southern part of Switzerland—1 hr from Milan. Airport mostly used is Milan Malpensa (50 mins away). Zurich train about 2 hrs away. Parents mostly fly w/ students and bring them to the grounds, but the program can provide transportation. Two sessions offered. Fees include everything upon arrival EXCEPT spending money, travel to & from the program and some optional sports if chosen. Optional trips offered at extra cost. Parent communication: musical production performed on the last night for parents. Tech policy: Unknown.



## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

International, structured summer programs with strong academics plus language and enrichment, delivered in an Italian-speaking part of Switzerland with weekend travel and performing arts woven in. High School, Middle School, Elementary (Chateau Des Enfants), and Minnows each have age-appropriate schedules, small classes, family-style community moments, and lots of staff check-ins. Optional cultural day trips and extra-cost weekend excursions complement academics, STEAM, and a culminating musical production for families. Les Tapies offers a unique, small, in-depth 3-week fine arts option (painting/drawing, architecture, photography) with outstanding food and professional instructors.



### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - 40–45 nationalities represented each year; truly international community.
  - Travel woven into weekends with well-structured day trips to Switzerland and Northern Italy.
  - Performing arts for all, including a musical production on the last night for parents.
  - Small academic classes (12–14) and passionate teachers; majors/minors allow choice.
  - Les Tapies: serious, in-depth arts study with professional staff and outstanding, locally sourced food.
  - Strong structure and supervision (mentors, frequent check-ins); family-style meals build community.



#### Lowlights

  - The only criticism students and parents have of Les Tapies is that it’s not long enough.
  - Free time is limited.
  - Must have proficiency in English unless coming specifically for ESL; ESL levels reduced from 6 to 3.
  - Optional trips are extra cost; travel to and from the program is not included.



#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

title: TASIS FRANCE — SHARI — 2-8-24
text:
# TASIS FRANCE



## 1 · Program Snapshot

Located in Italian speaking Southern part of Switzerland - 1hr from Milan. Airport mostly used is Milan Malpensa (50mins away). Zurich train about 2hrs away. Parents mostly fly w/ students and bring them to the grounds, but the program can provide transportation. 40-45 nationalities represented at the program each year. Two sessions offered. Fees include everything upon arrival EXCEPT spending money, travel to & from the program and some optional sports if chosen. Boarding and day offered at all age levels except Minnows (described below).
Why TASIS Switzerland? - All students take academics. Language and Enrichment. - Activities resemble summer camp: water activities, hikes, evening activities, games. - Travel woven in on weekends. - Performing arts woven in for all (ie: singing, learn ukulele, musical production performed on last night for parents).
Les Tapies – south of Lyon, France. Valence is the closest city (45 min away) and train stops there for pick ups. Airport is in Lyon 2hrs away, some parents drop off at program. Unique, small program focused ONLY on painting & drawing, architecture, and photography. 27 students max. 3 wks only.



## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Marc-Pierre Jansen.
4 Programs:
High School (14-17 y/o) (about 180 students total but some years 165-170) – boarding & day available.
Middle School (11-13 y/o) (about 130 students) – boarding & day available.
Elementary School Chateau Des Enfants (7-10 y/o) (about 100 students, 70% leave at the end of the day so around 30 boarders) – boarding & day available.
Minnows (4-6 y/o) – (about 30-40 students) – day only.
Teachers are mostly American and Canadian. Some from Italy and France as well. Students take 1 major and 1 minor per session. They can change courses within the 1st week if they’re really unhappy if there’s still space in other classes. Classes are pretty academic but don’t get credit because they’re not there long enough. 1/2 credit at some schools available but very rare. Just focused on enrichment, cultural experience, and grammatical improvement. The students feel accomplished and excited about their subject areas upon completion. They must have proficiency in English language unless they’re coming specifically for ESL. They used to offer 6 levels of English, now they only offer 3.
Les Tapies – Fernando Gonzales – 12–18-y/o (mostly 14-16 and can do 18 year old as long as they understand there is a lot of structure). Teachers are professionals or practicing artists and teachers. 27 students max. 8 staff to 27 students. Nine nationalities represented but 2/3 American, the rest from several other countries.



## 3 · Living Quarters

High School and Middle School: dorm and lights out 11pm (HS) and 10pm (MS).
Elementary: bedtime circle and goodnight in own language, chocolate and milk, lights out 9pm.
Les Tapies: Live in 6 separate houses with 4-5 students per house and 2 students per room.



## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Activities resemble summer camp: water activities. Elementary PM activities include choice of swim. Weekends for Elementary: Saturdays travel curtailed for age group (beach, etc) and Sundays are relaxed on campus with activities available (paddleboarding, hb riding, hanging out, etc).



## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

High School (14-17 y/o): Morning all assemble for orientation and announcements for the day, choose a course (ESL, writing, French, shark tank-like class, fine arts, musical theatre, digital photography, filmmaking, architecture, fashion, cooking, intro to investing, STEAM technology), do for 3-3.5 hrs. 12-14 students per class. Lunch. Minor chosen and all taught by teachers who are passionate about subject area but not as intense as the morning classes. Sports in afternoon 2x/week. Dinner. Report to mentors at 7:30, evening activity, cultural night 1x/wk, dorm and lights out 11pm. Weekends – day trips in Switzerland and Northern Italy, groups of 20 or so students to each location ie: tour of old city in Milan, see The Last Supper. Or train to Zurich, attend museum, chocolate factory, or FIFA Museum and then back for dinner. Free time is limited and lots of check-ins with staff. Optional trips offered – extra cost – Italy (Venice & Rome, Florence) & Switzerland (Interlaken & Bernese). All offered both sessions.
Middle School (11-13 y/o): Same structure as HS program but fewer offerings, all on website. Runs completely separate from HS program. May only be some overlap w/ talented MS athletes to play w/ HSers but very few. Family style dinner, sit together & facilitate conversation & community. Lights out 10pm. Trips offered but more curtailed than HS trips.
Elementary School Chateau Des Enfants (7-10 y/o): Nurturing, all choose a language (English, French, Italian). Morning meeting, language classes, sports, lunch family style by language (sit w/ teacher), rest time, singing class, STEAM hour class in language they’re studying, art courses, PM activities (choice of swim, arts, STEAM, etc). Older kids in musical theatre class. Bedtime circle and goodnight in own language, chocolate and milk, lights out 9pm. Weekends no trips but travel curtailed for age group on Saturdays (Luzerne transport museum, beach, etc) and Sundays are relaxed on campus with activities available (paddleboarding, hb riding, hanging out, etc).
Minnows (4-6 y/o): Operates about 100 yards from main campus. Kids enjoy music, languages (English French Italian), outdoor activities, rest time, lunch, story telling, most leave at 3pm, some stay and attend elementary program until 5pm.
Course selection: Students take 1 major and 1 minor per session; they can change within the first week if there’s still space.
Les Tapies (12–18 y/o): 8-10 hrs/day 3x/wk in major (it is a full-fledged art program, and they equate 3 wks of work to a whole year of school work, 4 hrs in the morning and 3 hrs in the afternoon and some evenings). Minors are 3 hrs 3x/wk and give students the opportunity to try other things outside their major. In architecture he breaks up the 3 wks into restoration, new building, and teamwork. Some evenings they’ll watch films in their subject area or attend lectures. Excursions devoted to augmenting what they learn in the classroom. They arrive on Saturday and go right to the open market to immerse themselves in the community.



## 6 · Program Highlights

\- All students take academics. Language and Enrichment.
\- Activities resemble summer camp: water activities, hikes, evening activities, games.
\- Travel woven in on weekends.
\- Performing arts woven in for all (ie: singing, learn ukulele, musical production performed on last night for parents).
Les Tapies: Unique, small program focused ONLY on painting & drawing (most popular about 50% of students), architecture (Fernando is the teacher), and photography. Lots of time spent outdoors with natural landscapes. In drawing and painting they use many mediums (oil, watercolor, etc) and work on landscapes, still life etc. Hand on, serious students learning in-depth in their subject area. The students pick Les Tapies because they want to be there. Passionate students of the arts, improve ability while there. Excursions devoted to augmenting what they learn in the classroom. Not tourists, they’re students.



## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Free time is limited and lots of check-ins with staff.
Middle School: Family style dinner, sit together & facilitate conversation & community. Elementary: lunch family style by language (sit w/ teacher).
Les Tapies food is outstanding, home cooked, local ingredients, allergies and specific food needs accommodated easily. Medical – meds given out by staff member. Dr is 5 min away in town. Hospital 45 min away.
Other health, safety, and food details: Unknown.



## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Nurturing for Elementary. Performing arts woven in for all and a musical production performed on last night for parents. Cultural night 1x/wk. The students feel accomplished and excited about their subject areas upon completion. They arrive on Saturday and go right to the open market to immerse themselves in the community. Passionate students of the arts at Les Tapies. Not tourists, they’re students.



## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Located in Italian speaking Southern part of Switzerland - 1hr from Milan. Airport mostly used is Milan Malpensa (50mins away). Zurich train about 2hrs away. Parents mostly fly w/ students and bring them to the grounds, but the program can provide transportation. Optional trips offered – extra cost – Italy (Venice & Rome, Florence) & Switzerland (Interlaken & Bernese). Fees include everything upon arrival EXCEPT spending money, travel to & from the program and some optional sports if chosen. Boarding and day offered at all age levels except Minnows (day only).
Les Tapies – south of Lyon, France. Valence is the closest city (45 min away) and train stops there for pick ups. Airport is in Lyon 2hrs away, some parents drop off at program. They arrive on Saturday and go right to the open market to immerse themselves in the community.
Parent communication: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown. Extra costs beyond those noted: Unknown.



## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Located in the Italian speaking Southern part of Switzerland (1 hr from Milan) and at Les Tapies in south of Lyon, France, the programs draw 40–45 nationalities and offer two sessions with boarding and day options (Minnows day only). All students take academics with Language and Enrichment, activities that resemble summer camp, travel woven in on weekends, and performing arts woven in for all with a musical production on the last night for parents. High School, Middle School, and Elementary follow structured days with majors and minors, family-style meals in some divisions, and limited free time with lots of check-ins with staff. Les Tapies is a unique, small, full-fledged art program (painting & drawing, architecture, photography; 27 students max, 3 weeks) with professional teachers, rigorous studio time, and excursions devoted to augmenting what they learn in the classroom.



### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - 40-45 nationalities represented at the program each year.
  - Two sessions offered.
  - Boarding and day offered at all age levels except Minnows.
  - All students take academics. Language and Enrichment.
  - Activities resemble summer camp; travel and performing arts woven in.
  - Students take 1 major and 1 minor per session (can change in week 1 if space).
  - Family style meals in Middle School and Elementary.
  - Optional cultural night 1x/wk and musical production performed on last night for parents.
  - Les Tapies: unique, small (27 students max), serious, in-depth arts focus with professional teachers.
  - Les Tapies food is outstanding, home cooked, local ingredients; allergies and specific food needs accommodated easily.
  - 8 staff to 27 students at Les Tapies.
  - Excursions devoted to augmenting classroom learning; not tourists, they’re students.



#### Lowlights

  - The only criticism the students and parents have is that it's not long enough.
  - Classes are pretty academic but don’t get credit because they’re not there long enough; 1/2 credit at some schools available but very rare.
  - They must have proficiency in English unless they’re coming specifically for ESL.
  - They used to offer 6 levels of English, now they only offer 3.
  - Free time is limited and lots of check-ins with staff.
  - Minnows (4-6 y/o) is day only.
  - Optional trips offered at extra cost (Italy & Switzerland).
  - Fees exclude spending money, travel to & from the program, and some optional sports.
  - Les Tapies fills in May usually.



#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

title: TAUNTON SCHOOL — LAURA TOLEDO — 1-12-24
text:
# TAUNTON SCHOOL



## 1 · Program Snapshot

Taunton Summer School is an academic program with co-curricular activities and excursions. Taunton School is based in the South West of England, in Somerset. The campus is all in one site, where the Prep School, the Senior School and the International School are located. The summer program is using the premises of the Prep School and the Senior School and students have access to the sports facilities of all schools (including 2 Olympic swimming pools, football pitches, sports fields, tennis courts, etc). They offer two summer programs: Junior Program from 8 to 12 years old and Senior Program from 13 to 17 years old. The summer programs start on June 29th for periods of two weeks. They also offer a Pre Sessional Program at the end of August, ideal for students who will integrate the English system from September. This can be a 10 or 15 day program.



## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

The teachers and staff of the summer programs are from Taunton School. They have a cap of 10% per nationality/spoken language. They do a placement test on arrival to put children in the right course. They have small classes (maximum 12 students but ideally 8).



## 3 · Living Quarters

Boarding Facilities: They use boarding houses from the Prep and Senior Schools. In the Junior program students typically share rooms for 3, whereas in the Senior program they can share a room in 2 or 3 or have a single room. They typically try to mix nationalities in the rooms to encourage students to speak English.



## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Students have access to the sports facilities of all schools, including 2 Olympic swimming pools.



## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Taunton Summer School is a "Boarding school" like experience with lessons from Monday to Friday, activities and 3 excursions a week (Wednesday afternoon, Saturday and Sunday all day, except arrival and departure day). English lessons are up to 20 h/week for the Junior course and 25 h/week for the Senior course. They offer two different English courses: Core English (corresponding to level from A1 to B2) and Advanced English (corresponding to level C1-C2). Activities are different for Junior course and Senior Course. Junior course activities are to be chosen between: Academic, Multi Sports, Cookery and STEM (these activities take place in the afternoons from Monday to Friday). Students can choose one activity per week. Senior course activities are to be chosen between: Academic, Future Business Leaders, Performing Arts, STEM, English Enrichment, Tennis Academy, Football Academy (these activities take place in the mornings from Monday to Friday). The Pre Sessional Program can be a 10 or 15 day program. It consists of 3 hours of English + 2 hours of Study Skills per day + activities and sports.



## 6 · Program Highlights

English learning is the core of their program. All on one campus with access to the Prep, Senior and International School facilities. Activities and 3 excursions a week complement lessons for a "Boarding school" like experience.



## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Unknown



## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Taunton Summer School is an academic program with co - curricular activities and excursions. Taunton School is based in the South West of England, in Somerset. The closest towns are Bristol and Exeter and London is 1h 40 minutes away. They offer free transfers from Bristol Airport and London Airports. The school is only 10 minutes walking distance from Taunton village and close to beaches and historical cities. The campus is all in one site, where the Prep School, the Senior School and the International School are located. The summer program is using the premises of the Prep School and the Senior school and students have access to the sports facilities of all schools (including 2 Olympic swimming pools, football pitches, sports fields, tennis courts, etc). The teachers and staff of the summer programs are from Taunton School. They offer two summer programs: - Junior Program from 8 to 12 years’ old - Senior Program from 13 to 17 years’ old The summer programs start on June 29 th for periods of two weeks. They have a cap of 10% per nationality/spoken language. They also offer a Pre Sessional Program at the end of August, ideal for students who will integrate the English system from September. This can be a 10 or 15 days program. It consists of 3 hours of English + 2 hours of Study Skills per day + activities and sports Taunton Summer School is a “Boarding school” like experience with lessons from Monday to Friday, activities and 3 excursions a week (Wednesday afternoon, Saturday and Sunday all day, except arrival and departure day). English lessons are up to 20 h/week for the Junior course and 25h/week for the Senior course. They offer two different English courses: Core English (corresponding to level from A1 to B2) Advanced English (corresponding to level C1 -C2) English learning is the core of their program. They do a placement test on arrival to put children in the right course. They have small classes (maximum 12 students but ideally 8). Activities are different for Junior course and Senior Course. Junior course activities are to be chosen between: Academic, Multi Sports, Cookery and STEM (these activities take place in the afternoons from Monday to Friday). Students can choose one activity per week. Senior course activities are to be chosen between: Academic, Future Business Leaders, Performing Arts, STEM, English Enrichment, tennis Academy, Footrball Academy (these activities take place in the mornings from Monday to Friday) Boarding Facilities: They use boarding houses from the Prep and Senior Schools. In the Junior program students typically share rooms for 3, whereas in the Senior program they can share a room in 2 or 3 or have a single room. They typically try to mix nationalities in the rooms to encourage students to speak English.

title: TANUGA — RENEE — 4-15-17
text:
# TANUGA



## 1 · Program Snapshot

Co-ed, currently about 200 campers. Would like 230. Can do 2 weeks (youngest campers – 1 year only), 3 weeks (1 st session) or 4 weeks (2 nd session).



## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Summer 2017 Tour with Renee & Margaret. Toured by Lindsay Rabinowitz – Unit Director. Director/part owner is Mark Codan - nicknamed Cody. Campers are primarily from the Detroit Metropolitan area with sprinklings from all over. New campers can feel comfortable here at just about any age.



## 3 · Living Quarters

10 kids per bunk/ 2 counselors. 2 toilets, 2 showers and 2 sinks per cabin.



## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

All water sports, strong skiing, sailing, etc.



## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Activities: 4 activities per day plus one optional/free activity period. Counselors are instructors.



## 6 · Program Highlights

Tanuga is a very spirited camp with tons of traditions. Big tripping program\! Tripping: All cabins do one trip with their cabin per session. Also offers a large array of optional trips such as rock climbing, kayaking, etc. One of the only Midwest camps we have seen with a trapeze. Offers horseback riding, both ring and trail riding. Ropes course for 12 years old and up.



## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Mess Hall: Small and intimate with Long rectangle tables. Campers eat family style and they can accommodate every allergy. PB Free. Very loud and spirited\!\! Wellness Ctr: 1 dr. and 2 nurses at all times. Hospital in Kalkaska and better hospital in Traverse City.



## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Tanuga is a very spirited camp with tons of traditions. New campers can feel comfortable here at just about any age. Very loud and spirited\!\! Margaret’s daughters went to Tanuga and LOVED It\!



## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Located in Kalkaska, MI about 7 hours from Chicago or about 3 ½ hours from Detroit, MI airport. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown. Extra costs: Unknown.



## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Co-ed camp in Kalkaska, MI, currently about 200 campers (would like 230). Sessions can be 2 weeks (youngest campers – 1 year only), 3 weeks (1st session), or 4 weeks (2nd session). Very spirited with tons of traditions and a big tripping program (all cabins do one trip per session, plus optional trips like rock climbing and kayaking), and one of the only Midwest camps with a trapeze. Strong water sports (skiing, sailing), horseback riding (ring and trail), ropes course for 12+, and a daily schedule of 4 activities plus an optional/free period.



### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Very spirited camp with tons of traditions.
  - Big tripping program; all cabins do one trip with their cabin per session.
  - Large array of optional trips such as rock climbing and kayaking.
  - One of the only Midwest camps we have seen with a trapeze.
  - Strong water sports, skiing, sailing.
  - Offers horseback riding, both ring and trail riding.
  - Activities: 4 activities per day plus one optional/free activity period.
  - PB Free; can accommodate every allergy.
  - Wellness Ctr: 1 dr. and 2 nurses at all times; nearby hospitals in Kalkaska and Traverse City.



#### Lowlights

  - Ropes course for 12 years old and up.
  - Youngest campers can do 2 weeks (1 year only).
  - Currently about 200 campers; would like 230.
  - Located about 7 hours from Chicago.
  - This is the first summer we are working with them.



#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

Campers are primarily from the Detroit Metropolitan area with sprinklings from all over. New campers can feel comfortable here at just about any age. Big tripping program\! One of the only Midwest camps we have seen with a trapeze. Tripping: All cabins do one trip with their cabin per session. Also offers a large array of optional trips such as rock climbing, kayaking, etc. Activities: 4 activities per day plus one optional/free activity period. Counselors are instructor. Offers horseback riding, both ring and trail riding Ropes course for 12 years old and up All water sports, strong skiing, sailing, etc. Mess Hall: Small and intimate with Long rectangle tables. Campers eat family style and they can accommodate every allergy. PB Free Very loud and spirited\!\! Wellness Ctr: 1 dr. and 2 nurses at all times. title: SUMMERLAND CAMPS — HEATHER — 12-14-23
text:
# SUMMERLAND CAMPS



## 1 · Program Snapshot

Director: Dr. Michael Bishop. Location: 2024-Final Summer Summerland shares grounds with Pocono Trails (PA), weight loss program-90 minutes from NYC and Philadelphia. 2025 and beyond, seeking to purchase new facilities near Tampa, Florida (closest to Tampa Airport). Therapeutic Camp program focuses on treatment for addiction (overuse, misuse) to video games, social media, cell phone, internet, all screens (quantity and quality) which often coincides with anxiety/depression, self-regulation issues, poor social-skills and declining school performance. Summer Program length 1-7 weeks- FUN outdoor adventure including, hiking, camping, surfing, snorkeling, fishing, mountain biking, boating, and wilderness survival while teaching necessary life skills. Also offers Thanksgiving, Winter, Spring and Pre-Camp programs. Spring Break trips are offered at various weeks so will fit for many different school district calendars. Using a Harm Reduction approach, the program seeks to improve social interaction, mental health, problem-solving skills, confidence building, executive functioning, time management, cognitive benefits, build healthy habits and moderation.



## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Enrollment: Kids 7-11 years old being enrolled in 2024 ONLY at Pocono Trails. Considered an Insight Oriented program, which typical kids of that age group cannot necessarily understand so benefits them the least. 2025 and beyond enrolling boys and girls ages 12-17, and young adults ages 18-21 (usually learn independent living skills, self-regulation and typically engage fully). Mostly males enroll. 60% have been diagnosed with ADHD, 1/5 high functioning Autism-lack meaningful daily activities. The typical camper was previously successful in school when the work was less difficult, but now has to put in more hours and focus. They are showing lazy behavior and attitude and becoming disenfranchised with school, looking for immediate gratification. If a child is not addicted to screens but is still becoming less successful in school, they would benefit from this program. Director: Dr. Michael Bishop. Field Staff-Career Professionals including Nurses, EMT’s and Licensed Mental Health practitioners.



## 3 · Living Quarters

Summerland shares grounds with Pocono Trails (PA), weight loss program-90 minutes from NYC and Philadelphia. 2025 and beyond, seeking to purchase new facilities near Tampa, Florida (closest to Tampa Airport). Housing details: Unknown.



## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Surfing, snorkeling, fishing, boating.



## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Groups are conducted up to twice per day, 12 sessions during a typical 6-night, 7-day adventure course. Choice structure: Unknown.



## 6 · Program Highlights

FUN outdoor adventure including hiking, camping, mountain biking, and wilderness survival while teaching necessary life skills. They utilize teamwork, cooperation, and communication. Young people can develop valuable social skills, make new friends, and learn to work together to achieve common goals. Goals often include improved concentration and improved grades. Teaches elite mindset, taking responsibility, positive attributes, and time management. Behavior Coaching (Individual and Group) - After kids return home, Summerland offers a step- down program (with the same counselor they saw throughout the program 1-3 times/week) to continue self-regulation and embrace structure upon reentry.



## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Behavior coaches and licensed therapy staff accompany the adventure trips and are present 24/7. Field Staff-Career Professionals including Nurses, EMT’s and Licensed Mental Health practitioners. A clinical review is required before acceptance into the program. They do not accept individuals that would interfere with the positive experience of other participants. Food excellent.



## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Non- competitive environment, group activities, no pressure to be the best. These are inclusive activities. Using a Harm Reduction approach, build healthy habits and moderation. Kids are not expected to become disinterested in screens as they will likely build careers in corresponding fields.



## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Location/travel: 2024 at Pocono Trails (PA), 90 minutes from NYC and Philadelphia. 2025 and beyond, seeking to purchase new facilities near Tampa, Florida (closest to Tampa Airport). Parent feedback: 100 Parents polled, 92% recommend. Tech policy and parent communication: Unknown. Costs: No financial extras except recommended $50 spending money for snacks.



## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Summerland is a Therapeutic Camp program focusing on treatment for addiction (overuse, misuse) to video games, social media, cell phone, internet, all screens, using a Harm Reduction approach. Summer Program length 1-7 weeks with FUN outdoor adventure while teaching necessary life skills, and groups conducted up to twice per day during a typical 6-night, 7-day adventure course. Enrollment in 2024 is kids 7-11 at Pocono Trails, with 2025 and beyond enrolling boys and girls ages 12-17 and young adults ages 18-21 in a non-competitive, inclusive environment. Behavior Coaching step-down after kids return home helps continue self-regulation and embrace structure, and 100 Parents polled, 92% recommend.



### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Harm Reduction approach focused on improving social interaction, mental health, executive functioning, and building healthy habits and moderation.
  - FUN outdoor adventure including hiking, camping, surfing, snorkeling, fishing, mountain biking, boating, and wilderness survival.
  - Groups up to twice per day (12 sessions in a typical 6-night, 7-day adventure course).
  - Behavior Coaching step-down program 1-3 times/week with the same counselor after kids return home.
  - Behavior coaches and licensed therapy staff accompany the adventure trips and are present 24/7.
  - 100 Parents polled, 92% recommend.
  - Non-competitive, inclusive environment.



#### Lowlights

  - Considered an Insight Oriented program, which typical kids 7-11 cannot necessarily understand so benefits them the least.
  - 2025 and beyond facilities are in flux (seeking to purchase new facilities near Tampa, Florida).
  - Clinical review required; they do not accept individuals that would interfere with the positive experience of other participants.
  - Housing details: Unknown.
  - Tech policy and parent communication specifics: Unknown.



#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown
