title: Camp Young Judea Midwest —  3-18-23
text:
# Camp Young Judea Midwest

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Co-Ed camp in Waupaca, WI. Youth movement camp. Part of Young Judea Zionist, Pluralist movement. 2, 4 week sessions twice. 100 kids. Total 150 people per session. Celebrating 50th anniversary. Previously was in Michigan. This is a Jr. Camp. Starts in 2nd grade. Of the Young Judea camps in NY, North Carolina, Texas, this is the farthest west.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Kids from every Jewish background. Not many orthodox. Pluralist means reform, conservative or orthodox. Zionist is important part of identity. Less religious than a Ramah. Prayer is more creative. Education is done by counselors. For the kids, by the kids educational. Youth movement camps give kids a lot of responsibility as teens, developing programming for younger kids. Education is led by kids. 11-14 kids in a bunk with 3 staff. 1/3 of staff is Israeli. Israeli Scouts do outdoors skills. Director is Rabbi. Director’s son goes to Beber, which is fancier. Category that loves this camp: Israelis living in the U.S. Israeli parents are big fans. Of the Chicago kids, some go to Chicago Jewish Day School, some Schechter.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Rustic facilities. Kids in bunks. Bathrooms are in bunks. Showers in bunks. A cleaning person cleans every day. Has one storm shelter which is air conditioned. Since COVID, everyone eats in a big tent outside. Camp Waupaca for boys was previous site.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Great Water Ski Program. Tubing. No one has to swim, but kids go to the beach at 4 pm every day.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Schedule: Flagpole, breakfast, prayer, clean bunks, choice time, lunch, age group time, choice time, beach, shower, dinner, evening activity by age group or all camp, goodnight activity. Choose two a day and they do that for a week and a half. Activity with age group every day. Activity with bunk. Beach time. Special day: sporting competition.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Climbing wall. Zip line. Ropes Course. Mountain Biking trails. Hiking. Basketball court. Baseball field. Two volleyball courts. Tennis courts. Pickleball court. Tetherball. Frisbee golf. Gaga. Youth movement camps give kids a lot of responsibility as teens. Education is done by counselors. For the kids, by the kids educational. Social action is a goal. Encourage deep critical thinking. Must have a connection with the state of Israel. Kids make friends across ages by interest. Teen progression: When they are teenagers (9th grade or above). 9th grade is overlap. Tel Yehuda is teen camp 9th grade and above; education is more mature. They talk about what Jewish practice is, questioning and reflecting on how you want to be as an adult. One year going into 11th grade is a summer in Israel. Can also be a CIT and go to Israel or go to teen camp and Israel. Graduate from HS - a year in Israel called Year Course. Biggest non-Yeshiva gap year program. Can get college credit. There are trips within course - Morocco, Uganda. Lots of opportunities for learning and expansion. They teach Hebrew experientially.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Has one storm shelter which is air conditioned. Since COVID, everyone eats in a big tent outside. Unknown food details, allergy policies, and medical staffing.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Youth movement camp. Part of Young Judea Zionist, Pluralist movement. Pluralist means reform, conservative or orthodox. Kids from every Jewish background. Not many orthodox. Zionist is important part of identity. Less religious than a Ramah. Prayer is more creative. Young people lead services. Services every morning. Creative prayer book. Serious Jewish flavor. Shabbat is a relaxing day. Beautiful singing. Kids in PJ’s all-day Saturday. Special traditions. Big tent view. Encourage deep critical thinking. Social action is a goal. Kids make friends across ages by interest. Must have a connection with the state of Israel. They teach Hebrew experientially.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Co-Ed Camp in Waupaca, WI, 3 1/2 hours in Wisconsin. Buses come from Chicago every session. Of the Young Judea camps in NY, North Carolina, Texas, this is the farthest west and has kids from Pittsburgh to California. Biggest pool from Chicago. Second group from Pittsburgh. Third from San Francisco. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown. Extra costs: Unknown. Travel logistics beyond Chicago buses: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Right fit kid for Young Judea is looking for a Jewish Experience within a Zionist, Pluralist youth movement camp in Waupaca, WI. Sessions are 2 and 4 weeks (twice), about 100 kids and a total of 150 people per session, with rustic bunks and an outdoor dining tent since COVID. Creative daily services, a relaxing Shabbat with beautiful singing and kids in PJ’s, and counselor-led, for-the-kids-by-the-kids education emphasize Israel connection, social action, and critical thinking. Standout activities include a great Water Ski Program, tubing, ropes and zip, plus choice-based scheduling with daily beach time and a special sporting competition day.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Youth movement camp. Part of Young Judea Zionist, Pluralist movement.
  - Education is done by counselors. For the kids, by the kids educational.
  - Great Water Ski Program; Tubing.
  - Young people lead services; creative prayer book; beautiful singing; Shabbat is a relaxing day.
  - 1/3 of staff is Israeli; Israeli Scouts do outdoors skills.
  - They teach Hebrew experientially.
  - Choose two a day and they do that for a week and a half.
  - Lots of opportunities for learning and expansion (Tel Yehuda, Israel summer, CIT, Year Course with college credit; trips to Morocco, Uganda).
  - Buses come from Chicago every session; biggest pool from Chicago (also Pittsburgh and San Francisco).

  

#### Lowlights

  - Rustic facilities.
  - Since COVID, everyone eats in a big tent outside.
  - Not many orthodox.
  - Director’s son goes to Beber, which is fancier.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Young Judea camps in NY, North Carolina, Texas
  - Tel Yehuda (teen camp)
  - Beber

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Co-Ed Camp in Waupaca, WI , 3 1/2 hours in Wisconsin. Buses come from Chicago every session. 2, 4 week sessions twice. 100 kids. Total 150 people per session. Youth movement camp. Part of Young Judea Zionist, Pluralist movement. Pluralist means reform, conservative or orthodox. Kids from every Jewish background. Not many orthodox. Zionist is important part of identity. Less religious than a Ramah. Ramah is prayer fluency. Prayer is more creative. Education is done by counselors. For the kids, by the kids educational. Social action is a goal. Big tent view. Encourage deep critical thinking. Must have a connection with the state of Israel. Youth movement camps - give kids a lot of responsibility as teens. Developing programming for younger kids. Education is lead by kids. Celebrating 50th anniversary. Previously was in Michigan. Camp Waupaca for boys was previous site. Rustic facilities. Kids in bunks. Has one storm shelter which is air conditioned. Since COVID, everyone eats at in big tent outside. Director’s son goes to Beber, which is fancier. Director is Rabbi. Young people lead services. Services every morning. Creative prayer book. Serious Jewish flavor. Shabbat is a relaxing day. Beautiful singing. Kids in PJ’s all-day Saturday. Special traditions. No one has to swim, but kids go to the beach at 4 pm every day. Free time to get kids together. Kids make friends across ages by interest. Of the Young Judea camps in NY, North Carolina, Texas, and this is the farthest west and has kids from Pittsburgh to California. Biggest pool from Chicago. Second group from Pittsburgh. Third from San Francisco. Of the Chicago kids, some go to Chicago Jewish Day School, some Schechter. This is a Jr. Camp. Starts in 2nd grade. When they are teenagers (9th grade or above). 9th grade is overlap. Tel Jehuda is teen camp 9th grade and love - education is more mature. They talk about what Jewish practice is. Questioning and reflecting on how you want to be as an adult. One year going into 11th grade is a summer in Israel. Can also be a CIT and go to Israel or go to teen camp and Israel. Graduate from HS - a year in Israel called Year Course. Biggest non-Yeshiva gap year program. Can get college credit. There are trips within course - Morocco, Uganda. Lots of opportunities for learning and expansion. Right fit kid for Young Judea - looking for a Jewish Experience. Great Water Ski Program. Tubing. Climbing wall. Zip line. Ropes Course. Mountain Biking trails. Hiking. Basketball court. Baseball field. Two volleyball court. Tennis courts. Pickleball court. Tetherball. Frisbee golf. Gaga. Schedule. Flagpole, breakfast, prayer, clean punks, choice time, lunch, age group time, choice time, beach, shower, dinner, evening activity by age group or all camp, goodnight activity. Choose two a day and they do that for a week and a half. Activity with age group every day. Activity with bunk. Beach time. Special day: sporting competition. 11-14 kids in a bunk with 3 staff. Bathrooms are in bunks. Showers in bunks. A cleaning person cleans every day. 1/3 of staff is Israeli. Israel Scots do outdoors skills. Category that loves this camp: Israelis living in the U.S. Israeli parents are big fans. They love positive stance on Israel. They teach Hebrew experientially.

title: Camp Chi —7-18-24
text:
# Camp Chi

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Chi – JCC CAMP OF CHICAGO. Co-ed. 600 plus campers, over 300 staff, campers and staff from Israel. Many session options. Very large camp. Strong Jewish and Israeli education programming. Keshet/special-needs inclusion program. $5 million donation providing major camp upgrades including golf, new fields, min-golf, putting green, and driving range. Future upgrades include new dining hall.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Co-ed with 600 plus campers and over 300 staff; campers and staff from Israel. Sportier boys second half. Director profile: Unknown.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Teen camp is mixed gender in same village with separate dining hall. Unknown cabin/bunk details.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Very large camp, lake only for swimming. 2 pools. Lake Delton for off site water skiing; campers take a bus.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Many session options. Unknown daily timetable and activity choice structure.

Tennis and pickleball. High and low ropes. Strong Jewish and Israeli education programming. Special outdoor education program for rising 6-8 graders (outpost program – 2 weeks). $5 million donation providing major camp upgrades including golf, new fields, min-golf, putting green, and driving range. Future upgrades include new dining hall. Keshet/special-needs inclusion program.

Keshet/special-needs inclusion program. Separate dining hall for teen camp. Health services: Unknown. Food details: Unknown.

Strong Jewish and Israeli education programming. Shabbat evening and day special programming. Kids dress up.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Lake Delton for off site water skiing; campers take a bus. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown. Extra costs: Unknown. Travel logistics: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Camp Chi is a co-ed JCC camp of Chicago with 600+ campers and 300+ staff, including campers and staff from Israel. Aquatics include 2 pools and a lake used only for swimming, with off-site water skiing at Lake Delton accessed by bus. Teen camp is mixed gender in the same village with a separate dining hall, and Shabbat evening and day feature special programming where kids dress up.

  

### Internal Insights

  - Teen camp is mixed gender in same village with separate dining hall.
  - Shabbat evening and day special programming; kids dress up.

#### Lowlights

  - Lake only for swimming; water skiing is off site via bus.
  - Unknown daily timetable and activity choice structure.
  - Health services and food details: Unknown.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

title: Camp Caribou — Monica — 8-15-22
text:
# Camp Caribou

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

They proudly turned 100 this year and you could also see the camp has tons of old traditions and a legacy. Many alumni send their kids there. For summer 2022, they have 265 boys per session. Each session is 3.5 weeks and many stay for 7 weeks especially as they grow older. Camp Caribou is a warm, caring camp. It is a sports all boys camp but the emphasis on character building is strong and the competition, if any, is positive.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

We were toured by Bobby Lerman, third generation owner and director. The camp is very much a family operation: we met Jerry his brother and wife Lori as well as their mom Martha who are all running the camp. Their Dad is also part of the operation and today was out on a sail boat with some old alumni who were visiting. He toured us on a golf cart and as he went along he greeted campers and counselors and introduced us to many on the way. He also picked up candy wrappers from the ground and excused himself as last night was color war breakout. CAMPER PROFILE/CAMP PHILOSOPHY - campers try and are active. You do not have to be a great athlete to do well. The philosophy of the camp is more about character building where kids are held accountable for their behavior. Emphasis on support and encouragement. He said every child will be known as an individual. We saw many kids playing street hockey, baseball, basketball and working in the wood-shop. Everyone was engaged and happy and very active. DEMOGRAPHICS - 35% of campers come from New England, 35% from NY/NJ and the remaining 30% from DC, Miami, LA, Texas, Chicago and 8-10% are internationals from Mexico, Spain, France, Venezuela, Brazil. BUNKING/STAFFING - 1 counselor for every four campers. All cabin counselors are male. Lori is the camp mom for more sensitive matters and Martha the camp grandma. Youngest kids are finishing 2nd grade (with a few 1st grade exceptions) and finish at the end of 10th grade. The last year is a LIT program where they teach kids some mentorship and leadership skills and they still live in a cabin all together. They tend to keep bunks together year after year, but will make changes if necessary. He did mention that they are successful integrating boys that start a little bit older as long as they want to participate and be active.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

BUNKING – 8-12 per bunk with 1 counselor for every four campers. All cabin counselors are male. Boys bunk by grade level, session length as well as experience. So new campers always have other new campers with them in the bunk. Bunks are determined by the rising grade. They tend to keep bunks together year after year, but will make changes if necessary. Bunks have bathrooms and showers and have been all recently renovated and are looking great. I also found them spacious and well organized. They are insulated and have commercial grade fans which are new and help keep them cool. There is an optional linen and towel rental service. Laundry service goes out 1x per week. Cabins are cleaned by kids and there is a cleaning crew that sanitizes the bathrooms.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

LAKE/POOL – no pool. Lake is beautiful and huge and very clean. He said his family and neighbors on the lake cleaned up the lake over the years and it looks fantastic. They have 4 waterski boats, fishing, canoeing, kayaks, paddles, sailboats and all the toys.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

PROGRAMING – kids try every activity in the 3 morning periods with their bunk group. In the afternoon they have three elective periods that they choose daily on things they want to learn more. Wednesdays and Sunday’s are special days where there are trips and special programming and they wake up an hour later on those days. Kids go on overnights also.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

They have every land sport and water sport and a lot of outdoor adventure as well. They do not have a play any longer since their kids were not willing to use up their electives to practice for the play. There are guitar and drums classes as well as a small arts and crafts program where they do friendship bracelets, duct tape wallets and general painting, drawing and cartooning. The wood-shop is amazing and a very big solid program. The cooking program is also very popular with a nice facility. Their tennis program is big with 9 courts both hard and clay. Beach volleyball is big. Baseball, soccer, street hockey, basketball and flag football. FACILITIES - they have amazing facilities and everything is in impeccable condition. The fields are very well taken care of.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

DINING HALL – Sit by bunk and the entire camp eats together. Food is served buffet style. They can accommodate all allergies as well as truly celiac, having a separate area in the kitchen for them. There is a special diet window where those kids serve themselves from. The dining hall is a beautiful log cabin with a stone chimney in the middle. There are old photos of camp and plaques of previous years’ activities all around the dining hall. Plaques are gorgeous and all made in the wood-shop which is amazing there. There is a great view of the lake from the dining hall and there are ceiling fans. There is a big salad bar and many food options. HEALTH CENTER – There are 5 nurses and 1 doctor full time. SAFETY/ACCESS – We drove right to the office and were greeted warmly by Bobby and family. There were no guards and gate was open.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

He mentioned that they take color war very seriously and you could feel a very strong spirit and sense of community as well as a warm sense of family. They proudly turned 100 this year and you could also see the camp has tons of old traditions and a legacy. Many alumni send their kids there. Camp Caribou is a warm, caring camp. The greatest learning happens when kids practice communal living and they grow there as people, says Bobby. They have a spirited community with lots of cheering and chanting and frequent campfires. There are rocks engraved with some important people who have made a mark at camp. He mentioned one being a kitchen staff and another being a waterfront director. SERVICES – There are no services of any type. They said their religion is the religion of camp and that they have something on Sunday morning run by Bobby called reflections. It is an opportunity for the camp band to perform and sometimes there are skits and readings.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

LOGISTICS – They have buses from Boston and NY as well as chaperoned flights from DC, Miami and LA. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown. EXTRA COSTS – private tennis and baggage and linens if needed.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Camp Caribou is a warm, caring, all-boys sports camp with a strong emphasis on character building, support, and accountability. For summer 2022, they have 265 boys per session, with 3.5-week sessions and many staying 7 weeks as they grow older. Mornings are structured by bunk to try every activity, afternoons offer three daily-choice electives, and the beautiful, very clean lake (no pool) anchors a big waterfront alongside standout programs like tennis, wood-shop, and cooking. The community is spirited with deep traditions (100th summer), strong alumni ties, and robust health and dining support (5 nurses and 1 doctor; allergy and celiac accommodations).

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - amazing facilities and everything is in impeccable condition.
  - Lake is beautiful and huge and very clean.
  - The wood-shop is amazing and a very big solid program.
  - The cooking program is also very popular with a nice facility.
  - Their tennis program is big with 9 courts both hard and clay.
  - Strong spirit and sense of community and a warm sense of family.
  - 100th summer with tons of old traditions and a legacy.
  - Health Center – There are 5 nurses and 1 doctor full time.

  

#### Lowlights

  - No pool.
  - They do not have a play any longer.
  - There were no guards and gate was open.
  - Private tennis and baggage and linens if needed are extra costs.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

title: Aloha Camps —Lisa — 8-8-24
text:
# Aloha Camps

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

ALOHA HIVE. This camp is specifically for girls ages 8-12 (or 13). They offer 3.5 weeks or 7 weeks, and they offer a 2 week option for girls age 8.5-10 years old. There were 104 girls in both first and second sessions; approx. 67 girls stay for 7 weeks. This camp is rustic and basic but sweet. This camp is owned by a foundation that owns lots of property. What sets this camp apart are the platform tents.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Lisa, Shari and Laurie were toured by Gretchen Straczewski. 30% of the campers are alumni. There were 104 girls in both first and second sessions; approx. 67 girls stay for 7 weeks. This camp is specifically for girls ages 8-12 (or 13). Aloha is for girls older, and the brother camp is Wanakila.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

They live in platform tents: 5 campers to 2 Counselors, they live across a street with a tunnel they use to cross. It is a hilly area. They have washhouses with all the facilities in there. Some bring mosquito nets. They bring their hard trunks.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

NO pool, everything is in the lake, swim instruction 4/week until you test out. Very pretty lake, they have Regattas, NO motorized, no toys, lots of boating.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Daily choice, 4 activity periods/day. Sign-up is at meals. No Color war, there is a banquet at the end.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

A\&C: basket making, copper enameling, knitting, embroidery, ceramics had 3 pottery wheels, cooking takes place in the DH, mostly baking. Performing arts: small and old theater but pretty on the lake. One show first session only. Some dance: lyrical and jazz. Gymnastics was basic with a balance beam and mats for tumbling. Sports are not competitive. Portable pickleball nets, on old tennis courts they play dodgeball on. But 2 other tennis courts. NO horses. Animals: Sheep, goats, chickens, and a vegetable garden. Outdoor adventure: some high ropes and a zip line, archery and riflery. They offer day and overnight trips, 5-6 per week. No day trips, only those who stay in between sessions.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Dining Hall is on the lake and very Rustic. Can handle most food allergies, seating is assigned weekly. There is one RN and the Doctor is a pediatrician in town.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

This camp is rustic and basic but sweet. Girls wear gray and green. There is a way to rent the uniforms. Sports are not competitive. No religion. They have Affinity groups such as LGBTQ. This camp is very basic but the girls looked happy, and many girls wore crosses. Jeanne D’Arc promotes lots of worship and praying, but not here.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Camp vans pick up at airports in Manchester and Boston. There is one bus from NYC. No calls home, only birthday calls. No packages, they post photos twice/week. There is a way to rent the uniforms. Tech policy: Unknown. Extra costs beyond tuition: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

This camp is specifically for girls ages 8-12 (or 13). They offer 3.5 weeks or 7 weeks; they offer a 2 week option for girls age 8.5-10 years old. This camp is rustic and basic but sweet; what sets this camp apart are the platform tents. NO pool, everything is in the lake, swim instruction 4/week until you test out; camp vans pick up at airports in Manchester and Boston, there is one bus from NYC, no calls home, only birthday calls, no packages, they post photos twice/week.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - What sets this camp apart are the platform tents.
  - Very pretty lake, they have Regattas, NO motorized, no toys, lots of boating.
  - Daily choice, 4 activity periods/day.
  - Sign-up is at meals.
  - A\&C: basket making, copper enameling, knitting, embroidery, ceramics had 3 pottery wheels, cooking takes place in the DH, mostly baking.
  - Outdoor adventure: some high ropes and a zip line, archery and riflery.
  - They offer day and overnight trips, 5-6 per week.
  - They have Affinity groups such as LGBTQ.
  - Can handle most food allergies, seating is assigned weekly.
  - Camp vans pick up at airports in Manchester and Boston.
  - There is one bus from NYC.
  - Performing arts: small and old theater but pretty on the lake.

  

#### Lowlights

  - This camp is rustic and basic.
  - NO pool, everything is in the lake.
  - Gymnastics was basic with a balance beam and mats for tumbling.
  - Sports are not competitive.
  - Portable pickleball nets, on old tennis courts they play dodgeball on.
  - No horses.
  - No calls home, only birthday calls.
  - No packages.
  - It is a hilly area.
  - They live across a street with a tunnel they use to cross.
  - Dining Hall is on the lake and very Rustic.
  - One show first session only.
  - No day trips, only those who stay in between sessions.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Whipperwill
  - Merriwood
  - Wa-klo
  - Arcadia
  - Runoia

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

ALOHA HIVE Lisa, Shari and Laurie were toured by Gretchen Straczewski They offer a 2 week option for girls age 8.5-10 years old. This camp is specifically for girls ages 8-12 (or 13). They offer 3.5 weeks or 7 weeks. There were 104 girls in both first and second sessions. Approx. 67 girls stay for 7 weeks. Aloha is for girls older, and the brother camp is Wanakila. This camp is rustic and basic but sweet. 30% of the campers are alumni. Camp vans pick up at airports in Manchester and Boston. There is one bus from NYC. Girls wear gray and green. There is a way to rent the uniforms. Daily choice, 4 activity periods/day. Sign-up is at meals. No Color war, there is a banquet at the end. They live in platform tents: 5 campers to 2 Counselors, they live across a street with a tunnel they use to cross. It is a hilly area. They have washhouses with all the facilities in there. Some bring mosquito nets. They bring their hard trunks. Dining Hall is on the lake and very Rustic. Can handle most food allergies, seating is assigned weekly. There is one RN and the Doctor is a pediatrician in town. No calls home, only birthday calls. No packages, they post photos twice/week. No religion. They have Affinity groups such as LGBTQ, NO pool, everything is in the lake, swim instruction 4/week until you test out. Very pretty lake, they have Regattas, NO motorized, no toys, lots of boating. A\&C: basket making, copper enameling, knitting, embroidery, ceramics had 3 pottery wheels, cooking takes place in the DH, mostly baking. Performing arts: small and old theater but pretty on the lake. One show first session only. Some dance: lyrical and jazz. Gymnastics was basic with a balance beam and mats for tumbling. Sports are not competitive. Portable pickleball nets, on old tennis courts they play dodgeball on. But 2 other tennis courts. NO horses. Animals: Sheep, goats, chickens, and a vegetable garden. Outdoor adventure: some high ropes and a zip line, archery and riflery. They offer day and overnight trips, 5-6 per week. No day trips, only those who stay in between sessions. This camp is owned by a foundation that owns lots of property. This camp is very basic but the girls looked happy, and many girls wore crosses. This camp can go with Whipperwill, Merriwood, Wa-klo ( but more going on here), Arcadia, Runoia. Jeanne D’Arc promotes lots of worship and praying, but not here. What sets this camp apart are the platform tents.

  
  

### Author: David Highfill

title: Raquette Lake — Jennifer — 8-2-24
text:
# Raquette Lake

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Raquette Lake is a brother/sister camp in the Adirondacks separated by a beautiful lake – need to take a boat across – about 10 minute boat ride. Raquette Lake is a 7 week camp, will allow 4 weeks for 1st summer only. They have 200 boys and 200 girls. Staff to camper ratio is 2:1. It is a uniform camp – wear camp colors – green and white.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Jennifer, Beth, Lisa and Shari toured on 7/11/24 with Gregg and Tara Lapidus, directors. Gregg and Tara live in Boca so number of FL campers is growing. They have 200 boys and 200 girls. Staff to camper ratio is 2:1. Religion – camp 80% Jewish but no services.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Bunks – average bunk size is 14 campers and 6 staff, toilets/sinks and showers are in the bunk except older boys use shower house.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Lake – is large and beautiful, offers motorized and nonmotorized water sports, mentioned they have a large sailing program as well as a custom fishing program. Girls also have water polo.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Programming: 5 structured activities and 1 elective. The elective period lasts for a period and a half and they choose it for the week. There is a brother/sister lunch once a week, socials once a week and on Saturdays there is usually a full camp event where boys and girls see each other.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Performing Arts - each age group puts on a show which is on Friday night. The week they have their show the group spends the whole week at theater and does not do other regular schedule. We observed their practice and the person leading it was very impressive; they also bring in professional set builders – but could be a bad fit for a camper who does not like theater as it is the main focus for a whole week. Arts & Crafts: indoor/outdoor building; 6 pottery wheels. Hobby night 2x week. Planning on adding more STEM which will be on boys side first and will include rocketry, 3D printing and robotics. Sports – adding a driving range; currently take boys off site for golf for additional fee. All fields are well taken care of. Campers can do intensives in waterskiing and tennis daily. Horses – on site on girls camp. They usually have 8 or 9 horses; about 35 girls riding this summer which is an additional fee. Once a week – campers go on a day trip – mini golf, water park, 6 Flags, bowling etc.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Dining hall – lovely as it overlooks the lake; campers sit with age group, not just their bunk. They eat family style, are nut free.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Camp is lovely with beautiful lake and manicured grounds. It is a uniform camp – wear camp colors – green and white. Religion – camp 80% Jewish but no services. We were invited to eat lunch with them on the girls side and it was very spirited with lots of cheering.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

They offer buses from NYC, Westchester, Long Island, North Jersey. They also offer a bus to the Hamptons at the end of the summer. Additional fees: currently take boys off site for golf for additional fee; riding is an additional fee. Parent communication and tech policies: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Raquette Lake is a brother/sister camp in the Adirondacks separated by a beautiful lake, with a boat ride of about 10 minutes between the boys’ and girls’ sides. It runs 7 weeks (with 4 weeks allowed for 1st summer only) and blends 5 structured activities with 1 elective chosen weekly. Signature offerings include a large sailing program, custom fishing, daily waterskiing and tennis intensives, robust theater with a full show week per age group, arts & crafts with 6 pottery wheels, and on-site riding on the girls’ side. The camp is uniform (green and white), nut free, about 80% Jewish with no services, and offers bus options from NYC, Westchester, Long Island, North Jersey, plus a Hamptons bus at the end of the summer.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Camp is lovely with beautiful lake and manicured grounds.
  - Large sailing program and a custom fishing program.
  - Girls also have water polo.
  - Performing Arts shows each Friday night with professional set builders; practice leader was very impressive.
  - Arts & Crafts with indoor/outdoor building and 6 pottery wheels; Hobby night 2x week.
  - Planning on adding more STEM (rocketry, 3D printing, robotics).
  - All fields are well taken care of.
  - Campers can do intensives in waterskiing and tennis daily.
  - Horses on site on girls camp (8 or 9 horses).
  - Weekly day trips (mini golf, water park, 6 Flags, bowling etc.).
  - Uniform camp – wear camp colors – green and white.
  - Staff to camper ratio is 2:1.
  - Buses from NYC, Westchester, Long Island, North Jersey; Hamptons bus at end of summer.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Could be a bad fit for a camper who does not like theater as it is the main focus for a whole week.
  - Older boys use shower house.
  - Golf off site and riding are additional fees.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Point o Pines
  - Brant Lake

title: TON-A-WANDAH — LINDSEY — 6-21-17
text:
# TON-A-WANDAH

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Ton-a-wandah is an all girls camp in North Carolina. There are three sessions of three weeks each; the first had 167 girls and the second two each have 200. Some kids stay two sessions. They do offer a starter session of one week for 6-11 year olds.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

The girls range from 6-16. Most girls begin between 4-6th grade. Most of the kids are from Florida and North Carolina. Director: Unknown.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

The bunks are divided into three separate areas by age. They were adequate with electricity and bathrooms in them but a separate shower house. There were wooden bunk beds that the kids get to choose. There were 12 campers and 2 counselors per bunk. They live out of the trunks. They do not provide linens. Laundry is done weekly.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

The lake is private and very nice. They have non-motorized water sports including a blob and blow up slide. They require all ages to swim and offer life guard certification.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

There are 6 periods in a day. They are structured for the youngest girls and choice for everyone else.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

They have a dance studio with wood floor and a gymnastics area but with no equipment. The arts and crafts area offered a variety of activities including several pottery wheels and a kiln (exposed). There is a drama area with costumes and there is one show per session. They offer baking. The sports are recreational and offer soccer, basketball, lacrosse, flag football, softball. There was an indoor basketball area. There are 2 tennis courts that were not great. They have 25 horses and riding is included. There is a large climbing wall. There is archery and riflery. There were girls practicing for an archery competition that was upcoming. There are 3 off-campus trips including white water rafting that are extra.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

There is a health center with 3 nurses. The nearest hospital is 10 minutes away. The dining hall overlooks the lake. The first week they sit by bunk then by tribe. The food is served family style. They do not use nuts in the food but have a peanut butter station. There are peanut butter crackers offered at the canteen. They can accommodate gluten free and vegetarian. They say a blessing that mentions the lord. Canteen is offered two times a day but candy is only offered twice a week and once a week "drink day".

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

They say there is no religious affiliation but there is non-denominational chapel service. There are tribes that compete throughout the summer. You are on the same tribe and if you had family that went there you are on the same tribe. The girls that attend here are pretty accepting and low-maintenance. They do one social a session. Brothers attend Camp Carolina, Rockmont, Pinnacle.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

The closest airports are Greenville and Asheville. They will do pickups but there are no chaperoned flights. Most of the kids are from Florida and North Carolina and get dropped off. Canteen is offered two times a day but candy is only offered twice a week and once a week "drink day". There are 3 off-campus trips including white water rafting that are extra. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown. Extra costs beyond tuition: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Ton-a-wandah is an all girls camp in North Carolina for girls ages 6-16 with three sessions of three weeks each and a one-week starter session for 6-11 year olds. Activities are broad and recreational, with 25 horses (riding included), a private and very nice lake with non-motorized water sports (including a blob and blow up slide), climbing, archery and riflery, arts, drama, and sports. There are 6 periods in a day—structured for the youngest girls and choice for everyone else—and tribes that compete throughout the summer. Most of the kids are from Florida and North Carolina; they will do pickups but there are no chaperoned flights.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Private and very nice lake; non-motorized water sports including a blob and blow up slide.
  - 25 horses and riding is included.
  - Large climbing wall.
  - They require all ages to swim and offer life guard certification.
  - Arts and crafts area offered a variety of activities including several pottery wheels and a kiln.
  - Dining hall overlooks the lake.
  - Starter session of one week for 6-11 year olds.
  - Canteen is offered two times a day (candy only twice a week; once a week "drink day").
  - Tribes that compete throughout the summer; hereditary tribe assignment.
  - Three sessions of three weeks each; some kids stay two sessions.

  

#### Lowlights

  - 2 tennis courts that were not great.
  - Gymnastics area but with no equipment.
  - Kiln (exposed).
  - They do not provide linens.
  - Separate shower house.
  - No chaperoned flights.
  - It is not as nice as Green Cove and Keystone.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Keystone
  - Rockbrook
  - Green Cove
  - Khadalea

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Most of the kids are from florida and north Carolina and get dropped off. There are three sessions of three weeks each. The first had 167 girls and the second two each have 200. Some kids stay two sessions. They do offer a starter session of one week for 6-11 year olds. They say there is no religious affiliation but there is non-denominational chapel service. The girls range from 6-16. The bunks are divided into three separate areas by age. They were adequate with electricity and bathrooms in them but a separate shower house. There were wooden bunk beds that the kids get to choose. There were 12 campers and 2 counselors per bunk. They live out of the trunks. They do not provide linens. Laundry is done weekly. There is a health center with 3 nurses. The nearest hospital is 10 minutes away. The dining hall overlooks the lake. The first week they sit by bunk then by tribe. The food is served family style. They do not use nuts in the food but have a peanut butter stations. There are peanut butter crackers offered the canteen. They can accommodate gluten free and vegetarian. They say a blessing that mentions the lord. Canteen is offered two times a day but candy is only offered twice a week and once a week “drink day”. There are 6 periods in a day. They are structured for the youngest girls and choice for everyone else. They have dance studio with wood floor and a gymnastics area but with no equipment. The arts and crafts area offered a variety of activities including several pottery wheels and a kiln (exposed). There is drama area with costumes and there is one show per session. They offer baking. title: WESTMONT — MICHELLE — 7-8-24
text:
# WESTMONT

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Coed camp located in Poyntelle, PA. 600 campers, no uniform required. Tuition is $13,450 for 2025 with $13,250 early bird by 8/31. 7 week camp with a 4 week option for first year. Family owned and operated.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Seen 7/1/24 tour given by Joey Silberfein, assistant director Michelle Burger, Emily Rothenberg, Mindy Rosen, Michele Gershwin, Riley McDonough and Heather Pasquale. Entering 11th grade is oldest. All specialists (always both male and female) are coaches who are knowledgeable in their area so the camp offers excellent instruction.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Bunks are not AC; ceiling fans and rafters. Bathrooms professionally cleaned; chore charts for rest. Laundry and towel service. 2 bunks connect to make 1 cabin. All building signs made by the woodworking specialist. Flowers everywhere to enhance the property.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Lake and heated 4 ft pool. Lake has sailing, motor sports, kayak, canoe, rowboat, 2 slides, fun aquatic apparatuses; all must wear life vests in lake. There are 1/2 mile, 1/4 mile and 1 mile lake swims and you get the lake award if you do all 3. Red Cross swim instruction.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Traditional schedule with your division with 1 or 2 electives (no need to pre commit). Culinary – 2 periods in a row; they make a full meal and recipe is sent home.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Cinema seats 80; indoor movie theater and good for rainy days. Horseback riding is pay per ride. Archery. Gymnastics (vestibule brand new). Basketball mini hoops and regular (2 courts lit). High and low ropes course updated 2 yrs ago. Zip line. Spin studio. Fitness center. Silk screen. Ceramics. Fine arts (painting, drawing). General (sewing, tie dye, lanyard). Woodworking. 3D printing. Circus/trapeze run by real performers—rings, silks, unicycle, juggling. Outdoor stage built 3 yrs ago. Amphitheater. Lacrosse. Roller hockey. Tennis (extra tennis offered at extra cost); 6/8 tennis courts are lit. Pickleball. Soccer. Culinary. Music studio for sound mixing; acoustic and electric guitar, drums and keys. Robotics uses Scratch program; Legos. Golf driving range—9 holes across the street. Go karts. Mountain bikes. Wiffle ball and baseball. 2 hockey arenas (covered and uncovered). Volleyball. Indoor gym for all indoor sports and use when rains. Nature with animals that get adopted out after each summer (built in 2020). Chocolate making. Variety of events at night: square dancing, Americas got talent, BMX, talent shows, etc. WIDS is a Westmont-only game (Westmont Indoor Dodgeball Soccer). Competition within camp is watermelon league and compete in all inter camp games. They compensate for tournaments where there are limited numbers of kids allowed to go, meaning they’ll invite another camp to them and host for the kids who couldn’t go.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Wellness center has 7-9 nurses on staff, built 8 yrs ago; always have a doctor on premise. Xray company comes to them. 1 night nurse. All must wear life vests in lake. 3 part salad bar at every meal. Range of food always. Can accommodate gluten free. Nut and sesame free. BBQ twice a week.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

No religion at camp. Variety of events at night: square dancing, americas got talent, bmx, talent shows, etc. Every night varsity girls (oldest) sing Alma mater and oldest boys Popeyes do the huddle. Attracts the all around kid. Some kids are extremely athletic and some don’t care to be. There’s something for everyone at Westmont. Flowers everywhere to enhance the property.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Campers come from tri state area; Philly and Maryland there’s 1/2 a bus. Bussing from Livingston and East Brunswick. Bussing from Westchester. 10-15 Florida campers. LI presence but it’s been stagnant and has not grown. All campers go on trips ranging from: Movies, bowling, Cooperstown, farms, Dorney Park, Lego land. 9th grade- Cornell. 10th grade- Syracuse. Big trips are camel back, kalahari, Hershey park, cape cod, Boston, Virginia Beach, DC, Chicago. No flights (they may fly home never there). Extra costs: Horseback riding is pay per ride. Tennis (extra tennis offered at extra cost). Parent communication: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Family owned and operated. Attracts the all around kid. Some kids are extremely athletic and some don’t care to be. There’s something for everyone at Westmont.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - All specialists (always both male and female) are coaches who are knowledgeable in their area so the camp offers excellent instruction.
  - Wellness center has 7-9 nurses on staff built 8 yrs ago; always have a doctor on premise; Xray company comes to them; 1 night nurse.
  - Lake and heated 4 ft pool; lake has sailing, motor sports, kayak, canoe, rowboat, 2 slides, fun aquatic apparatuses; there are 1/2 mile, 1/4 mile and 1 mile lake swims and you get the lake award if you do all 3.
  - Circus/trapeze run by real performers—rings, silks, unicycle, juggling.
  - WIDS is a Westmont-only game (Westmont Indoor Dodgeball Soccer); watermelon league and compete in all inter camp games; they’ll invite another camp to them and host for the kids who couldn’t go.
  - Family owned and operated.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Bunks are not AC; ceiling fans and rafters.
  - LI presence has been stagnant and has not grown.
  - Horseback riding is pay per ride.
  - Tennis (extra tennis offered at extra cost).
  - No flights (they may fly home never there).

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Tuition is $13,450 for 2025 with $13,250 early bird by 8/31. 7 week camp with a 4 week option for first year. Campers come from tri state area; Philly and Maryland there’s 1/2 a bus. Bussing from Livingston and East Brunswick. Bussing from Westchester. 10-15 Florida campers. LI presence but it’s been stagnant and has not grown. Bunks are not AC; ceiling fans and rafters. Spin studio Fitness center Silk screen Ceramics Fine arts (painting drawing) general (sewing tie dye lanyard) Woodworking 3d printing Red Cross swim instruction Circus/trapeze run by real performers- rings, silks, unicycle, juggling Outdoor stage built 3 yrs ago Amphitheater Lacrosse Roller hockey Tennis (extra tennis offered at extra cost) 6/8 tennis courts are lit Pickleball Soccer Culinary- 2 periods in a row, they make a full meal and recipe is sent home Music studio for sound mixing, acoustic and electric guitar drums and keys Robotics uses scratch program Legos Golf driving range- 9 holes across the street Go karts Mountain bikes Wiffle ball and baseball 2 hockey arenas (covered and uncovered) Volleyball Indoor gym for all indoor sports and use when rains Nature with animals that get adopted out after each summer built in 2020. Chocolate making Variety of events at night: square dancing, americas got talent, bmx, talent shows, etc. Every night varsity girls (oldest) sing Alma mater and oldest boys Popeyes do the huddle Entering 11th grade is oldest No religion at camp All campers go on trips ranging from: Movies, bowling, cooperstown, farms, dorney park, Lego land. 9th grade- Cornell 10th grade- Syracuse Big trips are camel back, kalahari, Hershey park, cape cod, Boston, Virginia Beach, DC, Chicago. No flights (they may fly home never there.) Traditional schedule with your division with 1 or 2 electives (no need to pre commit.) 3 part salad bar at every meal Range of food always Can accommodate gluten free Nut and sesame free BBQ twice a week WIDS is a westmont only game westmont indoor dodgeball soccer Competition within camp is watermelon league and compete in all inter camp games. They compensate for tournaments where there are limited numbers of kids allowed to go, meaning they’ll invite another camp to them and host for the kids who couldn’t go. Family owned and operated. Attracts the all around kid. Some kids are extremely athletic and some don’t care to be. There’s something for everyone at Westmont. Thanks, Michelle

title: SOCAPA — LIsa — 8-23-24
text:
# SOCAPA

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

SOCAPA’s Burlington, VT location is at Champlain College. Lisa and Shari were toured by Melissa Leo 7-9-24. They wait until April 1st to determine whether they are running programs at certain locations. They offer 2 sessions of either 2 or 3 weeks. Their other locations attract more students since they are located in LA and NYC.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Ages 13-18, from all over the US. For their first session they had 45 students, but when we were there they only had 20 students attend. The next session was going to be 40. They also have day students. Instructors are Professionals in their field. RAs assist in classes. They are in college or just graduated.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Dorms are single and doubles, bathrooms are in the hall. Many of the buildings have AC. Campus is very hilly.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

They can opt to sign out with a buddy and go to the waterfront in downtown Burlington.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

9-12pm, lunch, 1-5pm, 5:30pm is dinner. 7pm is evening activity. Maximum students in a class: 24.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Offered: Screenwriting, Dance, Acting, Film, Photography and Music. NO Musical theater. Fitness center has limited hours, there is basketball and a soccer field.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

No Health Center, just basic first aid, nearest hospital is 10 min away. Curfew is 10pm in the building on the floor, 10:30pm in their rooms. Dining Room is nice with AC, they can handle most food allergies.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Champlain College had their own summer program there.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

This location is not easy for international students to get to, as Burlington airport is airport pick up. Some fly in, some take a train or some drive up. They are allowed phones and laptops. Parent communication: Unknown. Extra costs: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

  - RAs assist in classes. They are in college or just graduated.

  

#### Lowlights

  - No Health Center, just basic first aid, nearest hospital is 10 min away.
  - This location is not easy for international students to get to as Burlington airport is airport pick up.
  - Fitness center has limited hours.
  - Campus is very hilly.
  - NO Musical theater.
  - When we were there they only had 20 students attend.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

title: WALDEN (MAINE-GIRLS) — LANA — 8-14-23
text:
# WALDEN (MAINE-GIRLS)

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Founded 1916. 142 all girls at camp now. Can take 150 max. 4 weeks first summer only or 7 weeks. Uniform. All camp spot only. Friday and Sunday night can wear own clothes. Can also wear own clothes to socials but no crop tops allowed. 2008 owners went into a volunteer board of family of Helen Cohen and also owned by a trust.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

We toured with director Kathy—director since 2011. Lives in Boston–West Roxbury during the year. Associate directors are Marnie Cerrato and Jen Gammel, assistant director, who we didn’t meet. Most Kids from Nyc Cali Florida. 60% of campers are related to alumni. Bea Bloomingdale was a camper here. All counselors are activity counselors.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

No electricity in bunks. 8-10 in a bunk. 2-3 bunks age group. 3-4 counselors. Girls can bring twinkle battery lights. Clothes in trunks next to bed instead of cubbies. Yellow bin for color clothes. Toilets and sinks in bunk with shower houses. Lots of trees and pine needles.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Instructional swim at all age. No bikinis for swim. Can wear any colorful bathing suit. No towel service. No inflatables on lake but have a slide. Will invest in additional kayaks and boats in future.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Tues/Thurs pm electives 2 periods. Every night sing taps at 10:00. 3 oldest age groups of 2-3 socials/summer.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Gorgeous but basic arts and crafts on the lake. drawing and painting. Tennis very important clay and hard courts. Also have pickle ball. Outdoor gymnastics pavilion. Soccer lax basketball volleyball softball kickball. Have a mix of athletic ability. Play and compete and not competitive. Archery low high ropes climbing wall. Wilderness trips. Canoe hiking climb g. 3 trips/summer. Color war is called team sports. Competition every weekend. Last weekend of camp is a final competition. Marathon. Every Saturday night a different age group puts on a musical. Lots of traditions during week. Dress rehearsal/ treat. Intercamps with Forest Acres, Mataponi. Brothers at Winnebago Andro Wigwam Skylemar Wildwood who they see 1x summer. Take oldest girls every spring to a camp in cape cod to plan their jobs for summer and themes for yearbook. Parent trap movie not filmed here but was based on this camp. Nancy meyers daughters went here.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Dining room family style. All ages tables and switch every week. Nut free and can accommodate all allergies. Sing at meals their own camp songs. They were baking shnekken German sweet rolls when we came in. No doctor on staff. Telehealth doctor. 2 full time nurses. And 2 part time.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Most important thing about who they are is about community and closeness. Campers walking arm in arm. Teaching manners being kind. Campfire every Sunday. 95% J also will take kids to church. No religion at camp. Lots of tradition at camp. Impressions-tradititional camping. Lots of emphasis on community.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Flights from la Miami Baltimore. Nj kids will bus from nyc or westchester. 4 packages/summer. Tech: No electricity in bunks; girls can bring twinkle battery lights. Extra costs: Only horseback riding extra if you want to ride everyday. Parent communication: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Founded 1916. Most important thing about who they are is about community and closeness; campers walking arm in arm; teaching manners being kind. 142 all girls at camp now; can take 150 max; 4 weeks first summer only or 7 weeks. Lots of tradition at camp with competition every weekend and every Saturday night a different age group puts on a musical.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Impressions-tradititional camping.
  - Lots of emphasis on community.
  - Tennis very important clay and hard courts.
  - Gorgeous but basic arts and crafts on the lake.

  

#### Lowlights

  - No electricity in bunks.
  - No doctor on staff. Telehealth doctor.
  - No towel service.
  - No inflatables on lake but have a slide.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Fernwood
  - Pinecliffe
  - Raquette Lake
  - Point o Pines
  - Romaca

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Associate directors are Marnie Cerrato and Jen Gammel assistant director who we didn’t meet 142 all girls at camp now. Can take 150 max Most Kids from Nyc Cali Florida. Flights from la Miami Baltimore. Nj kids will bus from nyc or westchester 4 weeks first summer only or 7 weeks. 60% of campers are related to alumni. Bea Bloomingdale was a camper here. Uniform. title: Reece — Beth — 7-14-24
text:
# Reece

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

3 or 6 weeks; 30 students per session (building more dorms to increase to 50 for 2025). Campers aged 10-18; CITs age 18-21. Programming uses a weekly theme. Building pool for 2025.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Bret Weiss (camp director) and Charlotte Lester (program director) toured Beth, Lisa Borg, Shari Levine, and Jennifer Markizon (also met Duncan Lester, CEO & Principal). Bret and Charlotte both work at school during the year and are C.P.I. certified (Child Protective Intervention – preventing children from hurting themselves). Staff mostly from Camp America with 1:3 ratio counselors to campers. CITs age 18-21.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Lodging: dorm style; long rectangles with counselor room then student room with 2-4 campers; then counselor room, then camper room, then counselor room; drawers and trunks. Though counselors are not directly in the rooms, they can hear everything through thin walls. Camper rooms have 2 sinks outside the bathroom and 1 toilet and shower inside the bathroom; all cleaned by housekeeping staff. All modern and recently built. Linens provided (towels are self-supplied). Laundry on-site weekly. Building more dorms to increase to 50 for 2025.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Waterfront: go to beach at Loon Lake 5x week (10 minutes away) for beach & boating & motorized waterski & wakeboard; have to wear lifejackets. No swim lessons. Can wear whatever bathing suit is comfortable. Building pool for 2025.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Programming: Weekly theme. 3 morning periods assigned; 1 afternoon choice then all camp goes to local beach. Sundays = theme day. 1 x week go for ice cream at Stewart’s; 1x week 6 Flags (groups walk around together by self-selected thrill level so comfortable with counselors).

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Creative: small art cabin with picnic tables in front to be outside; pinch pots, kits, painting, yarn art. Movie theater with a/c. Karaoke. Yoga as elective. Sports: soccer, basketball, flag football, volleyball, pickleball, gaga, frisbee (NO horses). Nature: beautiful large organic garden. Raise chickens and campers gather eggs that are used in kitchen. Outdoor Adventure: Mountain Biking. Trips: 6 Flags weekly; younger campers go river tubing; older campers go White Water Rafting; optional trip to nearby farm with petting zoo.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

No therapy on-site but can follow IEP with staff and clinical staff on-site. Mental Welfare Staff: Clinical Director & clinical counselor for Staff & Campers (MESH = Mental Emotional Social Health). Medical: a/c in infirmary; 1 full-time and 1 part-time RN/LPN. Glens Falls Hospital = 30 minutes away; urgent care in Warrensburg 15 minutes away. Dining Hall: beautiful modern pavilion with high ceiling and cement floor. 1 seating by age group. 3 meals plus 3pm and evening snack (no canteen). Lots of alternates. Not positioned to handle serious food allergies; definitely call and ask before referring. Lifejackets required at waterfront.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

NO Religious life. LGBTQIA: bunk based on discussion with family and discussed with roommate’s family. Can wear whatever bathing suit is comfortable. Modern facilities and a supportive, MESH-informed approach.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Transportation from Reece School by van and bring baggage along (104th St., Harlem, Manhattan, NY) or parents drive. Extra costs are pocket money for field trips ($50-$200 per session) and van transportation ($140). Communications: get electronics for 230 mins every 3 days so can call home, though most just want to play their games. Can receive packages but no food.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

3 or 6 week sessions for campers aged 10-18 (CITs 18-21) with a weekly theme, assigned morning periods, and an afternoon choice before heading to the local beach. Staff mostly from Camp America with a 1:3 counselor-to-camper ratio, and leadership is C.P.I. certified. Program highlights include Loon Lake 5x week for beach & boating & motorized waterski & wakeboard, a robust creative/sports/nature program, and weekly trips (ice cream, 6 Flags, river tubing/white water rafting). In general, can take mid to high functioning Autism; cerebral palsy who do not need all paved paths; downs; but each candidate needs an individual intake with case by case decision (no one who needs 1:1).

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Weekly theme; 3 morning periods assigned; 1 afternoon choice then all camp goes to local beach.
  - Waterfront at Loon Lake 5x week (10 minutes away) with beach & boating & motorized waterski & wakeboard; lifejackets required.
  - 6 Flags weekly; 1 x week go for ice cream at Stewart’s; younger campers go river tubing; older campers go White Water Rafting.
  - Creative and elective variety: art cabin, pinch pots, painting, yarn art, karaoke, yoga, movie theater with a/c.
  - Sports menu: soccer, basketball, flag football, volleyball, pickleball, gaga, frisbee.
  - Nature program with beautiful large organic garden; raise chickens and campers gather eggs used in kitchen.
  - Modern dorms and dining hall; linens provided; housekeeping cleans; laundry on-site weekly.
  - Clinical Director & clinical counselor for Staff & Campers (MESH); 1 full-time and 1 part-time RN/LPN; a/c in infirmary.
  - Staff mostly from Camp America with 1:3 ratio; leadership C.P.I. certified.
  - Building more dorms to increase to 50 for 2025; building pool for 2025.

  

#### Lowlights

  - No therapy on-site (can follow IEP with staff and clinical staff on-site).
  - Not positioned to handle serious food allergies; definitely call and ask before referring.
  - No swim lessons.
  - NO horses.
  - No one who needs 1:1.
  - Though counselors are not directly in the rooms, they can hear everything through thin walls.
  - NO Religious life.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Reece Camp Tour 7-14-24 Write up Beth Goldstein Bret Weiss (camp director) and Charlotte Lester (program director) toured Beth, Lisa Borg, Shari Levine, and Jennifer Markizon (also met Duncan Lester, CEO & Principal) Bret and Charlotte both work at school during the year and are C.P.I. certified (Child Protective Intervention – preventing children from hurting themselves) 3 or 6 weeks; 30 students per session (building more dorms to increase to 50 for 2025) Campers aged 10-18; CITs age 18-21 Transportation from Reece School by van and bring baggage along (104 th St., Harlam, Manhattan, NY) or parents drive Staff mostly from Camp America with 1:3 ratio counselors to campers Extra costs are pocket money for field trips ($50-$200 per session) and van transportation ($140) No therapy on-site but can follow IEP with staff and clinical staff on-site Programming: Weekly theme 3 morning periods assigned; Each camper has an individual schedule that is posted after breakfast every day. Schedules are a combination of required activities and camper choice. Campers attend activities with girls of all ages. Girls are with their cabins for inspection and rest hour. Evening activities are w/ bunk 4 x a week. Color war lasts the entire summer – ALL TEAM SPORTS get points towards Color War.

title: Seven Oaks — Carrie and Fiona — 7-18-23
text:
# Seven Oaks

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

These notes are in addition to the full review I did earlier in the year when I met with Ed which covers content and set up. Currently 2 sessions of 2 weeks with approximately 150 each (up from 43 last year which was the first year). For 2024 they are now looking at 3 two week sessions running until mid August and will move to 200 per session of which roughly a third will be on the English language programme. The summer school was fully sold out by March. Majority do one session only. 20% repeat clients this year and they work a 2 year content cycle so do not recommend more than 2 consecutive years. A significant proportion of the new joiners to the class about to start in September for the IB are from the summer school so if you have a client looking at a top English boarding school and who is academically strong, this is a good way in as they are using it as a prescreen (but they do have to pass the exam\!).

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Date: July 11th Fiona and I toured with the current director Ed Gibson. He is leaving to become Director of Admissions at Kings Canterbury School and will be replaced by Daniel (Dan) Simpson who we also met and toured with. His email is drs@sevenoaksschool.org and his cell is +44 1732 455133 (Can Liza change this in the remote please). Biggest group is British, followed by Italians (14%). Location and Language caps other than British/American. 61 Nationaliities. The 11-12 year old group is capped and is mostly in the english language programme. The biggest age group is 13-15 with many looking at applications to the school for sixth form (i.e. 11-12th grade - the exam for this is in October of 10th grade). Sevenoaks teachers teach all but the English language courses. They rotate in and out for the sessions (most do one session only). In addition ex Sennockians, usually uni students who are home and known to the staff are brought in to be activity leaders for sports. Other than English language teachers, there is no one involved not associated with the school.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Currently using 3 boarding houses and next year will add another. Laundry daily if named. Rooms are cleaned daily also. All linens supplied. Mix of singles, doubles and some triples. In the newer houses most are ensuite, in the older houses bathrooms are shared. All very clean. No friends can room with each other (this is non negotiable). Kids are mixed up by language too. All boarding houses have live-in house parents present. Boarding houses have lovely kitchens which are well stocked with food and snacks. In future boarding houses will be all boys or all girls. This year one is shared but the kids separated by hallways.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Unknown

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Kids do not return to the boarding house during the day (unless needed). Rest of activities spent in the main school including all 3 meals. Phones are removed at night by the house parents and the kids cannot use them during the lessons but are allowed to have them during breaks. This year there were some kids who did the English language course for the first 2 weeks and then moved onto the general programme afterwards and they foresee this happening more.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

The English language programme has proved popular and the local Sevenoaks School kids are joining that programme each day in the afternoon (these are kids who are already at the school). This provides a real opportunity for the kids to practice their English. Trips include london, oxford/cambridge, thorpe park (amusement center), bluewater (big american style mall). This is a very strong programme going from strength to strength.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Three strikes and you are out. First is a verbal warning, second involves parents etc and third out. No tolerance for drugs or alcohol. Phones are removed at night by the house parents and the kids cannot use them during the lessons but are allowed to have them during breaks. All 3 meals are in the main school. Boarding houses have lovely kitchens which are well stocked with food and snacks.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

International, academically focused cohort (61 Nationaliities) with intentional mixing by language; no friends can room with each other (this is non negotiable). Biggest group is British, followed by Italians (14%). Location and Language caps other than British/American. This is a very strong programme going from strength to strength.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Travel logistics: Unknown. Parent communication: His email is drs@sevenoaksschool.org and his cell is +44 1732 455133 (Can Liza change this in the remote please). Tech/phone policy: Phones are removed at night by the house parents and the kids cannot use them during the lessons but are allowed to have them during breaks. Extra costs: Unknown. I have attached a pdf with pictures.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

This is a very strong programme going from strength to strength. Currently 2 sessions of 2 weeks with approximately 150 each (up from 43 last year which was the first year), fully sold out by March. Sevenoaks teachers teach all but the English language courses and the biggest age group is 13-15, with 61 Nationaliities and the biggest group British, followed by Italians (14%). A significant proportion of the new joiners to the class about to start in September for the IB are from the summer school so for an academically strong client looking at a top English boarding school this is a good way in (but they do have to pass the exam\!).

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Sevenoaks teachers teach all but the English language courses.
  - The summer school was fully sold out by March.
  - Sessions expanding to 3 two week sessions with 200 per session; roughly a third on the English language programme.
  - International mix (61 Nationaliities) with location and language caps.
  - Local Sevenoaks School kids join the English language programme each afternoon, providing a real opportunity to practice English.
  - Laundry daily if named; rooms cleaned daily; live-in house parents.

  

#### Lowlights

  - No friends can room with each other (this is non negotiable).
  - Phones are removed at night and cannot be used during lessons (only during breaks).
  - Kids do not return to the boarding house during the day (unless needed).
  - The 11-12 year old group is capped and is mostly in the english language programme.
  - They work a 2 year content cycle so do not recommend more than 2 consecutive years.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Immerse
  - Reach
  - Oxford Summers
  - Charterhouse
  - Haileybury
  - Gordonstoun

Kind regards

title: SOCAPA LA — Melissa — 8-1-22
text:
# SOCAPA LA

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Socapa is a performing arts pre-college experience for teens, ages 13-18. They can take graduated seniors. They have 3 locations, NYC, Vermont, and LA. The LA location is set on the campus of Occidental College, just outside of downtown LA. It’s a beautiful and peaceful setting. The LA campus offers both residential and day school options. The max capacity for residential is 68. For the session taking place during my tour, they had the following programs/counts: Film-30 Music-10 Dance-7 Acting-16 Writing-13. The LA campus DOES NOT offer musical theater. The majority of their programs are 2 weeks long, and film making is the most popular.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

They can take graduated seniors. The most common age group is 15-16 year olds. All skill levels are welcome however most come with some experience. They can accommodate non binary and high functioning autism. 100% of the instructors have their masters. Many of the staff will do letters of recommendation for college. Half the population is typically from CA and the other half is out of state or international.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

The Dorms are single, double, and triple, depending on the size. There is AC in each room. The RA’s have their own room. The bathrooms and showers are shared communally on each floor. The dining hall is a short walk from the dorms.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Unknown

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

As a pre-college experience, the workload is intense but they are given so much one on one attention. The majority of their programs are 2 weeks long. On the weekends, breakfast is served and the rest of the day is spent off campus doing LA excursions.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Film making is the most popular. For the session taking place during my tour, they had the following programs/counts: Film-30 Music-10 Dance-7 Acting-16 Writing-13. Many of the staff will do letters of recommendation for college.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

All participants must be fully vaccinated and boosted. The dining hall is a short walk from the dorms and all meals are served here M-F. On the weekends, breakfast is served and the rest of the day is spent off campus doing LA excursions.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

It’s a beautiful and peaceful setting. This is an outstanding program for a kid who wants to immerse themselves in a discipline and not be overwhelmed by a large camp. They do a great job getting the kids to bond via icebreakers and team building in the first few days so the kids come away with mutual respect for their peers.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

The LA location is set on the campus of Occidental College, just outside of downtown LA. The LA campus offers both residential and day school options. Travel logistics: Unknown. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech: Unknown. Extra costs: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Socapa is a performing arts pre-college experience for teens, ages 13-18, set on the campus of Occidental College just outside of downtown LA. The LA campus offers both residential and day school options, with a max residential capacity of 68 and most programs running 2 weeks. All skill levels are welcome, the workload is intense with so much one on one attention, and 100% of the instructors have their masters. This is an outstanding program for a kid who wants to immerse themselves in a discipline and not be overwhelmed by a large camp.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Beautiful and peaceful setting on the campus of Occidental College.
  - Intense pre-college workload with so much one on one attention.
  - 100% of the instructors have their masters.
  - Many of the staff will do letters of recommendation for college.
  - They do a great job getting the kids to bond via icebreakers and team building in the first few days.
  - Film making is the most popular; strong enrollment across Film, Music, Dance, Acting, and Writing.
  - Can accommodate non binary and high functioning autism.
  - Offers both residential and day school options.

  

#### Lowlights

  - The LA campus DOES NOT offer musical theater.
  - Max capacity for residential is 68.
  - All participants must be fully vaccinated and boosted.
  - As a pre-college experience, the workload is intense.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - SOCAPA NYC
  - SOCAPA Vermont

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

They have 3 locations, NYC, Vermont, and LA. They can accommodate non binary and high functioning autism. The LA location is set on the campus of Occidental College, just outside of downtown LA. It’s a beautiful and peaceful setting. The LA campus offers both residential and day school options. All skill levels are welcome however most come with some experience. The max capacity for residential is 68. For the session taking place during my tour, they had the following programs/counts: Film-30 Music-10 Dance-7 Acting-16 Writing-13 The LA campus DOES NOT offer musical theater. The majority of their programs are 2 weeks long, and film making is the most popular. The Dorms are single, double, and triple, depending on the size. There is AC in each room. The RA’s have their own room. The bathrooms and showers are shared communally on each floor. All participants must be fully vaccinated and boosted.
