title: WA-KLO — AMANDA — 7-29-19
text:
# WA-KLO

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

All girls camp for 7-17 year olds in NH. They offer 2 (finishing 6th grade), 3, 4 or 7 weeks. 1st session has approx. 140 girls and 2nd session approx. 125. Unofficial brother camp is Kingswood for Boys (2 hrs away), see each other 1x per session for socials for campers finishing 7th-11th grade. They have the same sessions as Wa-Klo.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Emily, Wendy, Michelle and I were toured by Tammy Fortune, the director of Wa-Klo. 60% of campers come from the New England area - 20% NY, 20% CT, 20% MA, 15% are Int’l. Counselors must be 19 years old or older, all counselors are specialists.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Mix up bunks til 7th grade. No bunk requests. 1 toilet, 1 sink & 1 bathroom in bunks. Laundry 1x week off camp.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Lake is really pretty. 3 motor boats, crew for older campers, paddle boarding, sailing, windsurfing, waterskiing, no fishing, one-piece bathing suits.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

5 periods in a day - All choice except for swim (instruction 5 days a week until you pass level 4). 3 periods in the AM and 2 in the PM. Pick activities weekly. Girls travel individually to activities. Clinics on Sundays.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Sports activities include: soccer, tennis, basketball, volleyball, softball, field hockey and archery. Arts & crafts include: messy crafts, 2D art - painting and sketching, 3D art - clay, sculptures, model making and stained glass (12 yrs and up). Big dance room - hip hop, ballet, tap, jazz, modern, zumba and yoga. Other activities offered are: mountain biking, field archery, zipline and pet care/grooming (calves - bottle feed, goats, sheep, rabbits, donkeys & pony). Camp outs and weekly hikes are included.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

3 nurses on staff, pediatrician practice is 5 miles down the road. All campers must be vaccinated. Nut free and can accommodate gluten free. Daily tick/skin checks.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Big Sister program - have ice cream socials. Campers get bead necklaces for achievements which is presented in front of the whole camp. Sunday night all camper reflection in amphitheater - change themes each week. Girls wear white and white. They have color war throughout the summer, tribal games and council fire. Overall Wa-klo is a very sweet camp with nice down-to-earth girls. Grounds are pretty and clean. Girls were happy and friendly.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Visiting Day is end of 4 weeks. Most campers get dropped off but they do have a bus in Darien CT and The Bronx. No phone calls, post pics daily, one-directional emails, will call every new family within first 36 hours to give updates. No electronics, music player is ok, 1 package a session - No food. Families unpack campers if they drive up. Horses and guided trips not included.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

All girls camp for 7-17 year olds in NH with 2 (finishing 6th grade), 3, 4 or 7 weeks; 1st session has approx. 140 girls and 2nd session approx. 125. 5 periods in a day—All choice except for swim (instruction 5 days a week until you pass level 4), with 3 periods in the AM and 2 in the PM, and clinics on Sundays. Lake is really pretty with paddle boarding, sailing, windsurfing, waterskiing, and crew for older campers, plus sports, arts & crafts, dance, mountain biking, zipline and pet care/grooming. Overall Wa-klo is a very sweet camp with nice down-to-earth girls; grounds are pretty and clean, and girls were happy and friendly.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Counselors must be 19 years old or older, all counselors are specialists.
  - 5 periods in a day - All choice except for swim (instruction 5 days a week until you pass level 4).
  - Unofficial brother camp is Kingswood for Boys (2 hrs away), see each other 1x per session for socials for campers finishing 7th-11th grade.
  - Lake is really pretty.
  - Big Sister program - have ice cream socials.
  - Campers get bead necklaces for achievements which is presented in front of the whole camp.
  - 3 nurses on staff, pediatrician practice is 5 miles down the road.
  - All campers must be vaccinated.
  - Nut free and can accommodate gluten free.
  - Grounds are pretty and clean.

  

#### Lowlights

  - No bunk requests.
  - 1 toilet, 1 sink & 1 bathroom in bunks.
  - No phone calls, one-directional emails.
  - No electronics, music player is ok.
  - Horses and guided trips not included.
  - One-piece bathing suits.
  - Laundry 1x week off camp.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Merriwood
  - Lochearn
  - Waukeela
  - Kippewa

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Emily, Wendy, Michelle and I were toured by Tammy Fortune, the director of Wa-Klo. All girls camp for 7-17 year olds in NH. They offer 2 (finishing 6 th grade), 3, 4 or 7 weeks. 1 st session has approx. 140 girls and 2 nd session approx. 125. Unofficial brother camp is Kingswood for Boys (2 hrs away), see each other 1x per session for socials for campers finishing 7 th -11 th grade. They have the same sessions as Wa-Klo Visiting Day is end of 4 weeks Most campers get dropped off but they do have a bus in Darien CT and The Bronx. 60% of campers come from the New England area- 20% NY, 20% CT, 20% MA, 15% are Int’l 5 periods in a day-All choice except for swim (instruction 5 days a week unitl you pass level 4). 3 periods in the AM and 2 in the PM. title: WEEQUAHIC — WENDY — 9-29-23
text:
# WEEQUAHIC

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Toured by Cole Kelly, owner/director. When Cole took over, transitioning from a traditional camp, it had only 90 kids. Now = 450 kids (in each session) and are at capacity. About 70 percent or more stay the full 6 wks. ALL CHOICE PROGRAM – campers select activities for 3 day cycles and can continue their schedule or change activities every 3 days.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Toured by Cole Kelly, owner/director. Cole says they do get some fairly athletic campers (more than the other choice camps) and they enjoy participating in Wayne County intercamps. Campers were happy and busy - mainstream looking. About 70 percent or more stay the full 6 wks. Staff details: Unknown.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Unknown

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Nice pool and lake (motorized boating, inflatables, fishing etc). Active waterfront. Outdoor adventure/camp site area near the lake (new since Covid).

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

ALL CHOICE PROGRAM – campers select activities for 3 day cycles and can continue their schedule or change activities every 3 days. Majority of activities are single sex. They enjoy participating in Wayne County intercamps.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

One creativity center, a fairly modern, nice building where all arts and crafts, STEM, wood shop, rocketry, music and dance take place. The top floor of this building has a big rec room/arcade with Pop A Shot and other games. Well maintained sports fields, courts. Small circus area with trapeze. Zorba soccer (with inflatables). Off site horseback riding at stable near Westmont (this is the ONLY extra charge). New Since Covid: outdoor adventure/camp site area near the lake; cozy tree house/climbing structure which has been popular; "The Cove" a big air conditioned hangout lounge for the oldest kids, which seems to be helping keep the teens coming back; Marksmanship (like riflery, use bebe guns) and this has been very popular.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Dining Hall – nice and clean, square tables, one seating. Typically can handle all allergies/special diets. Health Center - One Manager/Administrator, 1 MD, 4 nurses - 24 hours per day (nearest hospital is Wayne Memorial, 20 min).

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Overall Impressions: Camp looks better than ever, more manicured and most facilities have fresh paint. It’s a very well laid out camp, easy to navigate. Campers were happy and busy - mainstream looking. We saw a flag football event going on, Zorba soccer and an active waterfront (plus many kids were out on intercamps) and the environment was spirited and energetic. "The Cove" seems to be helping keep the teens coming back.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Travel logistics: Unknown. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech: Unknown. Extra costs: Off site horseback riding at stable near Westmont (this is the ONLY extra charge).

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Weequahic is an all-choice program where campers select activities for 3 day cycles and can continue their schedule or change activities every 3 days. Nice pool and lake (motorized boating, inflatables, fishing etc), well maintained sports fields and courts, a creativity center with arts and crafts, STEM, wood shop, rocketry, music and dance, plus small circus with trapeze and Zorba soccer, create a spirited and energetic environment. New since Covid: an outdoor adventure/camp site area near the lake, a cozy tree house/climbing structure, "The Cove" (a big air conditioned hangout lounge for the oldest kids), and marksmanship (like riflery, using bebe guns). About 70 percent or more stay the full 6 wks, campers looked happy and busy—mainstream looking—and Cole says they do get some fairly athletic campers who enjoy participating in Wayne County intercamps.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - ALL CHOICE PROGRAM – campers select activities for 3 day cycles and can continue their schedule or change activities every 3 days.
  - Nice pool and lake (motorized boating, inflatables, fishing etc).
  - One creativity center where all arts and crafts, STEM, wood shop, rocketry, music and dance take place.
  - Top floor rec room/arcade with Pop A Shot and other games.
  - Well maintained sports fields, courts.
  - Small circus area with trapeze.
  - Zorba soccer (with inflatables).
  - New Since Covid: outdoor adventure/camp site area near the lake; cozy tree house/climbing structure; "The Cove" a big air conditioned hangout lounge for the oldest kids; Marksmanship (like riflery, use bebe guns).
  - Health Center - One Manager/Administrator, 1 MD, 4 nurses - 24 hours per day (nearest hospital is Wayne Memorial, 20 min).

  

#### Lowlights

  - Off site horseback riding at stable near Westmont (this is the ONLY extra charge).
  - At capacity.
  - Majority of activities are single sex.
  - Marksmanship (like riflery, use bebe guns).

  

#### Similar Camps

  - other choice camps
  - Iroquois Springs

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Toured by Cole Kelly, owner/director. Note: When Cole took over, transitioning from a traditional camp, it had only 90 kids. Now = 450 kids (in each session) and are at capacity. About 70 percent or more stay the full 6 wks. ALL CHOICE PROGRAM ? campers select activities for 3 day cycles and can continue their schedule or change activities every 3 days. Majority of activities are single sex. One creativity center, a fairly modern, nice building where all arts and crafts, STEM, wood shop, rocketry, music and dance take place. The top floor of this building has a big rec room/arcade with Pop A Shot and other games. Well maintained sports fields, courts. Cole says they do get some fairly athletic campers (more than the other choice camps) and they enjoys participating in Wayne County intercamps. Nice pool and lake (motorized boating, inflatables, fishing etc) Small circus area with trapeze Zorba soccer (with inflatables) Off site horseback riding at stable near Westmont (this is the ONLY extra charge) New Since Covid: - outdoor adventure/camp site area near the lake- cozy tree house/climbing structure which has been popular - ?The Cove? a big air conditioned hangout lounge for the oldest kids, which seems to be helping keep the teens coming back - Marksmanship (like riflery, use bebe guns) and this has been very popular Dining Hall ? nice and clean, square tables, one seating. Typically can handle all allergies/special diets. Health Center- One Manager/Administrator, 1 MD, 4 nurses- 24 hours per day (nearest hospital is Wayne Memorial, 20 min) Overall Impressions: Camp looks better than ever, more manicured and most facilities has fresh paint. It?s a very well laid out camp, easy to navigate. Campers were happy and busy- mainstream looking. We saw a flag football event going on, Zorba soccer and an active waterfront (plus many kids were out on intercamps) and the environment was spirited and energetic. Weequahic would be given with the other choice camps, and also Iroquois Springs (also 6 wks ).

title: Romaca — Carolina — 7-29-24
text:
# Romaca

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Girls Only. Full summer only (7 weeks) - not flexible with shorter weeks. Campers are 7 to 15 years old, with a maximum of 250 campers - have 215 this year. ROMACA Write up done by Carolina Lautenberg; visited with Karen Meister, Tammy Feldman, Pilar Vidal, Monica Hirsch and Alyssa Greenberger on 07/18/2024.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

We met with Carley. Most kids come from NE (Westchester, Manhattan, Long Island, Boston, Philly), plus Florida and California. Most come from private schools. The majority are Jewish, but they don't offer any religious service. They do a cookout on Saturday night.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

BUNKS: 5-8 kids/bunk + 3 counselors. Older girls all sleep together (huge bunk with extra closet space and more bathrooms). Beds are assigned, girls are mixed every year, they can make 1 bunk request.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Lake is on the other side of the road, but they have a bridge (girls never cross the street). On the waterfront, they have art and dance rooms, volleyball courts, yoga, and swim instruction. They offer waterski, kneeboard, wakeboard, and sailing. They have 2 motor boats.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Schedule: 6 periods, one being all camp swim. After 6th grade - full choice. 1 trip per session.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Most popular activities: gymnastics, arts, waterskiing, spinning, tennis. Gym with weight training/spinning/circuit for 6th grade and up. Has off-premises horses (extra cost). Gymnastics: cheerleader and gymnastics - all levels. Dance: contemporary, hip hop. Doesn't have ballet. Arts: ceramics, bracelets, painting, sewing. All seem very high-end/organized. They don't offer cooking currently (might return next year). Each age division does a show - 1 per division per session.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Dining hall - round tables, eat with your bunk. It has vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free options. Can accommodate most allergies, has families who send kosher meat. HEALTH CENTER: One doctor supervises nurses and has some parent doctors who rotate and stay for the week. The hospital is 15 minutes away.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Sister/Brother with Graylock, Winado, Taconic - every Wednesday they meet for dinner (brother/sister). Has socials with the boys from these camps. Overall, we found this camp to be very pleasing, girly, with lots of flowers, and excellent facilities. The girls attending are described as well-rounded, though we felt them to be a bit more "spoiled feeling".

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Buses from Westchester, Manhattan, Long Island and NJ. Has a Florida Chaperone Flight. Doesn't do airport pick-up. Communication: Facetime 4x/session, letters. Has a Parents visiting day and a grandparents visiting day. No uniform, only on field trips, picture days and intercamp. Horses are off-premises (extra cost).

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Romaca is for a well-rounded, down-to-earth, low-key girl who is looking to make lifelong friendships. Who doesn't fit: the ultra-competitive, who wants sports all day, isn't into spirit vibes, or is looking for a 1-year experience only. Overall, we found this camp to be very pleasing, girly, with lots of flowers, and excellent facilities. We would recommend this camp for girls entering 2nd or 3rd grade but not older, as most attendees have been coming since a young age.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Very pleasing, girly, with lots of flowers, and excellent facilities
  - Most popular activities: gymnastics, arts, waterskiing, spinning, tennis
  - Arts seem very high-end/organized
  - Gym with weight training/spinning/circuit for 6th grade and up
  - Bridge to the lake (girls never cross the street)
  - Dining hall has vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free options; can accommodate most allergies; has families who send kosher meat
  - Sister/Brother with Graylock, Winado, Taconic - weekly dinners and socials
  - Facetime 4x/session, letters
  - Buses from Westchester, Manhattan, Long Island and NJ; Florida Chaperone Flight

  

#### Lowlights

  - Full summer only (7 weeks) - not flexible with shorter weeks
  - Doesn't do airport pick-up
  - Doesn't have ballet
  - They don't offer cooking currently (might return next year)
  - Lake is on the other side of the road (though there is a bridge)
  - We felt them to be a bit more "spoiled feeling"
  - Better for girls entering 2nd or 3rd grade but not older
  - Ultra-competitive, who wants sports all day, isn't into spirit vibes, or is looking for a 1-year experience only does not fit

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Graylock
  - Winado
  - Taconic

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

ROMACA Write up done by Carolina Lautenberg Visited with Karen Meister, Tammy Feldman, Pilar Vidal, Monica Hirsch and Alyssa Greenberger on 07/18/2024 Girls Only - we met with Carley Full summer only (7 weeks) - not flexible with shorter weeks Campers are 7 to 15 years old, with a maximum of 250 campers - have 215 this year Most kids come from NE (Westchester, Manhattan, Long Island, Boston, Philly, plus Florida and California. Buses from Westchester, Manhattan, Long Island and NJ. Most come from private schools Has a Florida Chaperone Flight Doesn't do airport pick-up The majority are Jewish, but they don't offer any religious service. They do a cookout on Saturday night. Communication : Facetime 4x/session, letters. Has a Parents visiting day and a grandparents visiting day Schedule : 6 periods, one being all camp swim After 6th grade - full choice No uniform, only on field trips, picture days and intercamp BUNKS : 5-8 kids/bunk + 3 counselors. Older girls all sleep together (huge bunk with extra closet space and more bathrooms) beds are assigned, girls are mixed every year, they can make 1 bunk request Dining hall - round tables, eat with your bunk. It has vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free options. Can accommodate most allergies, has families who send kosher meat HEALTH CENTER: One doctor supervises nurses and has some parent doctors who rotate and stay for the week. The hospital is 15 minutes away. WHO BELONG S: Romaca is a well-rounded, down-to-earth, low-key girl who is looking to make lifelong friendships. Who doesn't fit: ultra-competitive, who wants sports all day, isn't into spirit vibes, or is looking for a 1-year experience only. Most popular activities: gymnastics, arts, waterskiing, spinning, tennis. Gym with weight training/spinning/circuit for 6th grade and up Has off-premises horses (extra cost) Gymnastics: cheerleader and gymnastics - all levels dance: contemporary, hip hop. Doesn't have ballet Lake is on the other side of the road, but they have a bridge (girls never cross the street). They have 2 motor boats They doen't offer cooking currently (might return next year) 1 trip per session Each age division does a show - 1 per division per session Sister/Brother with Graylock, Winado, Taconic - every wednesday they meet for dinner (brother/sister) Has socials with the boys from these camps Overall, we found this camp to be very pleasing, girly, with lots of flowers, and excellent facilities. The girls attending are described as well-rounded, though we felt them to be a bit more “spoiled feeling”. We would recommend this camp for girls entering 2nd or 3rd grade but not older, as most attendees have been coming since a young age.

title: WAZIYATAH — ALLISON — 10-21-20
text:
# WAZIYATAH

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Carl recently completed a questionnaire so please refer to that for detailed responses. A few things not covered in the questionnaire that were brought up are below.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

85% are session campers-around 200 per session. Will probably only grow marginally to about 215-220. Is an ok camp for older first-time campers. Biggest award they give out is Community Award for camper that prioritizes others experience over their own. First session is exactly the same as the first so they don’t really push six, seven or eight weeks. It is mostly just older kids who want to stay there all summer. You can do a 2 week rookie camp the 1 st year only but they don’t recommend as kids are just getting settled then they leave. About 100 campers come from the Greater Boston Area (about ¼ of camp make-up), 60-70 families from tri-state area, 50 or so from S. Florida (Dir. From Miami) and few International from Europe and South America. Typical camper is open-minded, encouraging, supportive, not super-competitive (or want break from competitive sports they play year-round, not type A, want traditional camp experience. Sports are taught by college athletes but not very competitive.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

They have also designated cabins in case they have to quarantine campers or staff.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Unknown

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

It is a choice program. Campers build schedule beginning of the week. They have 5-6 options per age group and repeats for 5 days.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

More importantly, they are a true co-ed camp in that all activities are done together. For example, they don’t offer lacrosse because the two are taught and played differently for boys and girls. They like to get kids out of their comfort zone and are very community centered. Sports are taught by college athletes but not very competitive. Do some inner-camp sports and those are single gender.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

COVID Plans: Ever evolving but renovations were made to the health center this past summer so they can further segregate sick children from those who maybe just need a band-aid. They have also designated cabins in case they have to quarantine campers or staff. They will be placing campers in cohorts/pods. Testing will be done 48 hrs. prior to arrival and 48hrs after arrival. They are also revising trips from activities like amusement or water parks to more nature adventure trips like rock climbing, white water rafting and camping. Will continue to alter and reevaluate best practices to ensure a safe summer. Food: Unknown.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

They like to get kids out of their comfort zone and are very community centered. Biggest award they give out is Community Award for camper that prioritizes others experience over their own. Typical camper is open-minded, encouraging, supportive, not super-competitive (or want break from competitive sports they play year-round, not type A, want traditional camp experience. Bug Juice 2017 was filmed there and is a great marketing piece for them (geared towards 8-10 year olds).

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

There is one bus from Newton, MA, chaperoned flight from NYC and Miami but half of campers arrive by car. Some campers send trunks ahead of time, some bring with and this may change due to covid-still evaluating. There are no phone call allowed, one-way email, allow packages daily (no food), hundreds of pictures taken daily and because small there are plenty for parents to see. Extra costs: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

More importantly, they are a true co-ed camp in that all activities are done together. It is a choice program, and campers build schedule beginning of the week with 5-6 options per age group that repeat for 5 days. They like to get kids out of their comfort zone and are very community centered; sports are taught by college athletes but not very competitive. COVID plans include health center renovations, designated cabins for quarantine, cohorts/pods, and testing 48 hrs. prior to arrival and 48hrs after arrival, with one bus from Newton, MA, chaperoned flight from NYC and Miami, half of campers arriving by car, no phone calls (one-way email), and packages daily (no food).

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - True co-ed camp in that all activities are done together.
  - It is a choice program. Campers build schedule beginning of the week.
  - Sports are taught by college athletes but not very competitive.
  - Community Award for camper that prioritizes others experience over their own.
  - Health center renovations, designated quarantine cabins, cohorts/pods, and testing 48 hrs. prior to arrival and 48hrs after arrival.
  - Bug Juice 2017 was filmed there and is a great marketing piece for them (geared towards 8-10 year olds).

  

#### Lowlights

  - There are no phone calls allowed.
  - They don’t offer lacrosse because the two are taught and played differently for boys and girls.
  - You can do a 2 week rookie camp the 1 st year only but they don’t recommend as kids are just getting settled then they leave.
  - Do some inner-camp sports and those are single gender.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

title: Wicosuta — SAM — 8-12-17
text:
# Wicosuta

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Grounds were beautifully manicured when you drive up. A lot of music playing, could feel the energy & spirit. Girls gave us a welcome cheer on 3 different occasions. They were spirited and friendly. All girls - 2 sessions - 3.5 weeks each; \~300 1st session, \~275 2nd session.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

We were toured by director Corey Dockswell, and met husband Justin - it’s their 12th summer at Wicosuta. The camper that best fits is down to earth, spirited, willing to participate, try new things, & active. Campers coming from Boston, FL, NY, NJ, CT, NH, TX, CA, International. Camper return rate 85%, staff 75%. Will take new campers up to 8th grade - older than that will have a hard time fitting in. About 65% Jewish.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

BUNKS: Full bathroom in bunks. Youngest girls - 6 campers/2 counselors; oldest has a maximum of 15 campers. First day of camp write Bunk Contract. Chore Charts. Outside cleaning staff daily. Switch up bunks every year - will take up to 3 friend requests - only guarantee 1.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Lake - Newfound Lake is beautiful. Tunnel to get to lake - a bit strange to have to pass through another camp to get there; also a bit close to some small private homes. Changing rooms there - girls bring backpacks with change of clothes. Skiing, small crafts, sailing, water toys. 1-piece bathing suit policy.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Youngest kids (2–3rd grade) - 1 daily choice; swim instruction daily, tennis twice weekly. 3–4th grade - 50% daily choice. 5th & older - 100% choice; instructional swim optional. Pick activities before you arrive, but can change at any time. Attendance taken at every period.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

NEW: Fitness Center – treadmills, bikes, yoga, medicine balls, free weights, pilates, cardio, circuit training. 5th grade & older can learn weight lifting; younger girls learn intro to fitness, yoga, basic equipment. Tree House Library – cute hangout spot with bean bag chairs, books & board games - girls can check out books daily. Dance Center - 26 dances performed - all types of dance. Intercamp competitions with Walt Whitman, Robindel. Outdoor Adventure - Rockwall, Ropes, Nature. 4th grade & older - 3 Day Adventure Trip - includes rock climbing school. Younger groups - on-site camping overnight. Tennis - Clay and Hard Courts (will be redone next year), Professional head coach with college-level assistant coaches. Gymnastics Center - all equipment - spring floors, full-size bars. Fields - beautifully kept - Softball, Field Hockey, Lacrosse, Soccer. Art Building - totally refurbished - 6 different activities; different rooms for activities (ceramics, drawing & painting, sewing/needlework, jewelry, digital photography, general arts & crafts). Cooking - baking, prep, measuring, some knife skills, cooking tips. Horseback Riding - optional - additional cost - can do private lessons & learn stable care - 10–15% campers participate.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Dining Hall - 1 seating - assigned tables by age - rotate weekly. Buffet style - breakfast & lunch; family style - dinner (dessert only a few times a week). Snack Shack (canteen) only once a week. Accommodates all allergies - separate chef/kitchen - peanut aware. Health Center - 6 RNs - nearest hospital is Speer - 20 minutes away; offsite pediatrician also available. Lice checks professional company; dogs used to check for bed bugs; spray for ticks and counselors do checks on campers regularly.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

A lot of music playing; could feel the energy & spirit. Girls gave us a welcome cheer on 3 different occasions; they were spirited and friendly. Values focused on the 3 C’s - Confidence, Competence, Community. Optional Shabat Friday nights - not religious. Church available Sunday mornings. All-camp campfire - weekly. UNIFORMS: mandatory for 1–5th graders; 5th & older - no mandated uniforms - only athletic wear.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Chaperone flights from Fort Lauderdale & LA into Boston. Communication - 1 phone call (optional), post photos daily, one-way email. Extra costs: Horseback Riding optional additional cost. Tech policy beyond one-way email and photo posts: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Wicosuta is an all-girls camp with 2 sessions (3.5 weeks each) and approximately 300 in 1st session and 275 in 2nd session. Values focus on the 3 C’s - Confidence, Competence, Community - with a spirited, friendly vibe (music playing; welcome cheers). Choice-based programming ranges from 1 daily choice in 2–3rd grade to 100% choice by 5th & older, and Newfound Lake is beautiful for skiing, small crafts, sailing, and water toys. The ideal camper is down to earth, spirited, willing to participate, try new things, & active, with robust health/food systems (separate chef/kitchen; peanut aware; 6 RNs).

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Grounds were beautifully manicured when you drive up; could feel the energy & spirit.
  - Values focused on the 3 C’s - Confidence, Competence, Community.
  - High return rates: campers 85%, staff 75%.
  - Choice ramps to 100% by 5th grade; attendance taken every period.
  - New Fitness Center and Tree House Library.
  - Beautifully kept fields and a totally refurbished Art Building.
  - Accommodates all allergies - separate chef/kitchen - peanut aware.
  - Health Center with 6 RNs; nearest hospital (Speer) 20 minutes; offsite pediatrician.
  - Chaperone flights from Fort Lauderdale & LA into Boston.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Tunnel to get to lake - a bit strange to have to pass through another camp; also a bit close to some small private homes.
  - Will take new campers up to 8th grade - older than that will have a hard time fitting in.
  - Tennis - Clay and Hard Courts (will be redone next year).

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Walt Whitman
  - Robindel

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

We were toured by director Corey Dockswell, & met husband Justin- It’s their 12 th summer at Wicosuta. Grounds were beautifully manicured when you drive up. A lot of music playing, could feel the energy & spirit. Girls gave us a welcome cheer on 3 different occasions. They were spirited and friendly. Values focused on the 3 C’s- Confidence, Competence, Community The camper that best fits is down to earth, spirited, willing to participate, try new things, & active. All girls- 2 sessions - 3.5 weeks each 300 1 st session, 275 2 nd session Campers coming from Boston, FL, NY, NJ, CT, NH, TX, CA , International Chaperone Flights from Fort Lauderdale & LA into Boston Camper return rate 85%, staff 75% Will take new campers up to 8 th grade- older than that will have a hard time fitting in. About 65% Jewish Optional Shabat Friday nights- not religious Church available Sunday mornings All camp campfire- weekly UNIFORMS: mandatory for 1-5 th graders 5 th & older- no mandated uniforms- only athletic wear 1 piece bathing suit policy BUNKS: Full Bathroom in Bunks Youngest girls- 6 campers/2 counselors, oldest has a maximum of 15 campers First day of camp write Bunk Contract Chore Charts Outside cleaning staff daily Switch up bunks every year- will take up to 3 friend requests- only guarantee 1 NEW: Fitness Center – treadmills, bikes, yoga, medicine balls, free weights, yoga, pilates, cardio, circuit training 5 th grade & older- can learn weight lifting, younger girls learn intro to fitness, yoga, basic equipment Tree House Library – cute hangout spot with bean bag chairs, books & board games- girls can check out books daily PROGRAMMING: Youngest kids (2-3 rd grade)- 1 daily choice- swim instruction daily, tennis twice weekly 3-4 th gradae- 50% daily choice 5 th & older- 100% choice, instructional swim optional Pick activities before you arrive, but can change at any time Attendance taken at every period FACILITIES/ACTIVITIES: Dance Center- 26 dances performed- all types of dance Intercamp competitions with Walt Whitman, Robindel Outdoor Adventure-Rockwall, Ropes, Nature 4 th grade & older- 3 Day Adventure Trip- includes rock climbing school Younger groups- on-site camping overnight Tennis- Clay and Hard Courts (will be redone next year), Professional head coach with college level assistant coaches Gymnastics Center- all equipment- spring floors, full size bars Fields- Beautifully kept- Softball, Field Hockey, Lacrosse, Soccer Art Building -totally refurbished- 6 Different Activities Different Rooms for activities Ceramics, drawing & painting, sewing/needlework, jewelry, digital photography, general arts & crafts Cooking- baking, prep, measuring, some knife skills, cooking tips Horseback Riding- optional- additional cost- can do private lessons & learn stable care- 10-15% campers participate Lake- Newfound Lake is beautiful- Tunnel to get to lake- & a bit strange to have to pass through another camp to get there, also a bit close to some small private homes Changing rooms there- girls bring backpacks with change of clothes- Skiing, small crafts, sailing, water toys Dining Hall-1 seating- assigned tables by age- rotate weekly Buffet style- Breakfast & Lunch, Family style- Dinner (dessert only few times a week), Snack Shack (canteen)only once a week Accommodates all Allergies- separate chef/kitchen- peanut aware Health Center- 6 RN’s – nearest hospital is Speer- 20 minutes away, offsite pediatrician also available Lice checks professional company- dogs used to check for bed bugs, spray for ticks and counselors do checks on campers regularly Communication- 1 phone call- optional, post photos daily, one-way email

title: Summer Discovery — Lisa — 8-16-24
text:
# Summer Discovery

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

SUMMER DISCOVERY @ DARTMOUTH COLLEGE. This is their first summer at Dartmouth. They had about 63 students in each (3) 2-week session. Very few stay for more than one 2-week session. 20% international students; lots of international students. Evenly divided among girls and boys. All Dartmouth professors are teaching. Beautiful campus.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Lisa, Laurie and Shari were toured by Hailey Hoot, Ben Cion and Chelsea Ehmke. Evenly divided among girls and boys. 20% international students; lots of international students. The students need to write 2 essays to be accepted. They tend to get younger students here and most are committed to wanting to attend Dartmouth. They are more naïve. All Dartmouth professors are teaching.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Singles and doubles in the dorm, 4 floors, they are old, single beds, some had small fireplaces and a half bath in the rooms otherwise facilities in the hall. G/B separate floors but they have coed floor. There is AC in the classrooms.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

There is an indoor pool and a gym, but there is an additional fee of $100, 75% pay.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

9am-11:15am is class, then lunch, then class until 4pm. 4-6pm are organized activities, they can use the gym, then 6pm dinner. Groups of 3 are allowed to wander into Hanover on their own. There are activities from 7-9pm but are optional. 4 classes/session are offered, different courses each session. NO homework.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

All Dartmouth professors are teaching. Full access to the library. They offer excursions to 6 Flags, hiking and the movies. Beautiful campus. Dartmouth sophomores were on campus as that is routine. There was also a debate camp.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

One nurse is on the bottom floor of the dorm where all meds are distributed, and they have a Doctor to speak to. Hospital is right there. Curfew is by 9pm to be inside the dorm, they can hang in the lounge until 10:30pm. Dining Hall is nice with lots of locally sourced food. They offer PB and there is a separate allergy area.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Lots of international students. Students tend to be younger and most are committed to wanting to attend Dartmouth; they are more naïve. Dartmouth sophomores were on campus as that is routine. There was also a debate camp. Groups of 3 are allowed to wander into Hanover on their own.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Pick-up at Logan airport. Cell phone and laptops allowed. Additional fee of $100 for indoor pool and gym (75% pay). Parent communication: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

This is Summer Discovery’s first summer at Dartmouth, with about 63 students in each of three 2-week sessions and 20% international students, evenly divided among girls and boys. All Dartmouth professors are teaching, there is a structured day (classes 9am–4pm, organized activities 4–6pm, optional 7–9pm), and NO homework. Safety and support are solid (nurse on-site, a Doctor to speak to, hospital right there, 9pm in-dorm curfew), with cell phones and laptops allowed and pick-up at Logan airport. Dorms are older (singles/doubles) and there is an additional $100 fee for indoor pool and gym access, with excursions to 6 Flags, hiking and the movies.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - All Dartmouth professors are teaching.
  - Beautiful campus.
  - Full access to the library.
  - Pick-up at Logan airport.
  - Dining Hall is nice with lots of locally sourced food.
  - One nurse is on the bottom floor of the dorm where all meds are distributed, and they have a Doctor to speak to. Hospital is right there.
  - Optional 7-9pm activities.
  - Groups of 3 are allowed to wander into Hanover on their own.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Dorms are old.
  - There is an additional fee of $100 for indoor pool and gym (75% pay).
  - They tend to get younger students here... They are more naïve.
  - Very few stay for more than one 2-week session.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

SUMMER DISCOVERY @ DARTMOUTH COLLEGE 7-9-24 Lisa, Laurie and Shari we toured by Hailey Hoot, Ben Cion and Chelsea Ehmke This is their first summer at Dartmouth, they had about 63 students in each (3) 2-week session. Very few stay for more than one 2-week session. 20% international students. All Dartmouth professors are teaching. Beautiful campus. Pick-up at Logan airport. Lots of international students. Cell phone and laptops allowed. Evenly divided among girls and boys. The students need to write 2 essays to be accepted, they tend to get younger students here and most are committed to wanting to attend Dartmouth. They are more naïve. 9am-11:15am is class, then lunch, then class until 4pm. 4-6pm are organized activities, they can use the gym, then 6pm dinner. Groups of 3 are allowed to wander into Hanover on their own. There are activities from 7-9pm but are optional. 4 classes/session are offered, different courses each session., NO homework. Dartmouth sophomores were on campus as that is routine. There was also a debate camp. There is AC in the classrooms. One nurse is on the bottom floor of the dorm where all meds are distributed, and they have a Doctor to speak to. Hospital is right there. Curfew is by 9pm to be inside the dorm, they can hang in the lounge until 10:30pm. Singles and doubles in the dorm, 4 floors, they are old, single beds, some had small fireplaces and a half bath in the rooms otherwise facilities in the hall. G/B separate floors but they have coed floor. Dining Hall is nice with lots of locally sourced food. They offer PB and there is a separate allergy are. There is an indoor pool and a gym, but there is an additional fee of $100, 75% pay. Full access to the library. They offer excusions to 6 Flags, hiking and the movies.

title: WAYNE FOR BOYS — JENNIFER — 7-14-23
text:
# WAYNE FOR BOYS

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

All boys 7 week camp – currently has 265 campers. Will allow one time 4 week intro session (at any age). Has 15 boys enrolled in 4 week session and most will likely extend.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

No team tryouts except for golf and tennis and campers can play on multiple teams. Director: Unknown.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Cabins – 10-12 boys with 3-4 counselors. Up through finishing 4th grade – bunks are assigned 3 male and 1 female counselor. No bunk beds for campers until 6th grade. Camp does provide shower and pool towels.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Waterfront: Unlike the girls camp, they do have a traditional heated pool and swim lessons are done 2x week in the pool until finishing 6th grade. Nice lake which they share with the girls camp. Campers do waterfront 5 times a week – 1x sail/ 1x waterski and 2x canoe.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Programming is 5 one hour long periods/daily. Younger campers get 1 elective daily. After finishing 4th grade – campers get 2 daily electives. Every Thursday – camp does a country club in the morning (where campers sleep in late) and then a special event in the afternoon. Every Monday – super league teams. We toured on a Thursday so did not see regular programming.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

They have a nice new covered outdoor basketball facility. Performing Arts – done with the girls camp which is across the lake.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Dining Hall – nice dining facility, campers eat in 1 seating but stagger the timing to prevent long lines. Campers eat buffet style except for Friday nights. They are nut aware.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Campers do not need to be great athletes but should like to play sports. Religion: Optional Friday night service, 20-30 boys attend, challah and grape juice is served. Boys with sisters at Wayne for Girls will see their sisters one time a week. They have a large canteen with a big game room.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Campers must write a letter home to mom and dad to gain access to the canteen. Travel logistics: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown. Extra costs: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

All boys 7 week camp – currently has 265 campers. Will allow one time 4 week intro session (at any age). Programming is 5 one hour long periods/daily with younger campers getting 1 elective daily; after finishing 4th grade campers get 2 daily electives. Overall Impression – camp looked good, a nice camp for a sporty boy looking for a full session camp.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - They have a nice new covered outdoor basketball facility.
  - Unlike the girls camp, they do have a traditional heated pool and swim lessons are done 2x week in the pool until finishing 6th grade.
  - Nice lake which they share with the girls camp.
  - Campers do waterfront 5 times a week – 1x sail/ 1x waterski and 2x canoe.
  - Every Thursday – camp does a country club in the morning (where campers sleep in late) and then a special event in the afternoon.
  - Every Monday – super league teams.
  - Performing Arts – done with the girls camp which is across the lake.
  - Dining Hall – nice dining facility, campers eat in 1 seating but stagger the timing to prevent long lines.

  

#### Lowlights

  - We toured on a Thursday so did not see regular programming.
  - All boys 7 week camp.
  - Will allow one time 4 week intro session (at any age).
  - Campers do not need to be great athletes but should like to play sports.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - WAYNE FOR GIRLS

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

All boys 7 week camp – currently has 265 campers. Will allow one time 4 week intro session- (at any age). Has 15 boys enrolled in 4 week session and most will likely extend. Programming is 5 one hour long periods/daily. Younger campers get 1 elective daily. After finishing 4 th grade- campers get 2 daily electives. No bunk beds for campers until 6 th grade. Camp does provide shower and pool towels. Dining Hall – nice dining facility, campers eat in 1 seating but stagger the timing to prevent long lines. title: Richer Education — Carrie — 4-2-23
text:
# Richer Education

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Richer Education (www.richereducation.co.uk) is a Day camp + private tutoring provider – London, UK. Contact: Rafael Hernandez, rafael@richereducation.co.uk, WhatsApp +44 7535225432; office +44 20 7871 0481. Runs Day Camps at 3 locations in the summer as well as holiday and half terms: Chelsea – Hampshire School (private primary school located just off the Kings Road – fantastic facilities for a London day school) with seriously good parking options for parents dropping off in front (cannot stress how unusual this is\!) - 10 min walk from S. Ken Tube; Options from 4+. South Kensington – Imperial College - easy to access on foot as Imperial is just behind the Science Museum; Parking however is not easy to find nearby so parents need to be aware; Only 9+ allowed at Imperial. Marylebone – University of Westminster – again easy access from Baker Street tube, parking limited but can be found on side streets; 4+. Fiona and I visited the Hampshire School location at the Easter break (quiet first week of the Easter holidays there were about 25 kids). On average over the summer they have 120 kids per week and are expecting over 1000 for the upcoming summer. Richer have been running for over 8 years and we were recommended them by a client.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Class sizes vary but range from 2 to 12 normally. Split tends to be near 50/50 but can’t be guaranteed. Clientele are a mix of local families (British and international) as well as families visiting from Europe, USA, Asia and the middle east. They will limit by nationalities if they feel a specific class is becoming dominated by one group. All classes require a good grasp of English except EFL which is a growing offer. Will take SEN kids (ADHD, ASD etc) as well as disabled BUT parents need to let them know in advance so they can arrange to ensure the curriculum works. If parents need a 1:1 this can also be arranged at an extra cost. Last year they had a severely visually impaired girl and bought the equipment to allow her to participate and now have that if needed. They are very willing to go the extra mile but pressed us to make it clear they need us and the parents to tell them beforehand. Rafael and his team are former or current teachers and he has just received his PhD in education. The quality of the groups and teaching is extremely high.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Day camp; no residential living quarters. Facilities are at Hampshire School (Chelsea), Imperial College (South Kensington), and the University of Westminster (Marylebone).

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Unknown

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

They have a lesson bank of over 3600 lessons and no class repeats so a child can do the same class more than once. If they cover a similar topic again, the approach will be different. Kids have breaks and use the outdoor playground at Hampshire and are taken outside at Imperial/Univ of Westminster but there are no organised sports. No child can leave the premises during the camp hours so all food (snacks and lunch) must be brought in with them.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

When we visited 2 children were having a dedicated lesson with the humanoid robots. Richer is the only camp in the UK to have these robots and each costs over £8K. The kids get to use them and programme them from a laptop provided by Richer. Truly unique. Another group in advanced science were dissecting a fish (2 kids were sitting this out and no one is forced) and the third group of 4-8 year olds were learning about wind energy and building a wind turbine as part of an engineering class. English as a foreign language is a growing demand and they can cater. They can and do run private tutoring classes at the schools especially for math (all levels up to age 18). They will not go to client's homes to run these but will run them in the "school environment". We have seen other camps at Imperial in the past and facilities are great (it is one of the top UK Science universities).

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Safeguarding is very important. Each group has a tutor who stays with the class even at breaks. In addition there is a non teaching supervisor in the room at all times. Kids are accompanied to the toilet. Trans kids are very welcome but they need to know as bathroom choice needs to be flagged so for example a boy identifying as a girl is only in the girls bathroom by themselves and they are asked not to discuss their transition with other children. Security is extremely tight with everyone receiving a dedicated QR code by email/text 48 hours in advance and this is used to check in and check out with parents receiving an automatic text when their child is signed in and out. Children from the age of 12 are allowed to leave and walk home if given permission in advance. There is a no phone use policy during the camp (phones are placed in a box and returned afterwards). All laptops used are internet blocked and they cannot bring their own. No photography and if they do take photos parents have 2 opportunities to restrict permissions. No child can leave the premises during the camp hours so all food (snacks and lunch) must be brought in with them. No nut policy and no sharing of food. Kids are coded by sticker if any allergies so the tutor can tell if someone brings in an allergen and is eating it next to the kid with an allergy. Epi pens are checked each day and must be in date. At least one tutor is first aid trained and all 3 locations are very close to A\&E’s (Chelsea and Westminster for Hampshire and Imperial and UCL Univ Hospital or St Mary’s Paddington for Univ of Westminster). Water bottles must be named and will be refiled by a staff member. Richer insists on an age appropriate dress code. No crop tops or extremely short skirts. They have no disrespecting teachers and other children policy and children who do so will be asked to leave with no refunds.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Fiona and I feel this is a super high quality programme. The quality of the groups and teaching is extremely high. Clientele are a mix of local families (British and international) as well as families visiting from Europe, USA, Asia and the middle east. They will limit by nationalities if they feel a specific class is becoming dominated by one group. They are very willing to go the extra mile. Kids have breaks and are taken outside but there are no organised sports. There is no direct equivalent in the UK.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Chelsea – Hampshire School (just off the Kings Road) has seriously good parking options for parents dropping off in front (cannot stress how unusual this is\!) and is a 10 min walk from S. Ken Tube. South Kensington – Imperial College is easy to access on foot as Imperial is just behind the Science Museum; Parking however is not easy to find nearby so parents need to be aware. Marylebone – University of Westminster has easy access from Baker Street tube; parking limited but can be found on side streets. Security uses a dedicated QR code for check in and check out with parents receiving an automatic text when their child is signed in and out. Children from the age of 12 are allowed to leave and walk home if given permission in advance. Reports are only provided at the end of the week for those doing EFL or having a private tutoring class; for the main camp parents receive a daily email after camp finishes which reviews what their child did that day. There is a no phone use policy during the camp (phones are placed in a box and returned afterwards). All laptops used are internet blocked and they cannot bring their own. They can and do run private tutoring classes at the schools especially for math (all levels up to age 18). They will not go to client's homes to run these but will run them in the "school environment". If parents need a 1:1 this can also be arranged at an extra cost. No child can leave the premises during the camp hours so all food (snacks and lunch) must be brought in with them.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Fiona and I feel this is a super high quality programme. There is no direct equivalent in the UK. Richer is the only camp in the UK to have these robots and the kids get to use them and programme them from a laptop provided by Richer. A must to offer for anyone looking for day camp in London and especially for EFL for those looking for day in London.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Super high quality programme; the quality of the groups and teaching is extremely high.
  - Only camp in the UK to have humanoid robots; truly unique.
  - Lesson bank of over 3600 lessons and no class repeats.
  - Security is extremely tight with dedicated QR code check in/out and automatic texts to parents.
  - Will take SEN kids (ADHD, ASD etc) as well as disabled; can arrange 1:1 at an extra cost; very willing to go the extra mile.
  - No nut policy and allergy stickers; Epi pens checked each day; at least one tutor is first aid trained; all 3 locations are very close to A\&E’s.
  - Seriously good parking options at Hampshire (rare for London day schools); easy access to tubes.
  - Daily email after camp finishes reviewing what their child did that day.
  - Clientele are a mix of local and visiting families (Europe, USA, Asia, and the middle east) and they will limit by nationalities if a class is becoming dominated.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Parking however is not easy to find nearby at Imperial.
  - Parking limited at Marylebone (side streets).
  - No organised sports.
  - Only 9+ allowed at Imperial.
  - Reports are only provided at the end of the week for those doing EFL or private tutoring (not for the main camp).
  - They will not go to client's homes to run private tutoring.
  - No child can leave the premises during the camp hours and all food (snacks and lunch) must be brought in.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - There is no direct equivalent in the UK.

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Richer Education www.richereducation.co.uk Day camp + private tutoring provider – London, UK Contact: Rafael Hernandez. rafael@richereducation.co.uk, whatsapp 44 7535225432; office 442078710481. Parking however is not easy to find nearby so parents need to be aware. Rafael and his team are former or current teachers and he has just received his PhD in education. The quality of the groups and teaching is extremely high. Another group in advanced science were dissecting a fish (2 kids were sitting this out and no one is forced) and the third group of 4-8 year olds were learning about wind energy and building a wind turbine as part of an engineering class. Kids have breaks and use the outdoor playground at Hampshire and are taken outside at Imperial/Univ of Westminster but there are no organised sports. English as a foreign language is a growing demand and they can cater. They can and do run private tutoring classes at the schools especially for math (all levels up to age 18). They will not go to client’s homes to run these but will run them in the “school environment”. Safeguarding is very important. Each group has a tutor who stays with the class even at breaks. In addition there is a non teaching supervisor in the room at all times. Kids are accompanied to the toilet. Trans kids are very welcome but they need to know as bathroom choice needs to be flagged so for example a boy identifying as a girl is only in the girls bathroom by themselves and they are asked not to discuss their transition with other children. All laptops used are internet blocked and they cannot bring their own. No photography and if they do take photos parents have 2 opportunities to restrict permissions. Reports are only provided at the end of the week for those doing EFL or having a private tutoring class. However, for the main camp parents receive a daily email after camp finishes which reviews what their child did that day. No nut policy and no sharing of food. Kids are coded by sticker if any allergies so the tutor can tell if someone brings in an allergen and is eating it next to the kid with an allergy. Epi pens are checked each day and must be in date. At least one tutor is first aid trained and all 3 locations are very close to A\&E’s (Chelsea and Westminster for Hampshire and Imperial and UCL Univ Hospital or St Mary’s Paddington for Univ of Westminster). Water bottles must be named and will be refiled by a staff member. Richer insists on an age appropriate dress code. No crop tops or extremely short skirts. CE View Fiona and I feel this is a super high quality programme. There is no direct equivalent in the UK. They are also occasionally offering options in Dublin and Milan as well as Saudi. A must to offer for anyone looking for day camp in London and especially for EFL for those looking for day in London.

title: WAUKEELA — SHARI — 9-15-22
text:
# WAUKEELA

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

BASIC DATA – weeks offered: 2 (any age, most extend), 3.5, 7. Ages 7-17 (CIT’s 17). Uniforms during the day. Open and accepting population. 12 girls there on scholarship.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

102 campers in 1st session (115 is great for them), 108 signed up for 2nd session, 23 girls do full summer. 65 staff. Ages 7-17 (CIT’s 17). Older campers welcome, a new 15y/o this year doing great. Open and accepting population.  
Shari, Lisa B, Beth & Jennifer toured by Gillian (Gill) Moore (we met Graham as well but he did not tour with us). Very lovely couple from U.K., previously worked at Trails End. Gill is a very lovely, approachable director; she seems to love her new role with Waukeela (she is friendly with previous director Jason Rubin).

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

ACCOMMODATIONS – 5 campers, 2 staff usually. Electricity in bunks with fans. Traditional with dark wood and previous campers’ names written in every single inch of space on walls, rafters and floors. Single metal beds, plastic drawers. One tree house with 4 girls, a rite of passage (not sure how it’s chosen who gets to live there). Store trunks.  
Facilities OUT OF BUNK called “Music Box” with private changing areas. Separate shower house. Laundry done on site, counselors sort. Bathroom facilities professionally cleaned. On Duty counselor outside bunk on patrol.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

AQUATICS – 3 different spots on their private, beautiful little environmental lake for diving, swimming and boating (sail, kayak, canoe, SUP, NO waterski). Also offer synchronized swimming. Juniors get swim lessons daily. Any bathing suit type allowed for both campers and staff. General swim on Sundays for all.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

PROGRAMMING – 5 periods/day; juniors structured; seniors have choice (their tags hang on a board by the office and they choose their activities). Saturday is special day.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Green and white (color war) throughout the summer and also have green and white race.  
SPORTS – soccer, basketball, lacrosse, volleyball. Matt Bonner (professional basketball player) comes to teach; his daughter attends camp. Tennis courts clay, also pickleball, golf driving range & putting green. Horseback riding off premises, 25 min away.  
CREATIVE – below lodge “Attic”, bright, organized, artsy with felt art, ribbons, threads, sewing, wood ornaments. Pottery – 4 wheels, kiln in separate area. Outdoor theatre area, 2 shows per session. Dance done in Attic, traditional building, dark, warm, campy with stage and round farmhouse wheel-like chandeliers.  
ORIENTEERING/OUTDOOR – 5 elements on high ropes, zip line, no climbing wall. Archery. Teach OLS across road in woods with teepee; they overnight there and if bad weather, there is a house they can sleep in over there.  
TRIPS – offer overnights with hikes, rock climbing, whitewater rafting, berry picking. Some optional, some mandatory. CIT’s do 7-day Maine trip.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

MEDICAL/EMOTIONAL – no doctor on site; Saco Medical 10 mins away with 24/7 on-call doctors. 3 nurses on site. Memorial Hospital in North Conway 20 mins away. Did not require Covid vaccination but recommended it. Big sister program = G-time – older girls lead the younger girls on what it’s like to be a girl now; and they’re part of a camp family.  
DINING – new, beautiful, wood floor, bright. Sit by bunk to start then mix it up. 1 seating, buffet style, rectangular tables with benches. Wolf food service she said is excellent. Salad bar, fruit always out. Nut free; can handle all allergies.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Lounge next to dining hall called Bung-a place with couches and good for meetings, quiet time. Has fireplace, very traditional with books, photos and lots of camp history. Uniforms during the day. Open and accepting population; good mix demographically.  
RELIGIOUS LIFE – Grace said at meals but no Lord or God, just thankful for food they’re about to eat. Full camp does a walk to the historic church across the lake on the last Sunday all together; all wear white and attend a non-religious service about camp tradition and sing songs.  
Overall impressions: It is a very accepting, well-run, rustic, well-rounded camp for campers from diverse backgrounds both demographically and culturally.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Parents mostly drive. Phone calls begin in 3rd week (2-weekers don’t get calls). Email one-way. Music only and Kindle allowed. Packages no candy (they confiscate). Letter writing required/encouraged to write all the time. Post pictures daily. Extra costs: Unknown. Transportation options beyond parent drive: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

It is a very accepting, well-run, rustic, well-rounded camp for campers from diverse backgrounds both demographically and culturally. Gillian (Gill) Moore is a very lovely, approachable director who seems to love her new role with Waukeela (she is friendly with previous director Jason Rubin). Programming includes 5 periods/day with juniors structured and seniors choosing their activities, plus green and white (color war) throughout the summer. The dining hall is new, beautiful and bright, and the camp is nut free and can handle all allergies; medical care includes 3 nurses on site with nearby clinics and a hospital.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Private, beautiful little environmental lake with three different spots for diving, swimming and boating
  - Juniors get swim lessons daily; synchronized swimming offered
  - 5 periods/day; juniors structured; seniors choose via tag board
  - Saturday is special day
  - Green and white (color war) throughout the summer; green and white race
  - New, beautiful dining hall; buffet style; salad bar; fruit always out; nut free; can handle all allergies
  - 3 nurses on site; Saco Medical 10 mins away; Memorial Hospital in North Conway 20 mins away
  - Creative “Attic” with sewing, felt art, wood ornaments, pottery (4 wheels, kiln); dance; outdoor theatre with 2 shows per session
  - High ropes (5 elements) and zip line; archery; OLS overnights in woods with teepee and backup house
  - Sports include soccer, basketball, lacrosse, volleyball; clay tennis courts; pickleball; golf driving range & putting green
  - Matt Bonner (professional basketball player) comes to teach; his daughter attends camp
  - Big sister program = G-time; part of a camp family
  - Lounge next to dining hall (Bung-a place) with couches, fireplace, books, photos and camp history
  - CIT’s do 7-day Maine trip
  - Open and accepting population; uniforms during the day

  

#### Lowlights

  - No waterski
  - No climbing wall
  - No doctor on site (24/7 on-call doctors nearby)
  - 2-weekers don’t get calls; phone calls begin in 3rd week
  - Horseback riding is off premises (25 min away)
  - Facilities out of bunk with separate shower house
  - Did not require Covid vaccination (recommended it)
  - Specific info was tough to obtain during the tour

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

title: VET SUMMER — SHARI — 7-22-21
text:
# VET SUMMER

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

2021 was 1st summer running this program, ran two 1wk sessions (25 students 1st session, 34 2nd session, 7 students did both weeks). About 26 students stayed overnight and 5 day students on average. Ages 12-17. Campers from NY, NJ, LV, CT, MA and other states in 2021, hoping for more in 2022. Maybe adding weeks for 2022, will let us know.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Tiffany toured us, been an educator for 22 years, is hoping to be hired again by Doug in 2022. Staff in 2021: 8 RA’s, 2 teachers, an EMT and Tiffany (approx 2-4:1 camper to staff ratio).

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Residential: Dorms were lovely, 2 students per room, air conditioned, clean, separated by gender by floor, can accommodate LGTBQ students, common room w/ kitchen to heat up snacks etc. All wheelchair accessible.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Unknown

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Day is: 7:30am wake-up, 8:20am breakfast, 9am-12pm class, 12pm-1pm lunch, 1pm-4pm class, 4pm-11pm fun activities w/ dinner 5pm-6pm. Can go to gym to play basketball during some free time/rest hour time. Mon, Tues & Thu are full days on campus, Weds is ½ day of class and a trip (Greenburgh Nature Center), Fri visit to Animal Hospital in Mt. Laurel, NJ. Sat is checkout, Sun is check-in. For students who stayed for the weekend in-between they did Target run, watched movie and did icebreakers.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

We observed a class in session where there was a guest speaker: a vet or vet tech who had her dogs (and toddler) with her. She was describing her favorite and saddest aspects about the business.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Food/Dining: provide 3 meals/day, day students lunch is included but can come for breakfast for extra charge if desired. Cafeteria manager to assist w/ allergies and can accommodate all. Many choices in dining hall (college campus therefore very accommodating). Evening activity will provide snack and/or students bring their own. Coffee House open for students at extra cost (dunkin donuts, etc). An EMT on staff in 2021.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Unknown

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Unknown travel logistics, parent communication, and tech policies. Coffee House open for students at extra cost (dunkin donuts, etc). Day students lunch is included but can come for breakfast for extra charge if desired.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Unknown

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Unknown

  

#### Lowlights

  - Unknown

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

Maybe adding weeks for 2022, will let us know. -Ages 12-17 -Staff in 2021: 8 RA’s, 2 teachers, an EMT and Tiffany (approx 2-4:1 camper to staff ratio) -Campers from NY, NJ, LV, CT, MA and other states in 2021, hoping for more in 2022 -Day is: 7:30am wake-up, 8:20am breakfast, 9am-12pm class, 12pm-1pm lunch, 1pm-4pm class, 4pm-11pm fun activities w/ dinner 5pm-6pm. Cafeteria manager to assist w/ allergies and can accommodate all. Many choices in dining hall (college campus therefore very accommodating). Evening activity will provide snack and/or students bring their own. title: WIGWAM — LANA — 8-14-23
text:
# WIGWAM

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Between 145 - 165 boys depending on session. He wants numbers higher. 100 campers full summer. 4 or 7 week sessions. Only offering 2 week session for youngest campers first summer only and only first 2 weeks of each session.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Met with Neil Goldberg. His first summer in owner's seat. Bob Strauss is still around. Cameron co-director. 4 age groups/45 kids per age group. 50% J.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Shower houses but sinks/ toilets in youngest bunks. Up till age 13 running water in bunks and then bathroom facility after that. Cabin tents (soft sides but hard roof) for oldest boys. Electricity in all bunks. 1 counselor to 4 campers in each bunk. Allow food in bunks.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Only sorted by ability in water sports. Sailing waterskiing and swimming. Train 15 year olds to be lifeguards. Can shower or swim/shower in lake with biodegradable soap every night.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Morning schedule by age group. Instructional in morning. Required tennis and golf and water sports. Participatory program don’t have to do sports like basketball until color war. We entered when all boys were in dining hall. Then the boys went to rest hour and then going to intercamp or outdoor activity trip like blueberry picking fishing hiking.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Rocketry is huge. They had built a huge rocket that he was excited to launch across the lake with the campers. Does socials with Tapawingo Walden Mataponi.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Nut free but have pb at head table. Can accommodate celiacs. 2 nurses on camp and doctor across lake.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Said camp is about community and sense of belonging. We entered when all boys were in dining hall. Lots of chanting and cheering. Camp will be changing name at some point. They are phasing out Native American names and logos around camp. Apache relay, changing bunk names, etc. Impressions-Neil is passionate about camp and he loves his home. He is looking to slowly make improvements. The kids were warm and down to earth.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

25% Boston 25% NYC. Buses from those areas. Chaperone flight from Tampa, Denver, LA. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown. Extra costs: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Between 145 - 165 boys depending on session, with 100 campers full summer. 4 or 7 week sessions, with a 2 week session only for the youngest campers their first summer and only the first 2 weeks of each session. Said camp is about community and sense of belonging, with a participatory program where you don’t have to do sports like basketball until color war and morning schedule by age group with instructional in the morning (required tennis and golf and water sports; only sorted by ability in water sports like sailing, waterskiing and swimming). Nut free but have PB at head table; can accommodate celiacs; 2 nurses on camp and doctor across lake.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Neil is passionate about camp and he loves his home.
  - The kids were warm and down to earth.
  - Rocketry is huge.
  - Said camp is about community and sense of belonging.
  - Buses from Boston and NYC; chaperone flights from Tampa, Denver, LA.
  - 1 counselor to 4 campers in each bunk.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Camp will be changing name at some point (phasing out Native American names and logos).
  - He wants numbers higher.
  - His first summer in owner's seat.
  - Shower houses for most; bathroom facility after age 13.
  - Allow food in bunks.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Adirondack Camp
  - Lake Owego
  - North Star

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Met with Neil Goldberg. His first summer in owners seat. Bob Strauss is still around. Cameron co-director Camp will be changing name at some point. They are phasing out Native American names and logos around camp. Apache relay, changing bunk names, etc. between 145 - 165 boys depending on session. He wants numbers higher. 100 campers full summer. 4 or 7 week sessions. Only offering 2 week session for youngest campers first summer only and only first 2 weeks of each session. 25% Boston 25% NYC. Buses from those areas. Tampa Denver LA chaperone flight. We entered when all boys were in dining hall. Lots of chanting and cheering Nut free but have pb at head table. Can accomodate celiacs Then the boys went to rest hour and then going to intercamp or outdoor activity trip like blueberry picking fishing hiking. Rocketry is huge. They had built a huge rocket that he was excited to launch across the lake with the campers. Shower houses but sinks/ toilets in youngest bunks. Up till age 13 running water in bunks and then bathroom facility after that. Cabin tents (soft sides but hard roof) for oldest boys. Electricity in all bunks. 1 counselor to 4 campers in each bunk. Allow food in bunks. Can shower or swim/shower in lake with biodegradable soap every night. 50% J Said camp is about community and sense of belonging. Participatory program don’t have to do sports like basketball until color war. Morning schedule by age group. 4 age groups/45 kids per age group. Instructional in morning. Required tennis and golf and water sports. Only sorted by ability in water sports. Sailing waterskiing and swimming. Train 15 year olds to be lifeguards. Does socials with Tapawingo Walden Mataponi 2 nurses on camp and doctor across lake. Impressions-Neil is passionate about camp and he loves his home. He is looking to slowly make improvements. The kids were warm and down to earth. I’d give with camps like Adirondack Camp, Lake Owego, North Star
