title: Laurelwood — Emily — 7-26-24
text:
# Laurelwood

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

CAMP LAURELWOOD MADISON, CT 07/25/24 EMILY ROTHENBERG TOURED BY LISA LEBOWITZ. Kids can attend 2, 3, 4 or 7 weeks. Hold a rookie week each session. Campers can extend.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Large contingency of kids from Stamford, CT. Also has kids from other CT towns, Westchester, NJ, LI, Queens, NYC, Virginia, Washington DC etc. 1st session there were about 185 kids. Second session about 80 kids. Many fewer. There are typically more girls than boys. Also have about 100-day campers from local area. Counselors are both US and International. A few alumni families visiting from Israel with their kids. Laurelwood is getting a new Director, Ben Chabakas, he is a school social worker.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Bunks are very simple. Bunk beds. Toilets and sinks are in bunk. Showers are not in bunk for younger kids; they are in shower houses nearby. About 10 kids per bunk and 2-3 counselors.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Lake is down a long path, have non-motorized water sports (kayak, canoe, paddleboard, inflatables). There are 2 pools. Used for swim instruction and free swim.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Programming is a mix of structured and elective. Travel by bunk to activities. Kids pick 1 elective per week. Older kids get more choice. Wednesday is field trip day. Go to Newport, Mystic etc. Older kids get more freedom at night. Extra canteen. Hang out outside.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Activities include: Softball, Soccer, Tennis, Basketball, Martial Arts, Ga-ga, Floor Hockey, Volleyball, Frisbee, High and Low Ropes Course, Theater, Disc Golf, Kickball, Ping Pong, Robotics. Kids help out with "The Scoop", the camp newspaper, Arts and Crafts, Woodworking. No horseback riding or gymnastics. Friday night service wear white for Shabbat. Camper led. Sometimes guests come from the community as speakers. Saturday morning service as well. Campers are a mix of reformed and conservative Jewish. A few non-Jewish campers. Oldest campers (rising 10th) take a humanitarian trip to Costa Rica. Following year kids are staff in training.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Security at gate. Also added armed security this summer. Kitchen is Kosher. Can handle all food allergies, gluten free, vegetarian etc. All camp eats together. Offer salad bars and breakfast bars plus hot meal. Nut free and sesame free. 2 RNs at camp and Doctor on call. Yale medical facility not far away about 20 minutes. Air-conditioned areas include health center, digital media center and rec hall.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Overall, Laurelwood is a simple camp, not fancy. Kids looked happy and engaged. The activities are more recreational, not intense instruction. Lisa said their specialty is building community and lifelong friendships.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

No bus transportation. Parents drop off. Camp is tech free. Only phone calls are for birthdays. Visiting day is only for 7-week campers. Extra canteen.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Overall, Laurelwood is a simple camp, not fancy. Kids looked happy and engaged. The activities are more recreational, not intense instruction. This camp would not work for a very sporty kid.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Security at gate. Also added armed security this summer.
  - Kitchen is Kosher. Can handle all food allergies, gluten free, vegetarian etc.
  - All camp eats together. Offer salad bars and breakfast bars plus hot meal.
  - Nut free and sesame free.
  - Programming is a mix of structured and elective. Kids pick 1 elective per week.
  - Travel by bunk to activities.
  - Wednesday is field trip day. Go to Newport, Mystic etc.
  - Friday night service wear white for Shabbat. Camper led.
  - Sometimes guests come from the community as speakers.
  - Oldest campers (rising 10th) take a humanitarian trip to Costa Rica.
  - 2 RNs at camp and Doctor on call. Yale medical facility not far away about 20 minutes.
  - Air-conditioned areas include health center, digital media center and rec hall.
  - Lisa said their specialty is building community and lifelong friendships.

  

#### Lowlights

  - No bus transportation. Parents drop off.
  - Second session about 80 kids. Many fewer.
  - Lake is down a long path.
  - Bunks are very simple.
  - Showers are not in bunk for younger kids; they are in shower houses nearby.
  - No horseback riding or gymnastics.
  - Visiting day is only for 7-week campers.
  - The activities are more recreational, not intense instruction.
  - This camp would not work for a very sporty kid.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

title: Lake Greeley — Shari — 8-13-23
text:
# Lake Greeley

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Lake Greeley – Shari & Lisa B. toured by Shannon Weisbrod 30 June 2023 – she’s been at camp 6 yrs (did not get title/role, they were running late so our time was limited). BASIC DATA – 200 campers 1 st session, low %age stay full summer, maybe 5 kids, most popular length is 3 wks (custom session) but middle 4 wks about 200 campers. Lots of in and out throughout the 8wks. Ages 6 (a few) to 15 as campers and 16 y/o are LIT’s but must have had 2yrs at camp to be LIT. Heaviest concentration of demographics from NY, NJ, CT, PA. Retention used to be 90%, now 75-80%.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

About 10% of counselors were former campers and they won’t bunk them together. Ages of counselors 19-23 mostly, counselor return rate about 50-60%. When asked how they monitor the "budding romance" if older kids are moving independently, she said there’s lots of supervision around camp, they have counselors back at the cabins for time-off usually and at orientation they talk about it so staff are "on top of it." Typical camper is: open-minded, tries new things, want to be outgoing and be an individual since lots of choice given. They do have a couple of transgender campers who are bunked by gender @ birth.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

ACCOMODATIONS – 9 campers w/ 2 staff and an LIT. Jr cabin was an octagonal shape, cute w/ blinds and clean. Intermediate cabin was bigger rectangular w/ curtains, facilities in bunks, keep bunks same year-to-year, do not mix sessions but take bunk requests so gets tricky sometimes. Older campers welcome. Hair dryers & flat/curling irons allowed. On duty counselor inside bunk when campers going to sleep.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

AQUATICS- swim instruction upon request but swim tested to specific level for lake. Co-ed @ lake, no policy for campers bathing suits unless a conversation needs to occur (appropriate coverage). Counselors wear 1-pieces. Small boating & aquapark on non-motorized lake. Tubing at Lake Wallenpaupack 3-4x/wk available. 1 non-heated pool.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

PROGRAMMING – 6 periods/day, ages 6-9 cabin group moves together, all structured with some parent choice woven in. Ages 10-15 full choice. Campers carry backpack w/ sunscreen, water bottle and have 5 minutes between periods.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

SPORTS- soccer, basketball, lacrosse, flag football, baseball, softball, rugby if they have counselor for it, volleyball/newcomb, skate park, gaga, bungee trampoline, field hockey if there’s interest (they have equipment), tennis (popular) private lessons available. Hb riding, English style, 8 horses on premises, no extra cost, about 4-5 riders per period but different year-to-year. Guaranteed ride 2x/session, stable mgmt., trail rides, ring rides, bring own helmet and boots but they can provide also. Sign up for inter-camps, no try-outs. CREATIVE- general arts & crafts, drawing, water colors, beading, ceramics, theme days (ie: pirate hats) for various projects. No woodworking in ’23. Their activities are all dependent on the instructors they hire each year so in some areas will be inconsistent. No big performances, only mini shows. Circus arts trapeze, spanish silk. Some dance available. Music lessons for guitar, drums, voice. Campers bring instruments from home and practice/perform at evening activity. Gymnastics indoor full facility very nice w/balance beam, parallel bars, uneven bars, vault, tumble track . Can work on routine from home but not professional in there to guide extensively. STEAM/HIGH TECH- digital and darkroom photography. COOKING – only on hobby nights a few times per summer. ORIENTEERING – High and low ropes, zip line, climbing wall, archery (intercamp), riflery, mtn biking. Extras: some activities (w/ permission from parents too) paintball, waterski/tubing, quads, 4-wheelers, trip to minor league baseball game, private tennis lessons (new). TRIPS – in ’22 intermediates and sr’s did a horseback overnight & was most popular, she didn’t know if they’d do it in ’23 at that point. Dorney Park, Minor league baseball in ’23. Rafting in ’24. PLANNED IMPROVEMENTS – pickleball, tac room @ barn, cabin lights, cabin siding.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

MEDICAL – 2-3 nurses/session, a/c rooms at health center, urgent care 15-20 mins away, Wayne Memorial hospital 30 mins. Can take a diabetic but has to be semi-aware of self care. Lice and athletes foot check upon arrival. Dogs on porch @ health center, sweet and calm. DINING- No allergy supervisor, sit by bunk, breakfast staggard seatings, lunch & dinner all together. NOT NUT FREE, p/b on separate table in cups, can handle other allergies but vegan is hard. Snacks in cabins from families, canteen nightly w/ $2 allowance as part of their tuition. Also camp store w/ paraphernalia and sundries for purchase. Products with nuts at canteen.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

RELIGIOUS LIFE – NONE. But can transport to church on Sundays if needed. Additional Notes: differentiating LG from other Buynak camps: smallest of the 3, their location, community driven, attention to kids/get to know the kids really well. In her 6 yrs they only had 3 campers sent home for behavior issues.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

NO BUSES\! Airport p/u @ EWR & JFK (extra cost). COMMUNICATION – 1 call per 2wk stay after 5 days of arrival. One way email. No electronics, packages ok-limit to non-food but if they do they say no nuts. Letter writing 1x/wk before dinner, post pictures daily. Extras: some activities (w/ permission from parents too) paintball, waterski/tubing, quads, 4-wheelers, trip to minor league baseball game, private tennis lessons (new).

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Lake Greeley is the smallest of the 3 Buynak camps; it is community driven with attention to kids and getting to know the kids really well. Ages 6 (a few) to 15 as campers and 16 y/o are LIT’s, with 6 periods/day and lots of choice (ages 10-15 full choice; younger groups move by cabin). The activity menu spans sports, riding (English), circus arts, gymnastics, music, high/low ropes, a non-motorized lake with aquapark and a non-heated pool, plus extras like paintball, waterski/tubing, quads, and private tennis lessons. Typical camper is open-minded, tries new things, want to be outgoing and be an individual since lots of choice given.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Smallest of the 3; community driven, attention to kids/get to know the kids really well.
  - Wide activity breadth (sports, riding, circus arts, gymnastics, ropes/zip, aquapark, music/arts; inter-camps with no try-outs).
  - 6 periods/day; ages 10-15 full choice; younger campers move by cabin with some parent choice woven in.
  - Strong supervision around camp; staff trained at orientation and "on top of" budding romance.
  - Airport p/u @ EWR & JFK (extra cost); heaviest concentration from NY, NJ, CT, PA.
  - Riding included (no extra cost) with 8 horses; guaranteed ride 2x/session.
  - Planned improvements: pickleball, tac room @ barn, cabin lights, cabin siding.

  

#### Lowlights

  - NOT NUT FREE; products with nuts at canteen; vegan is hard; no allergy supervisor.
  - Some activities are dependent on the instructors they hire each year so in some areas will be inconsistent; no big performances.
  - No woodworking in ’23.
  - NO BUSES.
  - Retention used to be 90%, now 75-80%.
  - Transgender campers are bunked by gender @ birth.
  - No electronics; 1 call per 2wk stay after 5 days of arrival (one way email).

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Cayuga
  - Lohikan

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

title: Leysin — Catherine — 3-22-24
text:
# Leysin

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Very nice American Boarding school located in French part of Switzerland in Leysin in a safe and beautiful village Leysin (1h30 from Geneva). Summer Program: Two 3 weeks session with 270/280 boarding kids each session. Very international. 06/24 to 07/2 and 07/15 to 08/03. 8800 CHF by session; 15800 CHF 2 sessions. The language is English except for some clubs who can be in French and language is French in the village. Very nice facilities on site and access to the sport facilities of the village.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Patricia Gomez. 300 students from 60 nationalities during the year with a good support to the kids. Staff acting as a bridge between school and families offering a family atmosphere. 2 Age groups 9 to 12 with supervision 24h ratio staff 1 to 5; 13 to 16 with authorization to go to the village (very safe) ratio staff 1 to 8.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Dorms: 6 dormitories by age and gender. Double or triple en suite. Wifi. Social areas with TV. Supervision 24h with teachers sleeping in the dorms.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Unknown

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

MORNING class 9h00-12h00, 3 choices (must choose at the registration): - Language: French or English, 3h a day, 15h a week. - Leadership track with 2 options (supplement of 500 CHF): Alpine Leadership program (outdoor program in group to develop self confidence and learn the essential to manage in a trip followed by an overnight camping and First Aid); Innovation creativity (around entrepreneurship, innovation, solving problems, completed by workshops in local Cie). - Enrichment courses: choice of 3 options amongst 7 proposals (1h each): Creative writing; Da Vinci Lab (STEAM); Our Planet (environment and science); Film and literature; Critical Thinking and logic (face decisions, fake news); Model United Nations (Young diplomat, debating topics); AI and Digital World (Benefit of AI and impact on the world). AFTERNOON 14h00-15h45 Clubs (must choose at the registration): Robotics and programming (in partnership with Apple); Performing Arts; Visual Arts creation on 2D and 3D; Cooking and baking; Hiking and climbing (indoor and outdoor); Team sports and Fitness. Activities 16h15-17h45: Music, Arts, hiking, Yoga, … up to 50 activities to choose each week for each day. DINNER 18h00-19h00. EVENING 19h30-21h30 social event: treasure hunt, night-club, songs, dance, …

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Leadership track with Alpine Leadership (outdoor program, overnight camping, First Aid) and Innovation creativity (entrepreneurship, solving problems, workshops in local Cie) (supplement of 500 CHF). Robotics and programming (in partnership with Apple). Enrichment highlights include Da Vinci Lab (STEAM), Model United Nations, Critical Thinking and logic, AI and Digital World, Creative writing, Film and literature, and Our Planet. Week ends: trips AS Bern, Montreux, jazz, medieval village, cheese and chocolate factory.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Very safe village. 24h supervision with teachers sleeping in the dorms. Staff ratios: 1 to 5 (ages 9-12) and 1 to 8 (ages 13-16). DINNER 18h00-19h00. Food details: Unknown.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Very international. Good support to the kids. Staff acting as a bridge between school and families offering a family atmosphere. Very nice American Boarding school.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Leysin (1h30 from Geneva). 8800 CHF by session; 15800 CHF 2 sessions. The language is English except for some clubs who can be in French and language is French in the village. Wifi. Ages 13 to 16 with authorization to go to the village (very safe). Parent communication: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown. Extra costs: Leadership track supplement of 500 CHF.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Very nice American Boarding school located in French part of Switzerland in Leysin in a safe and beautiful village (1h30 from Geneva). Two 3 weeks session with 270/280 boarding kids each session; very international. The language is English except for some clubs who can be in French, and there are 2 age groups 9 to 12 and 13 to 16. Dorms are 6 dormitories by age and gender with double or triple en suite, Wifi, and social areas with TV.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Very nice facilities on site and access to the sport facilities of the village
  - Leadership track with Alpine Leadership (overnight camping and First Aid) and Innovation creativity
  - Robotics and programming (in partnership with Apple)
  - Very international
  - Staff acting as a bridge between school and families offering a family atmosphere
  - Week ends: trips AS Bern, Montreux, jazz, medieval village, cheese and chocolate factory

  

#### Lowlights

  - Waterfront & Aquatics: Unknown
  - Parent communication: Unknown
  - Tech policy: Unknown
  - Food details: Unknown

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

LEYSIN AMERICAN SCHOOL Patricia Gomez Very nice American Boarding school located in French part of Switzerland in Leysin in a safe and beautiful village Leysin (1h30 from Geneva) 300 students from 60 nationalities during the year with a good support to the kids . Staff acting as a bridge between school and families offering a family atmosphere SUMMER PROGRAM Two 3 weeks session with 270/280 boarding kids each session. Very international 06/24 to 07/2 and 07/15 to 08/03 8800 CHF by session 15800 CHF 2 sessions The language is English except for some clubs who can be in French and language is French in the village. 2 Age groups 9 to 12 with supervision 24h ratio staff 1 to 5 13 to 16 with authorization to go to the village (very safe) ratio staff 1 to 8 Very nice facilities on site and access to the sport facilities of the village Dorms : 6 dormitories by age and gender. Double or triple en Suite. Innovation, solving problems completed by workshops in local Cie - Enrichment courses : choice of 3 options amongst 7 proposals(1h each) Creative writing, Da Vinci Lab (Steam) Our Planet (environnement and science) Film and litterature Critical Thinking and logic (face decisions, fake news) Model United Naitions(Young diplomat, debating topics,..) title: Lochearn — Lindsey — 9-14-24
text:
# Lochearn

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Lochearn Lindsey Binstock - Toured July 29, 2024. They have 2 sessions of 3.5 weeks. There are 180 girls per session. Girls from 23 states and 6 countries. People usually stick with the same session. Visiting day is departure day. It is non-uniform except Blue and Green Days and once a week a campfire where they wear uniform with tartan sash. It is Non religious except they say a Robert Burns poem before breakfast and dinner that does mention god.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

We (Emily S, Allison A, Natasha and I) were toured by Erin (asst director) and Eliza (co-director with mom). Have had new girls at 13/14 but depends on the camper. Campus leader on each line - young adults. Not college kids.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

There are 4 different lines of bunks. All are along water. Campus leader on each line - young adults. Not college kids. Junior line - 7,8,9 year olds. Subs line 10,11,12 year olds. Senior line CT house. Bathrooms are not in bunk (no toilets, no showers, no sinks). Bunks have electricity, but no outlets. It is definitely a walk to the facilities. Some 4 person cabins with 1 staff that live in. Younger girls are 4/2. Can request bunk mates. Some are connecting bunks.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

The waterfront is beautiful.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

They have a rotating schedule where they switch between A day and B Day. Every 5th day is a S day and those afternoons are "Blue and Green Days." We were there on an S day afternoon so were able to see their Blue and Green Day. A camper is on a team for life. On the first night it is very Harry Potter-esqe. They pull out a marble and that is your team. On A and B days there are 5 periods that are pre-assigned (but they are your choice) and then a 6th choice daily. The main 5 choices picked before camp. After 2 weeks then you can change activities. There is an outdoor adventure program and they go out every B day on hikes.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

S day campfires. Oldest campers do overnight white water rafting trip. 2nd oldest do day trip of mini golf and ice cream. Junior campers do on property camp out (mandatory). Campers leave rocks at the end of each session on the path from campfire. New rock every year. Only extra are riding (14 horses) 20% of girls ride and tennis plus (so extra tennis lessons). Brothers go to pemi and kingswood and they do sibling ice cream socials with both but no other socials with boys.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Highland hall is the oldest building on camp and houses the dining room. Breakfast and dinner are assigned. Mostly by cabin/ line. Lunch is free seat. Bathrooms are under highland hall. Lots of food options in addition to the main option. They have a garden and put those vegetables in salad bar. Food is at bar area and served buffet style. Always a pasta bar at dinner. Full time allergy chef…Separate everything. 4 full time nurses. Relationship with Dr 15 min away. Can handle diabetic.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

It is Non religious except they say a Robert Burns poem before breakfast and dinner that does mention god. It is non-uniform except Blue and Green Days and once a week a campfire where they wear uniform with tartan sash. A camper is on a team for life. On the first night it is very Harry Potter-esqe. The girls all seemed very nice.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Visiting day is departure day. No phone calls. They post pics - 3 full time photographers. Only extra are riding (14 horses) 20% of girls ride and tennis plus (so extra tennis lessons). Brothers go to pemi and kingswood and they do sibling ice cream socials with both but no other socials with boys.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Lochearn has 2 sessions of 3.5 weeks with 180 girls per session, with girls from 23 states and 6 countries. It is non-uniform except Blue and Green Days and once a week a campfire where they wear uniform with tartan sash, and it is Non religious except they say a Robert Burns poem before breakfast and dinner that does mention god. They have a rotating schedule where they switch between A day and B Day; every 5th day is a S day and those afternoons are Blue and Green Days; on A and B days there are 5 periods that are pre-assigned (but they are your choice) and then a 6th choice daily, and after 2 weeks then you can change activities. The waterfront is beautiful, there is an outdoor adventure program and they go out every B day on hikes, and there are 4 full time nurses and a full time allergy chef.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Blue and Green Days; a camper is on a team for life.
  - On the first night it is very Harry Potter-esqe; they pull out a marble and that is your team.
  - The waterfront is beautiful.
  - There is an outdoor adventure program and they go out every B day on hikes.
  - Oldest campers do overnight white water rafting trip.
  - Full time allergy chef…Separate everything.
  - 4 full time nurses; Relationship with Dr 15 min away. Can handle diabetic.
  - They have a garden and put those vegetables in salad bar; Always a pasta bar at dinner.
  - They post pics - 3 full time photographers.
  - Highland hall is the oldest building on camp and houses the dining room.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Bathrooms are not in bunk (no toilets, no showers, no sinks). It is definitely a walk to the facilities.
  - Bunks have electricity, but no outlets.
  - No phone calls.
  - Visiting day is departure day.
  - No other socials with boys.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Wa-Klo
  - Merriwood
  - Kippewa
  - Fernwood Cove

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Lochearn Lindsey Binstock - Toured July 29, 2024 We (Emily S, Allison A, Natasha and I) were toured by Erin (asst director) and Eliza (co-director with mom) They have 2 sessions of 3.5 weeks There are 180 girls per session. Girls from 23 states and 6 countries. People usually stick with the same session. Visiting day is departure day. Have had new girls at 13/14 but depends on the camper. It is non-uniform except Blue and Green Days and once a week a campfire where the where uniform with tartan sash. It is Non religious except they say a Robert Burns poem before breakfast and dinner that does mention god. They have a rotating schedule where they switch between A day and B Day. Every 5th day is a S day and those afternoons are “Blue and Green Days.” We were there on an S day afternoon so were able to see their Blue and Green Day. A camper is on a team for life. On the first night it is very Harry Potter-esqe. They pull out a marble and that is your team. On A and B days there are 5 periods that are pre-assigned (but they are your choice) and then a 6th choice daily. The main 5 choices picked before camp. After 2 weeks then you can change activities. There are 4 different lines of bunks. All are along water. Campus leader on each line - young adults. Not college kids Junior line - 7,8,9 year olds Subs line 10,11,12 year olds Senior line CT house - Bathrooms are not in bunk (no toilets, no showers, no sinks). Bunks have electricity, but no outlets. It is definitely a walk to the facilities. Some 4 person cabins with 1 staff that live in. Younger girls are 4/2. Can request bunk mates. Some are connecting bunks Highland hall is the oldest building on camp and houses the dining room. Breakfast and dinner are assigned. Mostly by cabin/ line. Lunch is free seat. Bathrooms are under highland hall. Lots of food options in addition to the main option. They have a garden and put those vegetables in salad bar. Food is at bar area and served buffet style. Always a pasta bar at dinner. Full time allergy chef…Separate everything. No phone calls. They post pics- 3 full time photographers There is an outdoor adventure program and they go out every B day on hikes. Oldest campers do overnight white water rafting trip. 2nd oldest do day trip of mini golf and ice cream. Junior campers do on property camp out (mandatory) S day campfires. The waterfront is beautiful Campers leave rocks at the end of each session on the path from campfire. New rock every year. Only extra are riding (14 horses) 20% of girls ride and tennis plus (so extra tennis lessons ) Brothers go to pemi and kingswood and they do sibling ice cream socials with both but no other socials with boys. 4 full time nurses Relationship with Dr 15 min away. Can handle diabetic The girls all seemed very nice. I would give it with Wa-Klo, Merriwood, Kippewa and Fernwood Cove.

title: Method Learning — Julie — 11-29-23
text:
# Method Learning

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Offers College Counseling, SAT/ACT test prep, tutoring for all ages from elementary to college and beyond, also specialty tutoring such as executive functioning and general test taking strategies. Offers college counseling and tutoring packages at three price points -basic to more comprehensive including test prep, financial literacy courses and college and career assessments -also offers pay as you go tutoring. Company offers many free resources such as webinars on a frequent basis. Goal of Method Learning for students: Use higher ACT and SAT scores to get accepted to more places and have more options leading to earning more in academic merit. Very knowledgeable on the new digital SAT and how to raise scores; very knowledgeable on ACT as well.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Owner and speaker: Tom Ehlers: tom@methodlearning.com 1-887-871-PREP. Tom, a Princeton graduate, began as an SAT tutor and grew his business to include College Admissions services with a goal to optimize college applications. College advisors: Dr. Amanda Sterk and Dr. Jessica Ruzicka- offers free consultations. Work to match students with a tutor who is a good fit and will adjust when necessary. Two price tiers of tutors based on experience.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Unknown

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Unknown

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Unknown

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Very knowledgeable on the new digital SAT and how to raise scores. Very knowledgeable on ACT as well, can direct families to pay for a service called ACT TIR to view their actual scored exams to see what mistakes were made. Helps students decrease anxiety around tests by preparing adequately, taking multiple practice tests, and examining incorrect answers. Company offers many free resources such as webinars on a frequent basis. Offers college counseling and tutoring packages at three price points - basic to more comprehensive including test prep, financial literacy courses and college and career assessments - also offers pay as you go tutoring. Offers specialty tutoring such as executive functioning and general test taking strategies.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Unknown

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Work to match students with a tutor who is a good fit and will adjust when necessary. College advisors offer free consultations. Company offers many free resources such as webinars on a frequent basis.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Sessions are mainly on zoom but offers in person in the NY metropolitan tri-state area. Offers college counseling and tutoring packages at three price points - basic to more comprehensive including test prep, financial literacy courses and college and career assessments - also offers pay as you go tutoring. Two price tiers of tutors based on experience. College advisors: Dr. Amanda Sterk and Dr. Jessica Ruzicka- offers free consultations. Can direct families to pay for a service called ACT TIR to view their actual scored exams to see what mistakes were made.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Method Learning offers College Counseling, SAT/ACT test prep, and tutoring for all ages, including specialty tutoring such as executive functioning and general test taking strategies. Sessions are mainly on Zoom, with in-person options in the NY metropolitan tri-state area, and the company provides many free resources such as frequent webinars and free consultations with college advisors Dr. Amanda Sterk and Dr. Jessica Ruzicka. Families can choose packages at three price points or pay as you go, with two price tiers of tutors based on experience. Goal of Method Learning for students: Use higher ACT and SAT scores to get accepted to more places and have more options leading to earning more in academic merit.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Very knowledgeable on the new digital SAT and how to raise scores.
  - Very knowledgeable on ACT; can direct families to pay for ACT TIR to review scored exams.
  - Helps students decrease anxiety around tests by preparing adequately, taking multiple practice tests, and examining incorrect answers.
  - College advisors (Dr. Amanda Sterk and Dr. Jessica Ruzicka) offer free consultations.
  - Work to match students with a tutor who is a good fit and will adjust when necessary.
  - Company offers many free resources such as webinars on a frequent basis.
  - Offers specialty tutoring such as executive functioning and general test taking strategies.

  

#### Lowlights

  - No information provided on daily timetable or session length/scheduling.
  - No information provided on health, safety, or food.
  - No information provided on living quarters or waterfront/aquatics.
  - Pricing specifics (exact dollar amounts) not provided.

  

#### Similar Camps

title: North Star — Emily — 7-21-23
text:
# North Star

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

North Star Camp – Poland, Maine. Co-ed with a total of about 215 kids. They offer 2, 3, 4 and 7-week sessions; the first 4 weeks and 7 weeks are the most popular sessions. Director Steven Bernstein gave us the tour (Emily R, Michele G and Liz M were on site; Brooke was on the phone).

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Kids come from 26 states and 17 countries. Counselors are a mix of International and some former campers. Activities are done by division, Juniors, Middles and Seniors. There are Counselor Heads of each division. There is a CIT program for kids going into 12th grade. Can choose to be a CIT or a camper at that point if they are not into taking care of kids. The following year you can be a counselor. There is also opportunity for Leadership training and community service. Steven is an enthusiastic Director that knows every kid by name and puts money into the camp each year. Three of Steven’s kids are working at the camp.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Boys’ and girls’ cabins are on opposite sides of camp. They bunk by gender at birth. Cabins tend to have 9-10 kids with 2-3 counselors. The youngest girls have 4 brand new cabins.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

There are 2 waterfront areas where kids can paddleboard, kayak, sail and canoe. The other area down the road is for waterskiing and wakeboarding. Swim lessons are optional. Kids need to take a swim test at the beginning of summer.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

There are 2 morning periods and 4 in the afternoon. Programming is all elective with many choices of activities such as woodworking, pottery, fabrics, drawing, stained glass, lake, sports, music, rocketry, mountain biking, culinary, outdoor adventure, flag football, theater, etc. Most activities are co-ed except some sports. Evening activities and special activities are very creative and fun like Food Network, Survivor, black light dance party, Moonlight Madness etc.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Rock band and music is popular and kids perform their songs at the end of the week. There is Dungeons and Dragons. There are always 2 musicals and 1 all division play that gets put on. There is also a video production and digital and photo design elective. Steven loves basketball and plays against the campers. He has a shooting machine. The baseball field has new turf and batting cages. Tennis courts are nice and that is a very popular elective. North Star plays intercamps but it is not a sports competitive camp. Kids do take trips outside of camp for outdoor adventure and ferry boat ride, sunset hikes. There are 5 camp fire areas. 1st 4 weeks there is an Eco Challenge with teams Fire, Water, Earth and Air and second half is World Games like Olympics. Steven is friends with Rachel and Jamie from MKN and they share special activity ideas.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Whole camp eats at the same time, younger kids in the dining hall and older kids in a tented area outside the dining hall. Dining hall was clean. Serve all kinds of food and can handle all allergies, gluten, vegetarian. There are three meals and morning and afternoon snack. Fresh fruit available all day. Campers sit with their cabin and eat family style. There is a breakfast bar and a salad bar at lunch and dinner. Health center is air conditioned with Nurses on staff. There is a Pediatrician on call all summer. The closest hospital is a 12-mile drive.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Kids at North Star use all different pronouns and LGBT is accepted. Kids are mixed religions. There are no religious services. Steven can provide bar mitzvah lessons to kids. Each week they honor campers for meeting the camp values, RICHS. Respect, Independence, Caring, Honor and Spirit. The kids seemed happy. Since it’s all elective, it’s not as structured as other camps, but kids were busy and not wandering.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

There is no charter flight but the camp will pick up kids at Logan in Boston or Portland. There is a bus from locations in New Jersey, New York, Darien, CT and Newton, MA. Additional fees include bus transportation, laundry service, linen rental, horseback riding and golf. Provide free linens and towels for international campers. Camp is well priced. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

North Star is a co-ed camp in Poland, Maine with about 215 kids and 2, 3, 4 and 7-week sessions (the first 4 weeks and 7 weeks are most popular). Programming is all elective with many choices (arts, music, sports, outdoor adventure, waterfront, theater), plus creative evening activities and big events like Eco Challenge and World Games. It feels accepting and progressive, with kids using different pronouns, LGBT accepted, mixed religions/no services, and weekly RICHS values recognition; kids seemed happy and busy. This is a well-priced, not particularly sporty camp that still offers access to sports and intercamps, making it a good fit for artsy kids who want options.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Enthusiastic Director that knows every kid by name and puts money into the camp each year.
  - Programming is all elective with many choices of activities (arts, music, theater, sports, outdoor adventure).
  - Rock band and music is popular; kids perform their songs at the end of the week; always 2 musicals and 1 all division play.
  - Accepting and progressive culture (kids use different pronouns; LGBT is accepted; mixed religions; no religious services).
  - Strong waterfront with two areas, plus waterskiing and wakeboarding; optional swim lessons with a beginning-of-summer swim test.
  - Facilities callouts: new turf baseball field and batting cages; nice tennis courts; video production and digital/photo design.
  - Signature events and trips: Eco Challenge, World Games, sunset hikes, ferry boat ride; creative evening activities.
  - Dining can handle all allergies (gluten, vegetarian); breakfast bar and salad bar.
  - Health center is air conditioned with Nurses on staff; Pediatrician on call all summer.

  

#### Lowlights

  - No charter flight.
  - Since it’s all elective, it’s not as structured as other camps.
  - Additional fees include bus transportation, laundry service, linen rental, horseback riding and golf.
  - They bunk by gender at birth.
  - Intercamps are played, but it is not a sports competitive camp.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Island Lake
  - Lindenmere
  - Independent Lake

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

NORTH STAR CAMP – Poland, Maine JULY 7, 2023 Emily R, Michele G and Liz M Director Steven Bernstein gave us the tour. Brooke was on the phone at the time. North Star is co-ed with a total of about 215 kids. They offer 2, 3, 4 and 7-week sessions. The first 4 weeks and 7 weeks are the most popular sessions. Kids come from 26 states and 17 countries. There is no charter flight but the camp will pick up kids at Logan in Boston or Portland. There is a bus from locations in New Jersey, New York, Darien, CT and Newton, MA. Whole camp eats at the same time, younger kids in the dining hall and older kids in a tented area outside the dining hall. Dining hall was clean. Serve all kinds of food and can handle all allergies, gluten, vegetarian. There are three meals and morning and afternoon snack. Fresh fruit available all day. Campers sit with their cabin and eat family style. There is a breakfast bar and a salad bar at lunch and dinner. There are 2 morning periods and 4 in the afternoon. Evening activities and special activities are very creative and fun like Food Network, Survivor, black light dance party, Moonlight Madness etc. Steven friends with Rachel and Jamie from MKN and they share special activity ideas. Programming is all elective with many choices of activities such as woodworking, pottery, fabrics, drawing, stained glass, lake, sports, music, rocketry, mountain biking, culinary, outdoor adventure, flag football, theater, etc. Rock band and music is popular and kids perform their songs at the end of the week. There is Dungeons and Dragons. There are also opportunities to do community service. Activities are done by division, Juniors, Middles and Seniors. There are Counselor Heads of each division. Most activities are co-ed except some sports. Steven loves basketball and plays against the campers. He has a shooting machine. The baseball field has new turf and batting cages. North Star plays intercamps but it is not a sports competitive camp. Tennis courts are nice and that is a very popular elective. There is also a video production and digital and photo design elective. There are always 2 musicals and 1 all division play that gets put on. Kids do take trips outside of camp for outdoor adventure and ferry boat ride, sunset hikes. There are 5 camp fire areas, 2 waterfront areas where kids can paddleboard, kayak, sail and canoe. The other area down the road is for waterskiing and wakeboarding. Swim lessons are optional. Kids need to take a swim test at beginning of summer. 1 st 4 weeks there is an Eco Challenge with teams Fire, Water, Earth and Air and second half is World Games like Olympics. Counselors are a mix of International and some former campers. There is a CIT program for kids going into 12 th grade. Can choose to be a CIT or a camper at that point if they are not into taking care of kids. The following year you can be a counselor. There is also opportunity for Leadership training and community service. Kids at North Star use all different pronouns and LGTB is accepted. They bunk by gender at birth. Kids are mixed religions. There are no religious services. Steven can provide bar mitzvah lessons to kids. Each week they honor campers for meeting the camp values, RICHS. Respect, Independence, Caring, Honor and Spirit. Boys’ and girls’ cabins are on opposite sides of camp. Cabins tend to have 9-10 kids with 2-3 counselors. The youngest girls have 4 brand new cabins. Health center is air conditioned with Nurses on staff. There is a Pediatrician on call all summer. The closest hospital is a 12-mile drive. Additional fees include bus transportation, laundry service, linen rental, horseback riding and golf. Provide free linens and towels for international campers. Steven is an enthusiastic Director that knows every kid by name and puts money into the camp each year. The kids seemed happy. Since its all elective, it’s not as structured as other camps, but kids were busy and not wandering. Three of Steven’s kids are working at the camp. Camp is well priced. Kids that would do well at North Star are not particularly sporty but want access to various activities. It seems like a very accepting and progressive camp and on the artsy side. Would refer this camp with Island Lake, Lindenmere and Independent Lake.

title: Lindenmere — Heather — 7-7-24
text:
# Lindenmere

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Visited July 1, 2024. All choice camp—Opened in 1935, being run by owners, the Garfinkels since 2007. 450 total campers first session, 50% stay all summer; 2nd session 2 weeks; 80% rate of return. Camp broken into Junior, Middle, Senior groups. Fully air conditioned camp on 200 acres (use half). Demographics: 75% Jewish but camp has no religious affiliation; most from Long Island, Manhattan, Philadelphia, Maryland and Florida. Kids all skill levels will succeed.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Camp Lindenmere Tour given by Kyle and Hayley Garfinkel (oversees Girls side) co-directors, along with their father, Mitch Garfinkel whom we met briefly. CE’s: Heather Pasquale, Michelle Burger, Riley McDonogh. 240 counselors; Rule of threes for counselors. Youngest campers are rising 2nd and rising 3rd graders; oldest are rising 11th (become LIT). Big little program mandatory.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Assign beds to campers. Fully air conditioned camp. Weekly laundry service. Unknown cabin/bunk sizes and layouts.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Lake—Built a path under the road to the lake—.2 mile walk to the beach/lake. 45 minutes including swim as part of instructional activities. Movies in the lake house. Camel beach appears on weekly field trip list. Unknown pool facilities, lifeguard staffing, and boating specifics.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Campers choose 3 majors before camp begins based on favorite activities; they are choosing for whole summer. 3 minors chosen nightly—get schedules next day, switch every day, chance to try new things. 2–5 once a week instructional activities 45 minutes including swim. Evening Activities are larger groups including the campers’ bunk but not necessarily their whole division. Activities are co-ed.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Activities: Olympics 1st session, Color war 2nd session. 8 tennis courts. Innovation center run by science teacher including website design and 3d printing—5 printers and stop motion animation, robotics, STEM Rocketry. Horses included in tuition, 10 horses and a pony, include stable management. Compete in intercamp—often with Pine forest, Canadensis, Nock- a- Mixon, Owego and Pocono Springs. Flag football tournament. Big basketball tournament—2 covered and lit courts. Ninja warrior course. ATV track. Fitness center—most for rising 9th graders and older. Street hockey, Flag Football, Tennis, Sand volleyball, Aeroball, Hockey, Lacrosse, Pickleball, Skate park. Weekly field trip all camp every Thursday to places like Dorsey park, Camel beach. Bring in fresh activities, example: Puppy Palace, Fallout fun, silent disco, monster truck shows etc.. Rec hall: 2 plays per session if camper’s major; Tech theater—sets, props, lights, sound. 5 rock shop studios. Explore world—like scouts. Mountain biking, hiking and explorers. Dj, podcast. Circus-Gymnastics, 2 flying trapeze. 2 Dance studios, Intercamp dance completion. Baseball tier 2 sport. Ultimate frisbee. Trapeze and horses can close out and then make 4th major. Movies in the lake house. Arts and Crafts: Glass art curriculums; Cosmetology and business plan to ‘sell’ products including lip balm, nail polish, soap making; Candle making, ceramics, jewelry, drawing, painting, stained glass, textiles and leather, fashion design, tie dye. Cooking. High and low ropes course. Outdoor adventure.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Health Center: 5 nurses 24/7; Doctors some weeks and telemedicine; urgent care 10 minutes away. 2 social workers—one on premises all 7 weeks. Dining Hall: Buffet style dining hall including omelet station, salad bar, chicken fingers, grilled cheese and pasta bar. Full celiac kitchen. Counselors must disperse at dining hall. Entire camp eats together. Real water and ice machines throughout campus, not coolers—Water bottles get put through camp dishwasher once a week.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Visiting Day: Many areas perform for the parents. Motivational service on Friday evenings. Ice cream socials—650 ice cream sundaes on Fridays. Friday night campfire—no awards. Big little program mandatory. All choice culture with co-ed activities. No uniform. Entire camp eats together.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Bus Transport; chaperoned flight from Florida. Camper Specialist Correspondence: Phone calls every Sunday 15 minutes; parents and campers can email through uploading program; camp posts 600 pics on Campanion/day and a blog. Can send packages. Canteen—Xbox and switches available, 5 nights per week. Visiting Day. Unknown tuition and extra fees.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Best fit for campers who want an all choice camp with 3 majors for the whole summer and nightly minors that switch every day. Families who value a fully air conditioned campus, robust healthcare (5 nurses 24/7, doctors some weeks, telemedicine) and a full celiac kitchen will appreciate the setup. Co-ed activities, intercamp competition, an innovation center/STEM, and horses included in tuition offer breadth from athletics to arts to tech. Demographics are 75% Jewish (no religious affiliation) with many from Long Island, Manhattan, Philadelphia, Maryland and Florida.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - All choice camp with 3 majors and nightly minors.
  - Opened in 1935; run by the Garfinkels since 2007.
  - Fully air conditioned; 200 acres (use half).
  - 450 campers first session; 50% stay all summer; 80% rate of return.
  - 240 counselors; Rule of threes for counselors.
  - Full celiac kitchen and robust buffet options.
  - Health Center: 5 nurses 24/7; doctors some weeks; telemedicine; urgent care 10 minutes away.
  - Innovation center (website design, 3D printing—5 printers, robotics, STEM Rocketry).
  - Horses included in tuition (10 horses and a pony) with stable management.
  - Weekly all-camp Thursday field trips (e.g., Dorsey park, Camel beach).
  - 600 pics on Campanion/day plus blog; scheduled Sunday phone calls.
  - Real water and ice machines; water bottles washed weekly.
  - Big tournaments and intercamp competition; 8 tennis courts; 2 covered and lit basketball courts; ninja course; ATV track; skate park.
  - Circus with 2 flying trapeze; dance studios; 5 rock shop studios; extensive arts (glass, stained glass, textiles and leather, fashion).
  - Friday traditions: motivational service, ice cream socials (650 sundaes), and campfire (no awards).

  

#### Lowlights

  - Lake access is via a path under the road with a 0.2 mile walk to the beach/lake.
  - Trapeze and horses can close out; may need to become a 4th major.
  - Fitness center is mostly for rising 9th graders and older.
  - Canteen video games (Xbox and Switches) available 5 nights per week.
  - Beds are assigned to campers (no self-selection noted).
  - 2nd session is 2 weeks only.
  - Waterfront specifics beyond the lake path and a swim period are not detailed.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Pine forest
  - Canadensis
  - Nock- a- Mixon
  - Owego
  - Pocono Springs

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Camp Lindenmere Tour given by Kyle and Hayley Garfinkel (oversees Girls side) co- directors, along with their father, Mitch Garfinkel whom we met briefly CE’s: Heather Pasquale, Michelle Burger, Riley McDonogh Visited July 1, 2024 All choice camp-Opened in 1935, being run by owners, the Garfinkels since 2007 Campers choose 3 majors before camp begins based on favorite activities, they are choosing for whole summer 3 minors chosen nightly-get schedules next day, switch every day, chance to try new things 2-5 once a week instructional activities 45 minutes including swim 450 total campers first session, 50% stay all summer-Kids all skill levels will succeed 2nd session 2 weeks 80% rate of return 240 counselors, Rule of threes for counselors Youngest campers are rising 2nd and rising 3rd graders, oldest are rising 11th (become LIT) No uniform Camp broken into Junior, Middle, Senior groups Fully air conditioned camp 200 acres use half Assign beds to campers Bus Transport, chaperoned flight from Florida Big little program mandatory Weekly laundry service Visiting Day: Many areas perform for the parents Motivational service on Friday evenings, Ice cream socials 650 ice cream sundaes on Fridays Friday night campfire-no awards Demographics: 75% Jewish but camp has no religious affiliation most from Long Island, Manhattan, Philadelphia, Maryland and Florida Health Center: 5 nurses 24/7, Doctors some weeks and telemedicine, urgent care 10 minutes away 2 social workers- one on premises all 7 weeks Camper Specialist Correspondence: Phone calls every Sunday 15 minutes parents and campers can email through uploading program camp posts 600 pics on Campanion/day and a blog Can send packages Dining Hall: Buffet style dining hall including omelet station, salad bar, chicken fingers, grilled cheese and pasta bar Full celiac kitchen Counselors must disperse at dining hall Entire camp eats together Real water and ice machines throughout campus, not coolers-Water bottles get put through camp dishwasher once a week Canteen-Xbox and switches available, 5 nights per week Activities: Olympics 1st session, Color war 2nd session 8 tennis courts Innovation center run by science teacher including website design and 3d printing-5 printers and stop motion animation, robotics, STEM Rocketry Horses included in tuition, 10 horses and a pony, include stable management Evening Activities are larger groups including the campers’ bunk but not necessarily their whole division Activities are co-ed Compete in intercamp- often with Pine forest, Canadensis, Nock- a- Mixon, Owego and Pocono Springs Flag football tournament Big basketball tournament-2 covered and lit courts Ninja warrior course ATV track Fitness center-most for rising 9th graders and older Street hockey Flag Football Tennis Sand volleyball Aeroball Hockey Lacrosse Pickleball Skate park Weekly field trip all camp every Thursday to places like Dorsey park, Camel beach Bring in fresh activities, example: Puppy Palace, Fallout fun, silent disco, monster truck shows etc.. Rec hall: 2 plays per session if camper’s major, Tech theater- sets, props, lights, sound 5 rock shop studios Explore world-like scouts Mountain biking, hiking and explorers Dj, podcast Circus-Gymnastics, 2 flying trapeze 2 Dance studios, Intercamp dance completion Baseball tier 2 sport Ultimate frisbee Trapeze and horses can close out and then make 4th major Movies in the lake house Arts and Crafts: Glass art curriculums, Cosmetology and business plan to ‘sell’ products including lip balm, nail polish, soap making, Candle making, ceramics, jewelry, drawing, painting, stained glass, textiles and leather, fashion design, tie dye Cooking High and low ropes course Outdoor adventure Lake-Built a path under the road to the lake-.2 mile walk to the beach/lake

title: Moosilauke — Jennifer — 7-14-22
text:
# Moosilauke

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

All boys – full for this summer with 145 boys each session. Offers 4.5 or 2.5 week sessions, 1 week session for ages 7-10 only. They do not offer full summer and there is a 1 day break between sessions. Currently have campers from 19 different states, decent amount from CA as Bill and Sabine work at a private school there. No religion at camp.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Jenn, Beth, Shari and Lisa toured Moosilauke on 6/29/22 with Bill – owner and director. Sisters attend Merriwood, Lochearn, Farwell and Wa-klo.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Bunks – typical 12 campers and 3 staff. Shower house.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Lovely lakefront. Swim instruction in the lake. Also offer wakesurfing, waterskiing, tubing, as well as non motorized water sports. According to Bill, waterfront is one of their 2 strongest areas.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Schedule: 2 assigned periods in the morning then a free period late morning; afternoon consists of 2 electives.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

According to Bill, waterfront and outdoor adventure are their 2 strongest areas. Until age 13 – boys have mandatory mountain biking, hiking and wilderness overnight trips. Older campers have a 3 day overnight adventure to Mt Lafayette. Also, there are weekly adventure trips that boys can sign up for. No formal performing arts program. Camp does have a lot of sports fields. They play intercamps with Pemi, Walt Whitman and Kingswood.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Dining Hall was charming – had a fireplace. Campers eat family style. Change tables weekly. Can accommodate food allergies. They do have a peanut free table but still in the air so would depend on the severity of the allergy. No canteen but serve snack after 4th period.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Overall impression: Camp looked great, met some happy and very polite boys. Bill talked a lot about their philosophy of character building. Stressed they have a zero tolerance on bullying.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Since camp experts last toured have changed their entrance, no longer share with Merriwood. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown. Travel logistics: Unknown. Extra costs: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Moosilauke is an all-boys camp that is full for this summer with 145 boys each session and offers 4.5 or 2.5 week sessions, plus a 1 week session for ages 7-10 only. It has a lovely lakefront and, according to Bill, waterfront and outdoor adventure are their 2 strongest areas, with swim instruction in the lake and wakesurfing, waterskiing, tubing, and non motorized water sports, plus mandatory mountain biking, hiking and wilderness overnights until age 13 and a 3 day overnight to Mt Lafayette for older campers. The daily schedule has 2 assigned periods in the morning then a free period late morning, and the afternoon consists of 2 electives; campers eat family style in a charming Dining Hall with a fireplace and the camp can accommodate food allergies. Overall impression: Camp looked great, with happy and very polite boys, a philosophy of character building, and a zero tolerance on bullying.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Lovely lakefront.
  - According to Bill, waterfront and outdoor adventure are their 2 strongest areas.
  - Camp looked great, met some happy and very polite boys.
  - Bill talked a lot about their philosophy of character building.
  - Stressed they have a zero tolerance on bullying.
  - They play intercamps with Pemi, Walt Whitman and Kingswood.
  - Camp does have a lot of sports fields.
  - All boys – full for this summer with 145 boys each session.

  

#### Lowlights

  - No formal performing arts program.
  - They do have a peanut free table but still in the air so would depend on the severity of the allergy.
  - They do not offer full summer and there is a 1 day break between sessions.
  - No canteen but serve snack after 4th period.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Pemi
  - Walt Whitman
  - Kingswood

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Moosilauke – Jenn, Beth, Shari and Lisa toured Moosilauke on 6/29/22 with Bill – owner and director. All boys – full for this summer with 145 boys each session. Offers 4.5 or 2.5 week sessions, 1 week session for ages 7-10 only. They do not offer full summer and there is a 1 day break between sessions. Currently have campers from 19 different states, decent amount from CA as Bill and Sabine work at a private school there. No religion at camp. Sisters attend Merriwood, Lochearn, Farwell and Wa-klo. Since camp experts last toured have changed their entrance, no longer share with Merriwood. Bunks – typical 12 campers and 3 staff. Shower house Dining Hall was charming – had a fireplace. title: NSLC — Lindsey — 5-5-24
text:
# NSLC

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

NSLC attracts all kinds of students of varying academic levels, including those that are autistic and have ADHD. High school are for those who have finished 9th grade, and the middle school program is for those who have finished 6th, 7th and 8th grades. The exception to that is Yale and they do not take students under 16 years old. The programs range in size with Georgetown having almost 500 kids on campus, but the individual programs within each campus vary between 50 or 100 students. They are filling up, but the wait lists are moving so clients should not be afraid to put their name on the wait lists.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

I (Lindsey Binstock) had coffee with Kelsey Pearson from National Student Leadership Conference (NSLC). Kelsey is Director of Operations for the Georgetown University site as well as a Co-Director of Domestic Educator Relations for the National Student Leadership Conference (NSLC). NSLC attracts all kinds of students of varying academic levels, including those that are autistic and have ADHD. There are some serious kids in the classes that are taking notes that most are just listening. The medicine and business majors are the more “sophisticated kids”.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

All of the dorms are air conditioned, except for Yale. The bathrooms depend on the site and are either in the rooms or the hallway. There is laundry access but most people don't do their laundry. The dorm is just for the NSLC students and their staff however the dining hall may have other college students eating there at the same time. Students choose their gender online and have options for their "preferred gender" and their "housing gender". Nowhere on the form does it say biological gender and no one asks. You can request a specific roommate and they can also provide a single for medical reasons. They can accommodate physically handicapped students.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Unknown

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

You can do multiple sessions but they try to encourage people to sign up for different campuses if they are going to do multiple sessions so they could have different activities in the evenings and weekends.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

The most popular concentrations are medicine, engineering, psychology and law.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

They do have a very strict drug and alcohol policy. They can handle students with diabetes and Duke is the best campus for that because they have a nurse there. The dining hall may have other college students eating there at the same time.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

There are some serious kids in the classes that are taking notes that most are just listening. The medicine and business majors are the more “sophisticated kids”. Most students do not come with a friend.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

50% of students get dropped off by parents and the other 50% take a shuttle from the airport. Middle school students can be commuters and approximately 40% are. All the high school students, however, must be residential. Students bring their phones, iPads and laptops. They do offer scholarships but they close on March 15th.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

NSLC attracts all kinds of students of varying academic levels, including those that are autistic and have ADHD. High school are for those who have finished 9th grade, and the middle school program is for those who have finished 6th, 7th and 8th grades (the exception to that is Yale and they do not take students under 16 years old). The programs range in size with Georgetown having almost 500 kids on campus, but the individual programs within each campus vary between 50 or 100 students, and middle school students can be commuters (approximately 40%) while all high school students must be residential. The most popular concentrations are medicine, engineering, psychology and law; they are filling up, but the wait lists are moving, and they do offer scholarships but they close on March 15th.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Program scale: Georgetown having almost 500 kids on campus; individual programs vary between 50 or 100 students.
  - Inclusive of students with autism and ADHD.
  - Strong interest areas: medicine, engineering, psychology and law.
  - Very strict drug and alcohol policy.
  - Can handle students with diabetes; Duke is the best campus for that because they have a nurse there.
  - Students choose their gender online with options for "preferred gender" and "housing gender"; roommate requests and medical singles available.
  - Middle school commuter option (\~40%) and airport shuttles available.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Yale does not take students under 16 years old.
  - All the high school students must be residential.
  - All of the dorms are air conditioned, except for Yale.
  - Bathrooms depend on the site and are either in the rooms or the hallway.
  - The dining hall may have other college students eating there at the same time.
  - Scholarships close on March 15th.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

I (Lindsey Binstock) had coffee with Kelsey Pearson from National Student Leadership Conference (NSLC). Kelsey is Director of Operations for the Georgetown University site as well as a Co-Director of Domestic Educator Relations for the National Student Leadership Conference (NSLC). NSLC attracts all kinds of students of varying academic levels, including those that are autistic and have ADHD. There are some serious kids in the classes that are taking notes that most are just listening. The medicine and business majors are the more “sophisticated kids”. High school are for those who have finished 9th grade, and the middle school program is for those who have finished 6 th , 7th and 8th grades. title: ISC Spain — Carrie — 7-8-22
text:
# ISC Spain

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

ISC is based in a student residence directly opposite the University of Alicante (all residences are outside of the campus). They do not have sole use of the facilities but share with current students – who are there until mid July; however, they take over entire floors. They have 10 classrooms in a next door building (not seen) and 4 in the dorm building. Also adjoining is the dining hall (they go out the main door and walk down the pavement).

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Drop off and visit with Vera Vallianatou, Educational Manager ISC. First week was 56 students. This week was over 100. On average they plan to have 100-120 per week. Most stay for 2 weeks but this year a lot doing one week. Mix of nationalities. No longer any Russians but largest groups this year are Americans, Italians, French and British and some expat Chinese. English is the language of the camp outside of class – ie kids are not forced to speak Spanish all the time.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Residence is modern and very clean and airy. Kids each have an ensuite room and two share a small foyer with a fridge and sink. Can ask to be with a friend in pairs or they will split up with different nationalities. Rooms have bed, desk, wardrobe, dresser and shower room. Cleaned weekly. No safe. ISC provides sheets and towels although students are asked to bring a beach towel. Basic soap not provided. Separate laundry room and a common space (although so far my daughter says they don’t use it). Lobby; shared common space; shared foyer; room ensuite.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Pool on site. The leisure option includes some beach time too. Transport in own mini bus or on a rented bus to the beach.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Classes of approximately 10. Classes run for 3 and ¾ hours in the morning. Placement test first morning with introductions. Afternoon lunch and siesta for 2 ½ hours. Late afternoon you choose (in advance) if you want to do sport/multi sport or leisure. Uni sports facilities close but they don’t walk. Shops near and the kids are taken to the shop on the first day to stock up on anything missing. Evening mix of activities off campus (ie in downtown Alicante) or games/cinema etc.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Weekend trips to waterparks and Madrid depending on the weeks chosen.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Security on the door but no one checking per se who is coming in or out. However at night they have a security guard on duty. Cafeteria style. Can cater for gluten free.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Appears well organised with good control over the kids. Will update with feedback from my daughter afterwards\!

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Dropped off my daughter and a friend at ISC on the evening of the 1st day of week 2 due to flight cancellations meaning they couldn’t go directly to Alicante. Transport in own mini bus or on a rented bus to the beach. Parent communication, tech policy, and extra costs: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

ISC is based in a student residence directly opposite the University of Alicante. Kids each have an ensuite room and two share a small foyer with a fridge and sink. Classes run for 3 and ¾ hours in the morning with a placement test the first morning, and late afternoon you choose (in advance) if you want to do sport/multi sport or leisure, with evening activities off campus (ie in downtown Alicante) or games/cinema. Weekend trips to waterparks and Madrid depending on the weeks chosen, with a mix of nationalities and English as the language of the camp outside of class.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Residence is modern and very clean and airy.
  - Pool on site.
  - Appears well organised with good control over the kids.
  - Can cater for gluten free.
  - Classes of approximately 10.
  - On average they plan to have 100-120 per week.
  - They take over entire floors.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Security on the door but no one checking per se who is coming in or out.
  - No safe.
  - They do not have sole use of the facilities but share with current students – who are there until mid July.
  - Basic soap not provided.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

ISC Spain July 3rd 2022 Drop off and visit with Vera Vallianatou, Educational Manager ISC Dropped off my daughter and a friend at ISC on the evening of the 1st day of week 2 due to flight cancellations meaning they couldn’t go directly to Alicante. ISC is based in a student residence directly opposite the University of Alicante (all residences are outside of the campus). They do not have sole use of the facilities but share with current students – who are there until mid July. However, they take over entire floors. title: Matoaka — Pilar — 8-25-22
text:
# Matoaka

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Matoaka Girls Camp. Karen, Monica, Aline and Pilar V. August 2nd, 2022. Demographics 325 girls per session. About 100 stay for 7 weeks. Ages 8 to 15. 20% from NE. 15% Florida. 15% DC area. 15% International. Sessions Two 3 ½ week sessions may extend to full 7 weeks. 2 weeks for new campers under age 10. Big sister - little sister program. Uniform Pink and purple. Uniforms for Mondays and outside of camp activities. They fill up during the fall.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Jason Silberman, owner director with wife Leslie, met us at the gate because it was locked. During our tour, Leslie stopped by briefly to greet us. 4 - 5 counselors in each bunk. Counselors on top bunks.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Bunks along the beautiful road with trees at each side. Porch for girls to leave shoes and equipment. 10 to 20 campers per bunk. Wood beds with 4 drawers under beds, shoe storage and shelves. Bathroom with toilets, showers, sinks and storage area. Speaker system in all cabins. Kids clean bunks and a cleaning crew comes in after. Sorority bunk for 15 year olds. Own towel service. Laundry at no extra charge.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Waterfront: Every activity has its own area. Lake has been treated so it is warm. Fishing. Skiing and wakeboarding. 150 kids per day. They must all use helmets. Aqua park. Sailing. Canoeing. Kayaking. Paddling. Swimming area. Swimming from one side of the lake to the other as a special activity. Girls need to train to participate. Pool with 3 long slides. Heated with no deep end pool for swimming instruction. Red Cross swimming. Younger campers must take at least 3 lessons a week.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Schedule All choice but have to do one activity of each type. 6 periods. 2 are free periods. 2 day Colorwar for full season campers. College League for session campers.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Activities Girls earn bracelets for activities they spend time at. Sports Rewards for working at a sport. Tennis. 9 courts with lights so they can play at night. They offer private tennis at extra cost. Golf with pitch and putt areas. Will be expanded for next summer. Archery. They have competitions. Spinning outside. Softball. Basketball. Beach Volleyball. Soccer. Gaga. Yoga. Lacrosse. Fitness studio for older campers. Gymnastics with a lot of options. Big fan. Will redo the gym and put in insulation. Inter camp competitions. Everyone makes the team. Adventure Rock climbing and ropes course. Rope swing. Ninja warrior. Mountain bike. Outdoor cooking. Riding English Style. 7 horses, about 50 girls. Extra cost. Arts & Crafts Traditional, including jewelry, sewing and ceramics. Stain glass. Large woodworking is very big. Each summer 15 year olds build a big chair that is put outside their bunk. Cooking Kitchen with 1 station. Artistic Theater with AC. Girls were practicing and sang for us. 5 staff directing them. One play each session. 40 to 50 girls per play. They use the theater during rainy days. Talent Show. Dance. Media arts, photo, video production. Next summer will add a Bowling alley with 4 lanes and AC for rainy days and birthday parties.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Gate with call button. Health 1 doctor. 2 nurses. 2 NPs. 2 hospitals 15 minutes away. Dining Hall They eat outside. Currently a tent and will build a wooden roof for next summer. Breakfast by bunk. Lunch and dinner are assigned so they can meet everyone. Buffet meals, salad and fruit bar. A special area in the kitchen for allergies, nut free, nut sensitive, gluten free, dairy free. They will make the allergy window bigger for next summer. Friday dinner in whites. Special dinner with special reflection after dinner, non denominational. Sunday gatherings are more festive.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Big sister - little sister program. Uniform Pink and purple. Uniforms for Mondays and outside of camp activities. Friday dinner in whites with special reflection (non denominational). Sunday gatherings are more festive. Awards given for participation or achievement. They have a purple rock outside of camp.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Transportation Pickup from Boston Logan, Portland Jetport, New Rochelle, New York, Fairfield and Hartford, Connecticut and outside of Boston. Escorted flights from LA, San Francisco, Palm Beach, Newark and Chicago. Private tennis at extra cost. Riding English Style extra cost. Laundry at no extra charge. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Matoaka Girls Camp serves girls ages 8 to 15 with 325 girls per session, about 100 stay for 7 weeks, and two 3 ½ week sessions that may extend to the full 7 weeks (plus 2 weeks for new campers under age 10). The schedule is all choice but campers have to do one activity of each type across 6 periods (with 2 free periods), and the program spans robust Sports, Adventure, Arts & Crafts, Theater, Dance, Media arts, and an extensive Waterfront. Health, safety, and food include 1 doctor, 2 nurses, 2 NPs with 2 hospitals 15 minutes away and buffet meals with a salad and fruit bar plus an allergy area (they eat outside under a tent now and will build a wooden roof for next summer). Traditions and culture include a big sister - little sister program, Friday dinner in whites with special reflection (non denominational), Sunday gatherings that are more festive, uniforms pink and purple, and they fill up during the fall.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - All choice but have to do one activity of each type.
  - Every activity has its own area.
  - Red Cross swimming. Younger campers must take at least 3 lessons a week.
  - Inter camp competitions. Everyone makes the team.
  - 9 courts with lights so they can play at night.
  - They fill up during the fall.
  - Big sister - little sister program.
  - Lake has been treated so it is warm.
  - Awards given for participation or achievement.
  - Speaker system in all cabins.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Dining Hall They eat outside. Currently a tent and will build a wooden roof for next summer.
  - They will make the allergy window bigger for next summer.
  - Will redo the gym and put in insulation.
  - They offer private tennis at extra cost.
  - Riding English Style. 7 horses, about 50 girls. Extra cost.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown
