title: Island Lake — Lindsey — 9-18-23
text:
# Island Lake

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Lindsey and Emily toured on July 7, 2023. The camp currently accommodates around 500 campers. There are vacant bunks available that could be renovated to accommodate additional campers. The camp boasts several amenities, including renovated dance studios with "Marley floors," a new canteen, and an ice cream shoppe, which are popular among the campers who visit six times a week. Additionally, there is an outdoor gym, a rock shop, and a skate park (some campers bring their own) and equipment is available for those who do not have.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

The biggest pulls right now are Manhattan and Westchester, although there are kids from all over the Northeast. 50% of kids stay full summer. There was an 80% return rate from last summer. Approximately 60% of the campers at the camp identify as Jewish. We definitely saw all types of kids. Activities are co-ed where appropriate; at art we noticed boys and girls sitting together. The gymnastics program is led by a head instructor and four additional instructors who can divide the campers based on their skill levels. Staff members always wear staff shirts.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Bunks are assigned based on the campers' interests and age, rather than by session. There are vacant bunks available that could be renovated to accommodate additional campers.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

There are lake and pool activities during every activity period.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

You cannot do the same major multiple periods in a day. Go-karting is offered as a minor activity that the entire bunk can participate in together.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

In rock shop campers are assigned to one of the 12 available bands, fostering a sense of musical community. The camp offers a wide range of activities to cater to diverse interests, including a popular circus program, gymnastics (led by a head instructor and four additional instructors who can divide the campers based on their skill levels), go-karting (offered as a minor activity that the entire bunk can participate in together), cooking, STEM, magic, woodworking (which is expanding), and a comprehensive arts program. There are 17 horses. There is a big theater program, with campers actively involved in all aspects, including set building, and they put on two shows per session. A well-stocked costume room is available for the campers' use. Renovated dance studios with "Marley floors," a new canteen, an ice cream shoppe, an outdoor gym, a rock shop, and a skate park (some campers bring their own) and equipment is available for those who do not have.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

There was a gated entrance with a guard, and the office is regularly staffed by a fully equipped police officer, although there doesn't seem to be a specific reason for their presence. Meal times at the camp are organized for campers to eat together as a bunk, with all campers eating at once. Staff members always wear staff shirts.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Activities are co-ed where appropriate. For example, at art we noticed boys and girls sitting together. Approximately 60% of the campers at the camp identify as Jewish. We definitely saw all types of kids. It's important to note that they don't have transgender campers and say they are not equipped to handle them, but it does have nonbinary campers who are welcomed and included.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

The biggest pulls right now are Manhattan and Westchester, although there are kids from all over the Northeast. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown. Extra costs: Unknown. Travel logistics: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Island Lake accommodates around 500 campers and offers a wide range of co-ed activities, including a popular circus program, gymnastics, go-karting, cooking, STEM, magic, woodworking, and a comprehensive arts program. Renovated dance studios with "Marley floors," an outdoor gym, a rock shop with 12 bands, a skate park, and a new canteen and ice cream shoppe that campers visit six times a week stand out. There are lake and pool activities during every activity period, 17 horses, and a big theater program that produces two shows per session with campers involved in set building and supported by a well-stocked costume room. Families come mainly from Manhattan and Westchester and across the Northeast, with an 80% return rate, about 50% staying full summer, and approximately 60% of campers identifying as Jewish.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Wide range of activities spanning circus, gymnastics, go-karts (as a minor), cooking, STEM, magic, woodworking (expanding), and comprehensive arts.
  - Renovated dance studios with "Marley floors."
  - Big theater program with two shows per session; campers build sets; well-stocked costume room.
  - Rock shop assigns campers to one of 12 bands, fostering musical community.
  - Lake and pool activities during every activity period.
  - 17 horses.
  - Outdoor gym and skate park; equipment is available for those who do not have; some campers bring their own.
  - New canteen and ice cream shoppe popular with campers (visited six times a week).
  - Activities are co-ed where appropriate; boys and girls seen sitting together at art.
  - 80% return rate; 50% stay full summer; campers from Manhattan, Westchester, and across the Northeast.

  

#### Lowlights

  - They don't have transgender campers and say they are not equipped to handle them.
  - Office regularly staffed by a fully equipped police officer, with no specific reason noted.
  - Vacant bunks available that could be renovated.
  - You cannot do the same major multiple periods in a day.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Island Lake Lindsey and Emily toured on July 7, 2023 There was a gated entrance with a guard, and the office is regularly staffed by a fully equipped police officer, although there doesn't seem to be a specific reason for their presence. The camp currently accommodates around 500 campers. There are vacant bunks available that could be renovated to accommodate additional campers. The camp boasts several amenities, including renovated dance studios with "Marley floors," a new canteen, and an ice cream shoppe, which are popular among the campers who visit six times a week. Additionally, there is an outdoor gym, a rock shop, and a skate park (some campers bring their own) and all equipment are available for those who do not have. In rock shop campers are assigned to one of the 12 available bands, fostering a sense of musical community. The camp offers a wide range of activities to cater to diverse interests, including a popular circus program, gymnastics (led by a head instructor and four additional instructors who can divide the campers based on their skill levels), go-karting (offered as a minor activity that the entire bunk can participate in together), cooking, STEM, magic, woodworking (which is expanding), and a comprehensive arts program. Activities are co-ed where appropriate. For example, at art we noticed boys and girls sitting together. There are 17 horses. There are lake and pool activities during every activity period. There is a bigtheater program, with campers actively involved in all aspects, including set building, and they put on two shows per session. title: Greenwood Trails — Pilar — 7-24-24
text:
# Greenwood Trails

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Greenwood Trails toured July 15, 2024 by Karen, Alyssa, Monica, Carolina, Tami and Pilar Vidal. Rustic based camp. Very hilly. Demographics Coed 160-170 kids per session, 180 max. 2, 3, 2 weeks sessions. Can combine. 50% stayed for second session, and some stay full session. Younger campers 8 to 12 years old and older campers 13 to 16 years old. Some kids going into 3rd. This is a small rustic camp for campers that want a relaxed camp experience.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Met by Director Andy Hicks, originally from England, lives in Long Island and has been at GT since 2018. He started at Tioga in 2001. Owner Owen Langbart. Ratio 1 counselor for every 3 campers. All counselors are specialist. 80% of counselors are lifeguard certified or rope certified. Head Counselors for boys and girls. CIT program for entering 10th and 11th grade. 2-year program. Jr. counselors on 3rd year. Campers come from Long Island, Bronx, Brooklyn, Manhattan, South CT and some from outside Boston. International campers, 2 Spain, 2 France.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Cabins Boys’ and girls’ bunks are across from each other. 16 beds in the smallest cabin for younger campers. 20 beds for older campers. Facilities in cabins. AM and PM chores. Rules made by cabin the first day, including consequences. Open to transgender kids, would place them with gender assigned at birth because the cabins have no special changing space. No gender-neutral cabins.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

New Pool – Swim training and free swim. Small water front – Canoeing, Fishing, Kayaking, Paddleboarding, small water park. Must wear lifejacket to go in the water.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Schedule First day is taster day. They choose activities at camp. 2 majors that go for 5 days. 2 minors that change daily. These can be traditional or non-traditional activities run by counselors such as nursery rhymes in Spanish. 8th grade and up is 100% elective. 7th grade and down have 1 cabin instructional swim period, 1 cabin period and 4 electives that change every day. Kids walk with a buddy from one activity to the next. Snack Shack every day at 3:30 pm. Mail, packages, snacks, drinks. One overnight campout on lower field. Sunday Music Festival with inflatables, BBQ and local band. No field trips. Except for trips staying for more than one session.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Activities Woodwork. Trapeze – Most popular program run by professionals. Drama. Ropes course at upper camp (we didn’t see it). Rock wall. Archery. Go Karts. Baseball batting cage. Basketball. Volleyball. Tennis. Pickleball – 2 courts on tennis courts. Sports available as fun activity. No intercamp games. Theater – Musical every session. Basic skate park only available for previous experience. Arts & Crafts. Pottery – Small. Kids allowed to make graffiti and leave their mark. Woodwork – can use machinery under supervision. 2 Nine square. Gaga. Outdoor cooking. Horseback riding – Must sign up the Thursday before the session starts. 30 minutes away at extra cost $70 per visit. 3 times a week can be up to 5.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Dining Hall Family style meals. Salad bar and pasta bar. Can accommodate picky eaters. Camp mom makes sure they eat properly. Nut free. Special diets cooked on special bench with separate utensils. Can do religious meals. Health Center NP 24/7, been at camp since before they were Greenwood Trails. Hospital 15-20 minutes away. Ambulance service available.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Rustic based camp. Very hilly. Camp spirit. Only experience with transgender kids has been with campers who have been with them before and they chose to stay with friends. This is a small rustic camp for campers that want a relaxed camp experience. No uniforms.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

JFK and Bradley pick up for an additional fee. Head Counselors communicate with parents the week before camp and 3 days into camp. Office phone forwarded to Andy’s cell. One way email, one phone call a week and letters. Pictures posted. Visiting day for campers staying for more than one session. Horseback riding is 30 minutes away at extra cost $70 per visit (must sign up the Thursday before the session starts; 3 times a week can be up to 5).

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Greenwood Trails is a small, rustic, very hilly coed camp with 160–170 kids per session (180 max) offering 2, 3, 2 weeks sessions that you can combine. Campers choose 2 majors that go for 5 days and 2 minors that change daily, with 8th grade and up 100% elective and a first-day taster day. Activities include a New Pool, a small water front (canoeing, fishing, kayaking, paddleboarding, small water park), and Trapeze – the most popular program run by professionals, plus ropes, woodwork, arts, sports, and a musical every session. Family style, nut free meals with special diets accommodated and an NP 24/7 support a relaxed camp experience.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Ratio 1 counselor for every 3 campers.
  - 80% of counselors are lifeguard certified or rope certified.
  - Trapeze – Most popular program run by professionals.
  - New Pool and a variety of waterfront options (with lifejackets).
  - Family style meals with nut free and special diets accommodated.
  - Health Center NP 24/7; hospital 15-20 minutes away; ambulance service available.
  - Strong choice model (majors/minors; 8th grade and up 100% elective).
  - Sunday Music Festival with inflatables, BBQ and local band.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Very hilly.
  - Small water front.
  - No gender-neutral cabins; would place transgender kids with gender assigned at birth because the cabins have no special changing space.
  - No intercamp games.
  - No field trips (except for trips staying for more than one session).
  - Basic skate park only available for previous experience.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Greenwood Trails Toured July 15, 2024 by Karen, Alyssa, Monica, Carolina, Tami and Pilar Vidal. We parked across the street and met by Director Andy Hicks, originally from England, lives in Long Island and has been at GT since 2018. He started at Tioga in 2001. Owner Owen Langbart. Rustic based camp. Very hilly. Demographics Coed 160-170 kids per session 180 max. 2, 3, 2 weeks sessions. Can combine. 50% stayed for second session, and some stay full session. Younger campers 8 to 12 years old and older campers 13 to 16 years old. Some kids going into 3 rd . Depends on the child. CIT program for entering 10 th and 11 th grade. 2-year program. Jr. counsellors on 3 rd year. Campers come from Long Island, Bronx, Brooklyn, Manhattan, South CT and some from outside Boston. International campers, 2 Spain, 2 France. Open to transgender kids, would place them with gender assigned at birth because the cabins have no special changing space. No gender-neutral cabins. Cabins Boys’ and girls’ bunks are across from each other. 16 beds in the smallest cabin for younger campers. 20 beds for older campers. Head Counsellors for boys and girls. Ratio 1 counsellor for every 3 campers. All counsellors are specialist. 80% of counsellors are lifeguard certified or rope certified. Am and Pm chores. title: Mataponi — Emily — 7-17-23
text:
# Mataponi

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

CAMP MATAPONI July 5, 2023 – Emily R, Michele G and Liz M. Currently 330 girls attend camp. Extremely high return rate – 94%. Mataponi allows girls to do 4 weeks first year only then full season. 95% of girls are Jewish. Programming is mostly structured with some free choice as the girls get older.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Director Dan Isdaner gave us the tour. Marcy was on a phone call so we just said hello. Marcy and Dan have been with the camp for 30 years. Dan is also a part owner of Crescent Cove along with the Feig’s. Nikki, Dan’s daughter works with the little kids. About 35% of staff are International, mostly from Australia.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Each bunk has a shower and sink and bathroom. 9-10 girls in a bunk with 3 counselors. Older girls’ bunks are up the path. Towel service provided for all campers. Reduces skin infections. Bunks cleaned and disinfected every day.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Beautiful lake with excellent program (waterski, paddle board, wind surf, wakeboard, sailing, tubing etc). Have an early morning waterski they offer at 7 AM that the girls love.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Programming is mostly structured with some free choice as the girls get older. Electives include Yoga, Zumba, archery, culinary, individual athletic training etc. Snack is at 3:15 PM.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Very strong tennis program, have 12 courts. Girls are fairly sporty, have basketball, soccer, lacrosse, field hockey, gymnastics. Don’t have to try out for intercamps. Also have great theater, performing arts, ropes course. Have 6-8 horses for onsite riding. Have an amazing new Art center that is sectioned off with pottery, sewing, stained glass, woodworking, jewelry, drawing etc. Socials are with Cedar, Skylemar, Wildwood, Bridgton. Also have brother/sister day. Sat night is theme dinner, give out gold feathers, 7 awards. Have a good number of special events and traditions, campfires. July 4 th , Carnival, Olympics etc. Weekly themed dinners. Also take trips out of camp.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Girls eat all together buffet style with salad bars. Serve fruit all day, healthy bowls, acai which girls love. Snack is at 3:15 PM. Girls sit with their bunks and counselors. No nuts (peanut free) and there is a separate kitchen to deal with all allergies, gluten, vegan etc. Towel service provided for all campers. Reduces skin infections. Bunks cleaned and disinfected every day.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Overall impressions: Music playing, girls looked busy and happy. Mataponi a more structured camp in terms of activities. Dan knew many of the girls names and the girls were friendly. Grounds of camp were clean and nicely maintained. Camp is uniform. Allowed non uniform at socials.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Tuition for 2024 will be $16,250. Additional charges include: transportation to and from camp (will be billed in relation to the camper’s origin and destination) and optional overnight trips for girls in grades 6-10. Pay extra for horseback riding and private tennis lessons. Buses come from Manhattan, Westchester, Philly and North Jersey. Very few LI kids. Girls do their phone calls over zooms. Found parents and kids loved that during covid. Also, many girls do academic or bat mitzvah tutoring sessions over zoom.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Currently 330 girls attend camp with an extremely high return rate – 94%. Programming is mostly structured with some free choice as the girls get older, and standouts include the beautiful lake with an excellent waterfront, a very strong tennis program (12 courts), an amazing new Art center, sports, theater and ropes. Girls eat all together buffet style with salad bars and are peanut free with a separate kitchen for allergies, and towel service and daily bunk cleaning help reduce skin infections. Buses come from Manhattan, Westchester, Philly and North Jersey, phone calls are over Zoom, and tuition for 2024 will be $16,250 with additional charges for transportation, optional trips, horseback riding and private tennis.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Beautiful lake with excellent program (waterski, paddle board, wind surf, wakeboard, sailing, tubing etc).
  - Very strong tennis program, have 12 courts.
  - Have an amazing new Art center that is sectioned off with pottery, sewing, stained glass, woodworking, jewelry, drawing etc.
  - Extremely high return rate – 94%.
  - Music playing, girls looked busy and happy.
  - Grounds of camp were clean and nicely maintained.
  - Towel service provided for all campers. Reduces skin infections.
  - Bunks cleaned and disinfected every day.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Camp is uniform. Allowed non uniform at socials.
  - Additional charges include: transportation to and from camp and optional overnight trips; pay extra for horseback riding and private tennis lessons.
  - Mataponi allows girls to do 4 weeks first year only then full season.
  - Very few LI kids.
  - Dan mentioned he has not received many referrals from Camp Experts this past year.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Fernwood
  - Pinecliffe

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

CAMP MATAPONI July 5, 2023 – Emily R, Michele G and Liz M Director Dan Isdaner gave us the tour. Marcy was on a phone call so we just said hello. Tuition for 2024 will be $16,250. Dan is also a part owner of Crescent Cove along with the Feig’s. Marcy and Dan have been with the camp for 30 years. Nikki, Dan’s daughter works with the little kids. Additional charges include: transportation to and from camp (will be billed in relation to the camper’s origin and destination) and optional overnight trips for girls in grades 6-10. Pay extra for horseback riding and private tennis lessons. Very strong tennis program, have 12 courts. Buses come from Manhattan, Westchester, Philly and North Jersey. Very few LI kids. Currently 330 girls attend camp. Extremely high return rate – 94%. Girls eat all together buffet style with salad bars. Serve fruit all day, healthy bowls, acai which girls love. Snack is at 3:15 PM. Girls sit with their bunks and counselors. No nuts (peanut free) and there is a separate kitchen to deal with all allergies, gluten, vegan etc. Mataponi allows girls to do 4 weeks first year only then full season. 95% of girls are Jewish. Girls do their phone calls over zooms. Found parents and kids loved that during covid. Also, many girls do academic or bat mitzvah tutoring sessions over zoom. About 35% of staff are International, mostly from Australia. Towel service provided for all campers. Reduces skin infections. Bunks cleaned and disinfected every day. Each bunk has a shower and sink and bathroom. 9-10 girls in a bunk with 3 counselors. Older girls’ bunks are up the path. Beautiful lake with excellent program (waterski, paddle board, wind surf, wakeboard, sailing, tubing etc) Have an early morning waterski they offer at 7 AM that the girls love. Have an amazing new Art center that is sectioned off with pottery, sewing, stained glass, woodworking, jewelry, drawing etc. Girls are fairly sporty, have basketball, soccer, lacrosse, field hockey, gymnastics. Don’t have to try out for intercamps. Also have great theater, performing arts, ropes course. Have 6-8 horses for onsite riding. Programming is mostly structured with some free choice as the girls get older. Electives include Yoga, Zumba, archery, culinary, individual athletic training etc. Socials are with Cedar, Skylemar, Wildwood, Bridgton. Also have brother/sister day. Camp is uniform. Allowed non uniform at socials. Sat night is theme dinner, give out gold feathers, 7 awards. Have a good number of special events and traditions, campfires. July 4 th , Carnival, Olympics etc. Weekly themed dinners. Also take trips out of camp. Overall impressions: Music playing, girls looked busy and happy. Mataponi a more structured camp in terms of activities. Dan knew many of the girls names and the girls were friendly. Grounds of camp were clean and nicely maintained. Dan mentioned he has not received many referrals from Camp Experts this past year. Would give Mataponi with Fernwood, Pinecliffe,

title: Farwell — Karen — 8-10-22
text:
# Farwell

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Tucked away in a private little area on a beautiful little lake, we finally found Farwell behind a series of homes. With 120 capacity, they only took 100 this year to make sure they had space to send those who potentially had Covid and they are happy to say they had no need to send anyone there. Two sessions of 4 week and 3 week, but can do shorter as well as stay 7. It is the oldest girls camp in the US – 1889.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

The majority of the kids are from New England and the East Coast with some from abroad. 65-75% return rate. Internationals can get linens. There is no religion. Very diverse community.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Each cabin has 6 girls, with 2 staff and they have added a half bath (one toilet and sink) to each cabin. Separate shower house. The camp is “very rustic” and a bit dirty.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Tucked away in a private little area on a beautiful little lake. Have all the water sports – both motorized and non-motorized. Swim is required for the lower level.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Period one is a choice period of 4-5 different programs, and then pick weekly choices. Riding, tennis and swim are grouped by skill, not age. Two sessions of 4 week and 3 week, but can do shorter as well as stay 7.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

It is the oldest girls camp in the US – 1889. Strong horse program from beginner to 9 levels with jumping, trail rides, and vaulting, and 4 rings. Campfire every Sunday.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

With 120 capacity, they only took 100 this year to make sure they had space to send those who potentially had Covid and they are happy to say they had no need to send anyone there. Food: Unknown.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

CAMP FARWELL - (with Karen Meister, Aline Rosenfeld, Monica Hirsch and Pilar Vidal) Marley was on a day off and Rachel Morgan gave us a tour. We found the kids to be happy but not really active. A competitive girl does not belong here. The camp is “very rustic” and a bit dirty, but Rachel was very sweet.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Internationals can get linens. 3-week program this year was $6332 and 4 weeks $8448. Not a bargain. Strong horse program from beginner to 9 levels with jumping, trail rides, and vaulting, and 4 rings. Extra cost. Travel logistics: Unknown. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

It is the oldest girls camp in the US – 1889, tucked away in a private little area on a beautiful little lake behind a series of homes. Period one is a choice period of 4-5 different programs, and then pick weekly choices, with riding, tennis and swim grouped by skill, not age, and swim required for the lower level. Strong horse program from beginner to 9 levels with jumping, trail rides, and vaulting, and 4 rings (extra cost), plus all the water sports – both motorized and non-motorized. We found the kids to be happy but not really active; a competitive girl does not belong here, and while the camp is “very rustic” and a bit dirty, Rachel was very sweet.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - It is the oldest girls camp in the US – 1889.
  - Tucked away in a private little area on a beautiful little lake.
  - Strong horse program from beginner to 9 levels with jumping, trail rides, and vaulting, and 4 rings.
  - Have all the water sports – both motorized and non-motorized.
  - Campfire every Sunday.
  - 65-75% return rate.
  - Very diverse community.
  - They are happy to say they had no need to send anyone there.

  

#### Lowlights

  - We found the kids to be happy but not really active.
  - The camp is “very rustic” and a bit dirty.
  - Not a bargain.
  - A competitive girl does not belong here.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

CAMP FARWELL - (with Karen Meister, Aline Rosenfeld, Monica Hirsch and Pilar Vidal) Marley was on a day off and Rachel Morgan gave us a tour. Tucked away in a private little area on a beautiful little lake, we finally found Farwell behind a series of homes. With 120 capacity, they only took 100 this year to make sure they had space to send those who potentially had Covid and they are happy to say they had no need to send anyone there. Two sessions of 4 week and 3 week, but can do shorter as well as stay 7. The majority of the kids are from New England and the East Coast with some from abroad. Period one is a choice period of 4-5 different programs, and then pick weekly choices. Swim is required for the lower level. 65-75% return rate. Internationals can get linens. There is no religion. Very diverse community. It is the oldest girls camp in the US – 1889. Strong horse program from beginner to 9 levels with jumping, trail rides, and vaulting, and 4 rings. Extra cost. Have all the water sports – both motorized and non-motorized. Each cabin has 6 girls, with 2 staff and they have added a half bath (one toilet and sink) to each cabin. Separate shower house. A competitive girl does not belong here. Campfire every Sunday. Riding, tennis and swim are grouped by skill, not age. We found the kids to be happy but not really active. The camp is “very rustic” and a bit dirty, but Rachel was very sweet. We felt, when we left, that if the prices were lower than elsewhere, it would make sense. But a 3-week program this year was $6332 and 4 weeks $8448. Not a bargain.

title: Exsportise — Catherine — 11-18-22
text:
# Exsportise

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Exsportise was founded in 1988 by 2 sportsmen as a high-quality sports camp. Added ESL later. Leading sports camp for 9 to 17 years old kids which offers great level sports, dance and music Academies. 65 nationalities (with a control on the % by nationalities). Some UK kids and around 2% US kids. 4 locations in private boarding schools with nice facilities with ESL possible in all centers. Ratio staff 1 to 4.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Ages 9 to 17. 65 nationalities (with a control on the % by nationalities). Some UK kids and around 2% US kids. Ratio staff 1 to 4. Academy ratios: Basketball ratio 1 to 10; Rugby ratio 1 to 10; Tennis only hard courts, ratio 1 to 6; Golf ratio 1 to 5 (extra fees). Director: Unknown.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Accommodation in separate areas for boys and girls. Rooms for 2 or 3. Dormitory up to 8 for the youngest kids. Curfew between 21h30 and 23h00 (depending on the age).

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Unknown

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

3 possibilities: English as a second language 15 hours a week + 1 Academy 15h a week; 2 Academies for a week 15h a day each; 1 Academy program for a week 30h. Can change every week. English as a second language test before arrival (Cambridge Assessment English online) or short speaking test on arrival. All level from A1 to C1 (minimum 1 year of English). Cambridge assessment optional at the end of the stay. Evening activities: Olympics, karaoke, race night, table tennis, …. TRUE ME part of the evening program to promote the development of students’ personal and social skills and self confidence. Duration: The minimum stay is 1 week with an arrival on Sunday and a departure on Saturday. For kids staying for 2 weeks minimum: trips recreational on Saturday and cultural on Sunday.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Academies: Basketball ratio 1 to 10; Dance (street dance, jazz, contemporary). Performance at the end of the week; Football (soccer in US) Arsenal Development Coaching; Golf with a 9 hole course on site and access to an 18 holes for players with a single number handicap, ratio 1 to 5 (extra fees); Hockey; Horsebackriding (Minimum 1 year experience), riding stables 20mn from school (extra fees); Music: instrumental or voice. Performance at the end of the week; Rugby (only for boys), ratio 1 to 10; Tennis only hard courts, ratio 1 to 6. 4 locations in private boarding schools: - Ardingly (new in 2023) close to Brighton. 9 to 14 years old. 60 kids by week. ESL and football for boys and girls (separately). - Oundle near Peterborough. 11 to 17 years old. 160 kids a week. Football, basket, tennis, horsebackriding, dance. - Seaford in Sussex. 10 to 17 years old. 170 kids a week. Football, tennis, golf, hockey, dance and music. - Worth in Sussex. 9 to 16 years old. 150 kids. Football, horsebackriding, tennis, hockey, dance and rugby. One Academy program available for Arsenal football, Golf, Hockey and Tennis available in the 4 locations. Two Academies program available at Oundle, Seaford, Worth. NEW FOR 2023: EXSPORTISE COACHING AWARD (ECA) for 15 to 17 years old students good level in football, hockey, tennis and want to become coach in the future.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Accommodation in separate areas for boys and girls. Curfew between 21h30 and 23h00 (depending on the age). Health and food: Unknown.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Leading sports camp with a truly international mix (65 nationalities) and ESL added later. TRUE ME is part of the evening program to promote the development of students’ personal and social skills and self confidence. Evening activities: Olympics, karaoke, race night, table tennis, ….

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Price 1525 £ by week with transfer included this year (Heathrow, Gatwick and St Pancras/ Stansted instead of Gatwick for Oundle). The minimum stay is 1 week with an arrival on Sunday and a departure on Saturday. For kids staying for 2 weeks minimum: trips recreational on Saturday and cultural on Sunday. Extras: Horsebackriding (200 £ by week), Golf (60 £ by week), Instrument renting (50 £ by week), Cambridge English test 60 £. Parent communication and tech: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Exsportise was founded in 1988 by 2 sportsmen as a high-quality sports camp, and added ESL later. A leading sports camp for 9 to 17 years old kids with great level sports, dance and music Academies across 4 private boarding school locations. 65 nationalities (with a control on the % by nationalities), with some UK kids and around 2% US kids. Flexible weekly programs: English as a second language 15 hours a week + 1 Academy 15h a week, 2 Academies, or a 1 Academy program 30h, with evening activities and curated trips for two-week stays.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Leading sports camp for 9 to 17 years old kids with great level sports, dance and music Academies.
  - 65 nationalities (with a control on the % by nationalities).
  - 4 locations in private boarding schools with nice facilities with ESL possible in all centers.
  - Ratio staff 1 to 4.
  - Arsenal Development Coaching; Dance with performance at the end of the week; Music (instrumental or voice) with performance at the end of the week.
  - NEW FOR 2023: EXSPORTISE COACHING AWARD (ECA) for 15 to 17 years old students who want to become coach in the future.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Rugby (only for boys).
  - Tennis only hard courts.
  - Minimum 1 year of English.
  - Horsebackriding (extra fees) and Golf (extra fees).
  - Instrument renting and Cambridge English test have extra costs.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

The camp is a leading sports camp for 9 to 17 years old kids which offers great level sports, dance and music Academies 65 nationalities (with a control on the % by nationalities) Some UK kids and around 2 % US kids 3 possibilities : - English as a 2 nd language 15hours a week + 1Academy 15h a week - 2 Academies for a week 15 h a day each - 1 Academy program for a week 30h Can change every week - English as a second language Test before arrival (Cambridge Assessment English online) or short speaking test on arrival All level from A1 to C1 (minimum 1 year of English) voice Cambridge assessment optional at the end of the stay - Academies : Basket ratio 1 to 10 Dance street dance, jazz , contemporary . title: Lake Owego — Lindsey — 10-2-23
text:
# Lake Owego

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Lake Owego July 5, 2023 -- Freedo Toured Lindsey, Joanne, Wendy and Dara. Lake Owego has 240 boys each session. 65% of campers opt for the full session. The camp offers a variety of activities that cater to the diverse interests of its campers.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Lake Owego takes pride in its dedicated staff, with about 15% of them being former campers. The camp attracts a diverse staff, with a significant number hailing from the Midwest. This brings fresh perspectives and experiences to enhance the camp's programs.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

To accommodate campers of varying session lengths, bunk assignments are organized by session, ensuring that every camper feels a sense of belonging and connection with their peers.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

They offer waterskiing at Lake Wallenpaupack, a half-day experience that allows campers to improve their skills. In recent years, water polo has gained popularity among campers.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Evening activities at Lake Owego offer a mix of sports and creative pursuits, including capture the flag and dodgeball.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Two shows, woodworking and arts and crafts, as well as rocketry, provide opportunities for campers to explore their creative and technical skills. Lake Owego places a strong emphasis on outdoor education and adventure. Every camper participates in an overnight trip. They also learn valuable skills such as smoking meats in a smoker, adding a unique and practical dimension to their camp experience. Freedo takes the boys on mountain biking adventures. In recent years, sports such as water polo, wrestling, and fencing have gained popularity among campers, offering a diverse range of physical activities to choose from. For older campers, the camp offers the opportunity to embark on big trips.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

In the dining hall, Lake Owego follows a single-sitting, buffet-style approach, ensuring that campers have ample time to enjoy their meals and connect with their fellow campers.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Freedo reads to the younger campers every night. This expansion highlights the camp's adaptability and commitment to meeting the evolving interests of its campers.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Transportation is included, and there is no canteen charge. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown. Travel logistics: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Lake Owego has 240 boys each session, with 65% opting for the full session. Bunks are organized by session to help every camper feel a sense of belonging with their peers. Programming spans creative, technical, and adventure offerings—two shows, woodworking, arts and crafts, rocketry, waterskiing at Lake Wallenpaupack, mountain biking, and every camper participates in an overnight—plus growing sports like water polo, wrestling, and fencing and evening activities such as capture the flag and dodgeball. The camp runs a single-sitting, buffet-style dining hall, transportation is included with no canteen charge, and older campers can embark on big trips.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Bunk assignments are organized by session to ensure belonging.
  - Half-day waterskiing at Lake Wallenpaupack.
  - Every camper participates in an overnight trip.
  - Campers learn smoking meats in a smoker.
  - Transportation is included; there is no canteen charge.
  - Freedo-led mountain biking adventures and nightly reading to younger campers.
  - Single-sitting, buffet-style dining hall.
  - Growing interest in water polo, wrestling, and fencing.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Unknown

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

title: Kawaga — Renee — 8-3-22
text:
# Kawaga

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

KAWAGA – Minocqua, WI. 108th summer. All Boys Sports Camp. Beautiful grounds/lake. 175/Session; 195 would be full. 60% stay full summer. 60% from Chicago and suburbs; Atlanta, Detroit, Denver, SF, FL and Ohio. Must love sports to be successful – don’t have to be the best athlete\! Very spirited\!

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

TOURED BY TY SIMPSON – NEW DIRECTOR FOR PAST 3 SUMMERS. 60% from Chicago and suburbs; Atlanta, Detroit, Denver, SF, FL and Ohio. Big brothers first year.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Smaller cabins with 5-6 campers/cabin. Bathrooms and showers outside of cabin. Youngest campers have bathrooms in cabin.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Beautiful grounds/lake. Water skiing huge\!

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Boys choose 2 activity periods (called clubs) in the AM and then play in leagues all afternoon.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

All Boys Sports Camp. Must love sports to be successful – don’t have to be the best athlete\! Very spirited\!

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

New Mess Hall for summer 2022. New chef with rave reviews. No frozen food, always a salad bar with soup and a protein.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Very spirited\! Must love sports to be successful – don’t have to be the best athlete\!

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Unknown

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Kawaga in Minocqua, WI is an All Boys Sports Camp with beautiful grounds/lake. Boys choose 2 activity periods (called clubs) in the AM and then play in leagues all afternoon. Smaller cabins with 5-6 campers/cabin, with bathrooms and showers outside of cabin (youngest campers have bathrooms in cabin). New chef with rave reviews; no frozen food, always a salad bar with soup and a protein, and water skiing is huge.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Beautiful grounds/lake
  - Water skiing huge\!
  - New Mess Hall for summer 2022
  - New chef with rave reviews
  - No frozen food, always a salad bar with soup and a protein
  - Very spirited\!
  - 175/Session. 195 would be full
  - 60% stay full summer

  

#### Lowlights

  - Bathrooms and showers outside of cabin
  - Must love sports to be successful – don’t have to be the best athlete\!

  

#### Similar Camps

  - OJIBWA – Eagle River, WI
  - New owners for summer 2022 – Ross and Lindsay Friedman; Lindsay acts as a camp mom.
  - 125 campers 1st session; 230 – 2nd session. Approx 75 stay full summer.
  - Ojibwa is a competitive sports camp where campers play in competitive leagues for most of the day\!
  - Very spirited and cult like – Ojibwa campers LOVE Ojibwa\!
  - 6 periods/day; 3 league play – bball, volleyball, floor hockey, softball, football, newcomb, soccer with age groups.
  - 1 water activity/day.
  - 1-Rec Period (free).
  - 1-Evening Program.
  - Strong Chicago area following but working hard to diversify – also families for St. Louis, Tampa, Denver, Detroit, FL, NC, TX.
  - Hockey players can skate on Sundays. Golfers can go out of camp to golf.
  - First 4 – Olympics; 2nd 4 – Collegiate Week.
  - Beautiful dining hall – campers eat by cabin/buffet style.
  - Friday night small service.
  - Can handle all allergies as well as Type 1 diabetes.

title: Greylock — Tami — 7-24-24
text:
# Greylock

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Greylock is an all boys camp that is owned by the husband of Belvoir Terrace. It is a 7 week program with exceptions made for the first summer of younger kids. With their request, they will do a 4 week session, but that is a one time thing. For international kids, they will be more flexible with the session dates. They have 300 boys and 150 staff and 25 kids are international. They have a 2:1 ratio for the younger boys, ages ten and eleven have a 3:1 ratio. Campers are placed by grade level. 7th graders who want to be a first-time camper can be successful.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Camp Experts: Tami Feldman, Alyssa Greenberger, Karen Meister, Monica Hirsch, Pilar Vidal, Carolina Lautenberg. Date: July 17, 2024. Tour: Ed, head counselor and has been at Greylock for 43 years (camper, counselor, director). Counselors have to be at least 18 years old and have a year of college completed. A GAP year does not count as a year of college. Each camper will talk to Michael and Jim before camp to see how they can be supported. They have department heads and counselors in each athletic area and the junior and seniors have different department heads so they can be separate when doing activities. Activities are run by pros.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

2017 the bunks were redone and over the years they have been touching them up. The younger kids have a female counselor but they do not sleep in their cabin. They have 3 shower houses and everyone showers at the same time. They have a lot of showers in the shower house so all the campers go at once.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

There is no pool and they "will never get a pool". Camp is on a beautiful lake. They use that as a pool with lanes marked, they have all water sports. Water polo is a sport most have never played and it is popular at camp. They have swim lessons and fun swim. Fun swim is daily.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

The campers hang out with their age group but don’t do activities with their bunks. They have round robin tournaments. They do one week rotations.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

There is roller hockey, 3 basketball courts for the senior side and tennis courts. Activities: ax throwing, zip line, theater - every age group does a show and the oldest do two shows, flag football regardless of age, 2200sf field house that is indoors with basketball that is used for rainy day activities and dodgeball, golf and learn to use the clubs and then they go across the street and have a field where they can let the ball go. They go from wood to an iron putter. They have golf as three stages and end with going to a real golf course. They have a weight room for staff but the 14 and 15 year olds can use it. They have a mountain bike program with hiking trails, overnights and structure for camping and then at 10 and 11 they go off campus for hiking and a sleepover. They have a nature museum with a bearded dragon and snakes. They focus on a lot of skill development. Reward enthusiasm and interest and not on how well one can do.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Doctor is always there. They do one week rotations. They also have a medical administrator for paperwork, RNs, LPNs, student nurses, athletic trainer and a student athletic trainer. Dining hall - two seatings with the younger eating first. They have two hot options with pasta with sauce, soup, desserts, a double salad bar. They have the same tables and same counselors for every meal. They are nut free and can take all allergies and diabetics.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Optional Friday night services and they have an express service that is twenty five minutes and ends with challah. They focus on a lot of skill development. Reward enthusiasm and interest and not on how well one can do. The campers hang out with their age group but don’t do activities with their bunks.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

They have 4 bus sites for pickup - NYC, NJ, Long Island and West Chester College. Most of the campers come from those areas. International families will fly to NYC and get the kids on a bus. Some will get dropped off by car which most of the families are renting cars. The Florida flight is chaperoned, and they will do an airport pickup. There are one way emails through Bunk 1 and a phone call policy. The camp does call home the first week with updates. No electronics. Extra costs: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Greylock is an all boys camp on a beautiful lake with a 7 week program, with a one-time 4 week session for the first summer of younger kids and more flexibility for international kids. They have 300 boys and 150 staff, a 2:1 ratio for the younger boys and 3:1 for ages ten and eleven, and activities are run by pros with department heads so juniors and seniors can be separate when doing activities. There is no pool (they "will never get a pool"); the lake is used as a pool with lanes marked, all water sports, swim lessons and daily fun swim, and water polo is popular. Families have 4 bus sites, a chaperoned Florida flight and airport pickup, Bunk 1 one-way emails and a phone call policy with the camp calling home the first week; the kind of child that wouldn’t be successful at Greylock is a boy who doesn’t like hanging out with friends and trying new things.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Camp is on a beautiful lake.
  - Activities are run by pros.
  - They focus on a lot of skill development.
  - Fun swim is daily.
  - Doctor is always there.
  - 2017 the bunks were redone and over the years they have been touching them up.

  

#### Lowlights

  - There is no pool and they "will never get a pool".
  - No electronics.
  - They have 3 shower houses and everyone showers at the same time.
  - It is a 7 week program with a one-time 4 week exception for the first summer of younger kids.
  - The campers hang out with their age group but don’t do activities with their bunks.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Camp Greylock Date: July 17, 2024 Camp Experts: Tami Feldman, Alyssa Greenberger, Karen Meister, Monica Hirsch, Pilar Vidal, Carolina Lautenberg Tour: Ed, head counselor and has been at Greylock for 43 years (camper, counselor, director) Greylock is an all boys camp that is owned by the husband of Belvoir Terrace. It is a 7 week program with exceptions made for the first summer of younger kids. With their request, they will do a 4 week session, but that is a one time thing. For international kids, they will be more flexible with the session dates. They have 300 boys and 150 staff and 25 kids are international. They have a 2:1 ratio for the younger boys, ages ten and eleven have a 3:1 ratio. Counselors have to be at least 18 years old and have a year of college completed. A GAP year does not count as a year of college. Campers are placed by grade level. Each camper will talk to Michael and Jim before camp to see how they can be supported. They have 4 bus sites for pickup- NYC, NJ, Long Island and West Chester College. Most of the campers come from those areas. International families will fly to NYC and get the kids on a bus. Some will get dropped off by car which most of the families are renting cars. The Florida flight is chaperoned, and they will do an airport pickup. 7 th graders who want to be a first-time camper can be successful. There are one way emails through Bunk 1 and a phone call policy. The camp does call home the first week with updates. There is roller hockey, 3 basketball courts for the senior side and tennis courts. They have department heads and counselors in each athletic area and the junior and seniors have different department heads so they can be separate when doing activities. Activities are run by pros. There is no pool and they “will never get a pool”. Camp is on a beautiful lake. They use that as a pool with lanes marked, they have all water sports. Water polo is a sport most have never played and it is popular at camp. They have swim lessons and fun swim. Fun swim is daily. They have a nature museum with a bearded dragon and snakes. The campers hang out with their age group but don’t do activities with their bunks. They have round robin tournaments. The younger kids have a female counselor but they do not sleep in their cabin. They have a lot of showers in the shower house so all the campers go at once. 2017 the bunks were redone and over the years they have been touching them up. No electronics. Activities: ax throwing, zip line, theater- every age group does a show and the oldest do two shows, flag football regardless of age, 2200sf field house that is indoors with basketball that is used for rainy day activities and dodgeball, golf and learn to use the clubs and then they go across the street and have a field where they can let the ball go. They go from wood to an iron putter. They have golf as three stages and end with going to a real golf course. They have a weightroom for staff but the 14 and 15 year olds can use it. They have two hot options with pasta with sauce, soup, desserts, a double salad bar. They have the same tables and same counselors for every meal. They are nut free and can take all allergies and diabetics. The kind of child that wouldn’t be successful at Greylock is a boy who doesn’t like hanging out with friends and trying new things.

title: Berkshire Hills — Dara — 8-23-23
text:
# Berkshire Hills

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Bunks, rec hall, dining hall, gym – all updated post covid. Adding security gate to the entrance for 2024. Building million dollar covered amphitheater for 2024. 1st session 315 kids, 2nd session 290 kids plus Culinary 20 kids 1st session, 36 kids 2nd session. Rising 11th graders LIT – have 29 this summer. Have lots of open indoor spaces for programming options. All have AC.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Joanne, Wendy & Dara. Toured by: Adam Weinstein – Director; Zach Weisbrot – Program Director.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Will bunk children by how they identify. Oldest kids have showers in the bunk, rest have shower house. Each shower stall has a private changing area. Each bunk has 4 private toilets & 2 sinks. Bathrooms were nice and clean. Culinary Program housing: bunked in motel style rooms with 2–3 kids each. AC & private bathroom.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Lake – no motorized sports. Very big wibbit.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

AM activities by bunk: through 7th grade: 2 activities plus instructional swim; 8th graders have 2 activities and free swim; 9th & 10th grade sleep in and have 2 activities. PM activities – all choose 2 electives plus snack time. Big rec center with canteen 1x/week younger kids, 2x for older. Culinary program kids join with their age groups in the afternoon for electives.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Trips: 7th & 8th Grade – Lake George; 9th & 10th – 5 days to Toronto & Niagra Falls or Boston & Cape Cod (rotate every other year); 11th Grade (LIT) - Philly. Intercamps with Pontiac, Scatico, Crane Lake, Kinde Ring. Very large ceramics studio. Large arts & crafts program – they do it all. They bring in people from Berkshire Art to guest teach. Nice basketball facility – bleacher seats with digital score board. Archery, lots of field space, outdoor cooking, animals, Pickelball lines on tennis courts. Has mountain biking. Big treehouse by the lake, everyone does an overnight. Horseback riding – offsite and extra $$. Culinary Program – 4th year of this program; rising 5th -11th graders. Cooking and learning kitchen skills. Have 2 chef’s running the program and bring in cooking instructors as Guest Chef’s.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Dining hall all buffet. Blessings on walls throughout. Kosher but no Mashgiach. Have separate allergy chef. 3 nurses on staff plus 1 rotating doctor. Adding security gate to the entrance for 2024.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Traditional camp is 90% Jewish. Culinary camp is 70%. Friday night Oneg – values based. No dress code. Wine, challah & blessings. Saturday - sleep in, have sweet breakfast. Do community service based projects during the day and close day with Havdalah service.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Horseback riding – offsite and extra $$. Unknown travel logistics, parent communication, and tech policy; unknown other extra costs.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Bunks, rec hall, dining hall, and gym are all updated post covid, and a million dollar covered amphitheater is being built for 2024 that will overlook the lake. Traditional camp is 90% Jewish (Culinary is 70%) with a values based Friday night Oneg (no dress code; wine, challah & blessings) and a relaxed Saturday with community service and Havdalah. Days run with bunk-based AM activities and choice PM electives, a very big wibbit on the lake (no motorized sports), plus inter-camps and grade-based trips. Dining is buffet and kosher (no Mashgiach) with a separate allergy chef; health coverage includes 3 nurses and a rotating doctor, and the Culinary Program (rising 5th–11th) features motel-style housing with AC/private bathrooms, 2 chefs, guest Chef instructors, and afternoon electives with age groups.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Bunks, rec hall, dining hall, gym – all updated post covid.
  - Building million dollar covered amphitheater for 2024. Will overlook the lake.
  - Have lots of open indoor spaces for programming options. All have AC.
  - Intercamps with Pontiac, Scatico, Crane Lake, Kinde Ring.
  - Very large ceramics studio. Large arts & crafts program – they do it all. They bring in people from Berkshire Art to guest teach.
  - Nice basketball facility – bleacher seats with digital score board.
  - Big treehouse by the lake, everyone does an overnight.
  - Very big wibbit.
  - Separate allergy chef.
  - 3 nurses on staff plus 1 rotating doctor.
  - Culinary Program – 4th year of this program; rising 5th -11th graders; have 2 chef’s running the program and bring in cooking instructors as Guest Chef’s.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Lake – no motorized sports.
  - Horseback riding – offsite and extra $$.
  - Kosher but no Mashgiach.
  - Oldest kids have showers in the bunk, rest have shower house.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Kinder Ring
  - Schodack
  - Surprise Lake
  - Nah-Jee-Wah

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Berkshire Hills Joanne, Wendy & Dara Toured by: Adam Weinstein – Director Zach Weisbrot – Program Director - Bunks, rec hall, dining hall, gym – all updated post covid. Adding security gate to the entrance for 2024. - 1 st session 315 kids, 2 nd session 290 kids plus Culinary 20 kids 1 st session, 36 kids 2 nd session - Rising 11 th graders LIT – have 29 this summer - Dining hall all buffet. Blessings on walls throughout. - Kosher but no Mashgiach - Traditional camp is 90% Jewish. Culinary camp is 70% - Friday night Oneg – values based. No dress code. Wine, challah & blessings. - Saturday - sleep in, have sweet breakfast. Do community service based projects during the day and close day with Havdalah service. - Will bunk children by how they identify. - Oldest kids have showers in the bunk, rest have shower house. Each shower stall has a private changing area. - Each bunk has 4 private toilets & 2 sinks. Bathrooms were nice and clean. - AM activities by bunk o Through 7 th grade: 2 activities plus instructional swim. o 8 th graders have 2 activities and free swim. o 9 th & 10 th grade sleep in and have 2 activities. - PM activities – all choose 2 electives plus snack time - Trips o 7 th & 8 th Grade – Lake George o 9 th & 10 th – 5 days to Toronto & Niagra Falls or Boston & Cape Cod (rotate every other year) o 11 th Grade (LIT) - Philly - Have lots of open indoor spaces for programming options. All have AC. - Intercamps with Pontiac, Scatico, Crane Lake, Kinde Ring - Lake – no motorized sports. Very big wibbit. - Horseback riding – offsite and extra $$ - Very large ceramics studio. Large arts & crafts program – they do it all. AC & private bathroom. - Culinary program kids join with their age groups in the afternoon for electives. - Cooking and learning kitchen skills - Have 2 chef’s running the program and bring in cooking instructors as Guest Chef’s Families touring here can also look at Kinder Ring, Schodack, Surprise Lake, Nah-Jee-Wah.

title: Cheley — Jaime — 9-1-22
text:
# Cheley

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Cheley just celebrated their 102nd summer. Starting in 1921 with 8 boys and 2 Native American counselors, now has campers from 45 states and a handful of international campers. It is a true outdoor adventure camp. Cheley is a true Brother/Sister camp. The camp is broken up into 8 divisions (4 on each side). Each Unit has its own “village” with its own bunks, bathhouse, lodge, and basketball court. Each camp has its own infirmary, dining hall, archery range, riflery range, store (canteen), and schedule. Being it is a brother/sister camp, there might only be 1-2 times a session where the campers are all together. Plus, Sunday Chapel. There is no color war or big/little program. Located in Estes Park, CO near Longs Peak (elevation 14,000), outdoors is essential for Cheley campers. In the off season, camp is used for school programs, weddings, and conferences.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Cheley Camps of Colorado August 31, 2022 Toured with Peggy Haynes. Peggy is the Enrollment Director. Peggy is a former camper, counselor, and former camp parent. She has been with the camp for a second time and just finished her 10th summer. She lives just outside camp, in Lyons CO. Jeff Cheley was in Denver for the day. Kim Betts has returned to Cheley from Friendship. She is the HR director and director of School Programs. Counselors come from all over the US. Counselors double as program assistants. Each counselor is thoroughly vetted and undergoes a background check. Counselors spend an intense 10-day training period prior to the start of the summer, everything from safety and hygiene to specialty training, risk management and handling homesickness. Units: Each unit has age groups and bunks with 12-14 campers, and 2-3 counselors. There are about 65 campers in a unit. Each unit has an individual unit director. Unit director report to girls director and boys director. Girls and Boys Director report to Jeff and Brooke Cheley. Peggy seemed to know each camper and their families.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

BUNKS: All bunk beds with shared dressers. Rods for hanging clothes and shoe cubbies. Camp provides sheets, pillowcases, pillow and 1 wool blanket. Campers are encouraged to bring additional blanket. No Heat and nights are chilly. No A/C in bunks. Not needed at this elevation. (It was 79 when I visited, and a rain shower dropped the temp to 70—in about 10 minutes) The oldest campers have the option of traditional cabins, tiny cabins (4–5 campers +1 counselor) or covered wagons. Covered Wagons are in a different part of camp and those staying are focused on equestrian. Each unit has a spacious bathhouse. There are generous toilets, sinks and shower stalls with curtains. Cubbies are there for campers to keep their toiletries. Campers are responsible for cleaning both their cabins and the bathhouse. Each unit has its own lodge, a large multi-purpose room where they can play games, play piano, hang out, etc. LAUNDRY: Laundry is done on property 1 time a week and they have a dedicated laundress for this.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

There is no lake or waterfront activities. They have a fishing pond that they stock with trout on a catch and release program.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

This is a total choice program starting at 9 years old. Campers will make their 1st, 2nd, and 3rd choices at the beginning of the week.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

OUTDOORS: Located in Estes Park, CO near Longs Peak (elevation 14,000), outdoors is essential for Cheley campers. Campers have the opportunity to go outside and hike and work their way up to hiking Longs Peak. (Hiking Longs Peak can happen towards the end of the session, weather and conditions permitting). Hiking Longs Peak and any “14’er” is a HUGE accomplishment. Campers can participate in overnights. Mountain Biking is popular and tons of well-maintained bikes with all the necessary gear. HORSES: Horseback riding is big at camp. They have 150 horses, and each unit has its own set of horses. The barn is impressive. Horses stay at the bottom of camp during the fall and winter. The equestrian department is its own full department. They have 2 large rings and several trails. They teach western riding, UNLESS there is a staff member who can teach English. Campers can choose from ½ day, full day, or overnight rides. And all rides are age appropriate. ARTS: Both the boys camp and the girls camps have individual art facilities. The art studio on the girls side is small-ish, but has a separate ceramics studio. The studio felt small and since there was no campers, it was difficult to see how full it gets. The boys’ side has a dedicated woodwork shop, with a dedicated woodwork staff. It is quite large, and Peggy said most boys participate in this choice activity. SPORTS: Each unit has their own basketball court, near the cabins. There is an all sports field near the chapel. Camp has gaga pits all over and soccer fields on both the girls and boys’ side. Boys and Girls each have their own archery and riflery range. There is an indoor climbing wall (or 3 walls) and a large Rec center, which has indoor basketball, 4 square, Zumba, square dancing, floor hockey, dance, etc. While counselors double as programming and are trained for multiple roles, Cheley hires a dedicated indoor rock-climbing specialist and a tech rock climbing specialist (for outdoor climbs).

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

INFIRMARY: Each division has its own infirmary with RNs on staff. Parent volunteer MD’s come up throughout summer for a week. Hospital is 10 minutes away. TRAINING: Counselors spend an intense 10-day training period prior to the start of the summer, everything from safety and hygiene to specialty training, risk management and handling homesickness. DINING HALL: The Staff at Cheley is good about dietary restrictions. They have both a food manager and chef that will speak with families regarding special diets. Can accommodate most diets. The meals are served family style and you are not required to sit with your bunk, but you are required to sit within your unit. Breakfast is 2 courses. Hot and Cold Cereal and a heartier 2nd course. Lunch is typical lunch food and dinner is a fuller meal. Dessert is only served at dinner.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

NO RELIGION though on Sunday mornings, the entire camp comes together for chapel. Chapel is a time for inspiration and reflection. Many campers share what they have learned, read poetry and what inspires them. Being it is a brother/sister camp, there might only be 1–2 times a session where the campers are all together. Plus, Sunday Chapel. There is no color war or big/little program. I felt more disappointed by the 2nd more than the 1st. It is meticulously maintained.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

TRAVEL LOGISTICS: Unknown. PARENT COMMUNICATION: Unknown. TECH: Unknown. CANTEEN/EXTRA COSTS: Each division has a store. Parents set a budget prior to camp and camp bills at the end of the session. In the morning, campers needing necessities (stamps, soap, etc.) can get it. Afternoons are for sweet/salty treats (9–12: 1 treat per day; 12–17: 2 treats). Afternoon treats are given around 4pm to hold over until dinner.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Cheley is a true Brother/Sister camp and a true outdoor adventure camp located in Estes Park, CO near Longs Peak. Cheley just celebrated their 102nd summer; starting in 1921 with 8 boys and 2 Native American counselors, now has campers from 45 states and a handful of international campers. This is a total choice program starting at 9 years old with hiking (including a progression to Longs Peak), mountain biking, horses (150 horses), arts, sports, and climbing. I would recommend this for a kid who could do an outward bound but isn’t quite old enough, or for a true outdoor adventure kid.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - It is a true outdoor adventure camp.
  - Cheley just celebrated their 102nd summer.
  - Counselors come from all over the US.
  - Each counselor is thoroughly vetted and undergoes a background check.
  - Counselors spend an intense 10-day training period prior to the start of the summer.
  - Each camp has its own infirmary, dining hall, archery range, riflery range, store (canteen), and schedule.
  - The camp is broken up into 8 divisions (4 on each side). Each Unit has its own “village”.
  - Hospital is 10 minutes away.
  - The Staff at Cheley is good about dietary restrictions. Can accommodate most diets.
  - They have 150 horses, and each unit has its own set of horses.
  - Mountain Biking is popular and tons of well-maintained bikes with all the necessary gear.
  - There is an indoor climbing wall (or 3 walls) and a large Rec center.
  - It is meticulously maintained.

  

#### Lowlights

  - There is no lake or waterfront activities.
  - There is no color war or big/little program.
  - Being it is a brother/sister camp, there might only be 1-2 times a session where the campers are all together.
  - The art studio on the girls side is small-ish.
  - No Heat and nights are chilly. No A/C in bunks.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Cheley Camps of Colorado August 31, 2022 Toured with Peggy Haynes. Peggy is the Enrollment Director. Peggy is a former camper, counselor, and former camp parent. She has been with the camp for a second time and just finished her 10 th summer. She lives just outside camp, in Lyons CO. Jeff Cheley was in Denver for the day. Kim Betts has returned to Cheley from Friendship. She is the HR director and director of School Programs. Cheley just celebrated their 102 nd summer. Starting in 1921 with 8 boys and 2 Native American counselors, now has campers from 45 states and a handful of international campers. It is a true outdoor adventure camp. Counselors come from all over the US. Counselors doubles as program assistants. Each counselor is thoroughly vetted and undergoes a background check. Counselors spend an intense 10-day training period prior to the start of the summer, everything from safety and hygiene to specialty training, risk management and handling homesickness. Cheley is a true Brother/Sister camp. Each camp has its own infirmary, dining hall, archery range, riflery range, store (canteen), and schedule. The camp is broken up into 8 divisions (4 on each side). Each Unit has its own “village” with its own bunks, bathhouse, lodge, and basketball court. Being it is a brother/sister camp, there might only be 1-2 times a session where the campers are all together. Plus, Sunday Chapel. There is no color war or big/little program. I felt more disappointed by the 2 nd more than the 1 st . Units: Each units have age groups and bunks with 12-14 campers, and 2-3 counselors. There are about 65 campers in a unit. Each unit has an individual unit director. Unit director report to girls director and boys director. Girls and Boys Director report to Jeff and Brooke Cheley. BUNKS: All bunk beds with shared dressers. Rods for hanging clothes and shoe cubbies. Camp provides sheets, pillowcases, pillow and 1 wool blanket. Campers are encouraged to bring additional blanket. No Heat and nights are chilly. No A/C in bunks. Not needed at this elevation. (It was 79 when I visited, and a rain shower dropped the temp to 70—in about 10 minutes) The oldest campers have the option of traditional cabins, tiny cabins (4- 5 campers +1 counselor) or covered wagons. Covered Wagons are in a different part of camp and those staying are focused on equestrian. Each unit has a spacious bathhouse. There are generous toilets, sinks and shower stalls with curtains. Cubbies are there for campers to keep their toiletries. Campers are responsible for cleaning both their cabins and the bathhouse. Each unit has its own lodge, a large multi-purpose room where they can play games, play piano, hang out, etc. LAUNDRY: Laundry is done on property 1 time a week and they have a dedicated laundress for this. INFIRMARY: Each division has its own infirmary with RNs on staff. Parent volunteer MD’s come up throughout summer for a week. Hospital is 10 minutes away. DINING HALL: The Staff at Cheley is good about dietary restrictions. They have a both a food manager and chef that will speak with families regarding special diets. Can accommodate most diets. The meals are served family style and you are not required to see with your bunk, but you are required to sit within your unit. Breakfast is 2 courses. Hot and Cold Cereal and a heartier 2 nd course. Lunch is typical lunch food and dinner is a fuller meal. Dessert is only served at dinner. CANTEEN: Each division has a store. Parents set a budget prior to camp and camp bills at the end of the session. In the morning, campers needing necessities (stamps, soap, etc.) can get it. Afternoons are for sweet/salty treats (9-12- 1 treat per day; 12-17 2 treats.) Afternoon treats are given around 4pm to hold over until dinner. NO RELIGION though on Sunday mornings, the entire camp comes together for chapel. Chapel is a time for inspiration and reflection. Many campers share what they have learned, read poetry and what inspires them. PROGRAMMING: This is a total choice program starting at 9 years old. Campers will make their 1 st , 2 nd, and 3 rd choices at the beginning of the week. OUTDOORS: Located in Estes Park, CO near Longs Peak (elevation 14,000), outdoors is essential for Cheley campers. Campers have the opportunity to go outside and hike and work their way up to hiking Longs Peak. (Hiking Longs Peak can happen towards the end of the session, weather and conditions permitting). Hiking Longs Peak and any “14’er” is a HUGE accomplishment. Campers can participate in overnights. Mountain Biking is popular and tons of well-maintained bikes with all the necessary gear. HORSES: Horseback riding is big at camp. They have 150 horses, and each unit has its own set of horses. The barn is impressive. Horses stay at the bottom of camp during the fall and winter. The equestrian department is its own full department. They have 2 large rings and several trails. They teach western riding, UNLESS there is a staff member who can teach English. Campers can choose from ½ day, full day, or overnight rides. And all rides are age appropriate. ARTS: Both the boys camp and the girls camps have individual art facilities. The art studio on the girls side is small-ish, but has a separate ceramics studio. The studio felt small and since there was no campers, it was difficult to see how full it gets. The boys’ side has a dedicated woodwork shop, with a dedicated woodwork staff. It is quite large, and Peggy said most boys participate in this choice activity. SPORTS: Each unit has their own basketball court, near the cabins. There is an all sports field near the chapel. Camp has gaga pits all over and soccer fields on both the girls and boys’ side. Boys and Girls each have their own archery and riflery range. There is an indoor climbing wall (or 3 walls) and a large Rec center, which has indoor basketball, 4 square, Zumba, square dancing, floor hockey, dance, etc. While counselors double as programming and are trained for multiple roles, Cheley hires a dedicated indoor rock-climbing specialist and a tech rock climbing specialist (for outdoor climbs) WATERFRONT: There is no lake or waterfront activities. They have a fishing pond that they stock with trout on a catch and release program. In the off season, camp is used for school programs, weddings, and conferences. Peggy seemed to know each camper and their families. It is meticulously maintained. I would recommend this for a kid who could do an outward bound but isn’t quite old enough. Or for a true outdoor adventure kid.

  
  

### Author: Jaime

### Updated: 2025-08-26
