title: Brilliantmont — Karen — 10-3-23
text:
# Brilliantmont

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Brillantmont has been around for 140 years. Built on a small but beautiful piece of property in Lausanne, they are a Swiss boarding school that services 90 students per year starting in 8th grade. They run on the British School system and prepare students, focusing strongly on the humanities for university, typically in Europe or the United States. All classes are held in English. During the summer, they can take up to 100 students and they typically attend for 2-3 weeks, although they offer a total of 6 weeks. Many of those who attend during the summer are trying it out for the boarding school. She likes to say they can differentiate themselves from the other Swiss boarding schools because they are really family-owned and operated, even during the summer.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Today I met with Sarah Frei, Head of Admissions and External Relations, with Brillantmont. She is in Miami for a conference of all the Swiss programs. Her husband is part of the family that founded the program, and it has been in the family since the start. She has been with the school for 30 years.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Boarding students share a room with a roommate, and sometimes there may be three per room. The rooms are very large and very modern looking. Bathrooms are in the room. There is a floor advisor with a ratio of 8 to 1.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Unknown

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

The day starts at 8:15 and runs till 4 with afternoon clubs, Friday night disco parties and weekend excursions. From January through April, weekend snow skiing is a big part of their program. They also have ski week in February. A student can attend for one semester only.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

They keep the nationality balance to no more than 15% per nation and get a nice well-rounded class based not only on academics but also what each student can add to the family balance. She likes to say they can differentiate themselves from the other Swiss boarding schools because they are really family-owned and operated, even during the summer. They basically adopt the students as their own while they are there.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Unknown

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

The school is one large family where everyone knows everyone. They basically adopt the students as their own while they are there. She will talk with parents happily and she really was very lovely. She was lovely and shared a bit of information that I can share.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Travel logistics: Unknown. Parent communication: She will talk with parents happily. Tech policy: Unknown. Extra costs: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Brillantmont has been around for 140 years and is a Swiss boarding school built on a small but beautiful piece of property in Lausanne. They run on the British School system, all classes are held in English, and they prepare students, focusing strongly on the humanities for university, typically in Europe or the United States. During the summer, they can take up to 100 students who typically attend for 2-3 weeks (out of a total of 6 weeks), and the day starts at 8:15 and runs till 4 with afternoon clubs, Friday night disco parties and weekend excursions. The school is one large family where everyone knows everyone, they are really family-owned and operated, and they basically adopt the students as their own while they are there.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Built on a small but beautiful piece of property in Lausanne.
  - Brillantmont has been around for 140 years.
  - They run on the British School system and prepare students, focusing strongly on the humanities for university, typically in Europe or the United States.
  - All classes are held in English.
  - They keep the nationality balance to no more than 15% per nation.
  - During the summer, they can take up to 100 students and they typically attend for 2-3 weeks, although they offer a total of 6 weeks.
  - From January through April, weekend snow skiing is a big part of their program; they also have ski week in February.
  - Boarding students share a room with a roommate (sometimes three); rooms are very large and very modern looking with bathrooms in the room.
  - There is a floor advisor with a ratio of 8 to 1.
  - They are really family-owned and operated; the school is one large family where everyone knows everyone.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Waterfront & aquatics: Unknown.
  - Food: Unknown.
  - Tech policy: Unknown.
  - Extra costs: Unknown.
  - Detailed travel logistics: Unknown.
  - A student can attend for one semester only.
  - Cost/tuition: Unknown.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Today I met with Sarah Frei, Head of Admissions and External Relations, with Brillantmont. She is in Miami for a conference of all the Swiss programs. She was lovely and shared a bit of information that I can share. Brillantmont has been around for 140 years. Her husband is part of the family that founded the program, and it has been in the family since the start. She has been with the school for 30 years. Built on a small but beautiful piece of property in Lausanne, they are a Swiss boarding school that services 90 students per year starting in 8 th grade. They keep the nationality balance to no more than 15% per nation and get a nice well-rounded class based not only on academics but also what each student can add to the family balance. The school is one large family where everyone knows everyone. They run on the British School system and prepare students, focusing strongly on the humanities for university, typically in Europe or the United States. All classes are held in English. Boarding students share a room with a roommate, and sometimes there may be three per room. The rooms are very large and very modern looking. Bathrooms are in the room. There is a floor advisor with a ratio of 8 to 1. The day starts at 8:15 and runs till 4 with afternoon clubs, Friday night disco parties and weekend excursions. From January through April, weekend snow skiing is a big part of their program. They also have ski week in February. A student can attend for one semester only. During the summer, they can take up to 100 students and they typically attend for 2-3 weeks, although they offer a total of 6 weeks. Many of those who attend during the summer are trying it out for the boarding school. She likes to say they can differentiate themselves from the other Swiss boarding schools because they are really family-owned and operated, even during the summer. They basically adopt the students as their own while they are there. She will talk with parents happily and she really was very lovely.

title: Baco — Beth — 7-29-24
text:
# Baco

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Baco and Chen-a-Wah Camp Tours 7-13-24 write up. Beth Goldstein, Allison Wortman and Avery Smith (alumna) toured Beth, Lisa Borg, Shari Levine, and Jennifer Markizon. “If nothing changes, nothing changes.” Very old school, family run, mom & pop vibe, neighborhood camp. Nothing has been built since we toured in 2019. No programming was added except pickleball on tennis courts. 7 weeks; 1x 4 week option any age. 180 boys / 130 girls (both max capacity); 70% alumni families. Current 1st-9th grade campers; 10th grade waiter/waitresses who put on coed play.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Key staff are all teachers; 1/3 international; 2/3 were campers; no staff shirt. Assign big bro / big sis on June 1st.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Very buggy and hilly campuses (with steep gravel paths rather than stairs or pavement). Buildings on both campuses are HOT. Cabins: 10-16 campers; 4-6 counselors; single beds close together with cubies; 9th graders and older have bunk beds; 1 shower / 1 sink / 1 toilet (for 20 people). Laundry: clothing on-site 1x week as a group; linens and towels laundered off-site. Keep cabin together year after year; campers unpack for selves. Can have hair dryer; make-up allowed but rarely used.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Lake: beautiful lake down steep hill on boys side and incline on girls side; no pools. In the water 2x day; swim lessons 4x week until age 13. 10th graders can get lifeguard certified. Semi-private motorized lake; 1 ski boat per campus; boating and sailing.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

6 period day; 5 assigned; 1 choice (“free swim” with some program areas open). Girls have tennis and swim daily. Every Saturday = family (bro/sis/cousin/framily) gathering. 7th grade and older has porch night 3x week. Socials consist of coed tennis; coed boating; coed hike; coed movies.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

4-day color war at end of session; 1 day color competition in beginning. “Bazaar Foundation” Carnival is big event that alumni and parents attend as a charity fundraiser. Creative: lame arts n crafts; tie dye and buttons glued on paper. 2 pottery wheels on boys’ campus. NO STEM. Performing Arts: old buildings; fans; 2-3 shows per summer. Music: elective for guitar, drums, keyboard. Fitness: boys have pavilion with old equipment for 8th and older. Basketball is very popular at Baco; also soccer, flag football; baseball, volleyball, roller hockey for boys, tennis & pickleball (a little lacrosse). Outdoor Adventure: ropes course on boys campus and climbing wall on girls campus are both used by both camps. Field trips to local amusements; coed longer trips age 13+ (Montreal, VT, Boston, Quebec).

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

O.D. inside Dining Hall. Dining Hall: hot and old school. 1 seating; boys have buffet; girls have family style. Can accommodate allergies (but not confident). Sit by bunk. Campers allowed to bring plastic tote of food to keep in cabin; parents replenish on visiting day (observed candy and junk food under beds in storage bins). Snacks 3x day including milk and cookies at night; no canteen. Have soda machines which kids have a cash account to use. Medical: 2 nurses on each campus with 1 shared parent doctor; nearest hospital in Glens Falls 45 minutes away; ambulance in town.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Very old school, family run, mom & pop vibe, neighborhood camp. 70% alumni families. Every Saturday = family (bro/sis/cousin/framily) gathering. “Inclusivity and Empathy.” Religion: Shabbat Blessings and services on Friday eve and Saturday morning led by 10th graders; Shabbat dinner.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Buses: 5-6 bus caravan Queens to Nyack to camp. Flights from Montreal and D.C. Extra fees: private lessons for tennis / basketball / gymnastics / tutoring. Baggage, Busing, trips. Communication: 3 phone calls; allow music player; no camera; camp moms take some photos that are posted daily.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Very old school, family run, mom & pop vibe, neighborhood camp. 7 weeks; 1x 4 week option any age; 180 boys / 130 girls; 70% alumni families. Nothing has been built since we toured in 2019; no programming was added except pickleball on tennis courts. I would be unlikely to recommend as I think it is primarily a Long Island - South Shore / Queens neighborhood camp or alumni who have moved to other areas.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Beautiful lake down steep hill on boys side and incline on girls side; no pools.
  - In the water 2x day; swim lessons 4x week until age 13.
  - 10th graders can get lifeguard certified.
  - Semi-private motorized lake; 1 ski boat per campus; boating and sailing.
  - Basketball is very popular at Baco.
  - “Bazaar Foundation” Carnival is big event that alumni and parents attend as a charity fundraiser.
  - Key staff are all teachers; 1/3 international; 2/3 were campers.
  - Shabbat Blessings and services on Friday eve and Saturday morning led by 10th graders; Shabbat dinner.
  - Field trips to local amusements; coed longer trips age 13+ (Montreal, VT, Boston, Quebec).

  

#### Lowlights

  - Buildings on both campuses are HOT.
  - Very buggy and hilly campuses (with steep gravel paths rather than stairs or pavement).
  - Nothing has been built since we toured in 2019.
  - No programming was added except pickleball on tennis courts.
  - Creative: lame arts n crafts; tie dye and buttons glued on paper.
  - NO STEM.
  - Dining Hall: hot and old school.
  - Can accommodate allergies (but not confident).

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Scatico

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Baco and Chen-a-Wah Camp Tours 7-13-24 Write up Beth Goldstein Allison Wortman and Avery Smith (alumna) toured Beth, Lisa Borg, Shari Levine, and Jennifer Markizon “If nothing changes, nothing changes” Very old school, family run, mom & pop vibe, neighborhood camp Nothing has been built since we toured in 2019. No programming was added except pickleball on tennis courts. Very buggy and hilly campuses (with steep gravel paths rather than stairs or pavement) Buildings on both campuses are HOT 7 weeks; 1x 4 week option any age 180 boys / 130 girls (both max capacity); 70% alumni families Current 1 st -9 th grade campers; 10 th grade waiter / waitresses who put on coed play Every Saturday = family (bro/sis/cousin/framily) gathering 7 th grade and older has porch night 3x week Socials consist of Coed tennis; coed boating; coed hike; coed movies Buses: 5-6 bus caravan Queens, to Nyack to camp Flights from Montreal and D.C. Shabbat diner Lake: beautiful lake down steep hill on boys side and incline on girls side; no pools In the water 2x day; swim lessons 4x week until age 13 10 th graders can get lifeguard certified Semi-private motorized lake; 1 ski boat per campus; boating and sailing Creative: lame arts n crafts; tie dye and buttons glued on paper 2 pottery wheels on boys’ campus NO STEM Performing Arts: Old Buildings; fans; 2-3 shows per summer Music: elective for guitar, drums, keyboard Fitness: Boys have pavilion with old equipment for 8 th and older Basketball is very popular at Baco; also soccer, flag football; baseball, volleyball, roller hockey for boys, tennis & pickleball (a little lacrosse) Outdoor Adventure: ropes course on boys campus and climbing wall on girls campus are both used by both camps Field trips to local amusements; coed longer trips age 13+ Montreal, VT, Boston, Quebec “Inclusivity and Empathy” I would be unlikely to recommend as I think it is primarily a Long Island - South Shore / Queens neighborhood camp or alumni who have moved to other areas. Possibly would give with Scatico

title: TIMBER LAKE — LARA — 7-11-17
text:
# TIMBER LAKE

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

The camp is beautiful. It is set high on a mountain, the views are spectacular and facilities are pristine. This is a coed camp with 480 campers. It is a full session camp with an all inclusive tuition. New This Summer: Teepee overnight village – still on camp but far enough away that you feel removed from camp (photo attached). No more indoor pool; they have built over it and created a building that houses their fitness rooms and media rooms with 3D printing, an on air radio station, green screen video making, a weight room and spin studio. There are NO horses. 4 hole golf course and driving range that is a scheduled and elective activity.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Meet with owners Jay and Mindy Jacobs briefly in their home on the lake on camp. We were toured by Boys Side Head Counselor/Assistant Director, Robbie Gothelf who started Timber Lake as a 10 year old camper and worked his way through the ranks to be a fulltime employee. There is a camp mom on boys side for the younger campers. Traditionally Timber Lake campers start going into 2nd/3rd grade; however, this summer they have an entire new bunk going into 5th grade. About 80% of the camp is Jewish with optional Friday night services.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Counselors unpack for the campers before their arrival. In the younger girl bunks EVERY camper had their clothes in individual containers clearly marked "shorts, tank tops, underwear, etc." that fit neatly into the cubbies. This came from the parents though not a camp suggestion or requirement. Towel service for both showers and pool\!\!\!

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

No more indoor pool. Daily swim instruction for 8-11 year olds in the heated pools. There is a private lake in the middle of camp with all water sports separated by areas.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

4 hole golf course and driving range that is a scheduled and elective activity. Daily swim instruction for 8-11 year olds.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

They implement their own Starfish Values Program (Sportsmanship, Tolerance, Appreciation, Respect, Friendship, Integrity, Sensitivity, Helpfulness) where children are recognized weekly in front of their peers. More info can be found on the website regarding this program. Overall this camp has everything\! We mean everything\! There is even a minor league quality ballpark (photo attached) with portable lights\! 3D printing, on air radio station, green screen video making, weight room and spin studio.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

They have their own fire truck with trained staff members on site located behind the dining hall if needed. They have 6 nurses and 1 full time doctor that rotates every two weeks. The whole camp dines together family style and there is real peanut butter served in the dining hall.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

The kids were very sweet and all looked super happy. NO package policy (with the exception of the first week for forgotten essentials). All xbox and the like video games taken out of the canteen area. TV’s only show baseball games or other sports. About 80% of the camp is Jewish with optional Friday night services.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

They are still running their $300 gift card touring promotion. Since they are not near any other camps they suggested we tell parents to tour them first on a Saturday so they can use the gift card for their overnight hotel stay if they are touring other camps the next day. NO package policy (with the exception of the first week for forgotten essentials). All xbox and the like video games taken out of the canteen area. TV’s only show baseball games or other sports.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

The camp is beautiful, set high on a mountain; the views are spectacular and facilities are pristine. This coed, full session, all inclusive camp with 480 campers has a private lake in the middle of camp with all water sports separated by areas, plus daily swim instruction for 8–11 year olds in the heated pools. New This Summer: a teepee overnight village, and the former indoor pool was built over to create fitness and media rooms (3D printing, on air radio station, green screen video making, weight room and spin studio), and there is even a minor league quality ballpark with portable lights. Culture and care notes include their Starfish Values Program with weekly recognition, whole-camp family style meals with real peanut butter, a NO package policy, Xbox and the like removed and TVs only showing sports, plus robust safety with 6 nurses, a rotating full time doctor, and their own fire truck on site.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Beautiful camp set high on a mountain; views are spectacular; facilities are pristine.
  - Coed, full session, all inclusive camp with 480 campers.
  - Teepee overnight village – still on camp but far enough away that you feel removed from camp.
  - Building with fitness rooms and media rooms: 3D printing, on air radio station, green screen video making, weight room and spin studio.
  - Private lake in the middle of camp with all water sports separated by areas.
  - Daily swim instruction for 8-11 year olds in the heated pools.
  - Minor league quality ballpark with portable lights.
  - 4 hole golf course and driving range that is a scheduled and elective activity.
  - Camp mom on boys side for the younger campers.
  - Towel service for both showers and pool.
  - Counselors unpack for the campers before their arrival.
  - Starfish Values Program with weekly recognition.
  - 6 nurses and 1 full time doctor that rotates every two weeks.
  - Own fire truck with trained staff members on site.
  - Whole camp dines together family style; real peanut butter served.
  - The kids were very sweet and all looked super happy.
  - $300 gift card touring promotion for families touring.

  

#### Lowlights

  - There are NO horses.
  - No more indoor pool.
  - They are not near any other camps.
  - All xbox and the like video games taken out of the canteen area.
  - TV’s only show baseball games or other sports.
  - NO package policy (with the exception of the first week for forgotten essentials).

  

#### Similar Camps

  - TIMBER LAKE WEST
  - TYLER HILL

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Meet with owners Jay and Mindy Jacobs briefly in their home on the lake on camp. We were toured by Boys Side Head Counselor/Assistant Director, Robbie Gothelf who started Timber Lake as a 10 year old camper and worked his way through the ranks to be a fulltime employee. The camp is beautiful. It is set high on a mountain, the views are spec tacular and facilities are pristine. New This Summer: Teepee overnight village – still on camp but far enough away that you feel removed from camp (photo attached) No more indoor pool. They have built over it and created a building that houses their fitness rooms and media rooms. They have 3D printing, on air radio station, green screen video making, weight room and spin studio NO package policy (with the exception of the first week for forgotten essentials) All xbox and the like video games taken out of the canteen area. TV’s only show baseball games or other sports. This is a coed camp with 480 campers. It is a full session camp with an all inclusive tuition Towel service for both showers and pool\!\!\! There is a “camp mom” on boys side for the younger campers. Traditionally Timber Lake campers start going into 2 nd /3 rd grade however, this summer they have an entire new bunk going into 5 th grade. There are NO horses. 4 hole golf course and driving range that is a scheduled and elective activity. The whole camp dines together family style and there is real peanut butter served in the dining hall. About 80% of the camp is Jewish with optional Friday night services They have their own fire truck with trained staff members on site located behind the dining hall if needed. Daily swim instruction for 8-11 year olds in the heated pools. There is a private lake in the middle of camp with all water sports separated by areas. They have 6 nurses and 1 full time doctor that rotates every two weeks. Counselors unpack for the campers before their arrival. In the younger girl bunks EVERY camper had their clothes in individual containers clearly marked “shorts, tank tops, underwear, etc.” that fit neatly into the cubbies. This came from the parents though not a camp suggestion or requirement. They implement their own Starfish Values Program (Sportsmanship, Tolerance, Appreciation, Respect, Friendship, Integrity, Sensitivity, Helpfulness) where children are recognized weekly in front of their peers. More info can be found on the website regarding this program. Overall this camp has everything\! We mean everything\! There is even a minor league quality ballpark (photo attached) with portable lights\! The kids were very sweet and all looked super happy. They are still running their $300 gift card touring promotion. Since they are not near any other camps they suggested we tell parents to tour them first on a Saturday so they can use the gift card for their overnight hotel stay if they are touring other camps the next day.

title: Fernwood Cove — Pilar — 8-22-22
text:
# Fernwood Cove

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Fernwood Cove Camp for girls. Traditional camp with activities all day long. Two 3 ½ week sessions. Rookie camp for those who have just completed 1st to 3rd grade. Demographics 200 girls this summer which is their max number. Ages 8 to 15 or completed 2nd to 9th grade. 15% international campers. CIT’s stay for the full summer. 85% return rate. Do have spaces available in March.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Toured by Jim Gill, owner director with wife Beigette Gill. “Welcome to our home”. They live at camp year round with their 2 children. They met at Fernwood and got married at camp during camp session. Jim has been part of Fernwood Cove since the opening in 1999. They bought the camp in 2004. Their house is in the middle of camp to accommodate Beigette’s wheelchair access. Camp has ramps in all buildings so she can get around easily. We met Beigette briefly. For next year they plan to have Team Leaders with no other responsibilities. Staff wear blue and white uniforms.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Bunks No electricity. 10 to 12 campers in each cabin. Minimum 2 counselors per bunk. Younger girls have 4 counselors. They provide bedding. Girls can bring their blankets. 1 cubby for each camper. 8th and 9th graders all stay in one bunk with no bathroom. They have a shower house.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

At lake: Swimming area. Red Cross swimming. Must take at least 1 week of swimming. Fishing boat. Jetski. Water trampoline. Water slide on separate dock. Sailing. 2 ski boats. Must be 10 years old for skiing and wakeboarding. It is a 2 ½ hour activity. Canoeing. Kayaking. Paddling.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Morning message. Schedule Elective with choice. 6 periods a day. Girls sign up for activities weekly. Each camper has their own schedule. Scheduled by age and skill level. One alternative day every week. PJ breakfast and bunk activities, and special activities in the afternoon. Awards are given at all camp camp fires for participation in activities and kindness. Trip options on special days. A nice and cozy library with lots of books is available during free time.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Activities Counselors teach activities. They have progression charts displayed for campers to see. Sports Tennis. Softball. Beach Volleyball. Archery. Gaga. Aerobics. Gymnastics with a lot of options including a foam pit. The instructor requested elements when they built the gymnastics building. Available for 6th grade and up. Adventure Mountain biking. Rock climbing and ropes course. Hiking trails. Nature and outdoors. Riding - Called Horsemanship 17 horses of their own. Locals work with horses during the winter. Horses are matched with the camper and spend the entire session with the same horse. The program includes grooming. Campers bring their own equipment. No skill level necessary. Riding camp cost depends on which program is selected. Beautiful barn, very clean. Arts & Crafts Traditional, including woodworking, jewelry, sewing and ceramics. Girls sign up for a specific activity such as mask or pinata making. Campers are grouped by skill level. Will build a new arts & crafts center in the future. Cooking Big kitchen with 2 cooking areas. Includes farming and vegetable garden. Artistic Music studio with drums, electric drums, microphones, keyboard, lots of guitars and ukuleles. Dance studio with ballet bars. They have a dance production at the end of each session. Theater with stage and piano. Theater production every session as well as mini theater sketches for those who don’t want to participate in the play.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Health 4 nurses. 2 hospitals 15 minutes away. Dining Hall At the beginning of camp they eat by cabin. Then they eat by themes so they get to meet everyone. Family style. Sunday buffet meals. Breakfast bar, salad bar at lunch and dinner. A special area in the kitchen for nut fee, nut sensitive, gluten free, dairy free, can accommodate allergies. Johnny Appleseed is their prayer. The dining hall is quiet during meals.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

“Welcome to our home”. Traditional camp with activities all day long. They encourage the girls to compliment each other on something other than their appearance. No pictures because camp is for the kids, not for the parents. No phone calls because if you are missing home you are missing what is happening in front of you. They make a few exceptions.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

They zoom with every family before camp. No pictures because camp is for the kids, not for the parents. Photos are provided after camp. No phone calls because if you are missing home you are missing what is happening in front of you. They make a few exceptions. Transportation Pickup from Boston Logan, Portland Jetport, Newton, Fairfield CT, Lincoln Center and Port Chester, NY. at extra charge. They started an escorted flight from LA. Uniform Green and white. Must buy at least one pair of shorts to see the color. The rest can be any type as long as it is the same green. Riding camp cost depends on which program is selected.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Fernwood Cove Camp for girls. Traditional camp with activities all day long. This camp is for a sweet girl who enjoys participating in different activities. Two 3 ½ week sessions for ages 8 to 15 (completed 2nd to 9th grade) with a max of 200 girls and 15% international campers.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - They live at camp year round with their 2 children.
  - Camp has ramps in all buildings so she can get around easily.
  - Counselors teach activities. They have progression charts displayed for campers to see.
  - Riding - Called Horsemanship 17 horses of their own. Horses are matched with the camper and spend the entire session with the same horse.
  - Big kitchen with 2 cooking areas. Includes farming and vegetable garden.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Bunks No electricity.
  - 8th and 9th graders all stay in one bunk with no bathroom.
  - No pictures because camp is for the kids, not for the parents.
  - No phone calls because if you are missing home you are missing what is happening in front of you.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

They live at camp year round with their 2 children. They met at Fernwood and got married at camp during camp session. Jim has been part of Fernwood Cove since the opening in 1999. They bought the camp in 2004. Their house is in the middle of camp to accommodate Beigette’s wheelchair access . Camp has ramps in all buildings so she can get around easily. We met Beigette briefly. Traditional camp with activities all day long. Morning message. They encourage the girls to compliment each other on something other than their appearance. They zoom with every family before camp. title: Canadensis — Allison — 8-22-22
text:
# Canadensis

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Allison, Emily S., and Lara were toured by Pam. 550 campers. 4 week option 1 time only finishing 1-5th grade. No intercamps summer 2022. Staff fully vaccinated.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Sr. Girls (9th graders) were on their Sr. Trip to DC. In 2022 they had 4 new rising 7th graders.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Bunks: Youngest kids live closest to the dining hall and then the older they get the farther from camp they get. 13 kids in a bunk - 4 counselors in every bunk. They provide bunk junk cubby; no bringing extra shelves. Separate room for clothes. Brings own towels for shower.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

2 heated pools - one for free play and one for instruction. Lake: Motorized lake with waterskiing; camp has lake 3 times a week in the schedule. Lake and pool have towel service. Zip line over the pool.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Every morning camp starts with a dance party. Activities: 6 total activity periods. 7th period is free before dinner, then camp eats dinner and then showers before evening activity. Lower camp has one elective period; Middle camp has two elective periods; Sr camp has three elective periods. Everyone goes through programming once a week to change elective. Activities are by bunk but electives can be co-ed. All age group wears a lanyard so the campers know what electives they are signed up for. Every age group has a camp out (on campus).

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Golf on campus. Archery. Flag football. 14 tennis courts. Covered hockey pavilion. All fields can be lit. Roller hockey. 2.2 mile trail around the lake. Can learn how to ride a Honda. Movie theater and music. All art rooms and dance studio are air conditioned. Electives: cheer, ninja warrior. Lower camp boys have gymnastics. Indoor basketball. Huge art building - 7 different art rooms - Ceramics; T-shirt studio; Glass fusion; Painting; General arts and crafts; Video and media (have their own green screen and YouTube channel); Photography. STEM program. Fitness center – on the schedule for older girls – has a weight room. Cooking program. Ropes course and climbing wall. Pickle ball.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

4 Ice machines and hydration stations throughout camp. One dining seating - Buffet style food. Nut free; can accommodate almost all dietary needs. Huge deck off of dining room with outdoor dining which overlooks the lake. Air conditioned health center. 1 doctor every week. 6 nurses. 1 person overseeing health center.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

When we arrived at camp it was during line up - very spirited and high energy. No uniform – everyone comes to camp with 2 blue and 2 gold shirts. 5 minute Shabbat service led by CITs. Dance party after service and then watch a highlight video of the week.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Buses: Westchester, NYC, Long Island, PA, Northern and Central Jersey. They meet every camper before. Travel with bags; don't go back to the bunk. 2 canteens - one for upper and one for lower. Extras: Private tennis lessons; Country club golf; Horseback riding (off site). Parent communication: Unknown. Tech: Unknown. Extra costs beyond tuition: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Every morning camp starts with a dance party and line up is very spirited and high energy. There are 6 total activity periods with a 7th free before dinner, electives vary by age, and everyone goes through programming once a week to change elective. Facilities include 2 heated pools (one for free play and one for instruction), a motorized lake with waterskiing, 14 tennis courts, a covered hockey pavilion, and a huge art building with 7 different art rooms, and all art rooms and the dance studio are air conditioned. The camp is nut free with an air conditioned health center (1 doctor every week and 6 nurses), and buses run from Westchester, NYC, Long Island, PA, and Northern and Central Jersey.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Very spirited and high energy line up and morning dance party.
  - Motorized lake with waterskiing and 2 heated pools.
  - 14 tennis courts, covered hockey pavilion, lit fields, and extensive arts (7 art rooms).
  - All art rooms and dance studio are air conditioned.
  - Fitness center – on the schedule for older girls – has a weight room.
  - Nut free; can accommodate almost all dietary needs.
  - Air conditioned health center with 1 doctor every week, 6 nurses, and 1 person overseeing the health center.
  - Buses from Westchester, NYC, Long Island, PA, Northern and Central Jersey.

  

#### Lowlights

  - 4 week option 1 time only finishing 1-5th grade.
  - No intercamps summer 2022.
  - Brings own towels for shower.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

title: Berkshire United — Wendy — 8-13-23
text:
# Berkshire United

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

BERKSHIRE SOCCER CAMP – All girls camp in East Otis, near Lenox MA. Opened in 2010, owners are exploring opportunities to open another one or more soccer camps in the Midwest or California. Run 8 one-week sessions (many girls stay 2 weeks). Session is actually Sunday – Thursday, which is less than a week, so many girls stay 2 sessions. 174 girls were there, which was their capacity.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Directors - Paul Titley and Katie Kreiner. Wendy/Joanne/Dara toured with Katie Kreiner. Girls entering 4th – 10th. Toward the end of summer, they get mostly 13–15-year-old girls getting ready to try out for their high school teams.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Facilities are simple but very well appointed and clean. AC in bunks. Small dining room with cozy porch and fireplace.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Lake offers trampolines, SUP, kayak, jetski. Electives include fishing or other lake activities.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Schedule - Two 90 minute soccer sessions per day and 2 electives (Arts & Crafts, fishing or other lake activities). Session is Sunday – Thursday.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Lovely setting and beautiful fields (grass and turf, plus a big indoor turf dome, which they really needed with the rainy weather). Fields are lit for night play. Arts & Crafts shop was substantial for a sports camp.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Nurses onsite, doctor in town.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Unknown

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

No cell phones, completely unplugged except for music player.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

BERKSHIRE SOCCER CAMP is an all girls camp in East Otis, near Lenox MA. Opened in 2010, they run 8 one-week sessions (many girls stay 2 weeks), with sessions actually Sunday–Thursday. Schedule: Two 90 minute soccer sessions per day and 2 electives (Arts & Crafts, fishing or other lake activities). Lovely setting and beautiful fields (grass and turf, plus a big indoor turf dome); fields are lit for night play.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Lowlights

  - No cell phones, completely unplugged except for music player.
  - Facilities are simple.

  - Unknown

title: Birch Hill — Wendy — 8-13-24
text:
# Birch Hill

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

BIRCH HILL in New Durham, New Hampshire. Toured July 21, 2024, with Joanne & Dara. CO- ED CAMP. Offers 2-4-6 or 8 wks; many campers do 4 wks. 167 campers presently, which is a good number for them.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Toured by Director Chrissy Morrell (her parents have owned/operated for many yrs, family from New Jersey and Chrissy lives in New England now). Many campers from New England, Philly area, Florida.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Bunks – simple and neat, have electricity and toilets/sinks/showers inside.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Lake – non motorized, nice, clean looking. No pool.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Program is choice based, create schedule prior to arrival, can change at camp, 5 activities per day.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Horseback riding – 5 horses, right on campus. Didn’t look very instructional, more novice, not much of a stable management program either. Decent fields and courts (tennis & pickleball) but athletics aren’t serious, no intercamps. 1 show per sessions, optional. Simple traditional A\&C – photography and music are popular. Mountain biking and can ride bikes around camp property, even down to the lake. Ropes course (adventure area seemed incomplete and not geared for younger campers).

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

3 nurses who are 24-7. No dr on site, use local urgent care. Food: Unknown.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Impression- mellow vibe, very down to earth kids, good for a 2 wk experience, not for the family who is looking for strong instruction.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

No Visiting Day (as most do 2 or 4 wks). No transportation. No chaperoned flight. NO extra fees. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech: Unknown. Extra costs: none noted.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Birch Hill in New Durham, New Hampshire is a co-ed, mellow, down to earth camp offering 2-4-6 or 8 wks (many campers do 4 wks). The program is choice based with 5 activities per day, and bunks are simple and neat with electricity and toilets/sinks/showers inside. The lake is non motorized (no pool), and activities include simple traditional A\&C, mountain biking, and horseback riding (5 horses on campus). Good for a 2 wk experience and families not looking for strong instruction; no transportation or chaperoned flight, no Visiting Day, and no extra fees.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - mellow vibe, very down to earth kids
  - choice based program; create schedule prior to arrival; can change at camp; 5 activities per day
  - Bunks – simple and neat, have electricity and toilets/sinks/showers inside
  - Lake – non motorized, nice, clean looking
  - Simple traditional A\&C – photography and music are popular
  - Mountain biking and can ride bikes around camp property, even down to the lake
  - Horseback riding – 5 horses, right on campus
  - NO extra fees

  

#### Lowlights

  - athletics aren’t serious, no intercamps
  - Didn’t look very instructional, more novice, not much of a stable management program
  - adventure area seemed incomplete and not geared for younger campers
  - No pool
  - No transportation. No chaperoned flight.
  - No Visiting Day

  

#### Similar Camps

  - GREENWOOD TRAILS
  - NORTH STAR
  - WINGATE KIRKLAND
  - EMERSON

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

WRITE UP: BIRCH HILL in New Durham, New Hampshire Toured July 21, 2024, with Joanne & Dara Toured by Director Chrissy Morrell (her parents have owned/operated for many yrs, family from New Jersey and Chrissy lives in New England now) Offers 2-4-6 or 8 wks. Many campers do 4 wks. No Visiting Day (as most do 2 or 4 wks). No transportation. No chaperoned flight. Many campers from New England, Philly area, Florida. NO extra fees. CO- ED CAMP. 167 campers presently, which is a good number for them. 3 nurses who are 24-7. No dr on site, use local urgent care Program is choice based, create schedule prior to arrival, can change at camp, 5 activities per day Bunks – simple and neat, have electricity and toilets/sinks/showers inside Lake – non motorized, nice, clean looking. No pool. Horseback riding – 5 horses, right on campus Didn’t look very instructional, more novice, not much of a stable management program either. Decent fields and courts (tennis & pickleball) but athletics aren’t serious, no intercamps 1 show per sessions, optional. Simple traditional A\&C – photography and music are popular. Mountain biking and can ride bikes around camp property, even down to the lake. opes course (adventure area seemed incomplete and not geared for younger campers) Impression- mellow vibe, very down to earth kids, good for a 2 wk experience, not for the family who is looking for strong instruction I would give with other co-ed, lower key session camps : GREENWOOD TRAILS, NORTH STAR and possibly WINGATE KIRKLAND, EMERSON.

title: Bienvenidos A Gijon — Beatriz — 3-14-24
text:
# Bienvenidos A Gijon

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

BIENVENIDOS A GIJON ( Welcome to Gijón ) Paula Sampedro and Nuria Sabadell. Nuria worked with Rassias as Language Program Manager in Spain. When Rassias stopped their activity she continued by herself with Paula. Spanish full immersion programmes in Gijon (Asturias), north of Spain, from beginners to advanced students. Gijon is a nice town located by the Cantabric sea and an hour by car from Picos de Europa National Park. Summer Programme: Spanish lessons combine with Sports for small groups aprox. 10-12 students, mainly girls high School students, 14-18 years old. Dates: Full Summer June 30th – July 25th (25 days in total). They´re flexible with dates and length of sessions, always minimum 2 weeks session.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Small groups aprox. 10-12 students, mainly girls high School students, 14-18 years old. Paula Sampedro and Nuria Sabadell. Nuria worked with Rassias as Language Program Manager in Spain; when Rassias stopped their activity she continued by herself with Paula. Normally Paula chaperones the group. Activities and excursions with the host family.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Accommodation: Students are placed in selected high end families, full board with single bedrooms in nice houses. Paula and Nuria take good care to match students with families. They send a detailed questionnaire for the family selection process. They like to place students in families with kids the same age so they share hobbies and socialize with host brother/sister group of friends. They prefer one student per family but can accommodate differently if the family requests it.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Located by the Cantabric sea. Students have access to Covadonga Sports Club where they can practice a huge variety of Sports: basket, tennis, field hockey, volley, swimming, handball, folk dance, chess. NOT SOCCER, SURF OR SAILING – these can be arranged in other facilities at extra fee.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Students have 3 hours/day Spanish lessons Monday–Friday. Language School supplies all materials for the Spanish course. Dynamic fun programme where students improve their communication skills by a topic method. Students have access to Covadonga Sports Club where they can practice a huge variety of Sports (basket, tennis, field hockey, volley, swimming, handball, folk dance, chess).

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Group Trips to explore Asturias: Oviedo (the old quarter, the Naranco) and Avilés (Niemeyer Cultural Center). Mining route (Trekking-trip). This route takes place in the heart of the Asturian Central Mountain and runs through the Protected Space of the Mining Valleys, highlighting the rich historical and cultural heritage of the region. Activities and excursions with the host family. They offer also Customized Language Immersion Programmes.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Full board homestay with selected high end families. Emergency telephone 24 hours, 7 days a week for parents and students. Under supervision and guidance from Bienvenidos a Gijón during the Madrid tour (if applicable).

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Dynamic fun programme where students improve their communication skills by a topic method. Gijon is a nice town located by the Cantabric sea and an hour by car from Picos de Europa National Park. They like to place students in families with kids the same age so they share hobbies and socialize with host brother/sister group of friends.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Travel: Arrival: Madrid Barajas Airport or Oviedo airport. If they have MINIMUM 4 full summer students they start the programme in Madrid and pick-up the students at Madrid Barajas Airport and enjoy 2–3 days visiting Madrid (tickets included to the main museums: Reina Sofía, el Prado, Círculo de Bellas Artes, El Retiro, el Estadio Santiago Bernabéu and always Shopping) under supervision and guidance from Bienvenidos a Gijón and hotel accommodation in downtown. Normally Paula chaperones the group. They travel in premium bus from Madrid to Gijon. If not, they will start in Oviedo and students will have to book their transportation to Oviedo, either by plane, taxi, train at their expense. Nuria will help families with travel arrangements. Departure: Students will return home from Oviedo airport. Dates: Full Summer June 30th – July 25th (25 days in total). They´re flexible with dates and length of sessions, always minimum 2 weeks session. Tuition: Full summer 5100€; Two weeks session 4250€. Not included in tuition: extra Spanish lessons, Sports not run at Covadonga Club or private Sports lessons, travel and health insurance, Flights and pocket money. Parent communication/tech: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Spanish full immersion programmes in Gijon (Asturias), north of Spain, from beginners to advanced students. Summer Programme: Spanish lessons combine with Sports for small groups aprox. 10–12 students, mainly girls high School students, 14–18 years old, with 3 hours/day Spanish lessons Monday–Friday. Students are placed in selected high end families, full board with single bedrooms in nice houses, and join group trips and activities to explore Asturias. Dates are flexible (minimum 2 weeks) with a Full Summer June 30th – July 25th option; Tuition is 5100€ for Full Summer and 4250€ for two weeks.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Spanish full immersion programmes in Gijon (Asturias), north of Spain, from beginners to advanced students.
  - Summer Programme: Spanish lessons combine with Sports for small groups aprox. 10-12 students, mainly girls high School students, 14-18 years old.
  - Students have 3 hours/day Spanish lessons Monday-Friday; Language School supplies all materials.
  - Students have access to Covadonga Sports Club (basket, tennis, field hockey, volley, swimming, handball, folk dance, chess).
  - Group Trips to explore Asturias: Oviedo (the old quarter, the Naranco) and Avilés (Niemeyer Cultural Center); Mining route (Trekking-trip).
  - Selected high end host families, full board with single bedrooms; careful matching and one student per family preferred.
  - Emergency telephone 24 hours, 7 days a week for parents and students.
  - If they have MINIMUM 4 full summer students they start the programme in Madrid with a 2–3 day tour (tickets included) and premium bus to Gijon.
  - They offer also Customized Language Immersion Programmes.

  

#### Lowlights

  - NOT SOCCER, SURF OR SAILING – these can be arranged in other facilities at extra fee.
  - Not included in tuition: extra Spanish lessons, Sports not run at Covadonga Club or private Sports lessons, travel and health insurance, Flights and pocket money.
  - If not starting in Madrid, students will have to book their transportation to Oviedo (plane, taxi, train) at their expense.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

BIENVENIDOS A GIJON ( Welcome to Gijón ) Paula Sampedro and Nuria Sabadell. Nuria worked with Rassias as Language Program Manager in Spain. When Rassias stopped their activity she continued by herself with Paula. Spanish full immersion programmes in Gijon (Asturias), north of Spain, from beginners to advanced students. Gijon is a nice town located by the Cantabric sea and an hour by car from Picos de Europa National Park. Summer Programme: Spanish lessons combine with Sports for small groups aprox. 10-12 students, mainly girls high School students, 14-18 yeras old. • Students have 3 hours/day spanish lessons Monday-Friday. Language School supplies all materials for the spanish course. Dynamic fun programme where students improve their communnication skills by a topic method. • Students have access to Covadonga Sports Club where they can practice a huge variety of Sports: basket, tennis, field hockey, volley, swimming, handball, folk dance, chess ... NOT SOCCER, SURF OR SAILING – these can be arranged in other facilities at extra fee. • Group Trips to explore Asturias: Oviedo (the old quarter, the Naranco) and Avilés (Niemeyer Cultural Center). Mining route ( Trekking-trip ).This route takes place in the heart of the Asturian Central Mountain and runs through the Protected Space of the Mining Valleys, highlighting the rich historical and cultural heritage of the region. • Activities and excursions with the host family. They offer also Customized Language Immersion Programmes. Accomodation : Students are placed in selected high end families, full board with single bedrooms in nice houses. Paula and Nuria take good care to match students with families. They send a detailed questionnaire for the family selection procees. They like to place students in families with kids the same age so they share hobbies and socialice with hostbrother/sister group of friends. They prefer one student per family but can accomodate differently if the family requests it. Emergency telephone 24 hours , 7 days a week for parents and students. Normally Paula chaperones the group. They travel in premium bus from Madrid to Gijon. Tuition: Full summer 5100€ .- Two weeks session 4250€.- Not included in tuition: extra spanish lessons, Sports not run at Covadonga Club or private Sports lessons, travel and health insurance, Flights and pocket money. If not, they will start in Oviedo and students will have to book their transportation to Oviedo, either by plane, taxi, train at their expense. Nuria will help families with travel arrangements. title: Canyon Creek — Melissa — 4-22-22
text:
# Canyon Creek

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Canyon Creek is a great camp for a family looking to introduce their child to a traditional camp for a few summers. Previously it was a very sports oriented camp but in the last few years they have added several adventure activities like dirt bikes, go carts, a ropes course and a rock climbing wall, in addition to the traditional arts activities. The camp is situated on 80 acres but only about 40 acres are currently being used at the camp. Where: Southern California, about 1 hour north of LA. Size: Typically about 180 campers per session. Session Length: 1 and 2 week sessions that can be combined for 3 or 4 week sessions.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Size: Typically about 180 campers per session. Staff and director: Unknown.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

The cabins have nice bathrooms and in-cabin showers but the sleeping quarters are a bit cramped.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

The lake area is about a 30 mins bus drive away which is where all the water ski activities take place. In a one week session for example, each camper will spend one day at the lake and then on the last day of camp they will have the option to go back to the lake for the afternoon.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Daily structure is a hybrid where the cabins travel as a group in the mornings and then in the afternoons have free choice of activities that they sign up for during lunch each day.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Previously it was a very sports oriented camp but in the last few years they have added several adventure activities like dirt bikes, go carts, a ropes course and a rock climbing wall, in addition to the traditional arts activities.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

They are very good at catering to various dietary needs. They do not offer sodas of any kind. Safety: Unknown.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

They have a lovely campfire area where skits and camp rituals take place each night.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Where: Southern California, about 1 hour north of LA. They are a technology free camp. Parent communication: Unknown. Extra costs: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Canyon Creek is a great camp for a family looking to introduce their child to a traditional camp for a few summers. Previously it was a very sports oriented camp but in the last few years they have added several adventure activities like dirt bikes, go carts, a ropes course and a rock climbing wall, in addition to the traditional arts activities. Where: Southern California, about 1 hour north of LA. Size: Typically about 180 campers per session; Session Length: 1 and 2 week sessions that can be combined for 3 or 4 week sessions.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Canyon Creek is a great camp for a family looking to introduce their child to a traditional camp for a few summers.
  - Added several adventure activities like dirt bikes, go carts, a ropes course and a rock climbing wall, in addition to the traditional arts activities.
  - Daily structure is a hybrid where the cabins travel as a group in the mornings and then in the afternoons have free choice of activities that they sign up for during lunch each day.
  - They are very good at catering to various dietary needs.
  - They have a lovely campfire area where skits and camp rituals take place each night.
  - They are a technology free camp.
  - In a one week session for example, each camper will spend one day at the lake and then on the last day of camp they will have the option to go back to the lake for the afternoon.

  

#### Lowlights

  - The lake area is about a 30 mins bus drive away.
  - The cabins have nice bathrooms and in-cabin showers but the sleeping quarters are a bit cramped.
  - The camp is situated on 80 acres but only about 40 acres are currently being used at the camp.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Canyon Creek is a great camp for a family looking to introduce their child to a traditional camp for a few summers. Previously it was a very sports oriented camp but in the last few years they have added several adventure activities like dirt bikes, go carts, a ropes course and a rock climbing wall, in addition to the traditional arts activities. The lake area is about a 30 mins bus drive away which is where all the water ski activities take place. In a one week session for example, each camper will spend one day at the lake and then on the last day of camp they will have the option to go back to the lake for the afternoon. They have a lovely campfire area where skits and camp rituals take place each night. The camp is situated on 80 acres but only about 40 acres are currently being used at the camp. They are a technology free camp and also do not offer sodas of any kind. title: WAH — NEE — 7-15-22
text:
# WAH

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Wah-Nee is co-ed. It is a 7 week camp, but will offer a one time 4 week session for campers going into 5th grade and below. The boys and girls share the gym, lake and pool, otherwise everything is separate.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Kids range in age from 7-17 and they currently have 400 campers evenly split. We were taken around in a golf cart by owner Dave Stricker. All sports counselors coach all year long. Dave is very eager to attract more kids from NJ and mentioned this several times to us.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Unknown

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

The boys and girls share the lake and pool. The lake looked beautiful and clean; Dave joked that we could drink the lake water. Two other camps were visiting for a swim meet. No motorboats.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Every field is lit for night play. Wah-Nee doesn’t do overnights anymore because 'their kids don’t like them'. Camper choice: Unknown.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

They have pickleball courts, which were in use while we were there. All sports counselors coach all year long. Wah-Nee is a very competitive camp. Not everyone will make a team. No golf, horseback riding and no motorboats. Staff walks around with fake money in their pockets called moose money; it’s a reward system for when they catch kids doing kind or good things, and they can cash in for rewards – like a special meal cooked by Dave.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Dining Hall - Wah-Nee is NOT nut free as they serve candy with nuts in the canteen. They can accommodate most diets – egg free, dairy free, gluten free/Celiac – but are not kosher and can’t really accommodate a kosher family – for example they serve BLTs. We were in the DH a few minutes before a meal and it was very festive. The waitrons (their name for the kid servers) serenaded us with a song about mac and cheese. Two other camps were visiting for a swim meet, and ate outside the dining hall on picnic tables. Dave mentioned this is an arrangement they have with the other camps for Covid reasons. Everywhere we went there were signs promoting code of conduct, sportsmanship, and kindness.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

We heard lots of singing and 'Wah-Nee Pride' chants. Dave stopped several kids along the way who sang the camp’s praises and raved about the friendships they have made. Wah-Nee is a very competitive camp. Not everyone will make a team. Dave said, 'couch potatoes would not do well here'. Staff walks around with fake money in their pockets called moose money; it’s a reward system for when they catch kids doing kind or good things. It was clear to all of us that Wah-Nee is beloved by its campers. This is definitely a camp where kids return year after year. Kids were very active, busy and happy.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

For summer 2022, they had 4 buses from Long Island (which included the NYC kids), 2 buses from Westchester and 1 bus from Livingston, NJ. Dave is very eager to attract more kids from NJ and mentioned this several times to us. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown. Extra costs: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Wah-Nee is co-ed with 400 campers evenly split, ages 7-17, and runs a 7 week camp with a one time 4 week session for campers going into 5th grade and below. The boys and girls share the gym, lake and pool, otherwise everything is separate, and every field is lit for night play with pickleball courts in use while we were there. Wah-Nee is a very competitive camp where not everyone will make a team, and 'couch potatoes would not do well here,' alongside lots of singing, 'Wah-Nee Pride' chants, and moose money rewarding kindness. The dining hall is not nut free but can accommodate most diets (not kosher), the lake looked beautiful and clean, and kids were very active, busy and happy.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Every field is lit for night play.
  - Pickleball courts.
  - All sports counselors coach all year long.
  - Signs promoting code of conduct, sportsmanship, and kindness.
  - Lots of singing and 'Wah-Nee Pride' chants.
  - Moose money reward system.
  - Lake looked beautiful and clean.
  - Beloved by its campers; kids return year after year.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Wah-Nee is NOT nut free as they serve candy with nuts in the canteen.
  - They are not kosher and can’t really accommodate a kosher family.
  - Not everyone will make a team.
  - No golf, horseback riding and no motorboats.
  - Wah-Nee doesn’t do overnights anymore.
  - Very competitive camp; 'couch potatoes would not do well here'.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Natasha, Risa and Lana toured Wah-Nee on Tues 7/12/2022. We were taken around in a golf cart by owner Dave Stricker. In addition to the on file questionnaire, here are our observations and extra facts. Wah-Nee is co-ed. It is a 7 week camp, but will offer a one time 4 week session for campers going into 5 th grade and below. The kids range in age from 7-17 and they currently have 400 campers evenly split. The boys and girls share the gym, lake and pool, otherwise everything is separate. For summer 2022, they had 4 buses from Long Island (which included the NYC kids), 2 buses from Westchester and 1 bus from Livingston, NJ. Dave is very eager to attract more kids from NJ and mentioned this several times to us. Dining Hall - Wah-Nee is NOT nut free as they serve candy with nuts in the canteen. They can accommodate most diets – egg free, dairy free, gluten free/Celiac- but are not kosher and can’t really accommodate a kosher family– for example they serve BLTs. We were in the DH a few minutes before a meal and it was very festive. The waitrons (their name for the kid servers) serenaded us with a song about mac and cheese. Two other camps were visiting for a swim meet, and ate outside the dining hall on picnic tables. Dave mentioned this is an arrangement they have with the other camps for Covid reasons. Everywhere we went there were signs promoting code of conduct, sportsmanship, and kindness. We heard lots of singing and “Wah-Nee Pride” chants. Dave stopped several kids along the way who sang the camp’s praises and raved about the friendships they have made. MISC: Every field is lit for night play. They have pickleball courts, which were in use while we were there. All sports counselors coach all year long. Wah-Nee is a very competitive camp. Not everyone will make a team. Dave said,“couch potatoes would not do well here”. Staff walks around with fake money in their pockets called moose money. It’s a reward system for when they catch kids doing kind or good things. They can collect the money and cash in for rewards – like a special meal cooked by Dave. No golf, horseback riding and no motorboats. The lake looked beautiful and clean, Dave joked that we could drink the lake water. Wah-Nee doesn’t do overnights anymore because “their kids don’t like them”. It was clear to all of us that Wah-Nee is beloved by it’s campers. This is definitely a camp where kids return year after year. Kids were very active, busy and happy.

  
  

### Author: NEE

### Updated: 2025-08-26

title: Cayuga — Emily — 7-7-24
text:
# Cayuga

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

CAMP CAYUGA, Honesdale, PA. Founded in 1950. Toured by Jack Engel, Operations Director 07/01/24, with Emily Rothenberg, Michele Gershwin, Riley McDonough, Michelle Burger and Heather Pasquale. Options are for 2, 4, 6 or 8 weeks. Camp runs later into August. Has a separate teen campus for ages 13-16; they have their own programming but use each other’s facilities.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

There are about 300 campers right now and 150 teens. Second session there will be more. Only 10 American counselors out of 125. Rest International.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Boys and girls’ cabins are separated on different sides of camp. Bunks have bunk beds, cubbies, shower and bathrooms and sinks in bunk. The staff cleans the toilets. Kids do bunk chores. Bunks nice. No towel service.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

There are 2 pools and a lake. Do swim instruction and free swim at the pool. Teen camp has its own pool. Lake does not have motorized activities just kayaking, canoeing, paddle boarding. There is a crossing guard to get over to teen camp where horseback riding and lake are.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

5th grade and above have more of an elective program. Younger kids are more structured and travel with their age group to activities. Teen camp more elective and less structured.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Activities include floor hockey, basketball, gymnastics, swimming, riflery, archery, golf, gaga, creative arts, sports, horseback riding, animal care (have goats, chickens, rabbits), extreme sports jumping (trampoline), photography, 4 wheeler around track, climbing wall, skate park, volleyball, paint ball, drama, ping pong, tether ball, circus. Evening Activities include all kinds of theme nights and games, pool parties, Survivor, talent shows etc. Very strong horseback riding program. No extra cost for horseback. 21 horses. There is a lower riding ring and upper, one more advanced. Do jumping. They do evening trail rides. Weight room for teen camp. Play intercamps mostly against Lake Greeley and Lohikan. No tryouts for intercamps. End of camp includes Camp Olympics, a banquet and prom.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Dining Hall: Junior camp eats first then senior camp. Have salad bars, cereal bars, fruit, yogurt etc. Camp is not gluten free. They are nut aware. There are 8-10 Nurses on staff, 2 Health Centers, separate one for teen camp. Health Center air conditioned. Hopsital/walk in clinic is 10 minutes away.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Not a competitive camp more recreation and well rounded. There is an optional Shabbat service Friday night. Kids are mixed religions at camp. Campers looked busy and happy.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Location: Honesdale, PA. Phone calls 1 time every 2 weeks. Camp posts 500 pictures a day for parents to see. No visiting day. There is canteen every night; there is a canteen fee. Canteen has snacks and merch. 2 weeks cost around $3900 and 4 weeks is $6800.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Overall, Cayuga is a large camp that offers many fun activities. It is more of a recreational camp than a competitive one; campers are there to have fun and try a bit of everything. Most campers attend 2 or 4 weeks. It’s an excellent program for horseback riding and camp is well priced; campers looked busy and happy.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Very strong horseback riding program.
  - No extra cost for horseback.
  - 21 horses; lower and upper riding rings (upper is more advanced); do jumping and evening trail rides.
  - Not a competitive camp more recreation and well rounded.
  - Has a separate teen campus for ages 13-16; they have their own programming but use each other’s facilities.
  - Teen camp more elective and less structured.
  - 2, 4, 6 or 8 week options; camp runs later into August.
  - Camp posts 500 pictures a day for parents to see.
  - 8-10 Nurses on staff; 2 Health Centers (separate one for teen camp); Health Center air conditioned; Hopsital/walk in clinic is 10 minutes away.
  - Nut aware.
  - Camp is well priced.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Only 10 American counselors out of 125. Rest International.
  - Camp is not gluten free.
  - No visiting day.
  - No towel service.
  - Phone calls 1 time every 2 weeks.
  - Lake does not have motorized activities.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Lake Greeley
  - Lohikan

title: Adirondack Woodcraft — Jennifer — 7-31-24
text:
# Adirondack Woodcraft

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Adirondack Woodcraft is a rustic coed camp with focus on wilderness adventure. Woodcraft means the art of living and surviving in the woods. They offer a 3 or 6 week session and intro 2-week for younger campers (ages 7-9). They also have 5-15 day campers from the area depending on the session. Camp is small—during session 1 they only had 30 campers, they had 70 enrolled for session 2; ideal size is 100 campers. Camp is currently 2/3rds male, 1/3 female campers.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Jennifer, Beth, Lisa and Shari toured 7/11/24 with owners/directors Doug and Christina Bartlett who live at the camp year round. Camp is currently 2/3rds male, 1/3 female campers. They also have 5-15 day campers from the area depending on the session. Camp is small—during session 1 they only had 30 campers, they had 70 enrolled for session 2; ideal size is 100 campers. Cabins have 6-7 campers and 1 staff per bunk. On each trip, there is at least 1 counselor who is a wilderness first responder and if there is water, then there will also be a lifeguard.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

Cabins – very rustic cabins with 6-7 campers and 1 staff per bunk. No facilities in the bunk. In the bathroom house we were in, toilets had curtains around them as opposed to doors.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Lake is nonmotorized and has sailing, canoeing, paddleboarding, kayaking. They use canoes a lot to leave camp for trips. No pool. If there is water on trips, then there will also be a lifeguard.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

There are 7 periods in a day, 5 structured and 2 choice plus general swim. Program activities are single sex. Every Wednesday – the campers go out on trips – hiking. Sundays are special programming and with the whole camp.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Outdoor adventure is their focus and they have a big emphasis on safety. Sports – recreational level. Arts – fairly basic. They are offering outdoor cooking. Campers can earn patches in different areas such as riflery, archery. Every age goes on an overnight trip. Younger campers do a 2 night trip. Older campers do a 5 day canoe trip.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Dining Hall – campers eat family style and sit by age group except 1 x week they can choose where to sit. Camp is nut free. Fruit available all day. Instead of paper napkins, they use recycled bandanas as they want to leave no trace behind. They do say a short grace before meals but no other religion. Outdoor adventure is their focus and they have a big emphasis on safety. On each trip, there is at least 1 counselor who is a wilderness first responder and if there is water, then there will also be a lifeguard.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Adirondack Woodcraft is a rustic coed camp with focus on wilderness adventure. Woodcraft means the art of living and surviving in the woods. Instead of paper napkins, they use recycled bandanas as they want to leave no trace behind. Camp very rustic and buggy. Christina and Doug the owners were great.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Unknown

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Adirondack Woodcraft is a rustic coed camp with a focus on outdoor wilderness skills and adventure. Camp very rustic and buggy. Christina and Doug the owners were great. I would recommend this camp for a non Jewish camper looking for a focus outdoor wilderness skills; I would give this camp with some of the companies that offer US hiking/camping trips like Apogee, Adventure Treks, Bold Earth – especially if camper is too young to travel with those companies, this camp could be a good option.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Outdoor adventure is their focus and they have a big emphasis on safety.
  - Christina and Doug the owners were great.
  - Every age goes on an overnight trip.
  - Campers can earn patches in different areas such as riflery, archery.
  - Lake is nonmotorized and has sailing, canoeing, paddleboarding, kayaking.
  - Camp is nut free. Fruit available all day.

  

#### Lowlights

  - Camp very rustic and buggy.
  - Arts – fairly basic.
  - Sports – recreational level.
  - No pool.
  - In the bathroom house we were in, toilets had curtains around them as opposed to doors.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Apogee
  - Adventure Treks
  - Bold Earth

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Woodcraft means the art of living and surviving in the woods. They offer a 3 or 6 week session and intro 2week for younger campers ( ages 7-9). They also have 5-15 day campers from the area depending on the session. Camp is small- we toured during session 1 in which they only had 30 campers, they had 70 enrolled for session 2. Ideal size is 100 campers. Camp is currently 2/3rds male, 1/3 female campers There are 7 periods in a day, 5 structured and 2 choice plus general swim. Program activities are single sex. Every Wednesday – the campers go out on trips – hiking and Sundays are special programming and with the whole camp. Cabins – very rustic cabins with 6-7 campers and 1 staff per bunk. No facilities in the bunk. In the bathroom house we were in, toilets had curtains around them as opposed to doors. Dining Hall – campers eat family style and sit by age group except 1 x week they can choose where to sit. Camp is nut free. Fruit available all day. Instead of paper napkins, they use recycled bandanas as they want to leave no trace behind. They do say a short grace before meals but no other religion. Lake is nonmotorized and has sailing, canoeing, paddleboarding, kayaking. They use canoes a lot to leave camp for trips. No pool. Sports – recreational level, Arts – fairly basic, they are offering outdoor cooking Outdoor adventure is their focus and they have a big emphasis on safety. Campers can earn patches in different areas such as riflery, archery Every age goes on an overnight trip. Younger campers do a 2 night trip. Older campers do a 5 day canoe trip. On each trip, there is at least 1 counselor who is a wilderness first responder and if there is water, then there will also be a lifeguard. Overall Impression: Camp very rustic and buggy. Christina and Doug the owners were great. I would recommend this camp for a non Jewish camper looking for a focus outdoor wilderness skills . I would give this camp with some of the companies that offer US hiking/camping trips like Apogee, Adventure Treks, Bold Earth – especially if camper is too young to travel with those companies, this camp could be a good option.
