title: Green Lane — Natasha — 8-3-22
text:
# Green Lane

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Natasha, Risa, Lana, Allison H and Dara visited Camp Green Lane on Friday 7/29/2022. We were toured by Jay Freedman, part-owner and director. Currently Green Lane offers 4 week (all ages) and 7 week sessions with 98% of the camp staying full. Jay said because of COVID, he plans to keep the 4 week option. For summer 2022, they have 330 campers enrolled – the most they have ever had.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Jay Freedman is part-owner and director. For summer 2022, they have 330 campers enrolled – the most they have ever had. 40% of the camp come from the Philadelphia suburbs. All supervisors are teachers/parents. 98% are Jewish.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

BUNKING – 8-12 per bunk for the younger kids, 14-17 per bunk for the older kids. Bunks are determined by the rising grade – 2 nd -5 th are Inters, 6 th -7 th Cadet/Debs, 8 th -9 th Seniors, 10 th -11 th Greeks. All supervisors are teachers/parents. Bunks are fully unpacked and ready to go when campers arrive. They tend to keep bunks together year after year, but will make changes if necessary. Bunks have bunk beds and everyone gets an ‘under the bed’ drawer. For 2023- Jay is hoping to add a new boys bunk and a new staff bunk.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

LAKE/POOL– small lake- non motorized activities. They have 2 pools. Free swim is every day.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

PROGRAMING – 7 day cycle. They have made-up names for each day –i.e. Funday instead of Monday. This gives them flexibility if it rains. It’s a 6 period day, a combination of bunk activities and electives. Jay mentioned that the heart of camp is the bunk, so they like to keep them together.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

It wasn’t a typical camp day as they were having a camp-wide triathlon. All courts and Gaga are lit for night play. They have a basic weight room that’s available for 14 and up. TRIPS – Golf and ice cream outings, all camp ice skating, minor league baseball games, Dorney Park for the younger kids, Hershey Park for 9 th -11 th , and 15-16yos have a 1 night trip to Lake Placid and day trip to Ocean City, NJ.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

There is a road that runs through the camp and we saw a few non-camp vehicles drive by. There are guards at both ends of the road where camp begins and ends. HEALTH CENTER – Rotating doctors (parents) by week and 5 nurses. Hospital is 12 minutes away. AC- office and health center. DINING HALL – Sit by bunk and the entire camp eats together. The DH can hold 550 people. Food is served family style by the Greeks (10 th /11 th graders). They can accommodate all allergies and are kosher style. When asked how a true kosher kid would eat at camp, Jay said they basically become vegetarian for the summer. A quick motzi is said at every meal. CANTEEN- Regular daily snack is served in the canteen (fruit/yogurt) and the candy type of canteen is every other night.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Campers were competing and counselors were running by their sides supporting and cheering them on. SERVICES – They have services every Friday- 98% are Jewish. There are "90 seconds" of Hebrew and the rest is a camper run themed service, think gratitude or friendship. Camper run services are done by division. During the service a COW (counselor of the week) is picked and its very spirited. It’s a privilege and big tradition. Green Lane is a warm, caring camp. Jay repeatedly referred to camp as a summer family and it felt that way. They even have a special engraved gazebo with all the married couple’s names on it who met at camp. The day we visited the canteen had an entire wall covered in post-it notes with kindness messages written by campers. There was one leashed dog on camp that campers seemed to love.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

40% of the camp come from the Philadelphia suburbs with 3 buses from Plymouth Landing, 1 bus from Cherry Hill, 1 bus from Long Island, 1 bus from Baltimore, and 1 bus from Northern NJ. They also have a chaperoned flight from Florida. PICTURES – 500-700 posted daily. EXTRA COSTS – HB riding at a farm nearby, private tennis and baggage. They have a partnership with Bebe Designs for Color War Packages. GL is not a uniform camp but campers are required to wear them when out on trips.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Green Lane is a warm, caring camp; Jay repeatedly referred to camp as a summer family and it felt that way. Currently Green Lane offers 4 week (all ages) and 7 week sessions with 98% of the camp staying full. Camp doesn’t have bells and whistles, and people aren’t picking GL for their lake, but it’s a traditional, happy place at a great price point. For summer 2022, they have 330 campers enrolled – the most they have ever had.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Warm, caring camp; felt like a summer family
  - Camp-wide triathlon with counselors cheering
  - Offers 4 week (all ages) and 7 week sessions; plans to keep 4 week option
  - Bunks fully unpacked and ready to go when campers arrive
  - All supervisors are teachers/parents
  - 500-700 pictures posted daily
  - All courts and Gaga are lit for night play
  - Basic weight room available for 14 and up
  - Trips: Dorney Park, Hershey Park, Lake Placid overnight, Ocean City, NJ day trip, all camp ice skating, minor league baseball
  - Can accommodate all allergies; kosher style; motzi at every meal

  

#### Lowlights

  - There is a road that runs through camp; we saw a few non-camp vehicles drive by (guards at both ends)
  - Small lake; non-motorized activities
  - True kosher kids basically become vegetarian for the summer
  - AC in office and health center
  - People aren’t picking GL for their lake
  - Not a uniform camp; uniforms required only when out on trips

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Canadensis
  - Pine Forest
  - Nock-A-Mixon

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

CAMP GREEN LANE Natasha, Risa, Lana, Allison H and Dara visited Camp Green Lane on Friday 7/29/2022. We were toured by Jay Freedman, part-owner and director. There is a road that runs through the camp and we saw a few non-camp vehicles drive by. There are guards at both ends of the road where camp begins and ends. It wasn’t a typical camp day as they were having a camp-wide triathlon. Campers were competing and counselors were running by their sides supporting and cheering them on. For summer 2022, they have 330 campers enrolled – the most they have ever had. 40% of the camp come from the Philadelphia suburbs with 3 buses from Plymouth Landing, 1 bus from Cherry Hill, 1 bus from Long Island, 1 bus from Baltimore, and 1 bus from Northern NJ. They also have a chaperoned flight from Florida. There was one leashed dog on camp that campers seemed to love. Currently Green Lane offers 4 week (all ages) and 7 week sessions with 98% of the camp staying full. Jay said because of COVID, he plans to keep the 4 week option. BUNKING – 8-12 per bunk for the younger kids, 14-17 per bunk for the older kids. Bunks are determined by the rising grade – 2 nd -5 th are Inters, 6 th -7 th Cadet/Debs, 8 th -9 th Seniors, 10 th -11 th Greeks. All supervisors are teachers/parents. Bunks are fully unpacked and ready to go when campers arrive. They tend to keep bunks together year after year, but will make changes if necessary. Bunks have bunk beds and everyone gets an ‘under the bed’ drawer. DINING HALL – Sit by bunk and the entire camp eats together. The DH can hold 550 people. Food is served family style by the Greeks (10 th /11 th graders). They can accommodate all allergies and are kosher style. When asked how a true kosher kid would eat at camp, Jay said they basically become vegetarian for the summer. A quick motzi is said at every meal. SERVICES – They have services every Friday- 98% are Jewish. There are “90 seconds” of Hebrew and the rest is a camper run themed service, think gratitude or friendship. Camper run services are done by division. During the service a COW (counselor of the week) is picked and its very spirited. It’s a privilege and big tradition. PROGRAMING – 7 day cycle. They have made-up names for each day –i.e. Funday instead of Monday. This gives them flexibility if it rains. It’s a 6 period day, a combination of bunk activities and electives. Jay mentioned that the heart of camp is the bunk, so they like to keep them together. Free swim is every day. LAKE/POOL– small lake- non motorized activities. They have 2 pools. HEALTH CENTER – Rotating doctors (parents) by week and 5 nurses. Hospital is 12 minutes away. AC- office and health center PICTURES – 500-700 posted daily EXTRA COSTS – HB riding at a farm nearby, private tennis and baggage. They have a partnership with Bebe Designs for Color War Packages. TRIPS – Golf and ice cream outings, all camp ice skating, minor league baseball games, Dorney Park for the younger kids, Hershey Park for 9 th -11 th , and 15-16yos have a 1 night trip to Lake Placid and day trip to Ocean City, NJ. All courts and Gaga are lit for night play. They have a basic weight room that’s available for 14 and up. GL is not a uniform camp but campers are required to wear them when out on trips. CANTEEN- The day we visited the canteen had an entire wall covered in post-it notes with kindness messages written by campers. Regular daily snack is served in the canteen (fruit/yogurt) and the candy type of canteen is every other night. For 2023- Jay is hoping to add a new boys bunk and a new staff bunk. Green Lane is a warm, caring camp. Jay repeatedly referred to camp as a summer family and it felt that way. They even have a special engraved gazebo with all the married couple’s names on it who met at camp. Camp doesn’t have bells and whistles, and people aren’t picking GL for their lake, but it’s a traditional, happy place at a great price point. When families tour Green Lane they are also touring Canadensis, Pine Forest and Nock-A-Mixon.

  
  

### Author: Natasha

title: Idyllwild — Melissa — 7-25-22
text:
# Idyllwild

  

## 1 · Program Snapshot

Idyllwild Arts is located in Idyllwild, CA, nearish to Palm Springs, in the mountains. The setting is incredible. Like you are walking around a gigantic tree house. It is probably a wonderful place for inspiration. During the school year Idyllwild Arts operates as a very selective Arts boarding school (9-12) with about 200 students. Comparable schools would be Interlochen and Walnut Hill. Thanks to the boarding school, the summer program benefits with beginners being able to use state of the art facilities. There is an outdoor amphitheater that is absolutely beautiful and the indoor concert hall is state of the art. The summer programs are geared towards beginners and non beginners. They offer dance, musical theater, writing, music instruments, acting, and more. As of now they only offer programs for one month per summer but they are hoping to add more weeks next summer. In terms of capacity, a full campus is just under 300 campers. This summer they have Session I and Session II, so around 600ish kids total.

  

## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Met with Becky Ackley, a relatively new Director. The staff is a combination of permanent and summer hires. Most programs are between 12-25 kids. Exceptions are the music programs (Jazz and Chamber Festival) which are more like 60-70 kids who make up large ensembles. Musical Theatre is the other larger program with around 30 kids.

  

## 3 · Living Quarters

The kids sleep in the dorms, 3 per dorm room, and there is a private bathroom for each dorm. I didn’t get to see the dorms. As of now there is no AC in the dorms but they are working on it. It usually gets fairly cold at night there in the summer.

  

## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

In their downtime, there is a pool.

  

## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

In their downtime, there is a pool, an outdoor basketball court, a grassy field for disc golf and other games, as well as hiking. Each session ends with performances for the parents and the community is sometimes invited.

  

## 6 · Program Highlights

Thanks to the boarding school, the summer program benefits with beginners being able to use state of the art facilities. There is an outdoor amphitheater that is absolutely beautiful and the indoor concert hall is state of the art. They offer dance, musical theater, writing, music instruments, acting, and more. The visual arts program (drawing/painting) fills out the fastest and usually has a waitlist. Exceptions are the music programs (Jazz and Chamber Festival) which are more like 60-70 kids who make up large ensembles. Each session ends with performances for the parents and the community is sometimes invited.

  

## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

They can take high functioning autistic kids as long as they are in the normal population at their school. COVID vaccination is not required. Food: Unknown. Health/medical services and broader safety protocols: Unknown.

  

## 8 · Vibe and Culture

The camp has a vibe that it’s for a more serious minded kid, even though that kid might be a beginner at whatever the focus of the program is. The setting is incredible. Like you are walking around a gigantic tree house. It is probably a wonderful place for inspiration.

  

## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Idyllwild, CA, nearish to Palm Springs, in the mountains. Each session ends with performances for the parents and the community is sometimes invited. This webpage has a lot of great pictures of the campus, including inside the dorms. Travel logistics, tech policies, and extra costs: Unknown.

  

## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Idyllwild Arts is a co-ed summer arts program in Idyllwild, CA, set in the mountains near Palm Springs with an incredible, inspiring campus. Backed by a very selective 9–12 arts boarding school, beginners and non-beginners use state-of-the-art facilities including a beautiful outdoor amphitheater and a state-of-the-art indoor concert hall. Programs include dance, musical theater, writing, music instruments, acting, and visual arts, with most programs between 12–25 kids and larger ensembles in Jazz and Chamber Festival plus a larger Musical Theatre cohort. Kids sleep in dorms (3 per room with a private bathroom; no AC yet), enjoy a pool and outdoor activities in their downtime, and each session ends with performances for parents.

  

### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Incredible mountain setting; like walking around a gigantic tree house.
  - State of the art facilities; outdoor amphitheater and indoor concert hall.
  - Summer program benefits from the selective arts boarding school (9–12) with about 200 students.
  - Geared towards beginners and non beginners; beginners can use state of the art facilities.
  - Visual arts fills fastest and usually has a waitlist.
  - Large ensembles in Jazz and Chamber Festival (60–70); Musical Theatre around 30.
  - Each session ends with performances for the parents; community is sometimes invited.

  

#### Lowlights

  - As of now there is no AC in the dorms but they are working on it.
  - I didn’t get to see the dorms.
  - As of now they only offer programs for one month per summer (hoping to add more weeks).
  - COVID vaccination is not required.
  - Relatively new Director.

  

#### Similar Camps

  - Interlochen
  - Walnut Hill

  

## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Idyllwild Arts Co-Ed Camp Met with Becky Ackley, a relatively new Director. Idyllwild Arts is located in Idyllwild, CA, nearish to Palm Springs, in the mountains. The setting is incredible. Like you are walking around a gigantic tree house. It is probably a wonderful place for inspiration. During the school year Idyllwild Arts operates as a very selective Arts boarding school (9-12) with about 200 students. Comparable schools would be Interlocken and Walnut Hill. Thanks to the boarding school, the summer program benefits with beginners being able to use state of the art facilities. There is an outdoor amphitheater that is absolutely beautiful and the indoor concert hall is state of the art. The staff is a combination of permanent and summer hires. Each session ends with performances for the parents and the community is sometimes invited. The summer programs are geared towards beginners and non beginners. The visual arts program (drawing/painting) fills out the fastest and usually has a waitlist. They offer dance, musical theater, writing, music instruments, acting, and more. As of now they only offer programs for one month per summer but they are hoping to add more weeks next summer. In terms of capacity, a full campus is just under 300 campers. Most programs are between 12-25 kids. Exceptions are the music programs (Jazz and Chamber Festival) which are more like 60-70 kids who make up large ensembles. Musical Theatre is the other larger program with around 30 kids. This summer they have Session I and Session II, so around 600ish kids total. The kids sleep in the dorms, 3 per dorm room, and there is a private bathroom for each dorm. I didn’t get to see the dorms. As of now there is no AC in the dorms but they are working on it. It usually gets fairly cold at night there in the summer. In their downtime, there is a pool, an outdoor basketball court, a grassy field for disc golf and other games, as well as hiking. They can take high functioning autistic kids as long as they are in the normal population at their school. COVID vaccination is not required. The camp has a vibe that it’s for a more serious minded kid, even though that kid might be a beginner at whatever the focus of the program is. This webpage has a lot of great pictures of the campus, including inside the dorms.

title: Chippewa Ranch — Renee — 9-6-23
text:
# Chippewa Ranch



## 1 · Program Snapshot

CHIPPEWA RANCH CAMP – EAGLE RIVER, WI. 226 campers/each session. 170 stay full summer. 99% stay full summer. Beautiful grounds and waterfront\!



## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Owned & directed by Cliff & Lisa Lissner. Asst Director – Sari Hirsch. 2-3 cabins/grade. Chicago area – 60-70%. Campers also from Ohio, FL, NY, LA, Mexico, AL, TX, Colorado\! Very strict that only 4 campers per city and 2 per school/cabin\!



## 3 · Living Quarters

12 campers/5 counselors per cabin\! Bathrooms and showers in cabins.



## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Beautiful grounds and waterfront\! Chippy Bat Ski Team for advanced skiers\!



## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

5 periods/day – choose activities every 2 weeks. A & B Days – 4 activities each day plus swimming\!



## 6 · Program Highlights

Strong Horseback riding program but becoming less popular\! 11 horses, eastern riding. Campers can also choose to participate in horse care. Amazing ropes course and climbing wall. Offers tripping but not big\! Big CIT and big sister program. Tan & White programming – Stay on team forever. Sports/Arts, etc. Well rounded program.



## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Beautiful Mess Hall, girls eat by cabin, huge salad bar everyday for lunch and dinner. Big cereal/granola and yogurt bar for breakfast\! On Sundays – sleep in and directors cook breakfast to order\!



## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Friday night campfire – value based. Wear Chippewa shirt on Fridays\! Tan & White programming – Stay on team forever.



## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Registration opens on Aug 15 and will be first come first serve. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech: Unknown. Extra costs: Unknown.



## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Chippewa Ranch Camp – Eagle River, WI, is owned & directed by Cliff & Lisa Lissner with Asst Director – Sari Hirsch. 226 campers/each session (170 stay full summer; 99% stay full summer) enjoy beautiful grounds and waterfront and a well rounded program. Highlights include a strong Horseback riding program (11 horses, eastern riding, with horse care), amazing ropes course and climbing wall, Chippy Bat Ski Team for advanced skiers, and big CIT and big sister program; A & B Days mean 5 periods/day with activities chosen every 2 weeks. Traditions like Tan & White programming (stay on team forever) and a Friday night campfire – value based – plus great food (huge salad bar, big cereal/granola and yogurt bar; Sunday sleep-in with directors cooking breakfast to order) round out the experience.



### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Beautiful grounds and waterfront\!
  - Amazing ropes course and climbing wall.
  - Chippy Bat Ski Team for advanced skiers\!
  - Big CIT and big sister program.
  - Tan & White programming – Stay on team forever.
  - Bathrooms and showers in cabins.
  - Beautiful Mess Hall, girls eat by cabin, huge salad bar everyday for lunch and dinner. Big cereal/granola and yogurt bar for breakfast\! On Sundays – sleep in and directors cook breakfast to order\!



#### Lowlights

  - Strong Horseback riding program but becoming less popular\!
  - Offers tripping but not big\!
  - Very strict that only 4 campers per city and 2 per school/cabin\!



#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

title: Junior 76ers — Jennifer — 8-1-24
text:
# Junior 76ers



## 1 · Program Snapshot

They run 3 weeks of overnight camps – 2 for boys (1 week for younger boys/1 week for teens), 1 week of overnight camp for girls plus multiple weeks of day camp. Overnight camp is located at Neumann University, a small school in Aston, PA. Campus is very small so easy for campers to navigate.



## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Jennifer and Beth toured on 7/1/24 with Eli Baylin, who is the site coordinator. They had 49 girls at the overnight camp when we visited and they had about 110 boys in their overnight session. The majority of campers come from PA, NY, NJ but he did mention that have had a few international groups. Each team has a coach and assistant coach assigned to them.



## 3 · Living Quarters

Dorms – students live in Oneall Hall. Nice air conditioned dorm rooms (doubles) which consist of 2 rooms sharing an en suite bathroom. Individual beds, can provide linens and towels for international students.



## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Unknown



## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Programming consists of skills stations, league games. They do their version of a draft the first day to assess skill levels and assign teams. They have a guest speaker Mon- Wed afternoon who is usually a former NBA player or coach - Mark Jackson was the guest speaker while we were there. On Thursday afternoons – they take all the campers down to the 76ers complex. Camp runs from 4pm Sunday to 10 am Friday.



## 6 · Program Highlights

On Thursday afternoons – they take all the campers down to the 76ers complex which he said is the highlight of the week. Each camper does receive a swag bag of 76ers merchandise and they all wear their 76ers jerseys when they tour the complex.



## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

ALL the buildings we were in - gym, dorm, dining room had air conditioning. Food - students eat in air conditioned dining hall. Served cafeteria style. Have separate food area for those with allergies. Students do need to bring some money for snack shack which is outsourced to another company and provides desserts and snacks.



## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Overall impression: Neumann college had nice air conditioned facilities and I think this camp is a good choice for a local – PA, NY, NJ, DE child who loves basketball. I do not think it is going to attract many students from further across the country.



## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

They will pick up at Philly airport. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown. Extra costs: students do need to bring some money for snack shack which is outsourced to another company and provides desserts and snacks.



## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Neumann college had nice air conditioned facilities and I think this camp is a good choice for a local – PA, NY, NJ, DE child who loves basketball. They run 3 weeks of overnight camps – 2 for boys (1 week for younger boys/1 week for teens), 1 week of overnight camp for girls plus multiple weeks of day camp. Camp runs from 4pm Sunday to 10 am Friday. On Thursday afternoons – they take all the campers down to the 76ers complex which he said is the highlight of the week.



### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Campus is very small so easy for campers to navigate.
  - ALL the buildings we were in - gym, dorm, dining room had air conditioning.
  - They will pick up at Philly airport.
  - They have a guest speaker Mon- Wed afternoon who is usually a former NBA player or coach - Mark Jackson was the guest speaker while we were there.
  - On Thursday afternoons – they take all the campers down to the 76ers complex which he said is the highlight of the week.
  - Each camper does receive a swag bag of 76ers merchandise and they all wear their 76ers jerseys when they tour the complex.
  - Each team has a coach and assistant coach assigned to them.
  - The majority of campers come from PA, NY, NJ.



#### Lowlights

  - I do not think it is going to attract many students from further across the country.



#### Similar Camps

  - Academy Camps
  - Berkshire United



## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

JUNIOR 76ers BASKETBALL CAMP Jennifer and Beth toured on 7/1/24 with Eli Baylin, who is the site coordinator They run 3 weeks of overnight camps – 2 for boys( 1 week for younger boys/1 week for teens), 1 week of overnight camp for girls plus multiple weeks of day camp They had 49 girls at the overnight camp when we visited and they had about 110 boys in their overnight session The majority of campers come from PA, NY, NJ but he did mention that have had a few international groups. They will pick up at Philly airport Overnight camp is located at Neumann University, a small school in Aston, PA. Campus is very small so easy for campers to navigate. ALL the buildings we were in - gym, dorm, dining room had air conditioning. Programming consists of skills stations, league games. They do their version of a draft the first day to assess skill levels and assign teams. Each team has a coach and assistant coach assigned to them. They have a guest speaker Mon- Wed afternoon who is usually a former NBA player or coach- Mark Jackson was the guest speaker while we were there On Thursday afternoons – they take all the campers down to the 76ers complex which he said is the highlight of the week. Each camper does receive a swag bag of 76ers merchandise and they all wear their 76ers jerseys when they tour the complex. Dorms – students live in Oneall Hall. Nice air conditioned dorm rooms ( doubles) which consist of 2 rooms sharing an en suite bathroom. Individual beds, can provide linens and towels for international students. Food- students eat in air conditioned dining hall. Served cafeteria style. Have separate food area for those with allergies. Students do need to bring some money for snack shack which is outsourced to another company and provides desserts and snacks. Camp runs from 4pm Sunday to 10 am Friday. Overall impression: Neumann college had nice air conditioned facilities and I think this camp is a good choice for a local – PA, NY, NJ, DE child who loves basketball. I do not think it is going to attract many students from further across the country. I would give it with some of the other 1 week sports camps that offer basketball like Academy Camps, Berkshire United depending on where the family is from.

title: Blue Ridge — Tami — 6-21-24
text:
# Blue Ridge



## 1 · Program Snapshot

Camp Blue Ridge is a co-ed camp located in Clayton, Georgia. Family owned camp; tour on June 20, 2024 was given by Rikki, Director (daughter of owner). When I toured, it was day 4 of camp being in session. Many kids were saying "hi" to Rikki when we were walking around; she is very well liked and the kids seemed happy. They have 50 activities. They seem to really encourage 5 weeks or 7 weeks. They have 270 kids for 2 weeks, 240 for 5 weeks, and 60 stay for 7 weeks. They tend to fill up in February for the summer and registration opens for returning campers in August.



## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Camp Expert- Tami Feldman, Atlanta, Georgia. Tour given by Rikki, Director (daughter of owner- family owned camp); she is very well liked and the kids seemed happy. Most of the kids are from South and Central Florida, some from Georgia and some from the Carolinas. The 5 week program is 75% South Florida. Staff are cabin and activity specialists. They do not go around with their cabin or their age group but the office does the schedules, so they make sure the activities are appropriately matched. They have a leadership program with big/littles and they have service hours and training programs.



## 3 · Living Quarters

The boys are on the opposite side of camp from the girls but activities are coed. The cabins have AC. Two staff do laundry once a week and sheets once a week. They have housekeeping that deep cleans the bathrooms (with bleach) and cabin floors daily. Kids also have to clean their cabin and the cleanest cabin gets to have the meal first. The staff has a hygiene checklist for the kids- brushing teeth, changing clothes, washing hair, etc.



## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

They have waterski with a jetski pulling. Kids are encouraged to do a swimming activity each day. If they do canoeing, they can do a rafting trip every Friday. It is a level 2 rapid, and any age can do it. They have fishing at their lake. At the waterfront, they can choose what they want to do- lawn games, sand volleyball, or go in the water. There is a pool that is very small with water polo. Older kids, rising 8th -11th grades, go to Lake Lanier.



## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

The campers choose all electives and every 3 days they turn in 7-8 choices. They have a 5 period schedule and can change or continue what they enjoy. The first day of camp they go around with their cabin to try everything. After that they make the schedules. The office does the schedules so they make sure the activities are appropriately matched. Every day after dinner there is an evening program.



## 6 · Program Highlights

They have 50 activities. They included wrestling, DJ, waterski with a jetski pulling, pickleball, baseball, soccer, lacrosse, football, tennis, golf range, cheer, volleyball, archery, dance, a weight room for older kids and staff in their off time, high ropes, rockwall, mountain biking, virtual reality (but limited since parents don’t want their kids doing that a lot)…the list goes on\! They have a volleyball league with camper/counselor two on two (one camper and one counselor). This is for a soccer, volleyball and pickleball league. Older kids, rising 8th -11th grades, go to Lake Lanier and have two weekly field trips. One evening they go to Stone Mountain for the Laser Light show, day trips to Helen and the Coke Museum in Atlanta. They also go to the drive in movie theater for a night time activity. They have weekly horseback at The Dillard house which is down the road from camp. If they do canoeing, they can do a rafting trip every Friday (level 2; any age can do it). They have fishing at their lake. At art, they had a variety of activities; they try to get all kids interested and a group of boys were making flying rocket ships and tanks. They have outdoor skills where they have campfire and shelter building and survival skills. They have hiking on campus and one hike can lead to a waterfall that feeds into the lake. Each age group has a camping trip. They have a drama performance every Saturday where a cabin performs for the camp. Theater is really popular even in the two week sessions. This year they have Cinderella first session and High School Musical second session. Kids can do backstage, props, choreography with the dance program, etc. They can work the play but not be "in" it. The music class always does the opening act of the play. Every day after dinner there is an evening program. They have spirited events and the energy of camp is what is fun. They have a big field with games, team all stars, an extended slip n slide, kickball- split by age group, Friday dance parties in the gym. Saturday is all camp relay races, mud runs, obstacle courses. They have a two day mini color war first session and a 4 day color war for the end of the second session. End of summer is prom which is a banquet and the oldest kids choose dinner, they have an all camp awards ceremony, and the oldest kids get to plant a tree anywhere they want on camp. They also get plaques if they have been there for over 5 years. They offer music lessons that is included in the tuition (keyboard, drums, ukulele, and guitar). They offer a cooking and baking class.



## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Fruit is left out all day for kids to grab if they are hungry. The cafeteria is kosher style. They have a canteen after evening snack. Parents put money in their account and the kids can buy something substantial- even a piece of pizza. They do have a regular snack before that which is crackers. The camp does monitor what is being purchased. The cafeteria always offers a salad and pasta bar at lunch and dinner. Breakfast is hot and cold cereal plus a hot entrée. They have dairy free, gluten free and can accommodate all allergies. The chef has been there 8 years. Three RNs live on campus and a Dr is always available on Teledoc. They have urgent care and a hospital across the street. Daily medicine is given in the cafeteria by the nurses; it makes it easily accessible for the kids.



## 8 · Vibe and Culture

When I toured, it was day 4 of camp being in session; many kids were saying "hi" to Rikki and she is very well liked and the kids seemed happy. Activities are coed. They have spirited events and the energy of camp is what is fun. Clean cabin competitions (the cleanest cabin gets to have the meal first). Theater is really popular even in the two week sessions. Leadership program with big/littles and service hours and training programs.



## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

They will have an open house in Atlanta in April and they are working on setting something up in Tampa for this year. They tend to fill up in February for the summer and registration opens for returning campers in August. They have a photographer and videographer who posts each week on a private facebook page for the parents. They have an email service where parents can email the kids; the children can handwrite back and the office scans it by email to the parents. Emails can come 7 days a week and are given to the kids at lunch. They are allowed packages once a week- no food but they encourage camp appropriate things like cabin activities. Virtual reality is offered but limited since parents don’t want their kids doing that a lot. Music lessons are included in the tuition. Costs beyond canteen money are Unknown. Travel logistics are Unknown.



## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Camp Blue Ridge is a co-ed, family owned camp in Clayton, Georgia with 50 activities and a spirited, happy vibe; when I toured on day 4, many kids were saying "hi" to Rikki, the Director (daughter of the owner). Campers choose all electives on a 5 period schedule and every 3 days they turn in 7–8 choices after trying everything the first day, with leagues, waterfront, field trips for older campers, theater every Saturday, and robust evening programs. The cafeteria is kosher style with salad and pasta bars, fruit out all day, allergy accommodations, and three RNs live on campus with a Dr available on Teledoc and urgent care and a hospital across the street. They seem to really encourage 5 weeks or 7 weeks (270 kids for 2 weeks, 240 for 5 weeks, 60 stay for 7 weeks), and overall I feel this camp would be good for a child that wants to enjoy sleepaway camp and have a lot of fun.



### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - 50 activities (wrestling, DJ, waterski with a jetski pulling, pickleball… the list goes on)
  - Family owned camp; Rikki (Director) is very well liked and the kids seemed happy
  - Campers choose all electives; 5 period schedule; every 3 days they turn in 7-8 choices
  - Older kids (rising 8th-11th) go to Lake Lanier and have two weekly field trips (Stone Mountain Laser Light show, Helen, the Coke Museum, drive in movie)
  - Weekly drama performance; theater is really popular; music class does the opening act
  - Kosher style cafeteria; salad/pasta bars; fruit out all day; dairy free, gluten free, allergies accommodated; chef 8 years
  - Three RNs live on campus; Dr on Teledoc; urgent care and a hospital across the street
  - Cabins have AC; daily housekeeping; laundry weekly; hygiene checklist
  - Spirited evening programs (extended slip n slide, kickball, Friday dance parties), color war, end of summer prom and awards



#### Lowlights

  - The pool is very small
  - Virtual reality is limited since parents don’t want their kids doing that a lot
  - They seem to really encourage 5 weeks or 7 weeks
  - Rikki was saying so much more is done 2nd session



#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown



## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Camp Blue Ridge June 20, 2024 Tour given by Rikki, Director (daughter of owner- family owned camp) Camp Expert- Tami Feldman, Atlanta, Georgia Camp Blue Ridge is a co-ed camp located in Clayton, Georgia. When I toured, it was day 4 of camp being in session. Many kids were saying “hi” to Rikki when we were walking around. She is very well liked and the kids seemed happy. They have 50 activities. They included wrestling, DJ, waterski with a jetski pulling, pickleball, baseball, soccer, lacrosse, football, tennis, golf range, cheer, volleyball, archery, dance, a weight room for older kids and staff in their off time, high ropes, rockwall, mountain biking, virtual reality (but limited since parents don’t want their kids doing that a lot)…the list goes on\! Older kids, rising 8 th -11 th grades, go to Lake Lanier and have two weekly field trips. One evening they go to Stone Mountain for the Laser Light show, day trips to Helen and the Coke Museum in Atlanta. They also go to the drive in movie theater for a night time activity. They have a leadership program with big/littles and they have service hours and training programs. The campers choose all electives and every 3 days they turn in 7-8 choices. They have a 5 period schedule and can change or continue what they enjoy. The first day of camp they go around with their cabin to try everything. After that they make the schedules. They have a volleyball league with camper/counselor two on two (one camper and one counselor). This is for a soccer, volleyball and pickleball league. Most of the kids are from South and Central Florida, some from Georgia and some from the Carolinas. The 5 week program is 75% South Florida. Staff are cabin and activity specialists. They do not go around with their cabin or their age group but the office does the schedules, so they make sure the activities are appropriately matched. Kids are encouraged to do a swimming activity each day. They have weekly horseback at The Dillard house which is down the road from camp. If they do canoeing, they can do a rafting trip every Friday. It is a level 2 rapid, and any age can do it. All activities are for any age at camp. They have fishing at their lake. At the waterfront, they can choose what they want to do- lawn games, sand volleyball, or go in the water. There is a pool that is very small with water polo. At art, they had a variety of activities. They try to get all kids interested and a group of boys were making flying rocket ships and tanks. They have outdoor skills where they have campfire and shelter building and survival skills. They have hiking on campus and one hike can lead to a waterfall that feeds into the lake. Each age group has a camping trip. Fruit is left out all day for kids to grab if they are hungry. The cafeteria is kosher style. They have a canteen after evening snack. Parents put money in their account and the kids can buy something substantial- even a piece of pizza. They do have a regular snack before that which is crackers. The camp does monitor what is being purchased. They cafeteria always offers a salad and pasta bar at lunch and dinner. Breakfast is hot and cold cereal plus a hot entrée. They have dairy free, gulten free and can accommodate all allergies. The chef has been there 8 years. They offer a cooking and baking class. They have music lessons that is included in the tuition. They offer keyboard, drums, ukulele, and guitar. Three RNs live in campus and a Dr is always available on Teledoc. They have urgent care and a hospital across the street. Daily medicine is given in the cafeteria by the nurses. It makes it easily accessible for the kids. They have a photographer and videographer who posts each week on a private facebook page for the parents. They have an email service where parents can email the kids, the children can handwrite back and the office scans it by email to the parents. Emails can come 7 days a week and are given to the kids at lunch. They are allowed packages once a week- no food but they encourage camp appropriate things like cabin activities. The boys are on the opposite side of camp from the girls but activities are coed. They have a drama performance every Saturday where a cabin performs for the camp. Theater is really popular even in the two week sessions. This year they have Cinderella first session and High School Musical second session. Kids can do backstage, props, choreography with the dance program, etc. They can work the play but not be “in” it. The music class always does the opening act of the play. Every day after dinner there is an evening program. They have spirited events and the energy of camp is what is fun. They have a big field with games, team all stars, an extended slip n slide, kickball- split by age group, Friday dance parties in the gym. Saturday is all camp relay races, mud runs, obstacle courses. They have a two day mini color war first session and a 4 day color war for the end of the second session. End of summer is prom which is a banquet and the oldest kids choose dinner, they have an all camp awards ceremony, and the oldest kids get to plant a tree anywhere they want on camp. They also get plaques if they have been there for over 5 years. Two staff do laundry once a week and sheets once a week. They have housekeeping that deep cleans the bathrooms (with bleach) and cabin floors daily. Kids also have to clean their cabin and the cleanest cabin gets to have the meal first. The staff has a hygiene checklist for the kids- brushing teeth, changing clothes, washing hair, etc. The cabins have AC. They seem to really encourage 5 weeks or 7 weeks. Rikki was saying so much more is done 2 nd session. They have 270 kids for 2 weeks, 240 for 5 weeks, and 60 stay for 7 weeks. They will have an open house in Atlanta in April and they are working on setting something up in Tampa for this year. They tend to fill up in February for the summer and registration opens for returning campers in August. Overall, I feel this camp would be good for a child that wants to enjoy sleepaway camp and have a lot of fun.




### Author: Tami

title: Bridgton Sports — Liz — 7-18-23
text:
# Bridgton Sports



## 1 · Program Snapshot

Bridgton, Maine. Camp is located on the campus of Bridgton Academy, a post graduate one year boys boarding school. Uses the entire 55 acre facilities including dorms, ice hockey rink, indoor basketball court, tennis courts, many athletic fields for baseball, soccer, lacrosse, flag football etc. 100/150 (max campers) per session. 2 3 week sessions offered. Toured on 7/5/23 by myself (Liz Hochman Mak, Michele Gershwin and Emily Rothenberg).



## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

2nd - 10th grade (finishing grades) - 6th - 9th grades are usually the most popular for enrollment. 60% Jewish/40% non Jewish so a good mix. Met with Brian Kooperman (Director) - Started camp 13 years ago.



## 3 · Living Quarters

Campers live in dorm style living with 2-3 campers per room and bathrooms/showers on floors. Counselors live in dorms with the campers.



## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

No pool, across the street there is a lake where kids can do water sports, activities and swim.



## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Campers have one major (soccer, ice hockey, lacrosse, baseball, basketball) and minors such as tennis, golf, fishing, weight room, arts and crafts, lake activities, archery, or one of the major sports listed above etc. At night are evening activities and after dinner is BSL (non traditional sports/fun activities). Casino nights.



## 6 · Program Highlights

There is a full ice hockey indoor arena which was very nice, a great indoor basketball court and nice large fields. Uses the entire 55 acre facilities including dorms, ice hockey rink, indoor basketball court, tennis courts, many athletic fields for baseball, soccer, lacrosse, flag football etc. 3 times during summer/session there are socials with girls camp Kent’s Hill (Brian’s brother is the director). Also, skating socials with Camp Mataponi.



## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Unknown



## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Campus felt a bit small because housed on a smaller private school. 60% Jewish/40% non Jewish so a good mix.



## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Buses from NJ/CT/Boston area. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown. Extra costs: Unknown.



## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Bridgton Sports Camp in Bridgton, Maine is located on the campus of Bridgton Academy and uses the entire 55 acre facilities including dorms, an ice hockey rink, an indoor basketball court, tennis courts, and many athletic fields. Campers have one major (soccer, ice hockey, lacrosse, baseball, basketball) and minors such as tennis, golf, fishing, weight room, arts and crafts, lake activities, archery, or one of the major sports listed above, with evening activities and BSL (non traditional sports/fun activities) and Casino nights. Two 3 week sessions are offered with 100/150 (max campers) per session for 2nd - 10th graders (6th - 9th are usually the most popular), and the community is 60% Jewish/40% non Jewish so a good mix. No pool (lake across the street) and the campus felt a bit small because housed on a smaller private school, but there are socials with girls camp Kent’s Hill and skating socials with Camp Mataponi.



### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - There is a full ice hockey indoor arena which was very nice.
  - Great indoor basketball court.
  - Nice large fields.
  - Uses the entire 55 acre facilities.
  - Campers have one major and minors such as tennis, golf, fishing, weight room, arts and crafts, lake activities, archery.
  - Evening activities and after dinner is BSL (non traditional sports/fun activities).
  - Casino nights.
  - 3 times during summer/session there are socials with girls camp Kent’s Hill.
  - Skating socials with Camp Mataponi.
  - Buses from NJ/CT/Boston area.



#### Lowlights

  - Campus felt a bit small because housed on a smaller private school.
  - No pool.



#### Similar Camps

  - Academy Camp
  - Arrow Wood
  - IMG
  - strong sports session camps



## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Bridgton Sports Camp Bridgton, Maine Toured on 7/5/23 by myself (Liz Hochman Mak, Michele Gershwin and Emily Rothenberg)- Met with Brian Kooperman (Director)-Started camp 13 years ago. -100/150 (max campers) per session -2 3 week sessions offered -2 nd -10 th grade (finishing grades)- 6 th -9 th grades are usually the most popular for enrollment -60% Jewish/40% non Jewish so a good mix. - Buses from NJ/CT/Boston area -Camp is located on the campus of Bridgton Academy, a post graduate one year boys boarding school. Uses the entire 55 acre facilities including dorms, ice hockey rink, indoor basketball court, tennis courts, many athletic fields for baseball, soccer, lacrosse, flag football etc. - Campers live in dorm style living with 2-3 campers per room and bathrooms/showers on floors. Counselors live in dorms with the campers. - Campers have one major (soccer, ice hockey, lacrosse, baseball, basketball) and minors such as tennis, golf, fishing, weight room, arts and crafts, lake activities, archery, or one of the major sports listed above etc. - At night are evening activities and after dinner is BSL (non traditional sports/fun activities). Casino nights. -3 times during summer/session there are socials with girls camp Kent’s Hill (Brian’s brother is the director). Also, skating socials with Camp Mataponi - There is a full ice hockey indoor arena which was very nice, a great indoor basketball court and nice large fields. Campus felt a bit small because housed on a smaller private school. -No pool, across the street there is a lake where kids can do water sports, activities and swim. Would give this camp with Academy Camp, Arrow Wood, IMG, or strong sports session camps.

title: Camp Zeke — Lana — 10-11-23
text:
# Camp Zeke



## 1 · Program Snapshot

Celebrates camp thru cooking sports fitness and arts. Elective programming. Huge culinary arts program. 6 weeks is full summer. 40% stay for 6 weeks. 250 campers/session. 2nd session a little more popular than 1st. Based in Rye, Westchester NY.



## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Isaac Mamaysky presented. He owns and started camp in 2012 with wife Lisa. Based in Rye, Westchester NY. Rachel could not join as she is in Israel. Campers in tri state 65-70%-Westchester NJ NYC CT. Also big from DC area and Florida flight. 250 campers/session. 2nd session a little more popular than 1st. 6 weeks is full summer. 40% stay for 6 weeks.



## 3 · Living Quarters

Bunks have ac and heat. Private bathrooms.



## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Private lake including motor sports. And have heated pool.



## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Elective programming. Programming includes sports and individual sports -krav maga martial arts, strength program at fitness center, gymnastics, yoga dance, circus arts-will do a performance at end of session. Added horseback riding a few years ago. Horses are less than a mile away at a riding facility at a riding school. 25 campers/session participate and there is extra fee. Music lessons, lots of art options. Theater-2 musicals-one at end of each session. Have traditional sports but not competitive. Can play in Wayne county tournaments. Huge culinary arts program. 3 full kitchens and produce cookbooks at end of every summer.



## 6 · Program Highlights

Celebrates camp thru cooking sports fitness and arts. Huge culinary arts program. 3 full kitchens and produce cookbooks at end of every summer. Private lake including motor sports. And have heated pool. Theater-2 musicals-one at end of each session. Circus arts-will do a performance at end of session. 2024 installing full amphitheater on lake and a ropes course.



## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Kosher supervised kitchen. Majority of campers don’t know kitchen is kosher.



## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Celebrates camp thru cooking sports fitness and arts. Have traditional sports but not competitive. Friday night Shabbat at the lake. No Hebrew at the service bc want to be inclusive to everyone including staff. Presented us with the same presentation they use for families.



## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Most families do not go on a group flight and book their own. Also big from DC area and Florida flight. Horseback riding has an extra fee. Parent communication: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown. Other extra costs: Unknown.



## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Camp Zeke celebrates camp thru cooking sports fitness and arts with elective programming and a huge culinary arts program. 6 weeks is full summer; 40% stay for 6 weeks, with 250 campers/session and the 2nd session a little more popular than 1st. Private lake including motor sports and a heated pool, and bunks have ac and heat with private bathrooms. Kosher supervised kitchen, Friday night Shabbat at the lake, and no Hebrew at the service bc want to be inclusive to everyone including staff.



### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Elective programming.
  - Huge culinary arts program. 3 full kitchens and produce cookbooks at end of every summer.
  - Private lake including motor sports. And have heated pool.
  - Bunks have ac and heat. Private bathrooms.
  - Kosher supervised kitchen. Majority of campers don’t know kitchen is kosher.
  - Friday night Shabbat at the lake. No Hebrew at the service bc want to be inclusive to everyone including staff.
  - 2024 installing full amphitheater on lake and a ropes course.
  - Added horseback riding a few years ago.



#### Lowlights

  - Have traditional sports but not competitive.
  - 25 campers/session participate and there is extra fee.
  - Most families do not go on a group flight and book their own.



#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown



## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Isaac Mamaysky presented. He owns and started camp in 2012 with wife Lisa. Based in Rye, Westchester NY. Rachel could not join as she is in Israel. 6 weeks is full summer. 40% stay for 6 weeks. 250 campers/session. 2nd session a little more popular than 1st. Campers in tri state 65-70%-Westchester NJ NYC CT. Also big from DC area and Florida flight. Most families do not go on a group flight and book their own. Presented us with the same presentation they use for families. Celebrates camp thru cooking sports fitness and arts. Elective programming Huge culinary arts program. 3 full kitchens and produce cookbooks at end of every summer. Programming includes sports and individual sports -krav maga martial arts, strength program at fitness center, gymnastics, yoga dance, circus arts-will do a performance at end of session. Added horseback riding a few years ago. Horses are less than a mile away at a riding facility at a riding school. 25 campers/session participate and there is extra fee. Music lessons, lots of art options. Theater-2 musicals-one at end of each session. Have traditional sports but not competitive. Can play in Wayne county tournaments. Private lake including motor sports. And have heated pool. Bunks have ac and heat. Private bathrooms. Kosher supervised kitchen. Majority of campers don’t know kitchen is kosher. Friday night Shabbat at the lake. No Hebrew at the service bc want to be inclusive to everyone including staff. 2024 installing full amphitheater on lake and a ropes course.

title: Danbee — Beth — 10-16-22
text:
# Danbee



## 1 · Program Snapshot

Danbee tour 7-5-22 Beth, Lisa, and Shari. Danbee Write up Toured 7/11/2022 by Lauren Heydt (and Annie Copp). 380 girls (230 stay 7 weeks). It was "league day" (which is like a mini color war) so we came into cheering and lots of fun energy! The girls were decked in their red and green and playing sports and cheering each other on.



## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

We happened to see Jay and Debbie in the parking lot, they were very nice but we toured with Lauren and the new Owner, Annie. She is adorable and has a young, nice husband (Zach) and they are due with a baby in October. They came from Pocono springs and are now running Danbee for the first summer. Annie is originally from Canada, very sweet. Lauren really knows all the campers and they love to talk to her. 20% international counselors, rest are Americans, many returning campers. Big sister is actually a camp "family" with 4-5 girls of mixed ages assigned 2nd night of camp.



## 3 · Living Quarters

Modern Cabins with facilities inside. OD mostly outside. A/C in the health center and welcome center only. There was a work out room, really nice which may have had A/C too because it was cool in there? Spin bikes and tv’s for exercise/workouts. Lots of nice, shady areas. Amazing field house near the fields, nature classes in the woods and ZERO bugs! 2023+ capital expenditures will focus on cabins.



## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Really nice lake/waterfront with 4-5 boats, SUP, pontoons. A lot of girls were down there and all in the water. Also, 2 heated pools.



## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Programming: When they are younger (until 4th grade) the camp chooses their schedule, once they are in the "middle section" they get a little more choice and as they get older, more electives. The last year they get more privileges and have a cool area they live in. 6 period day: 5 periods plus 1 daily choice for fun & fitness. Finish 2nd grade – the 1 daily choice above. Finish 3rd & 4th – 2 daily electives. Finish 5th – 3 electives. Finish 6th + – fully elective. Academies (extra charge): gymnastics, dance, tennis, soccer. They do offer private lessons but they are reasonable, $45/hour and parents can sign up ahead of time.



## 6 · Program Highlights

It was "league day" (which is like a mini color war) with cheering and lots of fun energy; girls were decked in red and green and playing sports and cheering each other on. Summer long color competition (Monday leagues). Big sister is actually a camp "family" with 4-5 girls of mixed ages assigned 2nd night of camp. They do some night activities with Winadu (they had a dinner with them the night before) and will see brothers once a week at least. And mah-kee-nac too, they get together 1-2x/week. They just had a show - Mary Poppins - and they have a nice theater/social hall. They do an "acres club" which is their community service and it’s very important to weave this into the program. There was a work out room, really nice... Spin bikes and tv’s for exercise/workouts. She said the older girls really like this newer addition.



## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

She said they have not had any cases of covid so far but we’re only a week and a half in. Everyone in staff shirts, very identifiable - everyone else in uniform. A/C in the health center and welcome center only. Health Center on site. Food: Unknown.



## 8 · Vibe and Culture

It was "league day" so we came into cheering and lots of fun energy. The girls seemed down to earth and sweet, and happy. Everyone in staff shirts, very identifiable - everyone else in uniform. But the older girls only wear uniforms half the time, they get a few days a week to pick what they want. Uniform until finishing 4th grade. Finishing 5th & 6th wear on weekend. Finishing 7th wear Danbee swag because love Danbee but no requirement. She said at first they get dressed up once and after a while they wear sweatpants to socials and that it's not the kind of camp where kids can bring blow dryers and straightening irons. Fri night optional service (more about good deeds than Hebrew). 30-60 attend church van on Sundays. Overall, we were very impressed with the staff, the kids and the energy of the camp. Very fun! And buttoned-up and the new Owners very nice.



## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Bus transportation: 2 buses from NYC (Museum of Natural History); 2 buses from Westchester, NY (Bloomingdale’s in White Plains); 1 NJ bus (Montvale); 1 NJ bus (Livingston Mall); 1 Philadelphia bus (Plymouth Meeting, PA); 1 Long Island, NY bus; 1 MA bus (Framingham, MA). Chaperoned FL flight with 30-40 campers. 1 Facetime per session. No packages. There will be no visiting day this year. Academies (extra charge): gymnastics, dance, tennis, soccer. Private lessons $45/hour (parents can sign up ahead of time). Tech/care items: not the kind of camp where kids can bring blow dryers and straightening irons.



## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

380 girls (230 stay 7 weeks) and a very fun, buttoned-up feel. It was "league day" (like a mini color war) when we toured, with cheering, red/green spirit, and lots of fun energy. The new Owner Annie (with husband Zach), formerly at Pocono springs, is running Danbee for the first summer and Lauren really knows all the campers. Overall, we were very impressed with the staff, the kids and the energy of the camp.



### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Really nice lake/waterfront with 4-5 boats, SUP, pontoons, plus 2 heated pools.
  - Amazing field house near the fields; nature classes in the woods and ZERO bugs!
  - Workout room with spin bikes and TVs; older girls really like this newer addition.
  - Summer long color competition (Monday leagues) and strong big-sister family culture.
  - Strong transportation network including multiple buses and a chaperoned FL flight.
  - Academies available (gymnastics, dance, tennis, soccer) and reasonable private lessons ($45/hour).
  - New ownership (Annie and Zach) brings fresh energy; Lauren is deeply connected to campers.



#### Lowlights

  - A/C in the health center and welcome center only.
  - No visiting day this year.
  - No packages.
  - Uniform until finishing 4th grade; older girls have partial-uniform days.
  - OD mostly outside.



#### Similar Camps

  - Winadu
  - Mah-Kee-Nac
  - Pocono Springs



## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Danbee tour 7-5-22 Beth, Lisa, and Shari Michele Gershwin shared her 2022 write up below. Additional remarks are in red Danbee Write up Toured 7/11/2022 by Lauren Heydt (and Annie Copp ) 380 girls (230 stay 7 weeks) Bus transportation 2 buses from NYC (Museum of Natural History) 2 buses from Westchester, NY (Bloomingdale’s in White Plains) 1 NJ bus (Montvale) 1 NJ bus (Livingston Mall) 1 Philadelphia bus (Plymouth Meeting, PA) 1 Long Island, NY bus 1 MA bus (Framingham, MA) Chaperoned FL flight with 30-40 campers 1 Facetime per session No packages We happened to see Jay and Debbie in the parking lot, they were very nice but we toured with Lauren and the new Owner, Annie. She is adorable and has a young, nice husband ( Zach ) and they are due with a baby in October. They came from Pocono springs and are now running Danbee for the first summer. Annie is originally from Canada, very sweet. Lauren really knows all the campers and they love to talk to her. It was "league day" (which is like a mini color war) so we came into cheering and lots of fun energy! Girls were decked in their red and green and playing sports and cheering each other on, lots of fun. She said they have not had any cases of covid so far but we’re only a week and a half in. And they are planning some trips. Everyone in staff shirts, very identifiable - everyone else in uniform. But the older girls only wear uniforms half the time, they get a few days a week to pick what they want but we didn’t see any out of uniform that day because of the red/green. Uniform until finishing 4 th grade Finishing 5 th & 6 th wear on weekend (a show for tours?) Finishing 7 th wear Danbee swag because love Danbee but no requirement Modern Cabins with facilities inside. OD mostly outside Programming: When they are younger (unto 4th grade) the camp chooses their schedule, once they are in the “middle section” they get a little more choice and as they get older, more electives. The last year they get more privileges and have a cool area they live in. 6 period day 5 periods plus 1 daily choice for fun & fitness Finish 2 nd grade – the 1 daily choice above Finish 3 rd & 4 th – 2 daily electives Finish 5 th – 3 electives Finish 6 th + - fully elective Summer long color competition (Monday leagues) Big sister is actually a camp “family” with 4-5 girls of mixed ages assigned 2 nd night of camp Academies (extra charge): gymnastics, dance, tennis, soccer They do some night activities with Winadu (they had a dinner with them the night before) and will see brothers once a week at least. And mah-kee-nac too, they get together 1-2x/week. She said at first they get dressed up once and after a while they wear sweatpants to socials and that its not the kind of camp where kids can bring blow dryers and straightening irons. The girls seemed down to earth and sweet, and happy. They just had a show - Mary Poppins - and they have a nice theater/social hall. There will be no visiting day this year. Fri night optional service (more about good deeds than Hebrew) 30-60 attend church van on Sundays. 20% international counselors, rest are Americans, many returning campers. Amazing field house near the fields, nature classes in the woods and ZERO bugs! Lots of nice, shady areas. A/C in the health center and welcome center only. There was a work out room, really nice which may have had A/C too because it was cool in there? Spin bikes and tv’s for exercise/workouts. She said the older girls really like this newer addition. Really nice lake/waterfront with 4-5 boats, SUP, pontoons. A lot of girls were down there and all in the water. Also, 2 heated pools They do an “acres club” which is their community service and it’s very important to weave this into the program. They do offer private lessons but they are reasonable, $45/hour and parents can sign up ahead of time. Overall, we were very impressed with the staff, the kids and the energy of the camp. Very fun! And buttoned-up and the new Owners very nice. 2023+ capital expenditures will focus on cabins Michele Gershwin

title: Akeela — Lisa — 8-13-24
text:
# Akeela



## 1 · Program Snapshot

2 separate 22-day sessions; about a dozen do both sessions but go home in between. They have 117 campers in each session. Finishing 3rd - age 16. 50/50 girls/boys. This is an LD camp that can be given with our other LD camps.



## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Lisa, Shari and Laurie were toured by Debbie Sasson (their 17th summer). They will take: ADD, ADHD, Autism spectrum…. neurodivergent. But not ODD, no behavioral issues; they are NOT therapeutic. Their counselors need to have one year of college, they prefer older special education and speech/language majors… and psych majors also. They have a 73% rate of return for campers, they try to keep the bunks the same each summer. They get new campers of all ages.



## 3 · Living Quarters

Cabins have 8 campers and 4 counselors (2 specialty, 2 regular counselors). All facilities are in the bunks: 2 each showers, sinks, toilets.



## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Swimming instruction takes place in the heated pool and in the lake 4/week all ages. No motorized water activities but Fishing and boating. One aqua park element.



## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

5 periods/day: 2 structured in the morning, 3 in the pm (1 cabin activity and 2 specialty activities which are weekly choice). Travel by bunk but all periods are mixed age and gender with weekly specialty events and a carnival on the last day of the session.



## 6 · Program Highlights

A&C is basic, ceramics has 2 wheels. Cooking is kept simple. Woodworking has an emphasis on safety with goggles and sanding with some small saws. Light STEM in the art barn, rocketry. The theater is open-sided, with an amphitheater on the other side. Dance is hip hop in The Lodge (all-purpose bldg.). Yoga is also offered. They have one field for soccer, and softball. Tennis and pickleball is offered as well as Gaga and a Golf driving range. There is an arena for floor hockey. No horses. Akeela has goats and chickens and a garden where the campers can choose to help out. They have High ropes, low ropes, zip line, archery and Climbing wall. They built a campsite out of sight and every bunk goes. Finishing 9th/10th grade they have a beach day and go to Burlington VT. BEYOND AKEELA takes place at Curry College where they learn life skills and visit some colleges.



## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Dining Hall is nice, it’s social time for the campers. They sit by bunk. They have PB. One NP and one RN with a pediatrician in town. These kids need to stay on their meds. No religion.



## 8 · Vibe and Culture

These kids HATE sports.



## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Buses from Tarrytown NY and Lexington MA. A handful of FL campers get picked up at Logan airport. Visiting day is pick up day for each session. One phone call home, one way email. They can bring Kindles, and these kids are avid readers. NO packages, photos are posted daily.



## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Akeela runs 2 separate 22-day sessions with 117 campers in each session. Campers are finishing 3rd - age 16, 50/50 girls/boys, and they will take: ADD, ADHD, Autism spectrum… neurodivergent, but not ODD, no behavioral issues; they are NOT therapeutic. The program has 5 periods/day with 2 structured in the morning and 3 in the pm (1 cabin activity and 2 weekly-choice specialty activities), plus weekly specialty events and a carnival on the last day. Swimming instruction takes place in the heated pool and in the lake 4/week; A&C is basic (ceramics has 2 wheels); they have High ropes, low ropes, zip line, archery and Climbing wall; BEYOND AKEELA takes place at Curry College where they learn life skills and visit some colleges.



### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - 2 separate 22-day sessions; about a dozen do both sessions but go home in between.
  - 117 campers in each session; finishing 3rd - age 16; 50/50 girls/boys.
  - They will take: ADD, ADHD, Autism spectrum… neurodivergent. But not ODD, no behavioral issues; they are NOT therapeutic.
  - Cabins have 8 campers and 4 counselors (2 specialty, 2 regular counselors); all facilities are in the bunks (2 each showers, sinks, toilets).
  - 5 periods/day with weekly choice; all periods are mixed age and gender.
  - Swimming instruction 4/week in heated pool and lake; Fishing and boating; one aqua park element.
  - High ropes, low ropes, zip line, archery, Climbing wall; goats and chickens and a garden.
  - 73% rate of return; they try to keep the bunks the same each summer.
  - Buses from Tarrytown NY and Lexington MA; FL campers picked up at Logan airport.
  - One NP and one RN with a pediatrician in town; these kids need to stay on their meds.



#### Lowlights

  - These kids HATE sports.
  - No motorized water activities.
  - No horses.
  - One phone call home, one way email; NO packages.
  - They have PB.
  - NOT therapeutic; will not take ODD or behavioral issues.



#### Similar Camps

  - Summit LD

But not ODD, no behavioral issues, they are NOT therapeutic. Buses from Tarrytown NY and Lexington MA. A handful of FL campers get picked up at Logan airport. Their counselors need to have one year of college, they prefer older special education and speech/language majors…and psych majors also.
