title: Putney Programs — Carrie and Fiona — 7-4-23
text:
# Putney Programs



## 1 · Program Snapshot

Met with Becca Shumlin who is the granddaughter of the founder of Putney Programs (1951). She will be taking over from her father and uncle with her sister eventually. Putney Programs at Oxford is the largest of Putney’s Academia Programs. The others are Paris and Sienna in Europe. They run two week sessions in the summer. July 1 to 14 and July 15 to July 28. Cost is 6990 + Airfare. This session has 180 students. Second session 170 students.



## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

For High school onwards but most popular age is 16. 60% girls. Mixture of nationalities but high percentage of Americans. Attracts motivated switched on students who have to provide a personal statement and two teacher reports. 20 residential pastoral staff mixture of British and American average age 24. 31 Academic Instructors all connected to Oxford. Biggest class size is 14; Average is 8. Met with Tom Kane the Program Director. Becca Shumlin will be taking over from her father and uncle with her sister eventually.



## 3 · Living Quarters

Divided between two colleges at Oxford: Worcester and University. Stunning locations. Students reside in the college where their major takes place. Mostly single en suite rooms and some doubles. Rooms cleaned daily. Laundry done by the students.



## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Unknown



## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Afternoons are for minors and a variety of evening events. Most popular majors are Sport Business Criminology and Architecture.



## 6 · Program Highlights

Stunning locations. Biggest class size is 14; Average is 8. 31 Academic Instructors all connected to Oxford. TOP CLASS PROGRAMME. HIGHLY RECOMMEND.



## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Zero tolerance on Alcohol and drugs. Breakfast and dinner included. Students eat together. Lunch extra.



## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Putney is the most liberal of the Putney Programmes. Students are allowed to go into Oxford. Attracts motivated switched on students. Students kept very busy and stimulated.



## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Cost is 6990 + Airfare. Lunch extra. Travel logistics, parent communication, and tech: Unknown.



## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Putney Programs at Oxford is the largest of Putney’s Academia Programs. They run two week sessions in the summer (July 1 to 14 and July 15 to July 28) with 180 students in the first session and 170 in the second. Attracts motivated switched on students, with small classes (biggest 14; average 8), stunning locations, and mostly single en suite rooms. TOP CLASS PROGRAMME. HIGHLY RECOMMEND.



### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - 20 residential pastoral staff mixture of British and American average age 24.
  - Students kept very busy and stimulated.
  - TOP CLASS PROGRAMME. HIGHLY RECOMMEND.



#### Lowlights

  - Cost is 6990 + Airfare.
  - Lunch extra.
  - Laundry done by the students.
  - Waterfront & aquatics: Unknown.
  - Parent communication and tech: Unknown.



#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

title: Road Less Traveled — Renee — 2-8-24
text:
# Road Less Traveled



## 1 · Program Snapshot

Middle School (6th-8th grades) and HS (9-12 grades) programs. Programs include: Wilderness Adv, Outdoor Activities, Service and Cultural immersion. Trips are currently ¾ full. No previous experience necessary.



## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Olly Cayless – Director of Camper Recruitment – presenter. Andy Shlensky – Owner. 12-14 students/trip with 3 leaders; most trips are split evenly boys/girls or 60/40. Most students travel solo.



## 3 · Living Quarters

Accommodations: Varies by trip from camping, hotels, hostels, homestays. Trips with no camping: FL, Bali, Thailand, Vietnam, Spain (1/2 camping), Galapagos. Website states accommodations on each trip's specific page.



## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Unknown



## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Unknown



## 6 · Program Highlights

Very bonding experience for the campers. No previous experience necessary. Programs include: Wilderness Adv, Outdoor Activities, Service and Cultural immersion.



## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Unknown



## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Very bonding experience for the campers.



## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Tech free – can bring phone for travel and will allow campers to call home ½ way through program. TRAVEL: Leader will meet campers at the gate or baggage claim – will be discussed with family prior to travel day. Campers can fly solo or join an escorted flight. Campers travel by private van throughout the trip.



## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

The Road Less Traveled offers Middle School (6th-8th) and HS (9-12) programs that include Wilderness Adv, Outdoor Activities, Service, and Cultural immersion. Groups are 12-14 students per trip with 3 leaders, and most trips are split evenly boys/girls or 60/40. It is tech free (phones for travel only, with a call home halfway), and leaders meet campers at the gate or baggage claim; campers travel by private van and can fly solo or join an escorted flight. Accommodations vary by trip—camping, hotels, hostels, homestays—with several trips that include no camping (FL, Bali, Thailand, Vietnam, Spain is 1/2 camping, Galapagos); no previous experience necessary.



### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - No previous experience necessary.
  - 12-14 students/trip with 3 leaders.
  - Most trips are split evenly boys/girls or 60/40.

  - Trips with no camping: FL, Bali, Thailand, Vietnam, Spain (1/2 camping), Galapagos.

title: Independent Lake — Michelle — 7-9-24
text:
# Independent Lake



## 1 · Program Snapshot

Independent Lake Tour given by Jeff Gould 7/8. Toured with Risa Goldberg, Natasha Kreizman, Lana Ast, Riley McDonough. 600 kids at camp, camp has tripled in size in ten years. Full choice camp. Trolley systems that take kids around from activity to activity.



## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Biggest zip code in camp is Montclair, NJ. Kids come from all over - NJ, Westchester, NYC, Brooklyn, Florida, DC area, 40 California kids. 20% are international. There is a head international counselor assigned to be there for the international kids. Many children of famous entertainment celebrities and politicians. Notably children of: Lauryn Hill, Matt Stone, Meryl Streep, Victor Cruz, Samantha Powers, Harrison Ford, Claire Danes. Many 12/13 yr olds come as first time campers who have left more traditional camps and programs. Department heads have masters degrees in their craft.



## 3 · Living Quarters

Bunks have all been recently re done. No AC. The sessions you’re signed up dictates bunking to reduce coming and going in the bunk. Eat with bunk for bunk bonding. Lit road leading up to elk view (youngest campers bunks).



## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Heated Pool and lake (lake shared with westmont). No instructional swim. No one in lake without a life vest. Lake activities (waterski, wakeboard, power boats, sailboats, paddle boats, kayaks, inflatables). Fishing; Fishing pier.



## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Kids given an orientation by 14 different department heads who pitch their activities; this is followed by a 'check it out day' where kids give their intended interests a try. Then kids choose 3 primes which stay and then 2 daily choices that change everyday. Youngest campers (6,7,8) do not have full agency in their schedule. It’s 'guided choice.' Full choice camp. Trolley systems that take kids around from activity to activity.



## 6 · Program Highlights

Participate in Wayne County competition. 1 day Olympics or color war per session. Lots of cerebral competition like chess, who can string a guitar faster, etc. The spirit is kinder than a traditional 3 day color war. Brand new facilities: Full digital arts and tech studio with green screen, YouTubing, movie making and AI; Light up signage; Health center; Fire pit; Canteen; Ropes course modeled after Taconic; Gymnastics pavilion; Lit road leading up to elk view (youngest campers bunks); New for 2025: redone indoor gym (for indoor sports such as basketball); Fishing pier. Activity offerings: Pickleball; Digital arts; Lake activities (waterski, wakeboard, power boats, sailboats, paddle boats, kayaks, inflatables); Music and instrument lessons (guitar, bass, drums); Vocals; Song writing (recording studio); Tennis; Circus with aerials, silks, rings, trapeze; Dance (2 pavilions); Gymnastics; Skate park; Fishing; Cooking; Go Karts; Fine arts (ceramics, photography, stained glass, drawing, painting, cartooning, anime, candle making, jewelry, sculpture, puppetry, knitting, crocheting, junk art, tie dye, plasterwork, graffiti); Performing arts (kids at camp who are on Broadway); Magic; 11 Horses (extra cost); Cross Fit; Soccer; Driving range (no holes); Volleyball; Ropes course; Rock climbing wall; Nature (hike, survival skills, build fires, plants, wildlife); Archery; Basketball; NO BASEBALL; NO SOFTBALL.



## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

No security guard at entrance. Health center. Dining: 2 seatings, older and younger campers. Dining hall has no AC. Stage in dining hall for musical and performing arts performances while kids eat. They can handle any allergy but 'it’s always a conversation.'



## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Director described his camp with the following: 'Intentional diversity,' 'not cut throat competitive,' 'social capital in being smart.' A culture of acceptance and inclusion. 'You can be anything here as long as you’re nice.' 'This is an AND camp. You can be a this AND a that. Not just one type of person.' 'Open hearted.' 'Open minded.' No uniform at all. No religion at all. The spirit is kinder than a traditional 3 day color war. Swag is sold at camp shows and performances for a 'concert feel.' This creates a 'music festival meets camp' type of dynamic. Director took much pride in the work that’s been put into revamping Independent Lake and he seems eager to watch more growth.



## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Sessions and tuitions for 2024: 1) 2 weeks @ $4850 for US and $5050 for international; 2) 3 weeks @ $7050 for US and $7350 for international; 3) 3 weeks @ $7050 for US and $7350 for international. 10% of the camp stays the full time. 5% sibling discount offered. 90% of parents drop off at camp. Busses offered from Livingston, NYC, pick ups at all airports. Performance day instead of visiting day; last day of each session. Everything from sports to Circus performs for parents. Swag is sold at camp shows and performances for a 'concert feel.' Tech policy: Unknown. Extra costs: 11 Horses (extra cost); Swag purchases at shows.



## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Independent Lake is a full choice camp with intentional diversity and a culture of acceptance and inclusion. Kids choose 3 primes that stay and 2 daily choices that change every day, with guided choice for the youngest campers. Brand new facilities support digital arts, circus, music, lake activities, and more, and the spirit is kinder than a traditional 3 day color war. Performance day instead of visiting day caps each session and creates a 'music festival meets camp' vibe.



### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Full choice camp with 3 primes and 2 daily choices (guided choice for ages 6–8).
  - 600 kids at camp; camp has tripled in size in ten years.
  - Intentional diversity; open-hearted, open-minded culture; social capital in being smart.
  - Brand new full digital arts and tech studio (green screen, YouTubing, movie making, AI).
  - Circus (aerials, silks, rings, trapeze), performing arts (kids on Broadway), and robust fine arts.
  - Heated pool and expansive lake program; no one in lake without a life vest.
  - Wayne County competition and a 1-day Olympics/color war with kinder spirit.
  - Department heads have masters degrees in their craft.
  - Trolley systems move campers between activities.
  - Bunks recently redone; lit road to youngest bunks; new for 2025: redone indoor gym.
  - Performance day for parents; 'music festival meets camp' dynamic.



#### Lowlights

  - No security guard at entrance.
  - No AC in bunks and dining hall.
  - No instructional swim.
  - NO BASEBALL; NO SOFTBALL.
  - Lake shared with westmont.



#### Similar Camps

  - Unknown

You can be a this AND a that. Not just one type of person.” “Open hearted.” “Open minded.” Bunks have all been recently re done. No AC. title: Poyntelle — Emily — 7-29-22
text:
# Poyntelle



## 1 · Program Snapshot

The former Lewis Village property is now owned by someone else, but they still refer to their older campers as LV. 220 campers 1st session – Max is 270. 4 or 7 week option. 70% of the campers are from the Tri-State area though they are growing in Maryland and Georgia. Strong population from Queens. 3 Divisions – Juniors (entering 2, 3, 4, 5), Inters (entering 6 & 7) and Lewis Village (entering 8, 9, 10). Upper camp is much lower in numbers than lower camp according to Tom.



## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Visited on 7/25/22 by Emily Salant, Allison Aspis and Lara Weinberg. Tom Troche toured us. He started with Poyntelle in March of 2022 after being at Kenmont/Kenwood for 25 years. He is still learning the lay of the land and is using this summer to become comfortable with traditions, etc before starting to change anything around. Campers can return after LIT summer to be a counselor.



## 3 · Living Quarters

Bunks: Younger kids have a very large common room when you walk in where all of the towels and some equipment are kept. Bunks can hold 15 people so usually 10-12 kids with 3 or 4 counselors. Bunks were spacious. Lewis Village campers live in converted trailers. Full and partial summer campers are in the same bunks. Every little campus of bunks has its own play space. No towel service.



## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

Water: Private motorized Lake. Finds his kids tube much more than waterskiing. Lewis Village has co-ed waterfront. Junior and Intercamp are single sex. The pool isn’t heated right now but looking to get it heated for 2023.



## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Programming: 6 period day. 3 am / 3 pm. Morning activities are structured. Afternoon activities are electives. Electives stay for 2-3 days and then they switch. These are much more relaxed than the morning periods. Instructional swim is an elective. Color war both sessions – 1st session 2 days, 2nd session 4 days.



## 6 · Program Highlights

LIT program for rising 11th graders. Come to camp for 2 weeks, Israel for 3 weeks and then back to camp for the last 2 weeks. 2 plays a summer: Junior camp 1st session, Upper camp 2nd session. 2023 pipeline: Fresh paint on all the buildings. Heating the pool. Turf Field. Rec Hall update.



## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

Dining: 1st session, 2 seatings; 2nd session, 1 seating. Sit by bunk at round tables. Can handle all allergies. Not Kosher but can take kids that need kosher only.



## 8 · Vibe and Culture

Religion: Say hamotzi at every meal. Fri night: Wear White. 40-minute service led by oldest campers. Give out value bracelets and recap the week. Mon are "Mitzvah Mondays" and they also learn a Hebrew word of the day. About 12% of campers not Jewish.



## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Post photos every day – no facial recognition. Travel logistics: Unknown. Parent communication beyond photos: Unknown. Tech policy: Unknown. Extra costs: Unknown.



## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Camp Poyntelle offers 4 or 7 week option with 220 campers 1st session – Max is 270, and 3 Divisions – Juniors (entering 2, 3, 4, 5), Inters (entering 6 & 7) and Lewis Village (entering 8, 9, 10). Programming is a 6 period day (3 am / 3 pm) with structured mornings and relaxed afternoon electives that stay for 2-3 days before they switch, and instructional swim is an elective. Waterfront includes a private motorized lake (finds his kids tube much more than waterskiing), LV co-ed waterfront with Junior and Intercamp single sex, and the pool isn’t heated right now but looking to get it heated for 2023. Dining can handle all allergies (not Kosher but can take kids that need kosher only), Color war runs both sessions, and there are 2 plays a summer plus an LIT program for rising 11th graders (2 weeks at camp, 3 weeks in Israel, then back to camp for the last 2 weeks).



### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Private motorized Lake.
  - Color war both sessions – 1st session 2 days, 2nd session 4 days.
  - 2 plays a summer: Junior camp 1st session, Upper camp 2nd session.
  - LIT program for rising 11th graders (2 weeks at camp, Israel for 3 weeks, then back to camp for the last 2 weeks).
  - Post photos every day – no facial recognition.
  - Can handle all allergies; not Kosher but can take kids that need kosher only.
  - 3 Divisions – Juniors, Inters and Lewis Village.
  - Bunks were spacious.
  - 70% of the campers are from the Tri-State area though they are growing in Maryland and Georgia.
  - Strong population from Queens.



#### Lowlights

  - Overall impression is that this camp needs some love.
  - He is still learning the lay of the land and is using this summer to become comfortable with traditions, etc before starting to change anything around.
  - The pool isn’t heated right now.
  - Upper camp is much lower in numbers than lower camp according to Tom.
  - No towel service.
  - The former Lewis Village property is now owned by someone else.



#### Similar Camps

  - Pinemere
  - Kinder Ring
  - Laurelwood
  - Berkshire Hills



## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Camp Poyntelle: Visited on 7/25/22 by Emily Salant, Allison Aspis and Lara Weinberg. Tom Troche toured us. He started with Poyntelle in March of 2022 after being at Kenmont/Kenwood for 25 years. He is still learning the lay of the land and is using this summer to become comfortable with traditions, etc before starting to change anything around. The former Lewis Village property is now owned by someone else, but they still refer to their older campers as LV. 220 campers 1 st session – Max is 270. 4 or 7 week option 70% of the campers are from the Tri-State area though they are growing in Maryland and Georgia. Strong population from Queens. Post photos every day – no facial recognition No towel service 3 Divisions – Juniors (entering 2, 3, 4, 5), Inters (entering 6 & 7) and Lewis Village (entering 8, 9, 10) LIT program for rising 11 th graders. Come to camp for 2 weeks, Israel for 3 weeks and then back to camp for the last 2 weeks. Campers can return after LIT summer to be a counselor. Upper camp is much lower in numbers than lower camp according to Tom. Programming: 6 period day. 3 am / 3 pm Morning activities are structured. Afternoon activities are electives. Electives stay for 2-3 days and then they switch. These are much more relaxed than the morning periods. Instructional swim is an elective. The pool isn’t heated right now but looking to get it heated for 2023. 2023 pipeline: Fresh paint on all the buildings Heating the pool Turf Field Rec Hall update Overall impression is that this camp needs some love. title: WAYNE FOR GIRLS — MICHELE — 7-1-24
text:
# WAYNE FOR GIRLS



## 1 · Program Snapshot

Wayne for Girls is 104 years old and 3rd generation owned/operated. The grounds are beautiful and well maintained. It’s a physically smaller campus (but has everything) and feels quaint and like a family atmosphere. The softball, volleyball, soccer, lacrosse fields and driving range are on one side of the road where the rest of camp is all together. They think of themselves as a true brother/sister camp with Wayne for Boys, but the programming is completely separate and the boys and girls see each other once a week. Brothers/sisters/cousins see each other twice a week. There is a little trail around the lake to connect the two camps; they are located at the opposite end of the lake. Offer 1 time only 4 week option for first time campers.



## 2 · Camper, Staff and Director Profile

Matt has great energy and will be our contact with families. He and his wife Adena work as a team and he made sure we met her during our tour. Counselors must be college age and this is how he keeps them at camp; they have their own bunks. Matt describes a typical camper as someone from the northeast, Jewish, not over the top, and has strength in relationships.



## 3 · Living Quarters

Bunks: all single beds for campers until 8th grade. Each bunk has little window curtains which gives it a warm vibe. 12-14 girls per bunk. Great bathrooms with shower doors on the showers\! They have a strict packing list. If a girl overpacks the extra items are all stored away in the attic.



## 4 · Waterfront & Aquatics

The lake is a gorgeous lake for PA\! All motorized boats/water toys. They do not have a pool and have a fresh water pool in the lake. Nice beach-like sand all around the lake.



## 5 · Daily Timetable & Choice

Programming – very traditional/structured. Girls travel together by age group – 3 bunks per division. One elective each day. They participate in lots of intercamp games. Everyone makes a team. Girls can come in and do early bird practice in the morning.



## 6 · Program Highlights

3 theater productions throughout the summer (this is only co-ed activity). 4 day traditional color war towards the end of summer. Nice, renovated fine arts building. Tie-dye/sculpture/digital photography/rock band with great rock studio. They have a dedicated night where all 20 of the bands play in a concert. Gymnastics – the coach was once ranked 9th in the world. Cheer/Dance Dome is a great space and they have a very robust dance program. Big tennis program and girls can take private lessons for no extra fee. Canteen building is new and a great space indoor/outdoor.



## 7 · Health, Safety, and Food

No AC at camp except health center. The health center was rebuilt 10 years ago. Dining Hall – all camp eats at one meal together. 11th/12th graders are waitresses. Campers sit by bunk. Recently renovated and extended the building to have extra space for a 'bar' area – they have breakfast bar, salad bar. They do have a chef for food allergies. Neat organic garden at the front of camp. Girls make food from it.



## 8 · Vibe and Culture

It feels quaint and like a family atmosphere. Their address is "Nice People Place" and they talk about being nice throughout their programming and the importance of talking and working things out.



## 9 · Travel Logistics, Parent Communication, Tech & Extra Costs

Matt will be our contact with families. No uniforms. Big tennis program and girls can take private lessons for no extra fee. Travel logistics: Unknown. Parent communication details: Unknown (beyond Matt being the contact). Tech policy: Unknown. Extra costs: Unknown.



## 10 · Ideal Fit & Four-Sentence Summary

Wayne for Girls is 104 years old and 3rd generation owned/operated, with beautiful, well-maintained grounds on a physically smaller campus that feels quaint and like a family atmosphere. As a true brother/sister camp with Wayne for Boys, programming is completely separate and the boys and girls see each other once a week (siblings/cousins twice), connected by a little trail around the lake. Programming is very traditional/structured: girls travel together by age group (3 bunks per division) with one elective each day, lots of intercamp games where everyone makes a team, and standout offerings like 3 theater productions, a 4 day traditional color war, a renovated fine arts/rock band program, gymnastics with a coach once ranked 9th in the world, dance in the Cheer/Dance Dome, and a big tennis program with private lessons for no extra fee. The lake is gorgeous for PA with all motorized boats/water toys, a fresh water pool in the lake (no separate pool), and nice beach-like sand, and the camp culture leans "Nice People Place"—they talk about being nice and the importance of talking and working things out.



### Internal Insights

#### Highlights

  - Beautiful and well maintained grounds; physically smaller campus (but has everything) that feels quaint and like a family atmosphere.
  - True brother/sister camp with Wayne for Boys; little trail around the lake.
  - Very traditional/structured programming; girls travel together by age group (3 bunks per division) with one elective each day.
  - Lots of intercamp games; everyone makes a team.
  - 3 theater productions (only co-ed activity).
  - 4 day traditional color war.
  - Nice, renovated fine arts building; tie-dye/sculpture/digital photography/rock band with great rock studio.
  - Dedicated night where all 20 bands play in a concert.
  - Gymnastics coach was once ranked 9th in the world; early bird practice.
  - Cheer/Dance Dome and very robust dance program.
  - Big tennis program; private lessons for no extra fee.
  - Gorgeous lake, all motorized boats/water toys; beach-like sand.
  - All camp eats at one meal together; breakfast bar and salad bar; chef for food allergies.
  - Neat organic garden; girls make food from it.
  - Canteen building is new and a great indoor/outdoor space.
  - Counselors must be college age.



#### Lowlights

  - No AC at camp except health center.
  - No separate pool (fresh water pool in the lake).
  - Physically smaller campus.
  - Programming is very traditional/structured with one elective each day.
  - Boys and girls see each other once a week (limited co-ed).
  - Strict packing list; overpacked items stored in attic.



#### Similar Camps

  - Wayne for Boys



## 11 · Original Write-Up (Verbatim)

Matt has great energy and will be our contact with families. He and his wife Adena work as a team and he made sure we met her during our tour. Wayne for Girls is 104 years old and 3 rd generation owned/operated. ? The grounds are beautiful and well maintained. It’s a physically smaller campus (but has everything) and feels quaint and like a family atmosphere. The softball, volleyball, soccer, lacrosse fields and driving range are on one side of the road where the rest of camp is all together. ? They think of themselves as a true brother/sister camp with Wayne for Boys, but the programming is completely separate and the boys and girls see each other once a week. Brothers/sisters/cousins see each other twice a week. There is a little trail around the lake to connect the two camps. They are located at the opposite end of the lake. ? Neat organic garden at the front of camp. Girls make food from it. ? The lake is a gorgeous lake for PA\! All motorized boats/water toys. They do not have a pool and have a fresh water pool in the lake. Nice beach like sand all around the lake ? Offer 1 time only 4 week option for first time campers. ? 3 theater productions throughout the summer (this is only co-ed activity) ? 4 day traditional color war towards the end of summer ? Programming – very traditional/structured. Girls travel together by age group – 3 bunks per division. One elective each day. They participate in lots of intercamp games. Everyone makes a team ? Nice, renovated fine arts building. Tie die/sculpture/digital photography/rock band with great rock studio. They have a dedicated night where all 20 of the bands play in a concert ? Gymnastics – the coach was once ranked 9 th in the world. Girls can come in and do early bird practice in the morning. ? Cheer/Dance Dome is a great space and they have a very robust dance program ? Bunks: all single beds for campers until 8 th grade. Each bunk has little window curtains which gives it a warm vibe. 12-14 girls per bunk. Great bathrooms with shower doors on the showers\! They have strict packing list. If a girl overpacks the extra items are all stored away in the attic. ? No uniforms ? No AC at camp except health center. The health center was rebuild 10 years ago ? Dining Hall – all camp eats at one meal together. 11 th /12 th graders are waitresses. Counselors must be college age and this is how he keeps them at camp. They have their own bunks. Campers sit by bunk. Recently renovated and extended the building to have extra space for “bar” area – they have breakfast bar, salad bar. They do have a chef for food allergies. ? Big tennis program and girls can take private lessons for no extra fee ? Canteen building is new and a great space indoor/outdoor. ? Matt describes a typical camper as someone from the northeast, Jewish, not over the top, and has strength in relationships. Their address is “Nice People Place” and they talk about being nice throughout their programming and the importance of talking and working things out.
